TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Borax[COLOR_REVERT] is an important boron compound, a mineral, and a salt of boric acid. Powdered borax is white, consisting of soft colorless crystals that dissolve in water. Borax is a component of many detergents, cosmetics, and enamel glazes, and is used also in biochemistry, metallurgy, gold mining, and many other fields. Borax was first discovered in dry lake beds in Tibet and was imported via the Silk Road to the Arabian Peninsula in the 8th century AD, but was popularized only in late 19th century.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]borace[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un importante composto di boro, un minerale e un sale di acido borico. Il borace in polvere รจ bianco, costituito da morbidi cristalli incolori che si dissolvono in acqua. Il borace รจ un componente di molti detergenti, cosmetici e smalti e viene utilizzato anche in biochimica, metallurgia, estrazione dell'oro e molti altri campi. Il borace fu scoperto per la prima volta negli aridi bacini lacustri del Tibet e fu importato, lungo la Via della seta, nella penisola arabica nell'VIII secolo, ma fu reso popolare solo alla fine del XIX secolo.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Boraks rodzimy[COLOR_REVERT], to minera³ z gromady boranów, zbudowany g³ównie z uwodnionego boranu sodu. Najczêœciej tworzy skupienia ziemiste, zbite i naskorupienia. Kryszta³y maj¹ pokrój tabliczkowy, s³upkowy โ czasami widaæ wyraŸne pionowe pr¹¿kowanie. Jest kruchy, przezroczysty, charakteryzuje siê s³odkawo-cierpkim smakiem, jest rozpuszczalny w wodzie.
TEXT.Russian: รรจรญรชร รซรผ (รรณรฐร , รรฎรฐร รชรฑ) รจรฌรฅรฅรฒ รธรจรฐรฎรชรจรฉ รฑรฏรฅรชรฒรฐ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรญรฅรญรจรฟ. รรญ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รชรฎรฌรฏรฎรญรฅรญรฒรฎรฌ รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รฌรฎรพรนรจรต รฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรข, รชรฎรฑรฌรฅรฒรจรชรจ, รฝรฌร รซรจ รจ รฃรซร รงรณรฐรจ. รรญ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฐรจ รฏรฐรจรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญรจรจ รกรณรดรฅรฐรญรปรต รฐร รฑรฒรขรฎรฐรฎรข รข รกรจรฎรตรจรฌรจรจ, รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รฎรฃรญรฅรณรฏรฎรฐรญรฎรฉ รครฎรกร รขรชรจ, รซรจรกรฎ ร รญรฒรจ-รฃรฐรจรกรชรฎรขรฎรฃรฎ รฑรฎรฅรครจรญรฅรญรจรฟ รครซรฟ รฑรฒรฅรชรซรฎรขรฎรซรฎรชรญร , รซรจรกรฎ รชร รช รดรซรพรฑ รข รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรณรฐรฃรจรจ, รญรฅรฉรฒรฐรฎรญรญรฎ-รงร รตรขร รฒรญร รฟ รนรจรฒรป รครซรฟ รฐร รครจรฎร รชรฒรจรขรญรปรต รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรฎรข, รฒรฅรชรฑรฒรณรฐรจรฐรฎรขร รญรปรต รครฎรกร รขรฎรช รข รชรณรซรจรญร รฐรจรจ, รจ รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รฏรฐรฅรครธรฅรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรจรชร รครซรฟ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รฑรฎรฅรครจรญรฅรญรจรฉ รกรฎรฐร .

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]brass instrument[COLOR_REVERT] is a musical instrument whose sound is produced by sympathetic vibration of air in a tubular resonator in sympathy with the vibration of the player's lips. Brass instruments are also called labrosones, literally meaning lip-vibrated instruments.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]instrument ร cuivre[COLOR_REVERT] est un instrument de musique dont le son est produit par la vibration sympathique de l'air dans un rรฉsonateur tubulaire en sympathie avec la vibration des lรจvres du joueur. Les instruments ร cuivre sont รฉgalement appelรฉs labrosones, signifiant littรฉralement instruments ร vibration labiale.
TEXT.Italian: Gli [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]strumenti musicali in ottone[COLOR_REVERT] (piรน correttamente chiamati "aerofoni a bocchino") sono una sottoclasse degli aerofoni, ossia degli strumenti musicali nei quali il suono รจ prodotto dalla vibrazione dell'aria immessa dal musicista, appoggiando le labbra ad un bocchino collegato ad un tubo cavo e soffiando; l'aria genera poi delle vibrazioni che producono il suono.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Instrument dêty[COLOR_REVERT], to instrument muzyczny, w którym Ÿród³em dŸwiêku jest drgaj¹cy wewn¹trz instrumentu s³up powietrza. Wysokoœæ dŸwiêku zale¿y od d³ugoœci rury, która tworzy instrument. Im d³u¿sza rura, tym ni¿szy maksymalny dŸwiêk mo¿na uzyskaæ, natomiast dŸwiêki wy¿sze uzyskuje siê skracaj¹c wysokoœæ s³upa powietrza na ró¿ne sposoby (w zale¿noœci od instrumentu). Barwa dŸwiêków zale¿y od konstrukcji instrumentu, rodzaju materia³u, z którego jest wykonany, rodzaju wibratora, sposobu zadêcia itd. TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Bread[COLOR_REVERT] is a food product obtained by fermenting, molding, leavening and subsequently baking in an oven a dough made from normal or wholemeal cereals flour and water. Bread can be packaged in different ways, as well as enriched or characterized by different ingredients, depending on the place where it is prepared.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]pain[COLOR_REVERT] est un produit alimentaire obtenu en faisant fermenter, en faรงonnant, en faisant lever puis en cuisant au four une pรขte faite de farine de cรฉrรฉales normales ou complรจtes et d'eau. Le pain peut รชtre conditionnรฉ de diffรฉrentes maniรจres, ainsi qu'enrichi ou caractรฉrisรฉ par diffรฉrents ingrรฉdients, selon le lieu oรน il est prรฉparรฉ.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]pane[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un prodotto alimentare ottenuto dalla fermentazione, formatura, lievitazione e successiva cottura in forno di un impasto a base di farina (normale o integrale) di cereali e acqua. Il pane puรฒ essere confezionato con diverse modalitร , arricchito e caratterizzato frequentemente da ingredienti differenti, a seconda del luogo in cui viene preparato.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Chleb[COLOR_REVERT], to pieczywo otrzymywane z wypieku ciasta, bêd¹cego jednorodnym po³¹czeniem m¹ki i wody, poddanego najczêœciej, ale nie zawsze, fermentacji alkoholowej, wyrobionego w bochny ró¿nego kszta³tu i wielkoœci.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]brick[COLOR_REVERT] is a building material used to make walls, pavements and other elements in masonry construction. Traditionally, a brick was made of clay, but its composition varied into a mix of clay-bearing soil, sand, and lime, or concrete materials. Bricks can be joined together using mortar, adhesives or by interlocking them. Bricks are produced in numerous classes, types, materials, and sizes which vary with region and time period, and are produced in bulk quantities.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]mattone[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un materiale da costruzione utilizzato per realizzare muri, pavimenti e altre costruzioni. Tradizionalmente, un mattone era fatto di argilla, ma la sua composizione รจ variata in un mix di terreno argilloso, sabbia e calce. I mattoni possono essere uniti insieme usando malta, adesivi o semplicemente incastrandoli fra loro. I mattoni sono prodotti in numerose forme, tipi, materiali e dimensioni, che variano a seconda della regione e del periodo di tempo e sono prodotti in grandi quantitร .

Ceg³y s³u¿¹ m.in. do wznoszenia œcian, murów, filarów, s³upów, a tak¿e fundamentów i œcian fundamentowych.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Bug[COLOR_REVERT] is a collective name for an order of animals comprising some 50,000 to 80,000 species such as the cicadas, aphids, plant-hoppers, leaf-hoppers, and shield bugs. They range in size from 1 mm (0.04 in) to around 15 cm (6 in), and share a common arrangement of sucking mouthparts. Most feed on plants, using their sucking and piercing mouthparts to extract plant sap. Some are parasitic, while others are predators that feed on other insects or small invertebrates. Some species are significant agricultural pests, damaging crops or being the vectors of serious viral diseases. Other species have been used for biological control of insect pests.
TEXT.German: Die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Insekten[COLOR_REVERT] (Insecta) sind die artenreichste Klasse im gesamten Tierreich. Mehr als 60% aller wissenschaftlich beschriebenen Tierarten sind Insekten. Sie verfรผgen รผber ein Exoskelett aus Sklerotin und Chitin, das deutlich sichtbar in drei Teile (Kopf, Brust und Hinterleib) unterteilt ist, und genau drei Beinpaare. Insekten sind fรผr frรผhe und naturnahe Kulturen eine gute Eiweiรquelle, denn ihre Kรถrper bestehen zu groรen Teilen aus Proteinen. Sie sind arm an Kohlenhydraten, aber ihr Energiewert ist mit dem von Vogel- oder Sรคugetierfleisch vergleichbar. Aktuell werden รผber 2000 Insektenarten als essbar eingestuft. Am hรคufigsten werden Kรคfer, Hautflรผgler wie Ameisen, Bienen oder Wespen, Heuschrecken und Schmetterlinge verzehrt. Verschiedene Insektenarten haben auch andere Nutzen fรผr den Menschen und werden deshalb gezielt gezรผchtet, so z.B. Honigbiene, Seidenspinnerraupe, Schlupfwespe (zur Schรคdlingsbekรคmpfung) und Schildlaus (zur Gewinnung von Farbstoffen und Lacken). Heute werden viele Insekten pharmazeutisch genutzt und in der Kriminalistik spielen die abgelegten Larven bestimmter Fliegen und Kรคfer eine wichtige Rolle.
TEXT.Italian: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Insetto[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un nome collettivo per un ordine di animali che comprende fra le 50.000 e le 80.000 specie animali, fra cui cicale, afidi, cavallette, cavallette e insetti scudo. Le dimensioni variano da 1 mm a circa 15 cm e condividono una disposizione comune di boccagli succhianti. La maggior parte si nutre di piante, usando i loro boccagli per estrarre linfa vegetale. Alcuni sono parassiti, mentre altri sono predatori che si nutrono di altri insetti o piccoli invertebrati. Alcune specie sono considerate dei parassiti in ambito agricolo, danneggiando colture o essendo vettori di gravi malattie virali. Altre specie sono state utilizzate per il controllo biologico dei parassiti.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Robakami[COLOR_REVERT] w jêzyku potocznym okreœla siê wszystkie bezkrêgowce o ob³ych kszta³tach i miêkkim ciele o d³ugoœci wiêkszej ni¿ szerokoœæ, np. larwy owadów takie jak czerw lub pêdrak, a nawet wszystkie drobne bezkrêgowce, zw³aszcza paso¿ytnicze lub szczególnie dokuczliwe, w tym owady biegaj¹ce (np. prusak, karaluch).

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Caesium[COLOR_REVERT] is a soft, silvery-golden alkali metal with a melting point of 28.5 ยฐC (83.3 ยฐF), which makes it one of only five elemental metals that are liquid at or near room temperature. German chemist Robert Bunsen and physicist Gustav Kirchhoff discovered caesium in 1860 by the newly developed method of flame spectroscopy. In 1967, the International System of Units used two specific wave counts from an emission spectrum of caesium-133 to co-define the second and the meter. Since then, caesium has been widely used in highly accurate atomic clocks.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cesio[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un metallo alcalino morbido argenteo-dorato, con un punto di fusione di 28,5 ยฐC, che lo rende uno dei soli cinque metalli elementari liquidi a temperatura ambiente o quasi. Il chimico tedesco Robert Bunsen e il fisico tedesco Gustav Kirchhoff scoprirono il cesio nel 1860 attraverso l'innovativo metodo della spettroscopia di fiamma. Nel 1967, il Sistema internazionale di unitร di misura adottรฒ due conteggi d'onda specifici da uno spettro di emissione di cesio 133 per definire il secondo e il metro. Da allora, il cesio รจ stato ampiamente utilizzato negli orologi atomici ad alta precisione.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cez[COLOR_REVERT], to miêkki, srebrnoz³oty metal alkaliczny topniej¹cy w temperaturze 28,5 ยฐC, co czyni go jednym z piêciu elementarnych metali wystêpuj¹cych w formie p³ynnej w okolicach temperatury pokojowej.
TEXT.Russian: ะฆะตะทะธะน - ัะตะปะพัะฝะพะน ะผะตัะฐะปะป, ะธะผะตััะธะน ัะธะทะธัะตัะบะธะต ะธ ั ะธะผะธัะตัะบะธะต ัะฒะพะนััะฒะฐ, ะฐะฝะฐะปะพะณะธัะฝัะต ััะฑะธะดะธั ะธ ะบะฐะปะธั. ะะตัะฐะปะป ัะฒะปัะตััั ััะตะทะฒััะฐะนะฝะพ ัะตะฐะบัะธะฒะฝัะผ ะธ ะฟะธัะพัะพัะฝัะผ, ัะตะฐะณะธััััะธะผ ั ะฒะพะดะพะน ะดะฐะถะต ะฟัะธ ัะตะผะฟะตัะฐัััะต ะฒ -116 ะณัะฐะดััะพะฒ ะฟะพ ะฆะตะปััะธั. ะะฐะธะผะตะฝะตะต ัะปะตะบััะพะพััะธัะฐัะตะปัะฝัะน ัะปะตะผะตะฝั ะฒ ััะฐะฑะธะปัะฝะพะผ ะธะทะพัะพะฟะต - ัะตะทะธะน-133. ะฆะตะทะธะน ะดะพะฑัะฒะฐะตััั ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ะธะท ะฟะพะปะปััะธัะพะฒ, ะฒ ัะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะบะฐะบ ัะฐะดะธะพะฝัะบะปะธะดั, ะพัะพะฑะตะฝะฝะพ ัะตะทะธะน-137, ะธะทะฒะปะตะบะฐัั ะธะท ะพัั ะพะดะพะฒ, ะพะฑัะฐะทัััะธั ัั ะฒ ัะตะทัะปััะฐัะต ัะดะตัะฝัั ัะตะฐะบัะพัะพะฒ.

TEXT.English: An [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]electronic calculator[COLOR_REVERT] is a small, portable, often inexpensive electronic device used to perform calculations, ranging from basic arithmetic to complex mathematics. The first solid-state electronic calculator was created in the early 1960s. Pocket-sized devices became available in the 1970s, especially after the Intel 4004, the first microprocessor, was developed by Intel for the Japanese calculator company Busicom.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]calculatrice รฉlectronique[COLOR_REVERT] est un petit appareil รฉlectronique portable, souvent peu coรปteux, utilisรฉ pour effectuer des calculs, allant de l'arithmรฉtique de base aux mathรฉmatiques complexes. La premiรจre calculatrice รฉlectronique ร semi-conducteurs a รฉtรฉ crรฉรฉe au dรฉbut des annรฉes 1960. Les appareils de poche sont devenus disponibles dans les annรฉes 1970, surtout aprรจs que l'Intel 4004, le premier microprocesseur, a รฉtรฉ dรฉveloppรฉ par Intel pour la sociรฉtรฉ japonaise de calculatrices Busicom.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]calcolatrice[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un piccolo dispositivo portatile, spesso economico, utilizzato per eseguire calcoli che vanno dall'aritmetica di base alla matematica complessa. Il primo calcolatore elettronico a stato solido รจ stato creato nei primi anni sessanta. Le versioni tascabili sono diventati disponibili negli anni settanta, in particolare dopo che l'Intel 4004, il primo microprocessore, รจ stato sviluppato da Intel per la societร giapponese di calcolatori Busicom.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]camel[COLOR_REVERT] is an even-toed ungulate, that bears distinctive fatty deposits known as "humps" on its back. Camels have long been domesticated and, as livestock, they provide food (milk and meat) and textiles (fiber and felt from hair). Camels are working animals especially suited to their desert habitat and are a vital means of transport for passengers and cargo.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chameau[COLOR_REVERT] est un ongulรฉ ร nombre pair de doigts, qui porte des dรฉpรดts graisseux distinctifs connus sous le nom de "bosses" sur son dos. Les chameaux sont domestiquรฉs depuis longtemps et, en tant que bรฉtail, ils fournissent de la nourriture (lait et viande) et des textiles (fibres et feutre ร partir des poils). Les chameaux sont des animaux de travail particuliรจrement adaptรฉs ร leur habitat dรฉsertique et constituent un moyen de transport vital pour les passagers et les marchandises.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kamele[COLOR_REVERT] (zur Abgrenzung von Lamas eigentlich Altweltkamele) sind Paarhufer, die einen (Dromedar) oder zwei (Trampeltier) markante Hรถcker auf dem Rรผcken tragen, die als Fettspeicher dienen. Wegen ihrer guten Anpassung an extreme Lebensrรคume, die es ihnen z.B. ermรถglicht, in der Wรผste lange ohne Wasser auszukommen, sind sie fรผr den Menschen sehr interessant. Seit ihrer Domestizierung im letzten Jahrtausend v.Chr. werden sie vor allem als Last-, Zug- oder Reittiere eingesetzt. Aber sie sind auch gute Rohstofflieferanten. Neben Fleisch, Milch, Leder und Wolle werden besonders in nomadischen Kulturen sogar ihre Ausscheidungen genutzt. Der Dung stellt einen hervorragenden Brennstoff dar und mit dem Urin werden z.B. Lรคuse aus den Haaren gewaschen. Seit dem 9. Jahrhundert v.Chr. ist eine militรคrische Nutzung des Kamels bekannt. Kamelreitereinheiten gibt es auch heute noch in รgypten und Indien. Im Spรคtmittelalter fรผhrten die Mongolen die Tradition der Kamelrennen ein, die sich in arabischen Lรคndern groรer Beliebtheit erfreut. Bei den Beduinen sind Kamele Statussymbole, denn sie stehen fรผr den Reichtum einer Familie.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cammello[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un ungulato, che porta sul dorso dei distintivi depositi di grasso, noti come "gobbe". I cammelli sono stati da lungo tempo addomesticati e, come bestiame, forniscono cibo (latte e carne) e tessuti (fibre e feltro dai peli). I cammelli sono animali da lavoro particolarmente adatti a vivere nel deserto e rappresentano un mezzo di trasporto vitale per passeggeri e merci.

Wielb³¹dy wykorzystywane s¹ jako wierzchowce, zwierzêta juczne, Ÿród³o mleka, we³ny, miêsa i skóry, a tak¿e w celach rozrywkowych (cyrki, wyœcigi wielb³¹dów). W przesz³oœci wykorzystywane by³y równie¿ do celów transportowych ze wzglêdu na to, ¿e potrafi³y przenosiæ ³adunki o masie wynosz¹cej 220โ270 kg.

ะพะฝะธ ะดะฐัั ะผะพะปะพะบะพ ะธ ะผััะพ ะธ ัะฒะปััััั ัะฐะฑะพัะธะผะธ ะถะธะฒะพัะฝัะผะธ.[PARAGRAPH:2]ะขะตัะผะธะฝ ยซะฒะตัะฑะปัะดยป ัะฐะบะถะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะฒ ะฑะพะปะตะต ัะธัะพะบะพะผ ัะผััะปะต ะดะปั ะพะฟะธัะฐะฝะธั ะปัะฑะพะณะพ ะธะท ัะตััะธ ะฒะตัะฑะปัะดะพะฟะพะดะพะฑะฝัั ัััะตััะฒ ะฒ ัะตะผะตะนััะฒะต Camelidae: ะดะฒัั ะฝะฐััะพััะธั ะฒะตัะฑะปัะดะพะฒ ะธ ัะตัััะตั ัะถะฝะพะฐะผะตัะธะบะฐะฝัะบะธั ะฒะตัะฑะปัะดะพะฒ: ะปะฐะผั, ะฐะปัะฟะฐะบะธ, ะณัะฐะฝะฐะบะพ ะธ ะฒะธะบัะฝัะธ.[PARAGRAPH:2]ะกัะตะดะฝัั ะฟัะพะดะพะปะถะธัะตะปัะฝะพััั ะถะธะทะฝะธ ะฒะตัะฑะปัะดะฐ ัะพััะฐะฒะปัะตั ะพั 40 ะดะพ 50 ะปะตั. ะะทัะพัะปัะน ะฒะทัะพัะปัะน ะฒะตัะฑะปัะด ัะพััะฐะฒะปัะตั 1,85 ะผ (6 ัััะพะฒ 1 ะดัะนะผ) ะฒ ะฟะปะตัะต ะธ 2,15 ะผ (7 ัััะพะฒ 1 ะดัะนะผ) ะฝะฐ ะณะพัะฑะธะฝะต. ะะพัะฑ ะฟะพะดะฝะธะผะฐะตััั ะฝะฐะด ัะตะปะพะผ ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ ะฝะฐ 30 ะดัะนะผะพะฒ (76,20 ัะผ). ะะตัะฑะปัะดั ะผะพะณัั ะฑะตะณะฐัั ัะพ ัะบะพัะพัััั ะดะพ 65 ะบะผ/ั (40 ะผะธะปั/ั) ะบะพัะพัะบะธะผะธ ััะฒะบะฐะผะธ ะธ ะฒัะดะตัะถะธะฒะฐัั ัะบะพัะพััั ะดะพ 40 ะบะผ /ั (25 ะผะธะปั/ั).[PARAGRAPH:2]ะัะบะพะฟะฐะตะผัะต ะพััะฐัะบะธ ัะบะฐะทัะฒะฐัั ะฝะฐ ัะพ, ััะพ ะฟัะตะดะบะธ ัะพะฒัะตะผะตะฝะฝัั ะฒะตัะฑะปัะดะพะฒ ัะฐะทะฒะธะปะธัั ะฒ ะกะตะฒะตัะฝะพะน ะะผะตัะธะบะต ะฒ ะฟะตัะธะพะด ะฟะฐะปะตะพะณะตะฝะฐ (ัะผ. ัะฐะบะถะต ะะฐะผะตะปะพะฟั), ะฐ ะทะฐัะตะผ ัะฐัะฟัะพัััะฐะฝะธะปะธัั ะฝะฐ ะฑะพะปัััั ัะฐััั ะะทะธะธ. ะะธัะตะปะธ ะดัะตะฒะฝะตะณะพ ะกะพะผะฐะปะธ ะธะปะธ ัะฐัััะฒะฐ ะัะฝั ะฒะฟะตัะฒัะต ะฟัะธัััะธะปะธ ะฒะตัะฑะปัะดะพะฒ ะทะฐะดะพะปะณะพ ะดะพ 2000 ะณะพะดะฐ ะดะพ ะฝะฐัะตะน ััั.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]candle[COLOR_REVERT] is an ignitable wick embedded in a block of wax, or another flammable solid substance such as tallow, that provides light, and in some cases, a fragrance. A candle can also provide heat or a method of keeping time.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]bougie[COLOR_REVERT] est une mรจche inflammable intรฉgrรฉe dans un bloc de cire, ou une autre substance solide inflammable comme le suif, qui fournit de la lumiรจre et, dans certains cas, un parfum. Une bougie peut รฉgalement fournir de la chaleur ou une mรฉthode pour mesurer le temps.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]candela[COLOR_REVERT] รจ uno stoppino infiammabile, incorporato in un blocco di cera o di un'altra sostanza solida infiammabile come il sego, che fornisce luce e, in alcuni casi, una fragranza. Una candela puรฒ anche fornire calore o essere usata per tenere il tempo.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Candy[COLOR_REVERT], specifically sugar candy, is a confection made from a concentrated solution of sugar in water, to which flavorings and colorants are added. Candies come in numerous colors and varieties and have a long history in popular culture.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]bonbons[COLOR_REVERT], en particulier les sucreries, sont des confiseries fabriquรฉes ร partir d'une solution concentrรฉe de sucre dans l'eau, ร laquelle sont ajoutรฉs des arรดmes et des colorants. Les bonbons se dรฉclinent en de nombreuses couleurs et variรฉtรฉs et ont une longue histoire dans la culture populaire.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]caramella[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un prodotto dolciario con un'elevata quantitร di zucchero e altri componenti, per lo piรน aromi naturali, latte, cacao, varie forme di agglutinanti (amido o gelatina) e, talune volte, coloranti e aromi artificiali.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cukierek[COLOR_REVERT], to wyrób cukierniczy wykonany ze stê¿onego roztworu cukru w wodzie, do którego dodawane s¹ aromaty i barwniki. Cukierki wystêpuj¹ w wielu ró¿nych kolorach, smakach i kszta³tach.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Canned food[COLOR_REVERT] is food processed and sealed in an airtight container (glass jars, steel cans or tin cans). Canning provides a shelf life typically ranging from one to five years, although under specific circumstances it can be much longer. Canning has been invented by French confectioner and brewer Nicolas Appert in 1809, by observing that food cooked inside a jar did not spoil unless the seals leaked. It would take another 50 years, though, for Louis Pasteur to demonstrate the role of microbes in food spoilage.
TEXT.Italian: Gli [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]alimenti in scatola[COLOR_REVERT] sono alimenti trasformati e sigillati in un contenitore ermetico (barattoli di vetro, lattine di acciaio o lattine di stagno). L'inscatolamento permette di mantenere il cibo per un tempo che varia da uno a cinque anni, anche se in circostanze specifiche puรฒ essere molto piรน lungo. L'inscatolamento รจ stato inventato dal pasticcere e birraio francese Nicolas Appert nel 1809, che osservรฒ che il cibo cotto all'interno di un barattolo non si rovinava, a meno che i sigilli non facessero trapelare il contenuto. Ci sarebbero voluti altri 50 anni, tuttavia, perchรฉ Louis Pasteur dimostrasse il ruolo dei microbi nel deterioramento degli alimenti.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dry ice[COLOR_REVERT] is the solid form of carbon dioxide. It is used primarily as a cooling agent. Its advantages include lower temperature than that of water ice and not leaving any residue (other than incidental frost from moisture in the atmosphere). It is useful for preserving frozen foods where mechanical cooling is unavailable. Dry ice sublimates at 194.65 ยฐK (-78.5 ยฐC; -109.3 ยฐF) at Earth atmospheric pressures. This extreme cold makes the solid dangerous to handle without protection due to burns caused by freezing (frostbite). While generally not very toxic, the out-gassing from it can cause abnormally elevated carbon dioxide blood levels.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ghiaccio secco[COLOR_REVERT] รจ la forma solida dell'anidride carbonica. Viene utilizzato principalmente come agente di raffreddamento. I suoi vantaggi includono la temperatura inferiore a quella del ghiaccio normale e non lasciare alcun residuo (oltre alle tracce di gelo accidentale dovute all'umiditร nell'atmosfera). ร utile per conservare cibi congelati laddove il normale raffreddamento meccanico non sia disponibile. Il ghiaccio secco sublima a 194,65 ยฐK (-78,5 ยฐC, con pressione atmosferica terrestre). Maneggiarlo senza protezione รจ pericoloso e puรฒ causare ustioni da congelamento. Sebbene generalmente non molto tossico, il degassamento puรฒ causare livelli di anidride carbonica nel sangue decisamente anomali.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Carbon fibers[COLOR_REVERT] are fibers about 5โ10 micrometers in diameter, composed mostly of carbon atoms. Carbon fibers have several advantages including high stiffness, high tensile strength, low weight, high chemical resistance, high temperature tolerance and low thermal expansion. These properties have made carbon fiber very popular in aerospace, civil engineering, military, and motor-sports, along with other competition sports. However, they are relatively expensive when compared with similar fibers, such as glass or plastic fibers.
TEXT.Italian: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fibre di carbonio[COLOR_REVERT] sono fibre di circa 5-10 micrometri di diametro, composte principalmente da atomi di carbonio. Le fibre di carbonio presentano numerosi vantaggi tra cui elevata rigiditร , elevata resistenza alla trazione, peso ridotto, elevata resistenza chimica, tolleranza alle alte temperature e bassa espansione termica. Queste proprietร hanno reso la fibra di carbonio molto popolare nell'industria aerospaziale, civile, militare e automobilistica, insieme ad altri sport da competizione. Tuttavia, sono relativamente costosi se confrontati con fibre simili, come fibre di vetro o di plastica.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Carbyne[COLOR_REVERT] is a general term for any compound whose molecular structure includes an electrically neutral carbon atom with three non-bonded electrons, connected to another atom by a single bond.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carcass[COLOR_REVERT] is the body of a dead animal. If it is in a state of putrefaction, it is called carrion.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carcasse[COLOR_REVERT] est le corps d'un animal mort. Si elle est dans un รฉtat de putrรฉfaction, on l'appelle charogne.
TEXT.German: Unter [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kadavern[COLOR_REVERT] versteht man tote Tierkรถrper, die noch nicht zu verwesen begonnen haben. In diesem Stadium sind sie fรผr die meisten Tiere und auch fรผr den Menschen noch verzehrbar. Spรคtestens wenn die bakterielle Fรคulnis einsetzt und entsprechende Giftstoffe produziert werden, die das Fleisch ungenieรbar machen, spricht man von Aas. Die beiden Begriffe werden im Deutschen aber inzwischen auรer in Fachkreisen weitgehend synonym verwendet.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carcassa[COLOR_REVERT] รจ il corpo di un animale morto. Qualora si trovi in stato di putrefazione, viene detto carogna.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carpet[COLOR_REVERT] is a textile floor covering typically consisting of an upper layer of pile attached to a backing. The pile was traditionally made from wool, but since the 20th century, synthetic fibers are often used, as these are less expensive than wool. Carpets are used for a variety of purposes, including insulating a person's feet from a cold tile or concrete floor, making a room more comfortable as a place to sit on the floor, reducing sound from walking, and adding decoration or color to a room.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]tappeto[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un rivestimento tessile da pavimento, costituito tipicamente da uno strato superiore di pelo attaccato a un supporto. Il pelo era tradizionalmente realizzato in lana, ma a partire dal XX secolo vengono spesso utilizzate fibre sintetiche, perchรฉ meno costose della lana. I tappeti sono usati per una varietร di scopi, come insulare i piedi di una persona dal pavimento freddo, rendere piรน comodo sedersi per terra o piรน confortevole una stanza, attutire i passi di una persona o rendere piรน decorata o colorata una stanza.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Carved emu eggs[COLOR_REVERT] are an art form made by carving a kalthi-parti, or emu egg. The practice began in the mid to late 19th century and, while it has been practiced by people in Australia from many backgrounds, it is often strongly associated with Aboriginal art. Some of the earliest carvers were not strictly Aboriginal and these carvers created very decorative and complicated designs that were popular at the time.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]oeufs d'รฉmeu sculptรฉs[COLOR_REVERT] sont une forme d'art rรฉalisรฉe en sculptant un kalthi-parti, ou oeuf d'รฉmeu. Cette pratique a commencรฉ au milieu et ร la fin du 19e siรจcle et, bien qu'elle ait รฉtรฉ pratiquรฉe par des personnes de nombreux horizons en Australie, elle est souvent fortement associรฉe ร l'art aborigรจne. Certains des premiers sculpteurs n'รฉtaient pas strictement aborigรจnes et ces sculpteurs ont crรฉรฉ des motifs trรจs dรฉcoratifs et compliquรฉs qui รฉtaient populaires ร l'รฉpoque.
TEXT.Italian: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]uova di emรน[COLOR_REVERT] intagliate sono una forma d'arte creata scolpendo un kalthi-parti, o uovo di emรน. La pratica รจ iniziata tra la metร e la fine del XIX secolo e, sebbene sia stata praticata da australiani di diversa estrazione etnico-sociale, รจ spesso fortemente associata all'arte aborigena. Alcuni dei primi intagliatori non erano aborigeni e questi intagliatori crearono disegni molto decorativi e complicati, molto popolari all'epoca.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cat[COLOR_REVERT] is a small carnivorous mammal. It is the only domesticated species in the family Felidae and often referred to as "domestic cat" to distinguish it from wild members of the family. The cat is similar in anatomy to the other feline species, has a strong flexible body, quick reflexes, sharp teeth and retractable claws adapted to killing small prey. Its night vision and sense of smell are well developed. Domestic cats are valued by humans for companionship and for their ability to hunt rodents.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]gatto[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un piccolo mammifero carnivoro. ร l'unica specie della famiglia dei felidi a essere stata addomesticata ed รจ spesso indicata come "gatto domestico" per distinguerlo dai membri selvatici della famiglia. Il gatto รจ simile in anatomia agli altri felidi, ha un corpo forte e flessibile, riflessi rapidi, denti aguzzi e artigli retrattili adattati all'uccisione di piccole prede. La sua visione notturna e l'olfatto sono ben sviluppati. I gatti domestici sono apprezzati dagli umani per la compagnia e per la loro capacitร di cacciare i roditori.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]mobile phone[COLOR_REVERT] is a portable telephone that can make and receive calls over a radio frequency link while the user is moving within a telephone service area. The radio frequency link establishes a connection to the switching systems of a mobile phone operator, which provides access to the public switched telephone network (PSTN). 21st century mobile phones also support a variety of other services, such as text messaging, MMS, email, Internet access, short-range wireless communications (infrared, Bluetooth), business applications, video games, and digital photography.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]telefono cellulare[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un telefono portatile che puรฒ effettuare e ricevere chiamate tramite un collegamento a radiofrequenza, mentre l'utente si sposta all'interno di un'area di servizio telefonico. Il collegamento in radiofrequenza stabilisce una connessione ai sistemi di commutazione di un operatore di telefonia mobile, che fornisce l'accesso alla rete telefonica pubblica commutata (PSTN). I telefoni cellulari del XXI secolo supportano anche una varietร di altri servizi, come messaggi di testo, MMS, e-mail, accesso a Internet, comunicazioni wireless a corto raggio (infrarossi, Bluetooth), applicazioni aziendali, videogiochi e fotografia digitale.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Telefon komórkowy[COLOR_REVERT], to przenoœny telefon zdolny do nadawania i odbierania po³¹czeñ na obszarze objêtym zasiêgiem telefonii komórkowej. Nowoczsne telefony posiadaj¹ dodatkowo wiele innych funkcji jak wysy³anie wiadomoœci SMS, MMS; po³¹czenie z internetem, po³¹czenie bezprzewodowe krótkiego zasiêgu (podczerwieñ, bluetooth), gry mobilne oraz aparat fotograficzny.
TEXT.Russian: รรฎรกรจรซรผรญรปรฉ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญ (รฒร รชรฆรฅ รจรงรขรฅรฑรฒรญรปรฉ รชร รช รฑรฎรฒรฎรขรปรฉ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญ รจรซรจ รชร รฐรฌร รญรญรปรฉ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญ) - รฝรฒรฎ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รฑรฎรขรฅรฐรธร รฒรผ รจ รฏรฐรจรญรจรฌร รฒรผ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญรญรปรฅ รงรขรฎรญรชรจ รทรฅรฐรฅรง รฐร รครจรฎรฑรขรฟรงรผ รขรฎ รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รฏรฅรฐรฅรครขรจรฆรฅรญรจรฟ รฏรฎ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎรฉ รฃรฅรฎรฃรฐร รดรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฉ รฎรกรซร รฑรฒรจ. รรญร รฎรฑรณรนรฅรฑรฒรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรณรฒรฅรฌ รฏรฎรครชรซรพรทรฅรญรจรฟ รช รฑรฅรฒรจ รฑรฎรฒรฎรขรฎรฉ รฑรขรฟรงรจ, รฏรฐรฅรครฎรฑรฒร รขรซรฟรฅรฌรปรต รฎรฏรฅรฐร รฒรฎรฐรฎรฌ รฌรฎรกรจรซรผรญรฎรฉ รฑรขรฟรงรจ, รฏรฐรฅรครฎรฑรฒร รขรซรฟรฟ รครฎรฑรฒรณรฏ รช รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญรญรฎรฉ รฑรฅรฒรจ รฎรกรนรฅรฃรฎ รฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรฟ. รร รฏรฐรฎรฒรจรข, รกรฅรฑรฏรฐรฎรขรฎรครญรฎรฉ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฒรฎรซรผรชรฎ รข รฏรฐรฅรครฅรซร รต รฐร รครจรณรฑร รฅรครจรญรจรทรญรฎรฃรฎ, รทร รฑรฒรญรฎรฃรฎ รกร รงรฎรขรฎรฉ รฑรฒร รญรถรจรจ. ร รครฎรฏรฎรซรญรฅรญรจรฅ รช รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญรจรจ, รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรฅ รฌรฎรกรจรซรผรญรปรฅ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญรป รฏรฎรครครฅรฐรฆรจรขร รพรฒ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รธรจรฐรฎรชรจรฉ รฑรฏรฅรชรฒรฐ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รณรฑรซรณรฃ, รฒร รชรจรต รชร รช รฎรกรฌรฅรญ รฒรฅรชรฑรฒรฎรขรปรฌรจ รฑรฎรฎรกรนรฅรญรจรฟรฌรจ, MMS, รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญร รฟ รฏรฎรทรฒร , รครฎรฑรฒรณรฏ รข รรญรฒรฅรฐรญรฅรฒ, รกรฅรฑรฏรฐรฎรขรฎรครญรณรพ รฑรขรฟรงรผ (รจรญรดรฐร รชรฐร รฑรญรปรฉ รฏรฎรฐรฒ, Bluetooth), รกรจรงรญรฅรฑ-รฏรฐรจรซรฎรฆรฅรญรจรฟ, รจรฃรฐรป รจ รดรฎรฒรฎรฃรฐร รดรจรจ. รรฎรกรจรซรผรญรปรฅ รฒรฅรซรฅรดรฎรญรป, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฏรฐรฅรครซร รฃร รพรฒ รฝรฒรจ รจ รกรฎรซรฅรฅ รฎรกรนรจรฅ รขรปรทรจรฑรซรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรปรฅ รขรฎรงรฌรฎรฆรญรฎรฑรฒรจ, รญร รงรปรขร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฑรฌร รฐรฒรดรฎรญรป.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cellulase capsule[COLOR_REVERT] is a capsule that allows humans to completely digest cellulose. Cellulase is an enzyme, produced chiefly by fungi, bacteria, and protozoans, that breaks down the cellulose molecule into monosaccharides ("simple sugars") such as beta-glucose, or shorter polysaccharides and oligosaccharides. By allowing humans to digest even grass or leaves, these capsules greatly help reducing world hunger.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]capsule de cellulase[COLOR_REVERT] est une capsule qui permet aux humains de digรฉrer complรจtement la cellulose. La cellulase est une enzyme, produite principalement par les champignons, les bactรฉries et les protozoaires, qui dรฉcompose la molรฉcule de cellulose en monosaccharides ("sucres simples") comme le bรชta-glucose, ou en polysaccharides et oligosaccharides plus courts. En permettant aux humains de digรฉrer mรชme l'herbe ou les feuilles, ces capsules contribuent grandement ร rรฉduire la faim dans le monde.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]capsula di cellulasi[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una capsula che consente a un essere umano di digerire completamente la cellulosa. La cellulasi รจ un enzima, prodotto principalmente da funghi, batteri e protozoi, che scompone la molecola di cellulosa in monosaccaridi ("zuccheri semplici") come il beta-glucosio, oppure in polisaccaridi e oligosaccaridi piรน corti. Permettendo agli esseri umani di digerire anche l'erba o le foglie, queste capsule contribuiscono alla riduzione della fame nel mondo.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kapsu³ki z celulaz¹[COLOR_REVERT], to kapsu³ki zawieraj¹ce celulazê, a wiêc enzym trawienny umo¿liwiaj¹cy trawienie celulozy. Celuloza jest to zwi¹zek chemiczny zaliczany do polisacharydów, który tworzy œciany komurkowe roœlin. Celulaza rozk³ada celulozê, a w wyniku tego rozk³adu powstaje glukoza.

TEXT.German: Zement
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฌรฅรญรฒ

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cement[COLOR_REVERT] is a binder, a substance used for construction that sets, hardens, and adheres to other materials to bind them together. Cement is seldom used on its own, but rather to bind sand and gravel (aggregate) together. Cement mixed with fine aggregate produces mortar for masonry, or with sand and gravel, produces concrete. Cement is the most widely used material in existence and is only behind water as the planet's most-consumed resource.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ciment[COLOR_REVERT] est un liant, une substance utilisรฉe dans la construction qui se fixe, durcit et adhรจre ร d'autres matรฉriaux pour les lier ensemble. Le ciment est rarement utilisรฉ seul, mais plutรดt pour lier le sable et le gravier (agrรฉgats). Le ciment mรฉlangรฉ ร des agrรฉgats fins produit du mortier pour la maรงonnerie, ou avec du sable et du gravier, produit du bรฉton. Le ciment est le matรฉriau le plus largement utilisรฉ au monde et se place juste derriรจre l'eau comme ressource la plus consommรฉe de la planรจte.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cemento[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una sostanza legante usata per la costruzione che fissa, indurisce e aderisce ad altri materiali per legarli insieme. Il cemento viene usato raramente da solo, ma piuttosto per legare insieme sabbia e ghiaia. Il cemento miscelato con un aggregato fine (come la sabbia) produce malta per muratura, oppure con sabbia e ghiaia produce calcestruzzo. Il cemento รจ il materiale piรน ampiamente usato esistente ed รจ secondo solo all'acqua come risorsa piรน consumata del pianeta.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cement[COLOR_REVERT], to hydrauliczne spoiwo mineralne, otrzymywane w cementowniach z surowców mineralnych (margiel lub wapieñ i glina) wypalonych na klinkier w piecu cementowym, a nastêpnie zmielenie otrzymanego spieku z gipsem, spe³niaj¹cym rolê regulatora czasu wi¹zania. Stosowany jest do przygotowywania zapraw cementowych, cementowo-wapiennych i betonów. Wykorzystywany jest do ³¹czenia materia³ów budowlanych.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cermet[COLOR_REVERT] is a composite material composed of ceramic (cer) and metallic (met) materials. It is ideally designed to have the optimal properties of both a ceramic, such as high temperature resistance and hardness, and those of a metal, such as the ability to undergo plastic deformation. Cermets are used in the manufacture of resistors (especially potentiometers), capacitors, and other electronic components which may experience high temperature. Some types of cermets are also being considered for use as spacecraft shielding, as they resist the high velocity impacts of micrometeoroids and orbital debris much more effectively than more traditional spacecraft materials.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cermet[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un materiale composito composto da materiali ceramici (cer) e metallici (met). ร progettato idealmente per avere le proprietร ottimali sia di una ceramica (come resistenza alle alte temperature e durezza), sia quelle di un metallo (come la capacitร di subire una deformazione plastica). I cermet vengono utilizzati nella fabbricazione di resistori (in particolare potenziometri), condensatori e altri componenti elettronici che potrebbero raggiungere temperature elevate. Alcuni tipi di cermet vengono anche considerati per la schermatura di veicoli spaziali, poichรฉ resistono agli impatti ad alta velocitร dei micrometeoriti e dei detriti orbitali in modo molto piรน efficace rispetto ai piรน tradizionali materiali spaziali.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cermet[COLOR_REVERT], to materia³ kompozytowy spieczony (sprasowany) z materia³ów ceramicznych i metalowych. TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Chalk[COLOR_REVERT] is a soft, white, porous, sedimentary carbonate rock, a form of limestone composed of the mineral calcite. It forms under reasonably deep marine conditions from the gradual accumulation of minute calcite shells (coccoliths) shed from micro-organisms called coccolithophores. Chalk has greater resistance to weathering and slumping than the clays with which it is usually associated, thus forming tall, steep cliffs. Because chalk is well jointed, it can hold a large volume of ground water, providing a natural reservoir that releases water slowly through dry seasons.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]gesso[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una roccia carbonatica morbida, bianca, porosa e sedimentaria, una forma di calcare composta da calcite minerale. Si forma in ambienti marini ragionevolmente profondi dall'accumulo graduale di minuscoli gusci di calcite (coccoliti) rilasciati da microrganismi chiamati coccolitofori. Il gesso ha una maggiore resistenza agli agenti atmosferici e al cedimento rispetto alle argille a cui รจ solitamente associato, formando cosรฌ alte e ripide scogliere. Poichรฉ il gesso รจ ben articolato, puรฒ contenere un grande volume di acque sotterranee, fornendo un serbatoio naturale che rilascia lentamente l'acqua durante le stagioni secche.

Jej podstawowym sk³adnikiem jest kalcyt (CaCO3). Zbudowana jest g³ównie z mikroskopowych skorupek kokolitów i otwornic oraz bardzo drobnoziarnistego mikrytu. Inne skamienia³oœci (np. ig³y g¹bek, ma³¿e, belemnity) wystêpuj¹ podrzêdnie. Jest to ska³a miêkka, niezbyt zwiêz³a, porowata, dziêki czemu ma ma³y ciê¿ar w³aœciwy i jest lekka.
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรซ - รฝรฒรฎ รฌรฟรฃรชร รฟ, รกรฅรซร รฟ รจ รฏรฎรฐรจรฑรฒร รฟ รฎรฑร รครฎรทรญร รฟ รฏรฎรฐรฎรคร , รฐร รงรญรฎรขรจรครญรฎรฑรฒรผ รจรงรขรฅรฑรฒรญรฟรชร รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรนร รฟ รจรง รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซร รชร รซรผรถรจรฒร . รร รซรผรถรจรฒ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รชร รฐรกรฎรญร รฒรฎรฌ รชร รซรผรถรจรฟ รจรซรจ CaCO3. รรญ รขรฎรงรญรจรชร รฅรฒ รฏรฎรค รครฎรฑรฒร รฒรฎรทรญรฎ รฃรซรณรกรฎรชรจรฌรจ รฌรฎรฐรฑรชรจรฌรจ รฏรฎรฐรฎรคร รฌรจ รฑ รฏรฎรฑรฒรฅรฏรฅรญรญรปรฌ รญร รชรฎรฏรซรฅรญรจรฅรฌ รชร รซรผรถรจรฒรฎรขรปรต รฑรซรฎรฅรข (รชรฎรชรชรฎรซรจรฒรฎรข), รฎรกรฐร รงรณรฅรฌรปรต รฎรฒ รฌรจรชรฐรฎรฎรฐรฃร รญรจรงรฌรฎรข, รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฌรปรต รชรฎรชรชรฎรซรจรฒรฎรดรฎรฐร รฌรจ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Charcoal[COLOR_REVERT] is a lightweight black carbon residue, produced by removing water and other volatile constituents from animal and plant materials. Charcoal is usually produced by slow pyrolysis, i.e. the heating of wood or other organic materials in the absence of oxygen. This process is called charcoal burning. The finished charcoal consists largely of carbon.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]charbon de bois[COLOR_REVERT] est un rรฉsidu de carbone noir et lรฉger, produit en รฉliminant l'eau et d'autres constituants volatils des matiรจres animales et vรฉgรฉtales. Le charbon de bois est gรฉnรฉralement produit par pyrolyse lente, c'est-ร -dire le chauffage du bois ou d'autres matiรจres organiques en l'absence d'oxygรจne. Ce processus est appelรฉ carbonisation. Le charbon de bois fini est composรฉ en grande partie de carbone.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carbone[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un residuo leggero di carbonio nero, prodotto rimuovendo l'acqua e altri componenti volatili da materiali animali e vegetali. Il carbone viene solitamente prodotto da una lenta pirolisi, ossia dal riscaldamento del legno o di altri materiali organici in assenza di ossigeno. Questo processo si chiama combustione del carbone. Il carbone finito รจ costituito in gran parte da carbonio.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wêgiel drzewny[COLOR_REVERT], to lekka, czarna substancja wytwarzana z drewna w procesie suchej destylacji. G³ównym sk³adnikiem jest wêgiel pierwiastkowy, zanieczyszczony popio³em i licznymi zwi¹zkami organicznymi. Wêgiel drzewny ma bardzo ma³¹ gêstoœæ (zachowuje czêœciowo strukturê œcian komórkowych drewna). Ma du¿e zdolnoœci adsorpcyjne dziêki silnie rozwiniêtej powierzchni. Jako paliwo ma ok. trzykrotnie wiêksz¹ wydajnoœæ energetyczn¹ ni¿ drewno. 1 kg wêgla drzewnego daje iloœæ energii podobn¹ do uzyskiwanej ze spalenia 1 litra benzyny. Jest jednak k³opotliwy w u¿yciu ze wzglêdu na zajmowan¹ przestrzeñ w stosunku do masy, poza tym kruszy siê i pyli przy manipulacji.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cheese[COLOR_REVERT] is a dairy product derived from milk that is produced in a wide range of flavors, textures, and forms. It comprises proteins and fat from milk, usually of cows, buffalo, goats, or sheep. Cheese is valued for its portability, long life, and high content of fat, protein, calcium, and phosphorus.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fromage[COLOR_REVERT] est un produit laitier dรฉrivรฉ du lait qui est produit dans une large gamme de saveurs, de textures et de formes. Il comprend des protรฉines et des graisses du lait, gรฉnรฉralement de vaches, de buffles, de chรจvres ou de moutons. Le fromage est apprรฉciรฉ pour sa portabilitรฉ, sa longue durรฉe de vie et sa haute teneur en matiรจres grasses, en protรฉines, en calcium et en phosphore.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]formaggio[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un prodotto lattiero-caseario derivato dal latte che viene prodotto in una vasta gamma di aromi, consistenze e forme. Comprende proteine e grassi del latte, solitamente di mucca, bufala, capra o pecora. ร un alimento molto importante per la sua facile trasportabilitร , la sua durata (a seconda della varietร ) e per l'alto contenuto di proteine, grassi, calcio e fosforo.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chemical substance[COLOR_REVERT] is a form of matter having constant chemical composition and characteristic properties. Chemical substances can be simple substances, chemical compounds, or alloys, can exist as solids, liquids, gases, or plasma, and may change between these phases of matter with changes in temperature or pressure. Chemical substances may be combined or converted to others by means of chemical reactions. No substance is entirely pure, and chemical purity is specified according to the intended use of the chemical.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]sostanza chimica[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una forma di materia con composizione chimica costante e proprietร caratteristiche. Le sostanze chimiche possono essere semplici sostanze, composti chimici o leghe, possono esistere come solidi, liquidi, gas o plasma e possono cambiare tra queste fasi della materia con variazioni di temperatura o pressione. Le sostanze chimiche possono essere combinate o convertite in altre per mezzo di reazioni chimiche. Nessuna sostanza รจ completamente pura e la purezza chimica รจ specificata in base all'uso previsto della sostanza chimica.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Substancja chemiczna[COLOR_REVERT], to substancja jednorodna, o sta³ym, okreœlonym sk³adzie chemicznym, jakoœciowym (co do rodzaju atomów pod wzglêdem liczby atomowej i ewentualnie, co do poszczególnych rodzajów atomów w cz¹steczce) i najczêœciej tak¿e iloœciowym (liczby atomów ró¿nych rodzajów w cz¹steczce); zbiór atomów lub cz¹steczek spe³niaj¹cych kryterium sta³oœci sk³adu.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Chocolate[COLOR_REVERT] is a usually sweet, brown food preparation of roasted and ground cacao seeds, that is made in the form of a liquid, paste, or in a block, or used as a flavoring ingredient in other foods. Chocolate is one of the most popular food types and flavors in the world, and many foodstuffs involving chocolate exist, particularly desserts. The earliest evidence of use traces to 19th century BC Central America.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chocolat[COLOR_REVERT] est une prรฉparation alimentaire gรฉnรฉralement sucrรฉe et brune, faite de graines de cacao torrรฉfiรฉes et moulues, qui se prรฉsente sous forme de liquide, de pรขte ou en tablette, ou utilisรฉe comme ingrรฉdient aromatisant dans d'autres aliments. Le chocolat est l'un des types d'aliments et des saveurs les plus populaires au monde, et de nombreux produits alimentaires contenant du chocolat existent, en particulier des desserts. Les premiรจres preuves d'utilisation remontent au 19e siรจcle avant J.-C. en Amรฉrique centrale.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cioccolato[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un preparato alimentare, generalmente dolce e di colore marrone, derivato dai semi di cacao tostati e macinati, che viene prodotta sotto forma di liquido, pasta o in un blocco o utilizzata come ingrediente aromatizzante in altri alimenti. Il cioccolato รจ uno dei tipi di alimenti e sapori piรน popolari al mondo e esistono molti cibi che contengono il cioccolato, in particolare i dessert. Le prime tracce di utilizzo risalgono all'America centrale del XIX secolo a.C.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Czekolada[COLOR_REVERT], to wyrób cukierniczy sporz¹dzany z miazgi kakaowej, t³uszczu kakaowego (mas³o kakaowe) lub innego t³uszczu roœlinnego, œrodka s³odz¹cego i innych dodatków, a w przypadku czekolady mlecznej tak¿e z mleka.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Chromite[COLOR_REVERT] is an iron chromium oxide mineral. Chromite is mined particularly to make stainless steel through the production of ferrochrome, which is an iron-chromium alloy. Chromite grains are commonly found in large mafic igneous intrusions, such as the Bushveld in South Africa and India. Chromite can be used as a refractory material, because it has a high heat stability. Chromium is extracted from chromite, and it is primarily used for production of corrosion-resistant super-alloys, nichrome, and stainless steel.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cromite[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un minerale ossido di ferro e cromo. La cromite viene estratta in particolare per produrre acciaio inossidabile attraverso la produzione di ferrocromo, una lega di ferro e cromo. I grani di cromite si trovano comunemente nelle grandi intrusioni femiche, come il Bushveld in Sudafrica e in India. La cromite puรฒ essere utilizzata come materiale refrattario, perchรฉ ha un'alta stabilitร al calore. Dalla cromite viene estratto il cromo, che viene primariamente usato per la produzione di superleghe resistenti alla corrosione, nichelcromo e acciaio inossidabile.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Chromit[COLOR_REVERT], to minera³ z grupy tlenków, zaliczany do grupy spineli chromowych. Wystêpuje w skupieniach ziarnistych i zbitych, niekiedy wystêpuje w formie bry³ lub gniazd. Jest twardy, stosunkowo ciê¿ki, kruchy, nieprzezroczysty. Czasami wykazuje s³abe w³aœciwoœci magnetyczne. Czêsto zawiera domieszki: ¿elaza, glinu, magnezu, manganu, cynku. Najwa¿niejsze Ÿród³o otrzymywania chromu (przemys³ metalurgiczny: stal chromowa, stopy z ¿elazem i niklem). U¿ywany w przemyœle farbiarskim, garbarskim, w³ókienniczym, szklarskim i materia³ów ogniotrwa³ych.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chronoton[COLOR_REVERT] is a sub-atomic particle with temporal properties, that exists in a state of temporal flux. Chronotons are often associated with the phenomenon of time travel.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chronoton[COLOR_REVERT] est une particule subatomique aux propriรฉtรฉs temporelles, qui existe dans un รฉtat de flux temporel. Les chronotons sont souvent associรฉs au phรฉnomรจne du voyage dans le temps.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cronotone[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una particella subatomica con proprietร temporali, che esiste in uno stato di flusso temporale. I cronotoni sono spesso associati al fenomeno del viaggio nel tempo.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cinnabar[COLOR_REVERT] is the bright scarlet to brick-red form of mercury sulfide. It is the most common source ore for refining elemental mercury, and is the historic source for the brilliant red or scarlet pigment termed "vermilion", and associated red mercury pigments. Cinnabar has been used for its color since antiquity in the Near East, Mesoamerica, and China. Associated modern precautions for use and handling of cinnabar arise from the toxicity of the mercury component, which was recognized as early as ancient Rome.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cinabro[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una forma di solfuro di mercurio, di colore rosso scarlatto brillante o rosso mattone. ร la forma grezza piรน comune del mercurio ed รจ storicamente la fonte del vermiglio, il pigmento rosso o rosso scarlatto, e dei pigmenti di colore rosso mercurio. Il cinabro รจ stato usato per il suo colore fin dall'antichitร in Medio Oriente, Mesoamerica e Cina. Le moderne precauzioni associate per l'uso e la manipolazione del cinabro derivano dalla tossicitร della componente di mercurio, di cui si era a conoscenza giร nell'antica Roma.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Clam[COLOR_REVERT] is a common name for several kinds of bivalve mollusks. The word is often applied only to those that are edible and live as in-fauna, spending most of their lives halfway buried in the sand of the seafloor or riverbeds. Clams have two shells of equal size, connected by two abductor muscles, and have a powerful burrowing foot. They live in both freshwater and marine environments; in the former, they prefer to burrow down into the mud. Clams have been and still are a food resource by all sorts of fauna, including humans.
TEXT.Italian: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Mollusco[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un nome comune per diversi tipi di molluschi bivalvi. La parola viene spesso applicata solo a coloro che sono commestibili e vivono come endofauna, trascorrendo la maggior parte della loro vita sepolti a metร nella sabbia del fondale marino. Le vongole hanno due gusci di uguali dimensioni, collegati da due muscoli adduttori, e hanno un potente piede scavatore. Vivono in ambienti sia di acqua dolce che marina; nel primo, preferiscono scavare nel fango. Le vongole sono state e sono ancora una risorsa alimentare per tutti i tipi di fauna, compresi gli umani.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Claytronic atoms[COLOR_REVERT] (or "Catoms") are individual nanometer-scale computers, which can interact with each other to form tangible objects that a user can interact with. This idea is more broadly referred to as "programmable matter". Claytronic atoms have the potential to greatly affect many areas of daily life, such as telecommunication, human-computer interfaces, and entertainment.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]atomes claytroniques[COLOR_REVERT] (ou "Catoms") sont des ordinateurs individuels ร l'รฉchelle nanomรฉtrique, qui peuvent interagir entre eux pour former des objets tangibles avec lesquels un utilisateur peut interagir. Cette idรฉe est plus largement dรฉsignรฉe sous le nom de "matiรจre programmable". Les atomes claytroniques ont le potentiel d'affecter considรฉrablement de nombreux domaines de la vie quotidienne, tels que les tรฉlรฉcommunications, les interfaces homme-machine et le divertissement.
TEXT.Italian: Gli [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]atomi claytronici[COLOR_REVERT] (o "catomi") sono singoli computer di scala nanometrica che interagiscono fra loro per produrre oggetti tangibili, che un essere umano puรฒ a sua volta utilizzare. Questi atomi hanno il potenziale di influenzare notevolmente molte aree della vita quotidiana, come le telecomunicazioni, le interfacce uomo-computer e l'industria dell'intrattenimento.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Clay[COLOR_REVERT] is an aluminum silicate composed mainly of fine-grained materials. Clay minerals have peculiar physical-chemical characteristics that give them a sensitive plasticity (if mixed with water) or refractory (if dehydrated) - properties that have allowed the development of the brick and ceramic industries.
TEXT.French: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]argile[COLOR_REVERT] est un silicate d'aluminium composรฉ principalement de matรฉriaux ร grains fins. Les minรฉraux argileux ont des caractรฉristiques physico-chimiques particuliรจres qui leur confรจrent une plasticitรฉ sensible (si mรฉlangรฉs ร l'eau) ou rรฉfractaire (si dรฉshydratรฉs) - propriรฉtรฉs qui ont permis le dรฉveloppement des industries de la brique et de la cรฉramique.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]argilla[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un silicato d'alluminio composto principalmente di materiali a grana fine. I minerali argillosi hanno caratteristiche fisico-chimiche peculiari che conferiscono loro una sensibile plasticitร (se miscelato con acqua) o refrattarietร (se disidratato) - proprietร che hanno permesso lo sviluppo dell'industria laterizia e ceramica.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Glina[COLOR_REVERT], to ilasta ska³a osadowa, powsta³a najczêœciej w okresie czwartorzêdu w wyniku nagromadzenia osadów morenowych. Glina od zarania dziejów stanowi podstawowy surowiec do wyrobu ceramiki. Do rêcznego formowania wyrobów ceramicznych u¿ywane s¹ wysokoplastyczne i plastyczne gliny bia³o oraz barwnie wypalaj¹ce siê. Dzisiaj stanowi¹ one cenny surowiec do produkcji ceramiki sanitarnej i p³ytek ceramicznych.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Clockpunk[COLOR_REVERT] is a subgenre of speculative fiction which is similar to steampunk. It portrays advanced technologies based on springs and clockwork, instead of current technologies, despite keeping a pre-modern design. Clockpunk is based very intensively on the works of Leonardo da Vinci and, as such, it is typically set during the Renaissance or in a Renaissance-like era.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Clockpunk[COLOR_REVERT] est un sous-genre de fiction spรฉculative similaire au steampunk. Il dรฉcrit des technologies avancรฉes basรฉes sur des ressorts et des horloges, au lieu des technologies actuelles, tout en conservant un design prรฉ-moderne. Clockpunk est trรจs intensรฉment basรฉ sur les oeuvres de Lรฉonard de Vinci et, en tant que tel, il se dรฉroule gรฉnรฉralement ร la Renaissance ou ร une รฉpoque similaire ร la Renaissance.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Clockpunk[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un sottogenere della letteratura fantascientifica simile allo steampunk. Ritrae tecnologie avanzate basate su molle e orologi, al contrario dei meccanismi attuali, pur mantenendo un design premoderno. Il Clockpunk si basa ampiamente sulle opere di Leonardo da Vinci e, pertanto, รจ tipicamente ambientato durante il Rinascimento o in uno scenario simile all'epoca rinascimentale.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Clockpunk[COLOR_REVERT], to odmiana science-fiction, która wyewoluowa³a ze steampunku. Akcja utworów rozgrywa siê przewa¿nie w erze preindustrialnej, chocia¿ rozmach, z jakim jest stosowana technologia, sugeruje co innego. Technika opiera siê przede wszystkim na sprê¿ynach, podstawowej mechanice (clockwork โ st¹d nazwa gatunku) i wynalazkach podobnych do urz¹dzeñ Leonarda da Vinci.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]dress[COLOR_REVERT] is a specifically weaved fabric to be worn by a human being, covering it wholly or partially, and defining its external appearance. Depending on the culture, some clothes can have a very particular cultural and social significance, such as marking the belonging to a given community or of a particular (civil, social, religious) status within the community.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]vรชtement[COLOR_REVERT] est un tissu spรฉcifiquement tissรฉ pour รชtre portรฉ par un รชtre humain, le couvrant totalement ou partiellement, et dรฉfinissant son apparence externe. Selon la culture, certains vรชtements peuvent avoir une signification culturelle et sociale trรจs particuliรจre, comme marquer l'appartenance ร une communautรฉ donnรฉe ou ร un statut particulier (civil, social, religieux) au sein de la communautรฉ.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]vestito[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un tessuto realizzato appositamente per essere indossato da un essere umano, coprendolo in tutto o in parte e definendone l'aspetto esteriore. Alcuni vestiti, in base alla cultura, possono rivestire un significato culturale e sociale molto particolare, come l'appartenenza a una data comunitร o la segnalazione di un particolare status (civile, sociale, religioso...) all'interno della comunitร .

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fabric[COLOR_REVERT] is a product with a flat, thin and flexible surface, made by intertwining threads perpendicular to each other; the operation necessary to make it is called weaving. It consists of two elements: the warp or chain (the set of threads stretched on the loom) and the weft (a single thread that runs the warp from one side to the other). Fabrics made their appearance in the Neolithic period and, depending on materials, weaves, colors and machinery involved, marked the cultural and technical evolution of human civilization.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]tessuto[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un manufatto a superficie piana, sottile e flessibile, realizzato tramite un intreccio di fili perpendicolari tra di loro; l'operazione necessaria per realizzarlo si chiama tessitura. ร costituito da due elementi: l'ordito o catena (l'insieme di fili tesi sul telaio) e la trama (un unico filo che percorre da una parte all'altra l'ordito). I tessuti fecero la loro comparsa nel neolitico e segnarono nei materiali usati, negli intrecci, nei colori e nelle attrezzature utilizzate per costruirli l'evoluzione culturale e tecnica della civiltร umana.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Tkanina[COLOR_REVERT], to wyrób w³ókienniczy p³aski powstaj¹cy w wyniku przeplatania ze sob¹ (wed³ug za³o¿onego splotu) wzajemnie prostopad³ych uk³adów nitek osnowy i w¹tku. Pierwsze wyroby przypominaj¹ce tkaninê tworzono ju¿ przed oœmioma tysi¹cami lat. Nie by³y to tkaniny w dzisiejszym rozumieniu, poniewa¿ sk³ada³y siê z luŸno splecionych ze sob¹ w³ókien, traw, pasków skór zwierzêcych, cienkich ga³êzi czy pn¹czy. Powstawa³y w ten sposób plecionki, struktur¹ przypominaj¹ce tkaniny.
TEXT.Russian: รรซรฎรขร รฒรชร รญรผ รจ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจรฟ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒรฑรฟ (รฒร รชรจรต รชร รช รฏรฎรธรจรข รจ รชรฐรฎรฉ) รชร รช รฑรจรญรฎรญรจรฌรป รครซรฟ รฒรฅรชรฑรฒรจรซรฟ. รรครญร รชรฎ, รฅรฑรฒรผ รฒรฎรญรชรจรฅ รฐร รงรซรจรทรจรฟ รข รฝรฒรจรต รฏรฎรญรฟรฒรจรฟรต. รรฅรชรฑรฒรจรซรผ รฎรฒรญรฎรฑรจรฒรฑรฟ รช รซรพรกรฎรฌรณ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรณ, รฑรครฅรซร รญรญรปรฉ รจรง รฏรฅรฐรฅรฏรซรฅรฒร รพรนรจรตรฑรฟ รขรฎรซรฎรชรฎรญ. รรชร รญรผ รฆรฅ รฎรกรฎรงรญร รทร รฅรฒ รซรพรกรฎรฉ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซ, รจรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฏรณรฒรฅรฌ รฏรซรฅรฒรฅรญรจรฟ, รขรฟรงร รญรจรฟ, รชรฐรพรทรชรฎรฌ, รจรซรจ รฑรชรซรฅรจรขร รญรจรฟ. รรฎรซรฎรฒรญรฎ รฎรฒรญรฎรฑรจรฒรฑรฟ รช รฃรฎรฒรฎรขรฎรฌรณ รชรณรฑรฎรช รฒรชร รญรจ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รกรปรฒรผ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญ รครซรฟ รถรฅรซรฅรฉ, รฒร รชรจรต รชร รช รฏรฎรชรฐรปรฒรจรฅ รชรฐรฎรขร รฒรจ. [NEWLINE][TAB]รร รฐรณรฑร รจรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฟรซรจรฑรผ รจรง รฒรชร รญรจ รงร รขรฑรพ รจรงรขรฅรฑรฒรญรณรพ รญร รฌ รจรฑรฒรฎรฐรจรพ. รร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ, รฏร รฐรณรฑร รฑรธรจรขร รซรจรฑรผ รจรง รซรผรญร , รชรฎรญรฎรฏรซรจ รจรซรจ รตรซรฎรฏรชร รข รฐร รงรซรจรทรญรปรต รดรฎรฐรฌร รต, รขรชรซรพรทร รฟ รตรฎรซรฑรฒ. รรครญร รชรฎ, รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรฅ รฏร รฐรณรฑร รฐรฅรครชรฎ รครฅรซร รพรฒรฑรฟ รจรง รญร รฒรณรฐร รซรผรญรปรต รขรฎรซรฎรชรฎรญ. รร รนรฅ รขรฑรฅรฃรฎ รฏร รฐรณรฑร รฑรครฅรซร รญรป รจรง รฑรจรญรฒรฅรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรต รขรฎรซรฎรชรฎรญ, รญร รทรจรญร รฟ รฎรฒ รญรฅรครฎรฐรฎรฃรฎรฃรฎ รญรฅรฉรซรฎรญร รซรจรกรฎ รจรง รฏรฎรซรจรฝรฑรฒรฅรฐร รจ รครฎรฐรฎรฃรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรนรจรต ร รฐร รฌรจรครญรปรต รขรฎรซรฎรชรฎรญ รจรซรจ รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรครญรปรต รขรฎรซรฎรชรฎรญ. รรฅรคร รขรญรจรฅ รณรฑรฏรฅรตรจ รข รฒรฅรตรญรฎรซรฎรฃรจรจ รฑรฅรฉรทร รฑ รฏรฐรฅรครซร รฃร รพรฒ รฌรญรฎรฃรฎ รขร รฐรจร รญรฒรฎรข รครซรฟ รจรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฏร รฐรณรฑรฎรข.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Coal[COLOR_REVERT] is a combustible black or brownish-black sedimentary rock, formed as rock strata called "coal seams". Coal is mostly carbon with variable amounts of other elements - chiefly hydrogen, sulfur, oxygen, and nitrogen. Coal is formed when dead plant matter decays into peat and is converted into coal by the heat and pressure of deep burial over millions of years. The extraction and use of coal, however, damages the environment, including by climate change, and causes many premature deaths and illness.
TEXT.German: Vor mehr als 300 Millionen Jahren begannen sich die Pflanzenkulturen in den Sumpfgebieten zu zersetzen. Sand- und Schlammschichten bedeckten die Pflanzenrรผckstรคnde und wurden von dem Gewicht des mit Wasser vermischten Sediments zusammengedrรผckt. Im Laufe der Jahre verhรคrteten sich die Rรผckstรคnde und wurden zu Kohle. In den 70er Jahren zรคhlte die Kohle weltweit zu den wichtigsten Brennstoffquellen. Bis heute werden Kohlevorkommen in aller Welt abgebaut. Aufkommende Bedenken hinsichtlich der Umweltvertrรคglichkeit der Kohleverbrennung fรผhrten indes zu einer verminderten Verwendung dieses Rohstoffs: Der Rauch, der bei der Verbrennung von Kohle entsteht, hat einen hohen Sรคuregehalt und verursacht den so genannten "sauren Regen", der Pflanzen und Tieren schadet. Trotz aller Bedenken fรถrdern einige der grรถรten Kohlebergwerke in den Vereinigten Staaten jรคhrlich mehr als 450000 Tonnen Kohle und machen den Kohlebergbau damit zu einer sehr profitablen Industrie.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carbone[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una roccia sedimentaria infiammabile di colore nero o brunastro, formata da strati di roccia chiamati "giunture di carbone". Il carbone รจ principalmente carbonio con quantitร variabili di altri elementi - principalmente idrogeno, zolfo, ossigeno e azoto. Il carbone si forma quando la materia vegetale morta decade in torba e viene convertita in carbone dal calore e dalla pressione causata dalla sedimentazione nel corso di milioni di anni. L'estrazione e l'uso del carbone, tuttavia, danneggia l'ambiente, anche a causa dei cambiamenti climatici, e causa molti decessi prematuri e malattie.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wêgiel kopalny[COLOR_REVERT], to ska³a osadowa powsta³a w wyniku gromadzenia siê i póŸniejszego przeobra¿enia szcz¹tków roœlinnych. Z³o¿ony jest on z szeregu zwi¹zków organicznych oraz mineralnych sk³adników nieorganicznych i wody. Zawiera takie pierwiastki jak: wêgiel, tlen, wodór, azot i siarkê. Mog¹ w nim wystêpowaæ tak¿e pewne iloœci pierwiastków rzadkich, np. arsen, uran czy german. Najczêsœciej spotykane rodzaje wêgla kopalnego to wêgiel kamienny oraz brunatny.
TEXT.Russian: ะะพะปะตะต 300 ะผะธะปะปะธะพะฝะพะฒ ะปะตั ะฝะฐะทะฐะด ัะฐััััะธะต ะฝะฐ ะฑะพะปะพัะฐั ัะฐััะตะฝะธั ะฝะฐัะฐะปะธ ัะฐะทะปะฐะณะฐัััั. ะกะปะพะธ ะฟะตัะบะฐ ะธ ะณััะทะธ ะฟะพะบััะฒะฐะปะธ ัะฐะทะปะฐะณะฐััะธะตัั ัะฐััะตะฝะธั, ะธ ัะฐะทะปะฐะณะฐััะธะตัั ัะฐััะตะฝะธั ะฑัะปะธ ัะถะฐัั ะพะฑัะธะผ ะฒะตัะพะผ ะฒะพะดั ะธ ะพัะฐะดะบะฐ. ะกะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะตะผ ัะฐััะธัะตะปัะฝะพะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะพ ะทะฐัะฒะตัะดะตะปะพ ะธ ะฟัะตะฒัะฐัะธะปะพัั ะฒ ัะณะพะปั. ะฃะณะพะปัะฝัะต ะผะตััะพัะพะถะดะตะฝะธั ัะฐะทัะฐะฑะฐััะฒะฐัััั ะฟะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะผะธัั, ะธ ัะณะพะปั ะฑัะป ะพะดะฝะธะผ ะธะท ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝัั ะธััะพัะฝะธะบะพะฒ ัะพะฟะปะธะฒะฐ ะฒ ะผะธัะต ะดะพ 1970-ั ะณะพะดะพะฒ. ะะพ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ัััะฐะฝะฐั ะพะทะฐะฑะพัะตะฝะฝะพััั ัะบะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะธะผะธ ะฟะพัะปะตะดััะฒะธัะผะธ ัะถะธะณะฐะฝะธั ัะณะปั ะฟัะธะฒะตะปะฐ ะบ ัะพะบัะฐัะตะฝะธั ะตะณะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะฝะธั. ะัะผ, ะพะฑัะฐะทัััะธะนัั ะฟัะธ ัะถะธะณะฐะฝะธะธ ัะณะปั, ะธะผะตะตั ะฒััะพะบะพะต ัะพะดะตัะถะฐะฝะธะต ะบะธัะปะพัั ะธ ัะพะทะดะฐะตั ััะปะพะฒะธั ะพะบััะถะฐััะตะน ััะตะดั, ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัะต ะบะฐะบ ยซะบะธัะปะพัะฝัะต ะดะพะถะดะธยป, ะบะพัะพััะต ะฒัะตะดะฝั ะดะปั ัะฐััะตะฝะธะน ะธ ะถะธะฒะพัะฝัั . ะะตัะผะพััั ะฝะฐ ัะบะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะธะต ะฟัะพะฑะปะตะผั, ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ะธะท ะบััะฟะฝะตะนัะธั ัะณะพะปัะฝัั ัะฐั ั ะฒ ะกะจะ ะฟะพ-ะฟัะตะถะฝะตะผั ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดัั ะฑะพะปะตะต 450000 ะผะตััะธัะตัะบะธั ัะพะฝะฝ ะฒ ะณะพะด, ััะพ ะดะตะปะฐะตั ะดะพะฑััั ัะณะปั ะพัะตะฝั ะฟัะธะฑัะปัะฝะพะน ะพััะฐัะปัั.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cobalt[COLOR_REVERT] is a chemical element that can be found in Earth's crust only in chemically combined form, save for small deposits found in alloys of natural meteoric iron. The free element, produced by reductive smelting, is a hard, lustrous, silver-gray metal. Cobalt-based blue pigments (cobalt blue) have been used since ancient times for jewelry and paints, and to impart a distinctive blue tint to glass. Cobalt is primarily used in lithium-ion batteries, and in the manufacture of magnetic, wear-resistant and high-strength alloys.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cobalto[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un elemento chimico che puรฒ essere trovato nella crosta terrestre solo in composti chimici, salvo piccoli depositi nelle leghe di ferro meteorico naturale. L'elemento libero, prodotto da fusione riduttiva, รจ un metallo duro e brillante, di colore grigio argento. I pigmenti blu derivanti dal cobalto (chiamati appunto "blu cobalto") sono stati usati fin dall'antichitร per gioielli e vernici e per conferire al vetro una distintiva tinta blu. Il cobalto viene utilizzato principalmente nelle batterie agli ioni di litio e nella produzione di leghe magnetiche ad alta resistenza.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kobalt[COLOR_REVERT], to pierwiastek chemiczny z grupy metali przejœciowych uk³adu okresowego. Ma 26 izotopów z przedzia³u mas 50โ75. Trwa³y jest tylko izotop 59, który stanowi 100% naturalnego kobaltu. Wystêpuje w skorupie ziemskiej w postaci ró¿nych minera³ów m.in. smaltynu, kobaltynu oraz skutterudytu. W postaci metalicznej w przyrodzie nie wystêpuje. Kobalt jest sk³adnikiem niektórych stopów o wysokich parametrach (tzw. nadstopy), poniewa¿ wyró¿nia siê wysok¹ temperatur¹ topnienia wynosz¹c¹ 1495 ยฐC oraz dobr¹ stabilnoœci¹ wymiarow¹ w wysokich temperaturach. Stopy te znajduj¹ zastosowanie w przemys³ach takich jak lotniczy oraz kosmiczny.
TEXT.Russian: ะะฐ ะพัะฝะพะฒะต ัะธะฝะธั ะฟะธะณะผะตะฝัะพะฒ ะบะพะฑะฐะปััะฐ (ัะธะฝะตะณะพ ะบะพะฑะฐะปััะฐ) ะธะทะณะพัะฐะฒะปะธะฒะฐะปะธัั ั ะดัะตะฒะฝะธั ะฒัะตะผะตะฝ ัะบัะฐัะตะฝะธั ะธ ะบัะฐัะบะธ, ะบะพัะพัะฐั ะฟัะธะดะฐะฒะฐะปะฐ ะธะผ ั ะฐัะฐะบัะตัะฝัะน ะณะพะปัะฑะพะน ะพััะตะฝะพะบ ััะตะบะปะฐ, ะฝะพ ัะฒะตั ะฑัะป ะฟะพะทะถะต ะฒะพััะพะทะดะฐะฝ ะฐะปั ะธะผะธะบะฐะผะธ ะธะท ะธะทะฒะตััะฝะพะณะพ ะผะตัะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะพะณะพ ะฒะธัะผััะฐ. ะจะฐั ัะตัั ะดะฐะฒะฝะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััั ะธะผั ะบะพะฑะฐะปััะพะฒะพะน ััะดั (ะฝะตะผ. ะะพะฑะปะธะฝัะบะฐั ััะดะฐ) ะดะปั ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ัะธะฝะธั ะฟะธะณะผะตะฝัะพะฒ, ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดะธะผัั ะธะท ะผะธะฝะตัะฐะปะพะฒ; ะพะฝะธ ะฑัะปะธ ัะฐะบ ะฝะฐะทะฒะฐะฝั, ะฟะพัะพะผั ััะพ ะพะฝะธ ะฑัะปะธ ะฝะฐะธะฑะพะปะตะต ะฑะตะดะฝั ััะตะดะธ ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัั ะผะตัะฐะปะปะพะฒ, ะธ ะฟะพะปััะฐะปะธ ัะดะพะฒะธััะต ะผััััะบ-ัะพะดะตัะถะฐัะธะต ะฟะฐัั ะฟัะธ ะฒัะฟะปะฐะฒะบะต.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Coca[COLOR_REVERT] is a plant native to western South America, that resembles a blackthorn bush, and grows to a height of 2-3 m (7-10 ft). The branches are straight, and the leaves are thin, opaque, oval, and taper at the extremities. A marked characteristic of the leaf is an areolated portion bounded by two longitudinal curved lines, one line on each side of the midrib, and more conspicuous on the under face of the leaf. Coca is known throughout the world for its psychoactive alkaloid, cocaine, used by natives as a stimulant.
TEXT.German: Der [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kokastrauch[COLOR_REVERT] (Erythroxylum coca) ist ein immergrรผner Strauch, der zur Familie der Rotholzgewรคchse (Erythroxylaceae) gehรถrt. Die bis zu 2,5 m hohe Pflanze mit der rรถtlichen Rinde und den roten Steinfrรผchten wรคchst in Hรถhen zwischen 300 und 2000 m รผber dem Meeresspiegel.[NEWLINE]Ursprรผnglich stammt der Kokastrauch von den Osthรคngen der Anden. Fรผr die indigene Bevรถlkerung stellte Koka dort bis zur Ankunft der spanischen Conquistadores die einzige reichhaltige Kalziumquelle dar. Die Blรคtter werden als Genuss- und Nahrungsergรคnzungsmittel verwendet, aber auch fรผr kultische und medizinische Zwecke genutzt. Sie helfen Hunger, Mรผdigkeit und Kรคlte zu verdrรคngen und sind sehr wirksam gegen die Hรถhenkrankheit, da sie die Sauerstoffaufnahme verbessern.[NEWLINE]1859 gelang es dem deutschen Chemiker Albert Niemann, Kokain aus den Pflanzen zu isolieren und dieses als schmerzbetรคubendes Medikament zu gebrauchen. Diese reine Verwendung fรผhrt aber zu schneller Abhรคngigkeit, weshalb Kokain im 20. Jahrhundert zu einer verbreiteten Droge wurde. Gleichzeitig wurde der Anbau von Kokastrรคuchern zum internationalen Politikum und der groรe politische Druck bedrohte viele Kokabauern in ihrer Existenz. Interessant ist die Tatsache, dass bei dem von den Ureinwohnern praktizierten Kauen der Kokablรคtter zusammen mit Kalk das Alkaloid Kokain vollstรคndig umgewandelt wird, sodass auch bei hรคufiger Einnahme keine Abhรคngigkeit hervorgerufen wird.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]coca[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una pianta originaria del Sud America occidentale, che ricorda un cespuglio di prugnolo e cresce fino a un'altezza di 2-3 m. I rami sono dritti e le foglie sono sottili, opache, ovali e affusolate alle estremitร . Una caratteristica marcata della foglia รจ una porzione areolata delimitata da due linee curve longitudinali, una linea su ciascun lato della parte centrale e piรน evidente sulla faccia inferiore della foglia. La coca รจ conosciuta in tutto il mondo per il suo alcaloide psicoattivo, la cocaina, usata dai nativi come stimolante.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Koka (Krasnodrzew pospolity)[COLOR_REVERT], to gatunek krzewu prawdopodobnie pochodz¹cy z rejonu Andów. Obecnie wystêpuje wy³¹cznie w uprawie. Liœcie koki, zawieraj¹ce kokainê w iloœci 0,25โ1,3%[4], s¹ wykorzystywane przez po³udniowoamerykañskich rdzennych mieszkañców jako u¿ywka. 10 ัััะพะฒ). ะะตัะฒะธ ะฟััะผัะต, ะฐ ะปะธัััั ัะพะฝะบะธะต, ะฝะตะฟัะพะทัะฐัะฝัะต, ะพะฒะฐะปัะฝัะต, ััะถะฐััะธะตัั ะฝะฐ ะบะพะฝัะฐั . ะัะปะธัะธัะตะปัะฝะพะน ะพัะพะฑะตะฝะฝะพัััั ะปะธััะฐ ัะฒะปัะตััั ะฐัะตะพะปะธัะพะฒะฐะฝะฝะฐั ัะฐััั, ะพะณัะฐะฝะธัะตะฝะฝะฐั ะดะฒัะผั ะฟัะพะดะพะปัะฝัะผะธ ะธะทะพะณะฝัััะผะธ ะปะธะฝะธัะผะธ, ะฟะพ ะพะดะฝะพะน ะปะธะฝะธะธ ั ะบะฐะถะดะพะน ััะพัะพะฝั ััะตะดะฝะตะน ะถะธะปะบะธ ะธ ะฑะพะปะตะต ะทะฐะผะตัะฝะพะน ะฝะฐ ะฝะธะถะฝะตะน ััะพัะพะฝะต ะปะธััะฐ.[PARAGRAPH:2]ะฆะฒะตัะบะธ ะผะฐะปะตะฝัะบะธะต, ัะฐัะฟะพะปะพะถะตะฝั ะฒะฝัััะธ ะณัะพะทะดะธ ะฝะฐ ะบะพัะพัะบะธั ัะตัะตัะบะฐั ; ะฒะตะฝัะธะบ ัะพััะพะธั ะธะท ะฟััะธ ะถะตะปัะพะฒะฐัะพ-ะฑะตะปัั ะปะตะฟะตััะบะพะฒ, ะฟัะปัะฝะธะบะธ ะฒ ัะพัะผะต ัะตัะดัะฐ, ะฐ ะฟะตััะธะบ ัะพััะพะธั ะธะท ััะตั ะฟะปะพะดะพะปะธััะธะบะพะฒ, ะพะฑัะตะดะธะฝะตะฝะฝัั ะฒ ััะตั ะบะฐะผะตัะฝัะน ะทะฐะฒัะทั. ะฆะฒะตัะบะธ ัะพะทัะตะฒะฐัั ะฒ ะบัะฐัะฝัะต ัะณะพะดั.

TEXT.English: The [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cocoa[COLOR_REVERT] tree is a small evergreen tree, native to the deep tropical regions of Mesoamerica. It is 4 to 8 m (13 to 26 ft) tall, with 1-2 cm (0.39-0-79 in) flowers. Its fruit, called a "cocoa pod", is ovoid, 15โ30 cm (5.9โ11.8 in) long and 8โ10 cm (3.1โ3.9 in) wide, ripening yellow to orange, and weighs about 500 g (1.1 lb) when ripe. The pod contains 20 to 60 seeds, usually called "beans", embedded in a white pulp. Seeds are the main ingredient of chocolate, while the pulp can be used to prepare juices, smoothies, jellies, and can also be distilled into an alcoholic beverage.
TEXT.German: Der [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kakaobaum[COLOR_REVERT] (Theobroma cacao) gehรถrt zur Familie der Malvengewรคchse (Malvales). Sein botanischer Name Theobroma stammt aus dem Griechischen und bedeutet so viel wie Gรถtterspeise. Er kann bis zu 15 m hoch werden, wird auf Plantagen aber meist gestutzt. Ein Baum kann tausende von Blรผten hervorbringen, aber nur sehr wenige davon erreichen die Reife und bilden die ellipsoiden Frรผchte mit den typischen Lรคngsrillen aus. Unter der ledrigen, harten Schale dieser Panzerbeeren befinden sich 30 bis 60 brรคunliche Samen (die Kakaobohnen) eingebettet in weiรes, schleimiges, aber sehr schmackhaftes Fruchtfleisch.[NEWLINE]Obwohl die Kakaopflanze aus Sรผdamerika stammt, wurde sie wohl etwa 1000 v.Chr. erstmalig in Mittelamerika angebaut. Den Funden nach zu urteilen nutzte man das Fruchtfleisch zur Herstellung eines alkoholischen Getrรคnks, die Kakaobohnen wurden noch nicht verwertet. Bei den Azteken galt der Kakao ab dem 14. Jahrhundert als heilige Pflanze und als Geschenk des Gottes Quetzalcoatl. Sie verwendeten die Bohnen als Opfergaben, Zahlungsmittel und zur Herstellung eines herben, gewรผrzten Getrรคnkes, das mit dem heutigen Kakao nichts zu tun hat. Als die Spanier 1519 Mexiko eroberten, brachten sie das "Braune Gold" mit nach Europa. Nach Deutschland kam der Kakao dann im 17. Jahrhundert zunรคchst als Arznei. Wegen allerlei nachgesagten Wirkungen entwickelte er sich zunรคchst beim Adel zum Modegetrรคnk. Spรคter begann man ihn mit Rohrzucker zu sรผรen, wodurch er den Geschmack der Europรคer besser traf.
TEXT.Italian: L'albero di [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cacao[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un piccolo albero sempreverde, originario delle regioni tropicali profonde della Mesoamerica. ร alto da 4 a 8 m, con fiori di 1-2 cm. Il suo frutto, chiamato "baccello di cacao", รจ ovoidale, lungo 15-30 cm e largo 8-10 cm, pesa circa 500 g quando matura ed cambia colore da giallo ad arancione a seconda del grado di maturazione. Il baccello contiene da 20 a 60 semi, generalmente chiamati "fagioli", incorporati in una polpa bianca. I semi sono l'ingrediente principale del cioccolato, mentre la polpa puรฒ essere utilizzata per preparare succhi, frullati, gelatine e puรฒ anche essere distillata per farne una bevanda alcolica.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kakao[COLOR_REVERT], to nasiona z owoców kakaowca w³aœciwego (Theobroma cacao). W jednym owocu znajduje siê 20โ60 nasion okrytych œluzowatym mi¹¿szem. Pierwotnie maj¹ barwê purpurow¹ do fioletowej, ale po osuszeniu s¹ czekoladowo-brunatne. Po obróbce nasion uzyskuje siê wykorzystywane w przemyœle spo¿ywczym, ale te¿ w medycynie i kosmetyce, produkty takie jak: masê kakaow¹, mas³o kakaowe i proszek kakaowy.
TEXT.Russian: Theobroma cacao, ัะฐะบะถะต ะบะฐะบ ะดะตัะตะฒะพ ะบะฐะบะฐะพ ะธ ัะพะบะพะปะฐะดะฝะพะต ะดะตัะตะฒะพ, ะฟัะตะดััะฐะฒะปัะตั ัะพะฑะพะน ะฝะตะฑะพะปััะพะต (4-8 ะผ (13-26 ัััะพะฒ) ะฒััะพัะพะน) ะฒะตัะฝะพะทะตะปะตะฝะพะต ะดะตัะตะฒะพ ะธะท ัะตะผะตะนััะฒะฐ ะะฐะปัะฒะพะฒัั , ะฟัะพะธะทัะฐััะฐััะตะต ะฒ ะณะปัะฑะพะบะธั ััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะธั ัะตะณะธะพะฝะฐั ะะผะตัะธะบะธ. ะะณะพ ัะตะผะตะฝะฐ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ะดะปั ะธะทะณะพัะพะฒะปะตะฝะธั ะบะฐะบะฐะพ-ะฟะพัะพัะบะฐ ะธ ัะพะบะพะปะฐะดะฐ.
