TEXT.English: The [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]coconut[COLOR_REVERT] tree is a member of the palm tree family and the only known living species of the genus Cocos. The term "coconut" can refer to the whole coconut palm, the seed, or the fruit. Coconuts are known for their versatility of uses, ranging from food to cosmetics. Coconut also has cultural and religious significance in certain societies, particularly in India, where it is used in Hindu rituals.
TEXT.French: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]noix de coco[COLOR_REVERT] est un membre de la famille des palmiers et la seule espรจce vivante connue du genre Cocos. Le terme "noix de coco" peut dรฉsigner le palmier entier, la graine ou le fruit. Les noix de coco sont connues pour leur polyvalence d'utilisations, allant de l'alimentation aux cosmรฉtiques. La noix de coco a รฉgalement une importance culturelle et religieuse dans certaines sociรฉtรฉs, particuliรจrement en Inde, oรน elle est utilisรฉe dans les rituels hindous.
TEXT.German: Die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kokosnuss[COLOR_REVERT] ist die Frucht der tropischen Kokospalme (Cocos nucifera). Diese Bรคumen wachsen vollkommen zweiglos bis zu einer Hรถhe von 20 bis 25 m. Kein anderer Baum hat das menschliche Bild von tropischen Kรผsten so sehr geprรคgt wie die Kokospalme.[NEWLINE]Kokospalmen werden seit mindestens 3000 Jahren angebaut. Kokosnรผsse werden als Nahrung und Getrรคnk geschรคtzt, wobei Kokosmilch roh und vergoren genossen wird. Die Kokospalme liefert heute auรerdem 8 % des Weltpflanzenรถlbedarfs. Ihr Holz liefert Baumaterial fรผr Hรผtten und ihre Blรคtter eignen sich perfekt als Dachbedeckung. Kokosfasern werden zum Flechten von Kรถrben und Seilen und die Schalen der Kokosnรผsse als Brennstoff verwendet.
TEXT.Italian: L'albero di [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cocco[COLOR_REVERT] appartiene alla famiglia delle palme ed รจ l'unica specie vivente conosciuta del genere Cocos. Il termine "cocco" puรฒ riferirsi all'intera palma, al seme o al frutto. Le noci di cocco sono famose per la loro versatilitร di utilizzo, che va dagli alimenti ai cosmetici. Il cocco ha anche un significato culturale e religioso in alcune societร , in particolare in India, dove viene utilizzato nei rituali indรน.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kokos[COLOR_REVERT], to gatunek z monotypowego rodzaju kokos (Cocos L.), nale¿¹cego do rodziny arekowatych. Wszystkie czêœci palmy kokosowej s¹ u¿yteczne. Z jednego drzewa mo¿na uzyskaæ do 75 โorzechówโ rocznie. Owoc zawiera du¿o nasyconych kwasów t³uszczowych i cukrów oraz stanowi bogate Ÿród³o ¿elaza i witaminy C, jak i potasu. Kopra zawiera do 70% t³uszczu, 14% cukrów i 7% bia³ka. W 100 g orzecha kokosu w³aœciwego znajduje siê du¿a iloœæ kwasu laurynowego (15,3 g), jak równie¿ wystêpuje 5,06 g kwasu mirystynowego. Mi¹¿sz orzecha jest bia³y i miêsisty, jadalny na surowo lub wysuszony po ugotowaniu. Starty na wiórki u¿ywany jest w cukiernictwie. Wnêtrze m³odego orzecha kokosowego wype³nia przezroczysty p³yn, tzw. โwoda kokosowaโ, która u¿ywana jest jako napój ch³odz¹cy i izotoniczny ze wzglêdu na du¿¹ zawartoœæ elektrolitów oraz po fermentacji do wyrobu galaretowatego deseru nata de coco.
TEXT.Russian: ะะพะบะพัะพะฒัะน ะพัะตั ะธะทะฒะตััะตะฝ ัะฒะพะตะน ัะฝะธะฒะตััะฐะปัะฝะพัััั, ััะพ ะฟะพะดัะฒะตัะถะดะฐะตััั ะผะฝะพะถะตััะฒะพะผ ะฟัะธะผะตะฝะตะฝะธะน ะตะณะพ ัะฐะทะปะธัะฝัั ัะฐััะตะน ะธ ะฒัััะตัะฐะตััั ะฒ ััะพะฟะธะบะฐั ะธ ััะฑััะพะฟะธะบะฐั . ะะพะบะพัั ะฒั ะพะดัั ะฒ ะตะถะตะดะฝะตะฒะฝัะน ัะฐัะธะพะฝ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ะปัะดะตะน. ะะพะบะพัั ะพัะปะธัะฐัััั ะพั ะปัะฑัั ะดััะณะธั ัััะบัะพะฒ, ะฟะพัะพะผั ััะพ ะพะฝะธ ัะพะดะตัะถะฐั ะฑะพะปััะพะต ะบะพะปะธัะตััะฒะพ ยซะฒะพะดัยป, ะฐ ะบะพะณะดะฐ ะพะฝะธ ะฝะตะทัะตะปัะต, ะพะฝะธ ะธะทะฒะตััะฝั ะบะฐะบ ะฝะตะถะฝัะต ะพัะตั ะธ ะธะปะธ ะถะตะปะตะนะฝัะต ะพัะตั ะธ, ะธ ะธั ะผะพะถะฝะพ ัะพะฑะธัะฐัั ะดะปั ะฟะธััั. ะ ะทัะตะปะพะผ ัะพััะพัะฝะธะธ ะพะฝะธ ะฒัะต ะตัะต ัะพะดะตัะถะฐั ะฝะตะผะฝะพะณะพ ะฒะพะดั ะธ ะผะพะณัั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐัััั ะบะฐะบ ัะตะผะตะฝะฝัะต ะพัะตั ะธ ะธะปะธ ะพะฑัะฐะฑะฐััะฒะฐัััั ะดะปั ะฟะพะปััะตะฝะธั ะผะฐัะปะฐ ะธะท ัะดัะฐ, ะดัะตะฒะตัะฝะพะณะพ ัะณะปั ะธะท ัะฒะตัะดะพะน ะพะฑะพะปะพัะบะธ ะธ ะบะพะบะพัะพะฒะพะณะพ ะฒะพะปะพะบะฝะฐ ะธะท ะฒะพะปะพะบะฝะธััะพะน ัะตะปัั ะธ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Coffee[COLOR_REVERT] is a darkly colored, bitter, slightly acidic brewed drink, prepared from roasted coffee beans, the seeds of berries from certain Coffea species that, once ripe, are picked, processed, and dried. Dried coffee seeds (referred to as "beans") are roasted to varying degrees, depending on the desired flavor. Roasted beans are ground and then brewed with near-boiling water to produce the beverage. Coffee has a stimulating effect in humans, primarily due to its caffeine content, and it is one of the most popular drinks in the world.
TEXT.German: Botanisch wird mit [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kaffee[COLOR_REVERT] (Coffea) eine Gattung immergrรผner Bรคume oder Strรคucher aus der Familie der Rรถtegewรคchse (Rubiaceae) mit รผber 100 bekannten Arten bezeichnet, darunter die fรผr Plantagenanbau bevorzugten Coffea arabica und Coffea canephora (bekannter als Coffea robusta). Interessant fรผr den Menschen sind die Steinfrรผchte, die Kaffeekirschen oder -beeren. Sie schmecken sรผร, besitzen aber nur wenig Fruchtfleisch, unter dem sich in der Regel zwei Samen, die sogenannten Kaffeebohnen befinden. Diese Bohnen dienen als Rohstoff fรผr das Heiรgetrรคnk Kaffee.[NEWLINE]Fast alle Arten haben ihre ursprรผngliche Heimat im tropischen Afrika. Nach einer Legende soll einst den Hirten aus dem im Sรผdwesten des heutigen รthiopien liegenden Kรถnigreichs Kaffa aufgefallen sein, dass ein Teil ihrer Ziegenherde, der von einem Strauch mit weiรen Blรผten und roten Frรผchten gefressen hatte, bis in die Nacht hinein munter umhersprang, wรคhrend die anderen Tiere mรผde waren. Die Hirten erzรคhlten den Mรถnchen eines nahe gelegenen Klosters davon, die sich auf die Suche nach den beschriebenen Pflanzen machten. Sie experimentierten mit den kirschenรคhnlichen Frรผchten herum und bereiteten schlieรlich einen Aufguss daraus zu, der eine belebende Wirkung entfaltete, sodass sie fortan bis tief in die Nacht hinein wach bleiben, beten und miteinander reden konnten.[NEWLINE]Heute nimmt man tatsรคchlich an, dass die Kaffa-Region der Ursprung des Kaffeegetrรคnks ist, denn dort wird er bereits im 9. Jahrhundert erstmalig erwรคhnt. Von รthiopien aus gelangte er im 14. Jahrhundert nach Arabien, das bereits ein Jahrhundert spรคter die Monopolstellung im Kaffeeanbau hatte.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]caffรจ[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una bevanda di colore scuro, amaro, leggermente acido, preparato con chicchi di caffรจ tostati, ossia i semi di bacche di alcune specie di Coffea che, una volta maturi, vengono raccolti, processati ed essiccati. I semi di caffรจ essiccati (chiamati "chicchi") vengono piรน o meno tostati, a seconda del sapore desiderato. I chicchi vengono poi macinati e quindi preparati con acqua quasi bollente per produrre la bevanda. Il caffรจ ha un effetto stimolante sull'uomo, principalmente a causa del suo contenuto di caffeina, ed รจ una delle bevande piรน popolari al mondo.

Pochodzi z Etiopii, w Europie pojawi³a siê oko³o XVI wieku. Jedna z najpopularniejszych u¿ywek na œwiecie i g³ówne Ÿród³o kofeiny.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]computer[COLOR_REVERT] is an automated programmable machine, capable of performing both complex mathematical calculations and other types of data processing. Conceived as a machine to automate some tasks (calculation, storage capacity, etc.), only from the second half of the 20th century it has evolved into a machine capable of performing a vast number of data processing.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ordinateur[COLOR_REVERT] est une machine programmable automatisรฉe, capable d'effectuer ร la fois des calculs mathรฉmatiques complexes et d'autres types de traitement de donnรฉes. Conรงu comme une machine pour automatiser certaines tรขches (calcul, capacitรฉ de stockage, etc.), ce n'est qu'ร partir de la seconde moitiรฉ du 20e siรจcle qu'il a รฉvoluรฉ en une machine capable d'effectuer un vaste nombre de traitements de donnรฉes.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]computer[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una macchina automatizzata programmabile, in grado di eseguire sia complessi calcoli matematici, sia altri tipi di elaborazioni dati. Concepito come una macchina per automatizzare alcuni compiti (calcolo, capacitร di memorizzazione, ecc.), solo a partire dalla seconda metร del XX secolo รจ evoluto in una macchina in grado di eseguire le elaborazioni dati piรน varie.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Computronium[COLOR_REVERT] is a material firstly hypothesized by Norman Margolus and Tommaso Toffoli of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 1991. It is a material that is to be used as "programmable matter", a substrate for computer modeling of virtually any real object.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]computronium[COLOR_REVERT] est un matรฉriau d'abord hypothรฉtisรฉ par Norman Margolus et Tommaso Toffoli du Massachusetts Institute of Technology en 1991. C'est un matรฉriau destinรฉ ร รชtre utilisรฉ comme "matiรจre programmable", un substrat pour la modรฉlisation informatique de pratiquement n'importe quel objet rรฉel.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]computronio[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un materiale inizialmente ipotizzato da Norman Margolus e Tommaso Toffoli del Massachusetts Institute of Technology nel 1991. ร un materiale che puรฒ essere usato come "materia programmabile", ossia un substrato per la modellazione al computer di qualsiasi oggetto reale.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]conscious material[COLOR_REVERT] is a substance that is conscious at the atomic and subatomic levels, and that can behave in very interesting and unpredictable ways.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]matรฉriau conscient[COLOR_REVERT] est une substance qui est consciente aux niveaux atomique et subatomique, et qui peut se comporter de maniรจre trรจs intรฉressante et imprรฉvisible.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Copper[COLOR_REVERT] is a pinkish-reddish metal, with extremely high electrical and thermal conductivity and very resistant to corrosion. It is easily workable, extremely ductile and malleable, but it is not suitable for machining with chip removal, because it has a rather pasty consistency. It can be easily recycled and its scraps have a high recovery value; it combines with other metals to form numerous metal alloys, such as bronze (with tin) and brass (with zinc). It has been used by humanity for over five millennia.
TEXT.German: Kupfer wird seit mehr als 5000 Jahren von den Menschen genutzt. Das weiche Metall kann in seinem natรผrlichen Zustand nur รผber einen kurzen Zeitraum verwendet werden, da beispielsweise Schwerter und Dolche aus Kupfer sehr schnell stumpf werden. Es sieht jedoch sehr gut aus und wurde im Allgemeinen fรผr Anhรคnger, Armbรคnder und andere dekorative Artefakte verwendet. Etwa um 3000 vor Christus erkannte man, dass ein deutlich hรคrteres und nรผtzlicheres Produkt entstand, wenn man Zink oder Zinn gemeinsam mit dem Kupfer schmolz: Bronze. Wenig spรคter waren Bronzewaffen, -rรผstungen und -werkzeuge weit verbreitet. Dies war der Beginn des Bronzezeitalters. [PARAGRAPH:1]Interessanterweise ist Bronze hรคrter als Eisen. Das Bronzezeitalter wurde von der Eisenzeit also nicht abgelรถst, weil Eisen das bessere Metall war, sondern weil Zinn und Zink selten geworden waren.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]rame[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un metallo rosato o rossastro, di conducibilitร elettrica e termica elevatissima e molto resistente alla corrosione. ร facilmente lavorabile, estremamente duttile e malleabile, ma non รจ idoneo a lavorazioni con asportazione di truciolo, perchรฉ ha una consistenza piuttosto pastosa. Puรฒ essere facilmente riciclato e i suoi rottami hanno un alto valore di recupero; si combina con altri metalli a formare numerose leghe metalliche, come il bronzo (con lo stagno) e l'ottone (con lo zinco). ร stato utilizzato dall'umanitร per oltre cinque millenni.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]MiedŸ[COLOR_REVERT], to pierwiastek chemiczny z grupy metali przejœciowych. Nazwa ³aciñska (a za ni¹ w innych jêzykach, w tym angielskim) pochodzi od Cypru, gdzie w staro¿ytnoœci wydobywano miedŸ. Ze wzglêdu na du¿e zapotrzebowanie i stosunkowo ma³e zasoby naturalne, miedŸ stanowi materia³ strategiczny. Wiêkszoœæ miedzi wydobywa siê jako siarczek w kopalniach odkrywkowych ze z³ó¿ porfiru miedziowego zawieraj¹cego do 1% miedzi. MiedŸ obok ¿elaza odegra³a wyj¹tkow¹ rolê w rozwoju cywilizacji ludzkiej. Pierwiastek ten znany jest od staro¿ytnoœci, od kiedy by³ podstawowym sk³adnikiem br¹zów, od których nazwano epokê br¹zu. G³ównym zastosowaniem miedzi jest produkcja przewodów elektrycznych (60%), pokryæ dachów i instalacji wodoci¹gowych (20%) oraz maszyn przemys³owych (15%). TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]copper wire[COLOR_REVERT] is a single electrical conductor made of copper. It can be insulated or uninsulated. A copper cable is a group of two or more copper wires bundled together in a single sheath or jacket. Copper wires and cables are used in power generation, power transmission, power distribution, telecommunications, electronics circuitry, and countless types of electrical equipment. It has been useful ever since telegraphs and electromagnets were invented.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fil de cuivre[COLOR_REVERT] est un conducteur รฉlectrique unique fait de cuivre. Il peut รชtre isolรฉ ou non isolรฉ. Un cรขble en cuivre est un groupe de deux ou plusieurs fils de cuivre regroupรฉs dans une seule gaine. Les fils et cรขbles en cuivre sont utilisรฉs dans la production d'รฉnergie, la transmission d'รฉnergie, la distribution d'รฉnergie, les tรฉlรฉcommunications, les circuits รฉlectroniques et d'innombrables types d'รฉquipements รฉlectriques. Il a รฉtรฉ utile depuis l'invention des tรฉlรฉgraphes et des รฉlectroaimants.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]filo di rame[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un conduttore elettrico fatto di rame. Puรฒ essere isolato o non isolato. Un cavo di rame รจ un gruppo di due o piรน fili di rame raggruppati insieme con una guaina. I fili e i cavi di rame sono utilizzati nella generazione, trasmissione e distribuzione di energia, telecomunicazioni, circuiti elettronici e innumerevoli tipi di apparecchiature elettriche. ร stato utile sin da quando sono stati inventati i telegrafi e gli elettromagneti.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Przewód miedziany[COLOR_REVERT], to pojedynczy przewodnik elektryczny wytworzony z miedzi. Mo¿e byæ odizolowany, lub nie. Kabel miedziany, to grupa twóch lub wiêcej przeodów zwiniêtych ze sob¹. Przewody miedziane s¹ wykorzystywane na przyk³ad w generacji pr¹du, przesyle, telekomunikacji, lub obwodach elektrycznych.
TEXT.Russian: ะญะปะตะบััะธัะตัะบะธะต ัะฒะพะนััะฒะฐ ะผะตะดะธ ัะบัะฟะปัะฐัะธัััััั ะฝะฐ ะผะตะดะฝัั ะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฐั ะธ ััััะพะนััะฒะฐะผะฐั , ัะฐะบะธั ะบะฐะบ ัะปะตะบััะพะผะฐะณะฝะธัั. ะะฝัะตะณัะฐะปัะฝัะต ัั ะตะผั ะธ ะฟะตัะฐัะฝัะต ะฟะปะฐัั ะฒัะต ัะฐัะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ั ะผะตะดัั ะฒะผะตััะพ ะฐะปัะผะธะฝะธั ะธะท-ะทะฐ ะตะณะพ ะฟัะตะฒะพัั ะพะดะฝะพะน ัะปะตะบััะพะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฝะพััะธ. ะะตะดะฝัะต ะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฐ ะธ ะบะฐะฑะตะปะธ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ะฒ ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดััะฒะต ัะปะตะบััะพัะฝะตัะณะธะธ, ะฟะตัะตะดะฐัะต ัะฝะตัะณะธะธ, ัะฐัะฟัะตะดะตะปะตะฝะธะธ ัะฝะตัะณะธะธ, ัะตะปะตะบะพะผะผัะฝะธะบะฐัะธัั , ัะปะตะบััะพะฝะฝัั ัั ะตะผะฐั ะธ ะฑะตััะธัะปะตะฝะฝัั ัะธะฟะฐั ัะปะตะบััะพะพะฑะพััะดะพะฒะฐะฝะธั. ะญัะพ ะฑัะปะพ ะฟะพะปะตะทะฝะพ ั ัะตั ะฟะพั, ะบะฐะบ ะฑัะปะธ ะธะทะพะฑัะตัะตะฝั ัะตะปะตะณัะฐัั ะธ ัะปะตะบััะพะผะฐะณะฝะธัั.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Maize[COLOR_REVERT], also known as corn, is a cereal grain first domesticated by indigenous peoples in southern Mexico about 10,000 years ago. The leafy stalk of the plant produces pollen inflorescences and separate ovuliferous inflorescences called ears that yield kernels or seeds. Corn has become a staple food in many parts of the world, however only a little percentage of its production is for human consumption: most is used for corn ethanol, animal feed and other products, such as corn starch and corn syrup, or even distilled fermentations (such as bourbon whiskeys).
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Mais[COLOR_REVERT] (Zea mays) ist ein Getreide aus der Familie der Sรผรgrรคser mit kolbenfรถrmigen Fruchtstรคnden, das bei der aktuellen Weltgetreideernte mit รผber einer Milliarde Tonnen auf dem ersten Platz liegt.[NEWLINE]Der Mais stammt ursprรผnglich aus einem Tal im sรผdlichen Mexiko. Angeblich wurde er dort bereits vor 7500 bis 12000 Jahren angebaut. Einige fossile Maiskolben, die Archรคologen in der Guila Naquitz Cave im Oaxaca-Tal in Mexiko fanden, sind etwa 6250 Jahre alt. Christoph Kolumbus entdeckte die Pflanze in der Karibik und brachte sie mit nach Spanien, wo bereits Anfang des 16. Jahrhunderts die ersten Felder mit Mais bebaut wurden.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]mais[COLOR_REVERT], anche noto come granoturco, รจ un cereale che รจ stato reso coltivabile circa 10.000 anni fa dalle popolazioni indigene del Messico meridionale. Il gambo frondoso della pianta produce infiorescenze polliniche e infiorescenze ovulifere separate (le "spighe"), che producono semi (i "chicchi"). Il granoturco รจ diventato un alimento base in molte parti del mondo, tuttavia solo una piccola percentuale della sua produzione รจ destinata al consumo umano: la maggior parte viene utilizzata per produrre bioetanolo, alimenti per animali e altri prodotti, come l'amido o lo sciroppo di mais, o addirittura distillati fermentati (come i whisky di bourbon).
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kukurydza[COLOR_REVERT] (Kukurydza zwyczajna), to gatunek roœliny jednorocznej z rodziny wiechlinowatych. Roœlina ciep³o- i œwiat³olubna. Nale¿y do zbó¿. Cechuje j¹ du¿a wydajnoœæ i wartoœæ pokarmowa. W krajach s³abo rozwiniêtych jest podstaw¹ wy¿ywienia, w krajach wysoko rozwiniêtych โ stosowana jest g³ównie jako pasza dla zwierz¹t gospodarskich.
TEXT.Russian: ะขะตัะผะธะฝ ยซะบัะบัััะทะฐยป ะฟัะธะผะตะฝัะตััั ะบ ะปัะฑะพะผั ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผั ะฟะธัะตะฒะพะผั ะทะตัะฝั ะธะปะธ ัััะบัะฐะผ ัะฐััะตะฝะธั ัะตะผะตะนััะฒะฐ ะทะปะฐะบะพะฒัั . ะะณะพ ัะฐะบะถะต ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐัั ยซะบัะบัััะทะพะนยป. ะ ะพะดะธะฝะพะน ะบัะบัััะทั ัะฒะปัะตััั ะดะพะปะธะฝะฐ ะฝะฐ ัะณะต ะะตะบัะธะบะธ, ะธ ััะธัะฐะตััั, ััะพ ะตะต ะฒััะฐัะธะฒะฐะฝะธะต ะฝะฐัะฐะปะพัั ะพั 7500 ะดะพ 12000 ะปะตั ะฝะฐะทะฐะด. ะัั ะตะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะธะต ะพััะฐะฝะบะธ ัะฐะผะพะณะพ ัะฐะฝะฝะตะณะพ ะฟะพัะฐัะบะฐ ะบัะบัััะทั, ะฝะฐะนะดะตะฝะฝะพะณะพ ะฒ ะฟะตัะตัะต ะะธะปะฐ ะะฐะบะธัั ะฒ ะดะพะปะธะฝะต ะะฐั ะฐะบะฐ ะฒ ะะตะบัะธะบะต, ะดะฐัะธัััััั ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ 6250 ะณะพะดะฐะผะธ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cotton fiber[COLOR_REVERT] is a fiber made of almost pure cellulose. It is most often spun into yarn or thread and used to make a soft, breathable textile. Cotton fibers occur naturally in colors of white, brown, pink and green, even though fears of contaminating the genetics of white cotton have led many cotton-growing locations to ban the growing of colored cotton varieties.
TEXT.French: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fibre de coton[COLOR_REVERT] est une fibre composรฉe de cellulose presque pure. Elle est le plus souvent filรฉe en fil et utilisรฉe pour fabriquer un textile doux et respirant. Les fibres de coton se prรฉsentent naturellement dans des couleurs blanches, brunes, roses et vertes, mรชme si la crainte de contaminer la gรฉnรฉtique du coton blanc a conduit de nombreux sites de culture du coton ร interdire la culture de variรฉtรฉs de coton colorรฉes.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fibra di cotone[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una fibra di cellulosa quasi pura. Viene spesso filata e utilizzata per creare un tessuto morbido e traspirante. Le fibre di cotone si presentano naturalmente nei colori bianco, marrone, rosa e verde, anche se i timori di contaminare i campi di cotone bianco hanno portato molte zone di coltivazione del cotone a vietare la coltivazione di varietร colorate.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]W³ókno bawe³niane[COLOR_REVERT], to w³ókno otaczaj¹ce nasiona roœliny okreœlanej t¹ sam¹ nazw¹ โ bawe³ny (Gossypium), maj¹ce wiele zastosowañ. S³u¿y m.in. do wytwarzania miêkkiej tkaniny โ najpopularniejszej w przemyœle tekstylnym, a tak¿e, ze wzglêdu na silne w³asnoœci absorpcyjne, do produkcji materia³ów opatrunkowych. W³ókno bawe³niane jest te¿ stosowane w mieszankach w³ókien, jako dodatek do we³ny czy lnu. Oprócz przemys³u tekstylnego, bawe³na znajduje zastosowanie w produkcji materia³ów opatrunkowych, sieci rybackich, filtrów do kawy, papieru (banknoty dolarowe, a tak¿e ekskluzywne materia³y piœmienne), prochu strzelniczego. Kiedyœ u¿ywano jej do produkcji wê¿y stra¿ackich.
TEXT.Russian: รรซรฎรฏรฎรช - รฝรฒรฎ รฌรฟรฃรชรฎรฅ, รฏรณรธรจรฑรฒรฎรฅ รธรฒร รฏรฅรซรผรญรฎรฅ รขรฎรซรฎรชรญรฎ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รฐร รฑรฒรฅรฒ รข รชรฎรฐรฎรกรฎรทรชร รต, รจรซรจ รงร รนรจรฒรญรปรฅ รชร รฏรฑรณรซรป รขรฎรชรฐรณรฃ รฑรฅรฌรฟรญ รตรซรฎรฏรทร รฒรญรจรชร รฐร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฉ รฐรฎรคร Gossypium. รรฎรซรฎรชรญร รฏรฎ รฑรณรฒรจ - รทรจรฑรฒร รฟ รถรฅรซรซรพรซรฎรงร . ร รฅรฑรฒรฅรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรฎรฌ รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรญรจรจ รขร รฒรญรปรฅ รธร รฐรจรชรจ รกรณรครณรฒ รจรฌรฅรฒรผ รฒรฅรญรครฅรญรถรจรพ รช รณรขรฅรซรจรทรฅรญรจรพ รฐร รฑรฑรฅรจรขร รญรจรฟ รฑรฅรฌรฟรญ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cotton[COLOR_REVERT] is a soft, fluffy staple fiber that grows in a boll, or protective case, around the seeds of the cotton plants. Under natural conditions, the cotton bolls will increase the dispersal of the seeds. The plant is a shrub native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, and was independently domesticated in the various continents. The use of cotton for fabric is known to date to prehistoric times, with some fragments of cotton fabric found that were dated around 6-5th millennium BC.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Baumwolle[COLOR_REVERT] (Gossypium) gehรถrt zur Familie der Malvengewรคchse (Malvaceae). Innerhalb der Gattung existieren etwa 50 verschiedene Arten, die allesamt als krautige Pflanzen oder Strรคucher wachsen. Ihre kugeligen oder elipsoiden Kapselfrรผchte enthalten giftige Samen, aus deren langen, wolligen, intensiv weiรen Samenhaaren die begehrte Naturfaser gewonnen wird.[NEWLINE]Baumwolle wird seit Jahrtausenden zur Herstellung leichter Kleidung verwendet. Funde aus der neolithischen Siedlung Mehrgarh in Indien beweisen, dass sie dort schon ca. 6000 v.Chr. angebaut wurde. Im zweiten vorchristlichen Jahrtausend gelangte Baumwolle ins Babylonische Reich in Mesopotamien und nach รgypten. Selbst die amerikanischen Vรถlker nutzten die Baumwolle schon lange, bevor im Mittelalter ihr Anbau und ihre Verarbeitung durch die Araber รผber Spanien und Italien in Europa eingefรผhrt wurde. Nicht nur Venedig und Augsburg verdanken einen Groรteil ihres frรผhen Reichtums dem Baumwollhandel, auch der Erfolg der britischen Ostindien-Kompanie beruht zum groรen Teil auf Baumwolle. Ende des 18./Anfang des 19. Jahrhunderts setzte sich die Baumwollfaser dann aufgrund ihrer grรถรeren Verfรผgbarkeit durch den zunehmenden Anbau gerade auch in den Kolonien nach und nach gegen die Wolle durch.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cotone[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una fibra di base morbida e soffice che cresce in una capsula, o custodia protettiva, attorno ai semi delle piante. In condizioni naturali, le capsule di cotone aumentano la dispersione dei semi. La pianta รจ un arbusto originario delle regioni tropicali e subtropicali di tutto il mondo ed รจ stata resa coltivabile in modo indipendente nei vari continenti. L'uso del cotone per realizzare tessuti รจ noto fino alla preistoria: alcuni frammenti di tessuto di cotone risalgono al VI-V millennio a.C.

W stanie dzikim wystêpuj¹ na s³abo nawodnionych obszarach wszystkich kontynentów, poza Australi¹ i Antarktyd¹. Piêæ gatunków ma znaczenie ekonomiczne, pozosta³e nie s¹ wykorzystywane. Bawe³na jest najwa¿niejsz¹ roœlin¹ w³óknodajn¹, która dostarcza 70% rocznego zu¿ycia tkanin odzie¿owych œwiata, u¿ytkowan¹ od kilku tysiêcy lat.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cows[COLOR_REVERT] (or cattle) are the most common type of large domesticated ungulates. Cattle are commonly raised as livestock for meat (beef or veal), for milk, and for hides, which are used to make leather. They are used as riding animals and draft animals (oxen or bullocks, which pull carts, plows and other implements). Another product of cattle is dung, which can be used to create manure or fuel. In some regions, such as parts of India, cattle have significant religious meaning.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Rinder[COLOR_REVERT], die Nachkommen des Bรผffels und des Bisons, erwiesen sich in der Vergangenheit wie in der Gegenwart als sehr nรผtzlich fรผr den Menschen. Ursprรผnglich durchstreiften sie als wilde Herden Asien, Afrika und Europa. In der Frรผhzeit dienten sie den Menschen als Handelsgut und Nahrungsquelle und verrichteten schwere Arbeiten. Spรคter wurde die Rolle des bevorzugten Lasttiers vom Pferd รผbernommen. Die Rรถmer waren die Ersten, die sich an der Viehzucht versuchten, um bestimmte Eigenschaften zu verstรคrken. Ihre Neuzรผchtungen zeichneten sich vor allem durch ihre Fรคhigkeit aus, selbst in unwirtlichen Gegenden รผberleben zu kรถnnen, die sich nicht fรผr die landwirtschaftliche Bebauung eigneten. Im Jahre 1521 brachten die Spanier Weidevieh nach Mexiko und in die Neue Welt, woraufhin vor allem in Texas und den benachbarten Regionen zahlreiche Viehzucht-Ranchen entstanden. Heute wird der Viehbestand allein in den Vereinigten Staaten von Amerika auf mehr als 100 Millionen Tiere beziffert.
TEXT.Italian: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]mucche[COLOR_REVERT] (o bovini) sono il tipo piรน comune di grandi ungulati domestici. I bovini vengono comunemente allevati come bestiame per carne (manzo o vitello) e per la produzione di latte e pelli. Sono usati come animali da sella e da tiro (carri, aratri e altri attrezzi). Un altro prodotto del bestiame รจ lo sterco, che puรฒ essere utilizzato per creare letame o carburante. In alcune regioni, come parti dell'India, i bovini hanno un significato religioso significativo.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Crabs[COLOR_REVERT] are a type of mainly aquatic crustacean, equipped with a robust carapace and two powerful claws, which they use to take, feed and defend themselves. Their abdomen is folded vertically and is, therefore, hidden. Many species are nocturnal, when there is less presence of potential predators. Feeding varies from species to species and includes animals, plants, and carcasses. They are generally edible; some species are quite valuable.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]crabes[COLOR_REVERT] sont un type de crustacรฉ principalement aquatique, รฉquipรฉ d'une carapace robuste et de deux puissantes pinces, qu'ils utilisent pour saisir, se nourrir et se dรฉfendre. Leur abdomen est pliรฉ verticalement et est, par consรฉquent, cachรฉ. De nombreuses espรจces sont nocturnes, lorsqu'il y a moins de prรฉsence de prรฉdateurs potentiels. L'alimentation varie d'une espรจce ร l'autre et comprend des animaux, des plantes et des carcasses. Ils sont gรฉnรฉralement comestibles ; certaines espรจces sont assez prรฉcieuses.
TEXT.German: Krabben bilden traditionell eine wichtige Nahrungsgrundlage der Menschen an der Ostkรผste der USA. In der Chesapeake Bay werden jรคhrlich Tausende von Krabben gefangen. Bekannte Krabbenarten sind die Alaska-Kรถnigskrabbe und die Kรถnigskrabbe.
TEXT.Italian: I [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]granchi[COLOR_REVERT] sono un tipo di crostaceo prevalentemente acquatico, dotato di un robusto carapace e di due potenti chele, che usano per prendere, cibarsi e difendersi. L'addome รจ ripiegato verticalmente ed รจ quindi nascosto. Molte specie sono notturne, quando la presenza di potenziali predatori รจ minore. L'alimentazione varia da specie a specie e comprende animali, piante e carcasse. Sono in genere commestibili e alcune specie sono anche piuttosto pregiate.

TEXT.English: After passing the "big, shiny and sciencey!" period, a highly developed civilization can enter a stage where technology continues to advance, but becomes a lot sleeker and subtler. At the same time, society gets epic. Jumpsuits will start being replaced by togas, robes and such garments, bustling mega-cities by brilliant arcologies. There will be crystals. Lots and lots of crystals. This world of tomorrow may end up looking much like Ancient Greece or Rome (the theme-park Cecil B. de Mille version at least) while still enjoying ultratech comforts.
TEXT.Polish: Po przejœciu przez fazê: "wielkie, œwiec¹ce i naukowe!", wysoko rozwiniêta cywilizacja mo¿e osi¹gn¹æ poziom, gdzie technologia dalej siê rozwija, ale staje siê znacznie bardziej subtelna. Tym samym spo³eczeñstwo zaczyna odchodziæ od modernistycznego stylu na rzecz powrotu do staro¿ytnoœci. têtni¹ce ¿yciem metropolie zostaj¹ zast¹pione przez nowoczesne arkologie, a kombinezony przez dawne togi oraz szaty. Dodatkowo wszêdzie s¹ kryszta³y, mnóstwo kryszta³ów. Œwiat przysz³oœci mo¿e w znacznym stopniu przypominaæ staro¿ytny Rzym lub Grecjê, jednoczeœnie ciesz¹c siê zapewnianym przez najnowsz¹ technologiê komfortem.
TEXT.Italian: Dopo aver superato il periodo "grande, lucido e scientifico!", una civiltร altamente sviluppata puรฒ entrare in una fase in cui la tecnologia continua ad avanzare, ma diventa molto piรน elegante e sottile. Allo stesso tempo, la societร diventa epica. Le tute cominceranno ad essere sostituite da toghe, vesti e indumenti simili, animate megalopoli da brillanti arcologie. Ci saranno cristalli. Un sacco di cristalli. Questo mondo di domani potrebbe finire per assomigliare molto all'antica Grecia o a Roma (almeno la versione del parco a tema di Cecil B. de Mille) pur godendo di comfort ultratecnologici.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Lead glass[COLOR_REVERT], commonly called crystal, is a variety of glass in which lead replaces the calcium content of a typical potash glass. It is retained from the Venetian word "cristallo" to describe the rock crystal imitated by Murano glassmakers. This naming convention has been maintained to describe decorative hollow-ware. The earliest known example is a blue glass fragment from Nippur (Mesopotamia) dated to 1400 BC. Lead glass also occurs in Han-period China (206 BC โ 220 AD), where it was cast to imitate jade, both for ritual objects such as big and small figures, as well as jewellery and a limited range of vessels. In medieval and early modern Europe, lead glass was used as a base in coloured glasses, specifically in mosaic tesserae, enamels, stained-glass painting, and bijouterie, where it was used to imitate precious stones. English entrepreneur George Ravenscroft was the first to produce clear lead crystal glassware on an industrial scale, seeking to find an alternative to Venetian cristallo. From the 18th century on, English lead glass became popular throughout Europe, and was being produced in France, Hungary, Germany, and Norway.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Szk³o o³owiowe (kryszta³owe)[COLOR_REVERT], to ezbarwna i przezroczysta odmiana szk³a stosowana do wyrobu przedmiotów dekoracyjnych, zawieraj¹ca pewn¹ iloœæ o³owiu wprowadzonego w postaci tlenków (glejty lub minii). Wprowadzenie o³owiu do szk³a powoduje zmianê szeregu w³asnoœci fizycznych szk³a: Znaczny wzrost wspó³czynnika za³amania œwiat³a co sprawia, ¿e szk³o o³owiowe ma specyficzny blask i jest przez to bardzo dobrym materia³em na wyroby dekoracyjne. Zmniejszenie elektrycznej przewodnoœci skroœnej. Wykorzystuje siê tê w³asnoœæ przy wytwarzaniu szkie³ do izolacji metalowych czêœci lamp. Ponadto szk³o o³owiowe jest bardziej miêkkie i mniej odporne na dzia³anie kwasów, przez co dobrze nadaje siê do rzeŸbienia i trawienia. Dodatek o³owiu zmniejsza te¿ lepkoœæ masy szklanej i u³atwia jej topienie, klarowanie oraz formowanie.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]vetro-cristallo[COLOR_REVERT] o cristallo al piombo, comunemente chiamato cristallo, รจ una varietร di vetro in cui il piombo sostituisce il contenuto di calcio di un tipico vetro di carbonato di potassio. Il nome รจ ispirato al cristallo di rocca usato dai vetrai di Murano. Questa denominazione รจ stata mantenuta per descrivere questo tipo di oggetti decorativi. Il primo esempio conosciuto รจ un frammento di vetro blu di Nippur (Mesopotamia) datato 1400 a.C. Il vetro-cristallo si trova anche nella Cina del periodo Han (206 a.C. - 220 d.C.), dove veniva fuso per imitare la giada, sia per oggetti rituali come figure grandi e piccole, sia per gioielli e una gamma limitata di vasi. Nell'Europa medievale e della prima etร moderna, il vetro al piombo era usato come base nei vetri colorati, in particolare nelle tessere di mosaico, negli smalti, nella pittura su vetro colorato e nella bigiotteria, dove veniva usato per imitare le pietre preziose. L'imprenditore inglese George Ravenscroft รจ stato il primo a produrre cristalli al piombo trasparenti su scala industriale, cercando di trovare un'alternativa al cristallo veneziano. Dal XVIII secolo in poi, il vetro al piombo inglese divenne popolare in tutta Europa e venne prodotto in Francia, Ungheria, Germania e Norvegia.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cybernetic chloroplasts[COLOR_REVERT] are artificial organelles (i.e. specialized subunits) in plant and algae cells. Their main role is to conduct photosynthesis, where the photosynthetic pigment chlorophyll captures the energy from sunlight and stores it in the ATP and NADPH energy-storage molecules, while freeing oxygen from water.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chloroplastes cybernรฉtiques[COLOR_REVERT] sont des organites artificiels (c'est-ร -dire des sous-unitรฉs spรฉcialisรฉes) dans les cellules des plantes et des algues. Leur rรดle principal est de rรฉaliser la photosynthรจse, oรน le pigment photosynthรฉtique chlorophylle capture l'รฉnergie de la lumiรจre solaire et la stocke dans les molรฉcules de stockage d'รฉnergie ATP et NADPH, tout en libรฉrant l'oxygรจne de l'eau.
TEXT.Italian: I [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cloroplasti cibernetici[COLOR_REVERT] sono organelli (cioรจ sotto-unitร specializzate) artificiali delle cellule di piante e alghe. Il loro ruolo principale รจ condurre la fotosintesi, dove la clorofilla pigmentata fotosintetica cattura l'energia dalla luce solare e la immagazzina nelle molecole di accumulo di energia ATP e NADPH, liberando ossigeno dall'acqua.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cybernetyczne chloroplasty[COLOR_REVERT], to sztuczne organella komurkowe, których g³ównym zadaniem jest przeprowadzanie procesu fotosyntezy.

TEXT.English: Cyberpunk is a subgenre of science fiction in a dystopian futuristic setting that tends to focus on a "combination of lowlife and high tech", featuring futuristic technological and scientific achievements, such as artificial intelligence and cybernetics, juxtaposed with societal collapse or decay. Much of cyberpunk is rooted in the New Wave science fiction movement of the 1960s and 1970s, when writers like Philip K. Dick, Michael Moorcock, Roger Zelazny, John Brunner, J. G. Ballard, Philip Josรฉ Farmer and Harlan Ellison examined the impact of drug culture, technology, and the sexual revolution while avoiding the utopian tendencies of earlier science fiction. Comics exploring cyberpunk themes began appearing as early as Judge Dredd, first published in 1977. Released in 1984, William Gibson's influential debut novel Neuromancer helped solidify cyberpunk as a genre, drawing influence from punk subculture and early hacker culture. Other influential cyberpunk writers included Bruce Sterling and Rudy Rucker. The Japanese cyberpunk subgenre began in 1982 with the debut of Katsuhiro Otomo's manga series Akira, with its 1988 anime film adaptation (also directed by Otomo) later popularizing the subgenre.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cyberpunk,[COLOR_REVERT] odmiana fantastyki naukowej, skupiaj¹ca siê na negatywnych konsekwencjach funkcjonowania ludzi w otoczeniu zaawansowanej technologii komputerowej i informacyjnej. Pojêcie cyberpunku mo¿na rozwa¿aæ równie¿ pod k¹tem postawy ¿yciowej, filozofii lub ideologii, gdzie porównania cz³owieka z maszyn¹ (cybernetyka) prowokuj¹ pytania na temat duszy, Boga oraz œwiadomoœci.
TEXT.Italian: Cyberpunk รจ un sottogenere della fantascienza in un'ambientazione futuristica distopica che tende a concentrarsi su una "combinazione di bassa vita e alta tecnologia", con risultati tecnologici e scientifici futuristici, come l'intelligenza artificiale e la cibernetica, giustapposti al collasso o al decadimento della societร . Gran parte del cyberpunk รจ radicato nel movimento di fantascienza New Wave degli anni '60 e '70, quando scrittori come Philip K. Dick, Michael Moorcock, Roger Zelazny, John Brunner, J.G. Ballard, Philip Josรฉ Farmer e Harlan Ellison esaminarono l'impatto della cultura della droga , la tecnologia e la rivoluzione sessuale evitando le tendenze utopistiche della precedente fantascienza. I fumetti che esplorano i temi del cyberpunk iniziarono ad apparire giร con Judge Dredd, pubblicato per la prima volta nel 1977. Pubblicato nel 1984, l'influente romanzo d'esordio di William Gibson, Neuromante, aiutรฒ a consolidare il cyberpunk come genere, traendo influenza dalla sottocultura punk e dalla prima cultura hacker. Altri influenti scrittori cyberpunk includevano Bruce Sterling e Rudy Rucker. Il sottogenere cyberpunk giapponese รจ iniziato nel 1982 con il debutto della serie manga Akira di Katsuhiro Otomo, con il suo adattamento cinematografico anime del 1988 (anch'esso diretto da Otomo) che in seguito ha reso popolare il sottogenere.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dark matter[COLOR_REVERT] is a form of matter thought to account for approximately 85% of the matter in the universe and about a quarter of its total energy density. Its presence is implied in a variety of astrophysical observations, and for this reason most experts think that dark matter is abundant in the universe and that it has had a strong influence on its structure and evolution. Dark matter is called dark because it does not appear to interact with observable electromagnetic radiation, such as light, and so it is undetectable by existing astronomical instruments.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]materia oscura[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una forma di materia che si ritiene rappresenti circa l'85% della materia nell'universo e circa un quarto della sua densitร energetica totale. La sua presenza รจ considerata implicita in seguito a numerose osservazioni astrofisiche, e per questo motivo la maggior parte degli esperti ritiene che la materia oscura sia abbondante nell'universo e che abbia avuto una forte influenza sulla sua struttura ed evoluzione. ร chiamata "oscura" perchรฉ non sembra interagire con le radiazioni elettromagnetiche osservabili, come la luce, e quindi non รจ rilevabile dagli strumenti astronomici esistenti.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ciemna materia[COLOR_REVERT], to hipotetyczna materia nieemituj¹ca i nieodbijaj¹ca promieniowania elektromagnetycznego. Jej istnienie zdradzaj¹ jedynie wywierane przez ni¹ efekty grawitacyjne. Wed³ug danych zebranych na podstawie obserwacji du¿ych struktur kosmicznych, interpretowanych w kategoriach równañ Friedmana i metryki Friedmana-Lemaรฎtre'a-Robertsona-Walkera, ciemna materia to ok. 27% bilansu masy-energii Wszechœwiata, obok materii zwyk³ej (widzialnej) i dominuj¹cej ciemnej energii. Postulat istnienia ciemnej materii jest obecnie dominuj¹cym wyt³umaczeniem obserwowanych anomalii w rotacji galaktyk oraz ruchu galaktyk w gromadach, ale nadal materia ta nie zosta³a odkryta, a jej natura pozostaje nieznana.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dates[COLOR_REVERT] are the edible sweet fruits of the palm tree, a flowering plant species in the palm family that can typically reach about 21โ23 m (69โ75 ft) in height. Dates are oval-cylindrical, 3 to 7 cm (1.2 to 2.8 in) long, and about 2.5 cm (0.98 in) in diameter. Dates have been a staple food of the Middle East and the Indus Valley for thousands of years. There is archaeological evidence of date cultivation in Arabia from the 6th millennium BCE.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]dattes[COLOR_REVERT] sont les fruits sucrรฉs comestibles du palmier, une espรจce de plante ร fleurs de la famille des palmiers qui peut gรฉnรฉralement atteindre environ 21-23 m (69-75 pieds) de hauteur. Les dattes sont ovales-cylindriques, de 3 ร 7 cm (1,2 ร 2,8 pouces) de long, et d'environ 2,5 cm (0,98 pouce) de diamรจtre. Les dattes sont un aliment de base du Moyen-Orient et de la vallรฉe de l'Indus depuis des milliers d'annรฉes. Il existe des preuves archรฉologiques de la culture des dattes en Arabie depuis le 6e millรฉnaire avant notre รจre.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Datteln[COLOR_REVERT] werden die sรผรen, essbaren Frรผchte der echten Dattelpalme (Phoenix dactylifera) genannt. Es handelt sich um eine ca. 15 bis 20 m hohe Wรผstenpflanze, deren Blรคtter die markanten 3 bis 5 m langen Wedel bilden, die im unteren Bereich mit Stacheln versehen sind.[NEWLINE]Fossilienfunde belegen, dass die Dattelpalme schon seit 50 Millionen Jahren existiert. Sie wurde bereits im alten Mesopotamien um etwa 5500 v.Chr. kultiviert und gelangte รผber das alte รgypten ins Rรถmische Reich, wo die Dattelpalme zu einer beliebten Gartenpflanze wurde.[NEWLINE]Die Dattelpalme ist Rekordhalter in altersbedingter Keimfรคhigkeit. Im Jahr 1963 wurden bei Ausgrabungen 2000 Jahre alte Dattelkerne gefunden, aus denen sich tatsรคchlich noch Pflanzen ziehen lieรen.
TEXT.Italian: I [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]datteri[COLOR_REVERT] sono i frutti dolci commestibili della palma da dattero, una pianta della famiglia delle palme che puรฒ raggiungere in genere un'altezza di circa 21-23 m in altezza. I datteri hanno una forma ovale-cilindrica, sono lunghi 3-7 cm e hanno un diametro di circa 2,5 cm. I datteri sono un alimento base del Medio Oriente e della valle dell'Indo da migliaia di anni. Esistono prove archeologiche della coltivazione di datteri in Arabia dal VI millennio a.C.

Maj¹ konsystencjê miêsisto-mazist¹, s¹ s³odkie i zawieraj¹ w œrodku jedn¹ pestkê. Daktyle s¹ owocami jadalnymi, zarówno po przetworzeniu, jak i na surowo. W Polsce dostêpne s¹ g³ównie daktyle suszone. U¿ywane s¹ jako przek¹ska lub dodatek do s³odyczy, kisieli mlecznych (budyniów) i ciast.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Deep space lifeforms[COLOR_REVERT] are space creatures that are highly adaptable to variations in gravity and atmospheric conditions. They are ideal for creating ecosystems in space habitats.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]formes de vie de l'espace profond[COLOR_REVERT] sont des crรฉatures spatiales hautement adaptables aux variations de gravitรฉ et de conditions atmosphรฉriques. Elles sont idรฉales pour crรฉer des รฉcosystรจmes dans les habitats spatiaux.
TEXT.Italian: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]forme di vita spaziali[COLOR_REVERT] sono creature extraterrestri che hanno la caratteristica di potersi adattare facilmente alle variazioni di gravitร e di condizioni atmosferiche. Sono ideali per la creazione di ecosistemi negli habitat spaziali.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Deep space nanobots[COLOR_REVERT] are tiny robots that can easily travel and work in space. They are useful in construction and manufacturing.
TEXT.French: Les [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]nanobots de l'espace profond[COLOR_REVERT] sont de minuscules robots qui peuvent facilement voyager et travailler dans l'espace. Ils sont utiles dans la construction et la fabrication.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]deer[COLOR_REVERT] is a ruminant mammal, with long, powerful legs, a diminutive tail and long ears. Male deers also grow and shed new antlers each year. Deers have played a role in mythology, religion, and literature throughout history, as well as in heraldry. Deer hunting has been a popular activity since at least the Middle Ages and remains a resource for many families today. They have been hunted for their meat, their skin (that makes for soft, strong buckskin), and their antlers (used for handles for knives).
TEXT.German: Wรคhrend der umgangssprachliche Begriff Hirsch im Deutschen eher den Rothirsch als spezielles Exemplar bezeichnet, sind in der Familie der [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hirsche[COLOR_REVERT] eine Vielzahl an Geweihtrรคgern zusammengefasst: Rehe, Rotwild, Damwild, Rentiere und Elche sind die in Europa bekanntesten. In der Frรผhzeit wurden Hirsche vom Menschen hauptsรคchlich wegen ihres Fleisches und ihres Fells gejagt. Auรerdem stellten die stolzen Geweihe der Mรคnnchen bis in die Neuzeit begehrte Trophรคen dar.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cervo[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un mammifero ruminante, con zampe lunghe e potenti, una coda minuta e orecchie lunghe. Gli esemplari maschi, inoltre, rinnovano i propri palchi di corna ogni anno. I cervi hanno avuto un ruolo lungo la storia umana nella mitologia, nella religione, nella letteratura e nell'araldica. La caccia al cervo รจ stata un'attivitร popolare almeno dal Medioevo e rimane oggi una risorsa per molte famiglie. I cervi sono stati cacciati per la loro carne, la loro pelle (che rende la pelle di daino morbida e resistente) e le loro corna (usate per i manici dei coltelli).
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Jeleñ[COLOR_REVERT], to rodzaj ssaków z podrodziny jeleni (Cervinae) w obrêbie rodziny jeleniowatych (Cervidae). Jelenie s¹ zabijane, jako gruba zwierzyna, dla miêsa, skór i poro¿a. S¹ hodowane na farmach (jako zwierzêta gospodarskie) oraz w parkach i ogrodach zoologicznych.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]diamond nanothread[COLOR_REVERT], also called carbon nanothread, is a one-dimensional carbon crystalline nanomaterial. Nanothreads consist of a stiff, strong carbon core surrounded by hydrogen atoms, and are more than 20,000 times thinner than a human hair. They are synthesized by compressing liquid benzene to extreme pressure of 20 GPa (around 200,000 times the air pressure at the surface of the Earth), and then slowly relieving that pressure. Their extremely high specific strength is used for space elevators and in other applications related to transportation, aerospace, and sports equipment.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]nanofilamento in diamante[COLOR_REVERT], chiamato anche nanofilamento di carbonio, รจ un nanomateriale di carbonio cristallino unidimensionale. I nanofilamenti sono costituiti da un nucleo di carbonio rigido circondato da atomi di idrogeno e sono oltre 20.000 volte piรน sottili di un capello umano. Sono sintetizzati comprimendo il benzene liquido a una pressione estrema di 20 GPa (circa 200.000 volte la pressione dell'aria sulla Terra) e quindi alleviando lentamente quella pressione. La loro resistenza estremamente elevata viene utilizzata per gli elevatori spaziali e in altre applicazioni relative al trasporto, al settore aerospaziale e alle attrezzature sportive.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nanoniæ wêglowa[COLOR_REVERT], to jednowymiarowy krystaliczny nanomateria³ wêglowy ze wi¹zaniami sp3. Tetraedryczne wi¹zania sp3 jego wêgla s¹ podobne do diamentów. Nanonitki maj¹ zaledwie kilka atomów œrednicy i s¹ ponad 300 000 razy cieñsze od ludzkiego w³osa.
TEXT.Russian: ะะปะผะฐะทะฝะฐั ะฝะธัั - ััะพ ัะตัััะตั ะณัะฐะฝะฝัะน ัะณะปะตัะพะดะฝัะน ะบัะธััะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะธะน ะฝะฐะฝะพะผะฐัะตัะธะฐะป (ะฟะปะพัะฝะพัะฟะฐะบะพะฒะฐะฝะฝะฐั ัะณะปะตัะพะดะฝะฐั ััััะบัััะฐ sp3). ะะณะพ ััััะบัััะฐ ะฟะพั ะพะถะฐ ะฝะฐ ััััะบัััั ะฐะปะผะฐะทะฐ. ะะะข ะธะผะตัั ะฒัะตะณะพ ะฝะตัะบะพะปัะบะพ ะฐัะพะผะพะฒ ะฒ ะฟะพะฟะตัะตัะฝะธะบะต, ะฑะพะปะตะต ัะตะผ ะฒ 20000 ัะฐะท ัะพะฝััะต ัะตะปะพะฒะตัะตัะบะพะณะพ ะฒะพะปะพัะฐ. ะะฝะธ ัะธะฝัะตะทะธัััััั ะฟััะตะผ ัะถะฐัะธั ะถะธะดะบะพะณะพ ะฑะตะฝะทะพะปะฐ ะดะพ ัะบัััะตะผะฐะปัะฝะพะณะพ ะดะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธั 20 ะะะฐ (ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ ะฒ 200000 ัะฐะท ะฑะพะปััะต ะดะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธั ะฒะพะทะดัั ะฐ ั ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพััะธ ะะตะผะปะธ), ะฐ ะทะฐัะตะผ ะผะตะดะปะตะฝะฝะพะณะพ ัะฑัะพัะฐ ััะพะณะพ ะดะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธั. ะั ััะตะทะฒััะฐะนะฝะพ ะฒััะพะบะฐั ัะดะตะปัะฝะฐั ะฟัะพัะฝะพััั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะดะปั ะบะพัะผะธัะตัะบะธั ะปะธััะพะฒ ะธ ะดััะณะธั ะฟัะธะผะตะฝะตะฝะธะน, ัะฒัะทะฐะฝะฝัั ั ััะฐะฝัะฟะพััะฝัะผ, ะฐััะพะบะพัะผะธัะตัะบะธะผ ะธ ัะฟะพััะธะฒะฝัะผ ะพะฑะพััะดะพะฒะฐะฝะธะตะผ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Diamond[COLOR_REVERT] is a solid form of carbon with its atoms arranged in a crystal structure. Because the arrangement of atoms is extremely rigid, few types of impurity can contaminate it: small numbers of defects or impurities may color diamond blue (boron), yellow (nitrogen), brown (defects), green (radiation exposure), purple, pink, orange or red. Diamonds have the highest hardness and thermal conductivity of any natural material, properties that are utilized in major industrial applications such as cutting and polishing tools. Most natural diamonds have ages between 1 and 3.5 billion years.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]diamante[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una forma solida di carbonio, i cui atomi sono disposti in una struttura cristallina. Poichรฉ la disposizione degli atomi รจ estremamente rigida, pochi tipi di impuritร possono contaminarla: piccoli difetti o impuritร possono dare al diamante un colore blu (boro), giallo (azoto), marrone (difetti), verde (esposizione alle radiazioni), viola, rosa, arancione o rosso. I diamanti hanno il piรน alto grado di durezza e conducibilitร termica di qualsiasi altro materiale naturale e per questo sono usati come utensili da taglio e lucidatura. La maggior parte dei diamanti naturali ha un'etร compresa tra 1 e 3,5 miliardi di anni.

TEXT.English: Dieselpunk is based on the aesthetics of the period between the end of the World War I and the beginning of World War II. This sub-genre is sometimes named Decopunk, referring to the Art Deco art style (including its Streamline Moderne variant). The genre combines the artistic and genre influences of the period (including pulp magazines, serial films, film noir, art deco, and wartime pin-ups) with postmodern technology and sensibilities.
TEXT.French: Le Dieselpunk est basรฉ sur l'esthรฉtique de la pรฉriode entre la fin de la Premiรจre Guerre mondiale et le dรฉbut de la Seconde Guerre mondiale. Ce sous-genre est parfois nommรฉ Decopunk, en rรฉfรฉrence au style artistique Art Dรฉco (y compris sa variante Streamline Moderne). Le genre combine les influences artistiques et de genre de l'รฉpoque (y compris les magazines pulp, les films ร รฉpisodes, le film noir, l'art dรฉco et les pin-ups de guerre) avec la technologie et les sensibilitรฉs postmodernes.
TEXT.Italian: Dieselpunk รจ basata sull'estetica nel periodo tra la fine della prima guerra mondiale e l'inizio della seconda. Questo sottogenere viene spesso chiamato Decopunk, in riferimento alla corrente artistica Art Deco (inclusa la sua variante moderna). Il genere combina le influenze artistiche di questo periodo (incluse riviste di tendenza, serie tv, film noir, art deco e pin-up) con la tecnologia e la sensibilitร post-moderna.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dieselpunk,[COLOR_REVERT] nurt stylistyczny w fantastyce naukowej (oraz szerzej โ w kulturze), pochodna cyberpunku z akcj¹ osadzon¹ najczêœciej w latach 20., 30. i 40. Panuje futurystyczny entuzjazm, w którym wa¿n¹ czêœci¹ t³a s¹ elementy nowoczesnoœci takie jak kina, teatry, samochody, tramwaje, telegramy, telefony, radia czy samoloty.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]digitized membrane[COLOR_REVERT] is a synthetically created membrane, usually intended to work with interactive glass. It is used to form a tactile surface for users that, when touched, generates electrical impulses sent to a computer, that proceeds to analyze and interpret the impulses to provide the required action.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]membrane numรฉrisรฉe[COLOR_REVERT] est une membrane crรฉรฉe synthรฉtiquement, gรฉnรฉralement destinรฉe ร fonctionner avec du verre interactif. Elle est utilisรฉe pour former une surface tactile pour les utilisateurs qui, lorsqu'elle est touchรฉe, gรฉnรจre des impulsions รฉlectriques envoyรฉes ร un ordinateur, qui procรจde ร l'analyse et ร l'interprรฉtation des impulsions pour fournir l'action requise.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]membrana digitalizzata[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una membrana creata sinteticamente, di solito destinata a funzionare con vetri interattivi. Viene utilizzata per formare una superficie tattile per gli utenti che, quando viene toccata, genera impulsi elettrici inviati a un computer, che procede all'analisi e all'interpretazione degli impulsi per fornire l'azione richiesta.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]dishwasher[COLOR_REVERT] is a machine for cleaning dishware and cutlery automatically. Unlike manual dish-washing, which relies largely on physical scrubbing to remove soiling, the mechanical dishwasher cleans by spraying hot water, typically between 45 and 75 ยฐC (110 and 170 ยฐF), at the dishes, with lower temperatures used for delicate items. In addition to domestic units, industrial dishwashers are available for use in commercial establishments, such as hotels and restaurants, where many dishes must be cleaned.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]lavastoviglie[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una macchina per la pulizia automatica di stoviglie e posate. A differenza del lavaggio manuale delle stoviglie, che si basa in gran parte sul lavaggio fisico per rimuovere lo sporco, la lavastoviglie meccanica pulisce spruzzando acqua calda (in genere tra 45 e 75 ยฐC) ai piatti, con temperature piรน basse utilizzate per oggetti delicati. Oltre alle unitร domestiche, lavastoviglie industriali sono disponibili per l'uso in strutture commerciali, come hotel e ristoranti, dove รจ necessario pulire molti piatti.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]floppy disk[COLOR_REVERT], also known as diskette, is a type of disk storage composed of a disk of thin and flexible magnetic storage medium, sealed in a rectangular plastic enclosure lined with fabric that removes dust particles. Floppy disks are read and written by a floppy disk drive (FDD). The prevalence of floppy disks in late 20th century culture was such that many electronic and software programs still use the floppy disks as save icons. By the end of 20th century, they have been superseded by data storage methods with much greater capacity and transfer speeds, such as USB flash drives, flash storage cards, optical discs and eventually cloud storage.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]floppy disk[COLOR_REVERT], noto anche come dischetto, รจ un tipo di archiviazione su disco composta da un disco di supporto magnetico sottile e flessibile, sigillato in un involucro di plastica rettangolare rivestito in tessuto che rimuove le particelle di polvere. I floppy disk vengono letti e scritti da un floppy disk drive (FDD). La prevalenza dei floppy disk nella cultura della fine del XX secolo รจ stata tale che molti programmi elettronici e software usano ancora i floppy disk come icone di salvataggio. Giร alla fine del XX secolo, sono stati sostituiti da metodi di archiviazione dei dati con capacitร e velocitร di trasferimento molto maggiori, come unitร flash USB, schede di memoria flash, dischi ottici e infine il cloud storage.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dyskietka[COLOR_REVERT], dysk wymienny, przenoœny noœnik magnetyczny o niewielkiej pojemnoœci, umo¿liwiaj¹cy zarówno odczyt, jak i zapis danych. Noœnikiem danych jest wiruj¹cy kr¹¿ek z wytrzyma³ego tworzywa sztucznego (najczêœciej mylaru โ politereftalanu etylenu w postaci cienkiej folii) pokryty warstw¹ magnetyczn¹. W drugiej dekadzie XXI wieku dyskietki zosta³y niemal wyparte przez pamiêci USB i pamiêci flash, które cechuj¹ jeszcze mniejsze rozmiary i znacznie wiêksza pojemnoœæ.
TEXT.Russian: รรจรกรชรจรฉ รครจรฑรช, รจรซรจ รครจรฑรชรฅรฒร - รฝรฒรฎ รญรฎรฑรจรฒรฅรซรผ รจรญรดรฎรฐรฌร รถรจรจ, รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรนรจรฉ รจรง รฒรฎรญรชรฎรฃรฎ รจ รฃรจรกรชรฎรฃรฎ รฌร รฃรญรจรฒรญรฎรฃรฎ รญรฎรฑรจรฒรฅรซรฟ, รงร รฏรฅรทร รฒร รญรญรฎรฃรฎ รข รฏรฐรฟรฌรฎรณรฃรฎรซรผรญรฎรฌ รฏรซร รฑรฒรจรชรฎรขรฎรฌ รญรฎรฑรจรฒรฅรซรฅ รฑ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรทร รฒรฎรฉ รฏรฎรครชรซร รครชรฎรฉ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐร รฟ รงร รครฅรฐรฆรจรขร รฅรฒ รทร รฑรฒรจรถรป รฏรปรซรจ. รรญรจ รทรจรฒร รพรฒรฑรฟ รจ รฏรจรธรณรฒรฑรฟ รทรฅรฐรฅรง รดรซรฎรฏรฏรจ-รฏรฐรจรขรฎรค (รรร). รรจรฑรชรฅรฒรป, รจรงรญร รทร รซรผรญรฎ รชร รช 8-รครพรฉรฌรฎรขรปรฅ (200 รฌรฌ) รฌรณรซรผรฒรจรฌรฅรครจร รจ รฏรฎรงรฆรฅ รข 5.25-รครพรฉรฌรฎรขรปรฉ (133 รฌรฌ) รจ 3,5 รครพรฉรฌร (90 รฌรฌ) รร รงรฌรฅรฐรป, รกรปรซรจ รฏรฎรขรฑรฅรฌรฅรฑรฒรญรฎรฉ รดรฎรฐรฌรฎรฉ รตรฐร รญรฅรญรจรฟ รคร รญรญรปรต รจ รฎรกรฌรฅรญร รฑ รฑรฅรฐรฅรครจรญรป 1970-รต รจ รข รฏรฅรฐรขรฎรฌ รครฅรฑรฟรฒรจรซรฅรฒรจรจ 21-รฃรฎ รขรฅรชร .
รรฎรกร รชรจ

TEXT.English: The [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]domestic dog[COLOR_REVERT] is a member of the canines genus, and is the most widely abundant terrestrial carnivore. Dogs vary widely in shape, size and colors. The dog was the first animal to be domesticated, and has been selectively bred over millennia for their behaviors, sensory capabilities, and physical attributes. Their long association with humans has led dogs to be uniquely attuned to human behavior, and to perform many roles for humans, such as hunting, herding, pulling loads, protection, assisting police and military, companionship and, more recently, aiding disabled people and therapeutic roles. This influence on human society has given them the sobriquet of "man's best friend".
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cane domestico[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un membro del genere canino ed รจ il carnivoro terrestre piรน diffuso. I cani variano ampiamente in forma, dimensioni e colori. Il cane รจ stato il primo animale ad essere addomesticato dall'uomo ed รจ stato allevato in modo selettivo nel corso dei millenni per il comportamento, le capacitร sensoriali e gli attributi fisici. La loro lunga associazione con gli esseri umani ha portato i cani ad avere una sintonia unica con il comportamento umano e ad assumere molti ruoli di ausilio nella caccia, nella pastorizia, nel trasporto di oggetti, nella protezione e assistenza alla polizia e ai militari, come animale da compagnia e, piรน recentemente, nell'aiuto alle persone disabili e in ruoli terapeutici. Questa influenza sulla societร umana รจ valsa loro il soprannome di "migliore amico dell'uomo".
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pies domowy[COLOR_REVERT], udomowiony gatunek (lub podgatunek) ssaka drapie¿nego z rodziny psowatych (Canidae), traktowany przez niektóre ujêcia systematyczne za podgatunek wilka, a przez inne za odrêbny gatunek. Od czasu jego udomowienia powsta³o wiele ras, znacznie ró¿ni¹cych siê morfologi¹ i cechami u¿ytkowymi. Rasy pierwotne powstawa³y g³ównie w wyniku presji œrodowiskowej. Rasy wspó³czesne uzyskano w wyniku doboru sztucznego. Pocz¹tki udomowienia psa szacuje siê na ok. 12โ17 tys. lat temu, by³ on u¿ywany przez myœliwych z Syberii, pojawia siê te¿ w Palestynie oraz w Iraku. Do najstarszych oœrodków udomowienia psa zaliczyæ mo¿na obszary Europy, gdzie znaleziono najstarsze szcz¹tki tego zwierzêcia, czyli rejony Danii, Niemiec i Anglii, a tak¿e odkrycia z okolic Izraela, Iranu, Japonii, czy Turcji. Odkryto tak¿e, ¿e na terenach Idaho, w Stanach Zjednoczonych 10 000 lat temu tak¿e ¿y³ przedstawiciel psa domowego.
TEXT.Russian: รรฎรฌร รธรญรฟรฟ รฑรฎรกร รชร รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฎรครขรจรครฎรฌ รฎรกรปรชรญรฎรขรฅรญรญรฎรฃรฎ รขรฎรซรชร . รรฅรฐรฌรจรญ "รครฎรฌร รธรญรฟรฟ รฑรฎรกร รชร " รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รครซรฟ รฎรกรฎรงรญร รทรฅรญรจรฟ รครฎรฌร รธรญรจรต รจ รครจรชรจรต รฏรฎรฐรฎรค. รรฎรกร รชร รกรปรซร รฏรฅรฐรขรปรฌ รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรปรฌ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รกรปรซรฎ รฎรครฎรฌร รธรญรฅรญรฎ, รจ รกรปรซรฎ รญร รจรกรฎรซรฅรฅ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รซร รฑรผ รข รฎรตรฎรฒรฅ รจ รชร รช รครฎรฌร รธรญรฅรฅ รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรฎรฅ รข รถรฅรซรฎรฌ รข รจรฑรฒรฎรฐรจรจ รทรฅรซรฎรขรฅรทรฅรฑรฒรขร .

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]donkey[COLOR_REVERT], or ass, is a domesticated member of the horse family, and adapted to marginal desert lands. The donkey has been used as a working animal for at least 5,000 years, principally as drought or pack animals. Working donkeys are often associated with those living at or below subsistence levels, but continue to fill important roles in many places today. Small numbers of donkeys are kept for breeding or as pets in developed countries.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]รขne[COLOR_REVERT], ou baudet, est un membre domestiquรฉ de la famille des รฉquidรฉs, adaptรฉ aux terres dรฉsertiques marginales. L'รขne est utilisรฉ comme animal de travail depuis au moins 5 000 ans, principalement comme animal de trait ou de bรขt. Les รขnes de travail sont souvent associรฉs ร ceux vivant au niveau de subsistance ou en dessous, mais continuent ร remplir des rรดles importants dans de nombreux endroits aujourd'hui. Un petit nombre d'รขnes sont gardรฉs pour l'รฉlevage ou comme animaux de compagnie dans les pays dรฉveloppรฉs.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Esel[COLOR_REVERT] werden bereits seit mindestens 5000 Jahren als Arbeitstiere genutzt. Zunรคchst wurden Esel auch als Reit- und Zugtiere verwendet. Spรคter wurden sie in der Regel von den schnelleren und krรคftigeren Pferden verdrรคngt. Einzig in gebirgigen Regionen wurden sie weiter genutzt, weil sie im Gegensatz zu Pferden schwindelfrei sind. Wegen ihrer Zรคhigkeit setzte man sie auรerdem weiter als Packtiere z.B. in Mรผhlen ein, wo sie Mehlsรคcke transportieren mussten. Von Eseln wird hรคufig auch die Milch und das Leder, seltener auch das zรคhe Fleisch (meist zur Herstellung von Wurst) verwendet. In der Traditionellen Chinesischen Medizin stellen Gelees oder Pulver aus zermahlener Eselshaut ein potentes Heilmittel dar. Im Mittelalter wurde Eselshaut auรerdem bevorzugt fรผr die Herstellung von Pergament genutzt.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]asino[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un membro addomesticato della famiglia dei cavalli, adattatosi a vivere in terre semi-desertiche. L'asino รจ stato usato come animale da lavoro per almeno 5.000 anni, principalmente come animale da tiro o da soma. L'utilizzo degli asini in ambito lavorativo รจ spesso associato a coloro che vivono a livelli di sussistenza o inferiori, anche se gli asini continuano a ricoprire ruoli importanti ancora oggi. Una piccola frazione viene usata per la riproduzione o come animali domestici nei paesi sviluppati.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Osio³ zwyczajny[COLOR_REVERT], udomowiony gatunek ssaka z rodziny koniowatych (Equidae). Charakteryzuje siê du¿¹ g³ow¹, d³ugimi uszami, cienkim ogonem z kit¹ na koñcu, ma³ymi wymaganiami pokarmowymi i odpornoœci¹ na brak wody. Osio³ zosta³ udomowiony przez cz³owieka wczeœniej ni¿ koñ. Pierwsze informacje dotycz¹ce udomowienia pochodz¹ z Doliny Nilu z ok. 4000 p.n.e., czyli 6 tys. lat temu. Na tych terenach wystêpowa³y obydwa ¿yj¹ce do dzisiaj podgatunki os³a afrykañskiego. Przypuszcza siê, ¿e z ich krzy¿ówek powsta³ osio³ domowy (Equus africanus f.asinus).

TEXT.German: Getrockneter Fisch
TEXT.Russian: รรฟรซรฅรญร รฟ รฐรปรกร

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dried fish[COLOR_REVERT] is fish that has been processed in order to remove all traces of water from it, inhibiting the growth of microorganisms and, therefore, allowing a better preservation. Water is usually removed by evaporation (air drying, sun drying, smoking or wind drying) but, in the case of freeze-drying, food is first frozen and then the water is removed by sublimation. Bacteria, yeasts and molds need the water in the food to grow, and drying effectively prevents them from surviving.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]pesce essiccato[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un pesce a cui รจ stata tolta ogni traccia di acqua, inibendo la crescita di microrganismi e, quindi, consentendone una migliore conservazione. L'acqua viene solitamente rimossa per evaporazione (essiccazione all'aria, al sole o tramite fumo o vento) ma, in caso di liofilizzazione, il cibo viene prima congelato e quindi l'acqua rimossa per sublimazione. I batteri, i lieviti e le muffe sfruttano l'acqua all'interno del pesce per svilupparsi e l'essiccazione impedisce loro di sopravvivere.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Suszone ryby[COLOR_REVERT], to ryby, z których w wyniku suszenia usuniêta zosta³a woda, co spowalnia rozwój drobnoustrojów i dziêki temu zapewnia d³u¿sz¹ przydatnoœæ do spo¿ycia.

TEXT.German: Getrocknete Frucht
TEXT.Russian: รรณรตรฎรดรฐรณรชรฒรป

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dried fruit[COLOR_REVERT] is fruit from which the majority of the original water content has been removed either naturally or through a drying process. Dried fruits retain most of the nutritional value of fresh fruits. Dried fruits have a long tradition, dating back to the 4th millennium BC in Mesopotamia, and are prized because of their sweet taste, nutritive value, and long shelf life. Nearly half of the dried fruits sold are raisins, followed by dates, prunes, figs, apricots, peaches, apples and pears.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]frutta secca[COLOR_REVERT] รจ la frutta dalla quale รจ stata rimossa la maggior parte dell'acqua in essa contenuta, naturalmente o attraverso un processo di essiccazione. La frutta secca conserva la maggior parte del valore nutrizionale della frutta fresca. La frutta secca ha una lunga tradizione, che risale al IV millennio a.C. in Mesopotamia, ed รจ apprezzata per il gusto dolce, il valore nutritivo e la lunga durata. Quasi la metร dei frutti secchi venduti รจ uvetta, seguita da datteri, prugne, fichi, albicocche, pesche, mele e pere.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Suszone owoce[COLOR_REVERT], to owoce, z których usuniêta zosta³a wiêkszoœæ wody, zarówno w sposób naturalny, jak i w procesie suszenia. Suszone owoce zachowuj¹ wiêkszoœæ sk³adników od¿ywczych. Maj¹ one d³ug¹ historiê, siêgaj¹c¹ a¿ do 4 wieku przed nasz¹ er¹ do rejonu mezopotamii. Owoce zakonserwowane w ten sposób s¹ cenione ze wzglêdu na swoj¹ s³odycz, sk³adniki od¿ywcze, oraz d³ug¹ przydatnoœæ do spo¿ycia. Ponad 50% sprzedawanych obecnie suszonych owoców stanowi¹ rodzynki.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dried meat[COLOR_REVERT] is meat that has been processed in order to remove all traces of water from it, inhibiting the growth of microorganisms and, therefore, allowing a better preservation. Water is usually removed by evaporation (air drying, sun drying, smoking or wind drying) but, in the case of freeze-drying, food is first frozen and then the water is removed by sublimation. Bacteria, yeasts and molds need the water in the food to grow, and drying effectively prevents them from surviving.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]carne essiccata[COLOR_REVERT] รจ carne a cui รจ stata tolta ogni traccia di acqua, inibendo la crescita di microrganismi e, quindi, consentendone una migliore conservazione. L'acqua viene solitamente rimossa per evaporazione (essiccazione all'aria, al sole o tramite fumo o vento) ma, in caso di liofilizzazione, il cibo viene prima congelato e quindi l'acqua rimossa per sublimazione. I batteri, i lieviti e le muffe sfruttano l'acqua all'interno del pesce per svilupparsi e l'essiccazione impedisce loro di sopravvivere.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Suszone miêso[COLOR_REVERT], to miêso, z którego w wyniku suszenia usuniêta zosta³a woda, co spowalnia rozwój drobnoustrojów i dziêki temu zapewnia d³u¿sz¹ przydatnoœæ do spo¿ycia.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]drug[COLOR_REVERT] is any substance which, when absorbed or taken by a living organism, causes a temporary psycho-physiological change in the subject. In pharmacology, drugs (also called "medicine") is a chemical used to treat, cure, prevent or diagnose a disease or to promote well-being. Traditionally, drugs were obtained through extraction from medicinal plants, but recently they can be produced also from organic synthesis.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]drogue[COLOR_REVERT] est toute substance qui, lorsqu'elle est absorbรฉe ou prise par un organisme vivant, provoque un changement psycho-physiologique temporaire chez le sujet. En pharmacologie, les drogues (รฉgalement appelรฉes "mรฉdicaments") sont des substances chimiques utilisรฉes pour traiter, guรฉrir, prรฉvenir ou diagnostiquer une maladie ou pour promouvoir le bien-รชtre. Traditionnellement, les drogues รฉtaient obtenues par extraction ร partir de plantes mรฉdicinales, mais rรฉcemment, elles peuvent รฉgalement รชtre produites par synthรจse organique.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]droga[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una qualsiasi sostanza che, quando viene assorbita o assunta da un organismo vivente, provoca un temporaneo cambiamento psico-fisiologico nel soggetto. In farmacologia la droga, chiamata anche "farmaco", รจ una sostanza chimica utilizzata per trattare, curare, prevenire o diagnosticare una malattia o per promuovere il benessere. Tradizionalmente le droghe venivano ottenute attraverso l'estrazione dalle piante medicinali, ma piรน recentemente anche dalla sintesi organica.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Substancja psychoaktywna[COLOR_REVERT], to substancja chemiczna (zwi¹zek chemiczny) lub mieszanina substancji maj¹ca bezpoœredni lub nastêpczy wp³yw na funkcjonowanie mózgu, czego efektem s¹ zmiany postrzegania, nastroju, œwiadomoœci, procesów poznawczych (np. zapamiêtywania) i zachowania. Substancje psychoaktywne lub u¿ywki je zawieraj¹ce mog¹ byæ u¿ywane w celach niemedycznych zwi¹zanych z wywo³aniem okreœlonych zmian œwiadomoœci (np. kawa, napoje alkoholowe, heroina, kokaina, œrodki pochodzenia konopnego), w niektórych kultach religijnych (np. zawieraj¹cy meskalinê kaktus pejotl, grzyby psylocybinowe), w medycynie (np. opioidy i kannabinoidy do ³agodzenia bólu, stymulanty do ³agodzenia objawów narkolepsji i ADHD, leki przeciwdepresyjne i leki przeciwpsychotyczne w neurologii i psychiatrii).

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]dye[COLOR_REVERT] is a colored substance that chemically bonds to the substrate to which it is being applied. The dye is generally applied in an aqueous solution, and may require a mordant to improve the fastness of the dye on the fiber. Despite being usually soluble in water, some dyes can be rendered insoluble with the addition of salt to produce a lake pigment. The majority of natural dyes are derived from plant sources (roots, berries, bark, leaves, wood, fungi and lichens), but most dyes are man-made from petrochemicals.
TEXT.German: Das Fรคrben von Stoffen und Fasern gehรถrt schon seit mehr als drei Jahrtausenden zu den vom Menschen praktizierten Handwerken. Durch das Eintauchen der Materialien in Farbe lieร sich das natรผrliche Weiร von Seide, Wolle und Baumwolle in verschiedene Farbnuancen einfรคrben. Natรผrliche Fรคrbemittel wurden aus unzรคhligen Quellen, einschlieรlich Muscheln, Tieren und Pflanzen, gewonnen, obwohl diese nicht immer dauerhaft und meist auch teuer waren. Moderne Fรคrbemittel basieren auf kรผnstlichen oder verarbeiteten Substanzen wie Kohle und anderen Chemikalien. Ein Land mit einer ausgedehnten Fรคrbeindustrie hat den Vorteil, dass die zur Herstellung von Fรคrbemitteln notwendigen Verfahren leicht fรผr andere Zwecke abgewandelt werden kรถnnen. Wรคhrend des 1. Weltkrieges zeigte es sich in Deutschland, dass Fabriken, in denen kรผnstliche Fรคrbemittel hergestellt wurden, kurzerhand so umstrukturiert werden konnten, dass sie stattdessen Sprengstoffe, giftiges Gas und anderes Kriegsmaterial produzierten.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]colorante[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una sostanza colorata che si lega chimicamente al substrato su cui viene applicata. Il colorante viene generalmente applicato in una soluzione acquosa e puรฒ richiedere un mordente per migliorare la soliditร del colorante sulla fibra. Nonostante sia solitamente solubile in acqua, alcuni coloranti possono essere resi insolubili con l'aggiunta di sale per produrre una lacca. La maggior parte dei coloranti naturali sono derivati da fonti vegetali (radici, bacche, corteccia, foglie, legno, funghi e licheni), ma la maggior parte dei coloranti in quanto tali รจ un derivato del petrolio.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Barwniki[COLOR_REVERT], to zwi¹zki chemiczne wybiórczo absorbuj¹ce promieniowanie elektromagnetyczne w zakresie widzialnym (o d³ugoœci fali od ok. 400 do 780 nm) warunkuj¹ce barwê organizmów roœlinnych i zwierzêcych albo nadaj¹ce barwê przedmiotom u¿ytkowym; s³u¿¹ do barwienia (barwienie w³ókien) m.in. w³ókien naturalnych i chemicznych, tworzyw sztucznych, skóry, papieru, ¿ywnoœci. TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]3,4-Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA)[COLOR_REVERT], commonly known as "ecstasy", is an illegal psychoactive drug, primarily used as a recreational drug. MDMA acts primarily by increasing the activity of serotonin, dopamine, and noradrenaline in parts of the brain. When taken by mouth, effects begin in 30 to 45 minutes and last 3 to 6 hours. The desired effects include altered sensations, increased energy, empathy, and pleasure. Adverse effects include addiction, memory problems, paranoia, difficulty sleeping, teeth grinding, blurred vision, sweating, and a rapid heartbeat. Deaths have been reported due to increased body temperature and dehydration.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]3,4-metilenediossimetanfetamina (MDMA)[COLOR_REVERT], comunemente nota come "ecstasy", รจ una droga psicoattiva illegale, utilizzata principalmente come droga ricreativa. L'MDMA agisce principalmente aumentando l'attivitร di serotonina, dopamina e noradrenalina in alcune parti del cervello. Se assunta per via orale, gli effetti iniziano dopo 30-45 minuti e durano da 3 a 6 ore. Gli effetti desiderati includono sensazioni alterate, maggiore energia, empatia e piacere. Gli effetti collaterali includono dipendenza, problemi di memoria, paranoia, difficoltร a dormire, digrignamento dei denti, visione offuscata, sudorazione e battito cardiaco accelerato. Sono stati segnalati decessi a causa dell'aumento della temperatura corporea e della disidratazione.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]3,4-Metylenodioksymetamfetamina (MDMA)[COLOR_REVERT], to pó³syntetyczna substancja psychoaktywna wykazuj¹ca dzia³anie empatogenne, euforyczne i psychodeliczne. G³ówne efekty dzia³ania MDMA to otwartoœæ, lekkie halucynacje, pobudzenie, euforia, empatia, intensyfikacja odbioru bodŸców zewnêtrznych, g³êbsze odczuwanie muzyki i ruchu, czasami zmiana poczucia czasu, czêsto subiektywne uczucie jednoœci z otaczaj¹cym œwiatem, a tak¿e wzmocnienie odczuæ zwi¹zanych z dotykiem, co sprawia, ¿e fizyczny kontakt z drugim cz³owiekiem jest przyjemniejszy. Efekty uboczne za¿ywania obejmuj¹: podniesienie ciœnienia, przyspieszenie pulsu, szczêkoœcisk, suchoœæ w ustach.

TEXT.English: An [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]egg[COLOR_REVERT] is a female germ cell, consisting essentially of shell, yolk and egg white. Throughout history, it has also become a food that can be consumed directly or as an ingredient in many cuisines around the world. The most used is the chicken egg, but the eggs of other birds, fishes, turtles, mollusks, insects, etc. are also consumed.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]oeuf[COLOR_REVERT] est une cellule germinale femelle, composรฉe essentiellement de coquille, de jaune et de blanc d'oeuf. Tout au long de l'histoire, il est รฉgalement devenu un aliment qui peut รชtre consommรฉ directement ou comme ingrรฉdient dans de nombreuses cuisines ร travers le monde. Le plus utilisรฉ est l'oeuf de poule, mais les oeufs d'autres oiseaux, poissons, tortues, mollusques, insectes, etc. sont รฉgalement consommรฉs.
TEXT.German: Ein Groรteil der Tiere (z.B. Fische, Amphibien, Reptilien, Vรถgel und selbst einige wenige Sรคugetiere) legt [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Eier[COLOR_REVERT]. Diese bestehen aus der weiblichen Keimzelle, Nรคhrstoffen fรผr die Entwicklung des Embryos und einer schรผtzenden Hรผlle. Da Eier รผber einen hohen Nรคhrwert verfรผgen, sind sie eine begehrte Nahrungsquelle, die sowohl von vielen Tierarten als auch vom Menschen genutzt wird. Am hรคufigsten wird weltweit das Hรผhnerei fรผr Speisen (z.B. Kuchen, Gebรคck, Nudeln) verwendet. Heutzutage sind Eier neben der Ernรคhrung auch fรผr die Herstellung von Vakzinen wichtig, 90% des weltweiten Grippeimpfstoffs wird mit Hilfe von Eiern produziert.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]uovo[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una cellula germinale femminile, costituita essenzialmente da guscio, tuorlo e albume. Lungo la storia, รจ diventato anche un alimento consumabile direttamente o come ingrediente in numerosi piatti delle cucine di tutto il mondo. Il piรน utilizzato รจ l'uovo di gallina, ma si consumano anche le uova di altri volatili, di pesci, di tartarughe, di molluschi, di insetti, eccetera.
