TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]geoglyph[COLOR_REVERT] is a large design or motif (generally longer than 4 m) produced on the ground and typically formed by clastic rocks or similarly durable elements of the landscape, such as stones, stone fragments, live trees, gravel, or earth. A positive geoglyph is formed by the arrangement and alignment of materials on the ground in a manner akin to petroforms, while a negative geoglyph is formed by removing patinated clasts to expose unpatinated ground in a manner akin to petroglyphs.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]geoglifo[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un grande disegno o motivo (generalmente piรน lungo di 4 metri) prodotto sul terreno e tipicamente formato da rocce clastiche o elementi del paesaggio similmente durevoli, come pietre, frammenti di pietra, alberi vivi, ghiaia o terra. Un geoglifo positivo รจ formato dalla disposizione e dall'allineamento dei materiali sul terreno in un modo simile ai petroformi, mentre un geoglifo negativo รจ formato rimuovendo i clasti patinati per esporre il terreno in modo simile ai petroglifi.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Geoglif[COLOR_REVERT], to rysunek, figura lub wzór na ziemi sporych rozmiarów (zwykle wiêkszy ni¿ 4 metry). Najczêœciej tworzony poprzez wykopanie niewielkich rowków, u³o¿enie kamieni, lub w inny sposób umo¿liwiaj¹cy odró¿nienie powsta³ych linii od reszty gruntu stanowi¹cego w tym przypadku t³o dla powsta³ego w ten sposób obiektu. Geoglify zwykle przedstawiaj¹ zwierzêta, ludzi. Mog¹ równie¿ przedstawiaæ figury geometryczne w tym czêsto linie, a tak¿e inne abstrakcyjne wzory.

TEXT.German: Geothermale Energie
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฎรฒรฅรฐรฌ. รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรฟ

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Geothermal energy[COLOR_REVERT] is thermal energy generated and stored in the Earth. This kind of energy originates from the original formation of the planet and from radioactive decay of materials. With water from hot springs, geothermal energy has been used for bathing since Paleolithic times and for space heating since ancient Roman times, but it is now better known for electricity generation. Geothermal power is cost-effective, reliable, sustainable, and environmentally friendly, but has historically been limited to areas near tectonic plate boundaries. Technological advances during 20th and 21st century have dramatically expanded the range and size of viable resources, especially for applications such as home heating, opening a potential for widespread exploitation.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]energia geotermica[COLOR_REVERT] รจ l'energia termica generata e immagazzinata nella Terra. Questo tipo di energia proviene dalla formazione originale del pianeta e dal decadimento radioattivo dei materiali. Assieme all'acqua proveniente da sorgenti termali, l'energia geotermica รจ stata utilizzata per la balneazione fin dal Paleolitico e per il riscaldamento degli spazi fin dai tempi dei romani, ma ora รจ meglio conosciuta per la generazione di elettricitร . L'energia geotermica รจ economica, affidabile, sostenibile e rispettosa dell'ambiente, ma รจ stata storicamente limitata alle aree a ridosso dei confini fra placche tettoniche. I progressi tecnologici nel XX e XXI secolo hanno notevolmente ampliato la gamma e le dimensioni degli utilizzi, in particolare per il riscaldamento domestico, aprendo un potenziale sfruttamento diffuso.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Energia geotermalna[COLOR_REVERT], to energia cieplna ska³, wody i gruntu pod powierzchni¹ Ziemi, zaliczana do odnawialnych Ÿróde³ energii. Proces odnawiania Ÿróde³ geotermalnych jest jednak powolny, st¹d przy ma³ym strumieniu ciep³a geotermalnego pobieranie du¿ej iloœci ciep³a mo¿e doprowadziæ do wych³odzenia ska³ lub spadku ciœnienia w zbiorniku.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ghee[COLOR_REVERT] is a class of clarified butter that originated in ancient India. It is commonly used in Middle Eastern, Indian subcontinent, and Southeast Asian cuisine, traditional medicine, and religious rituals.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ghee[COLOR_REVERT] est une classe de beurre clarifiรฉ originaire de l'Inde ancienne. Il est couramment utilisรฉ dans la cuisine du Moyen-Orient, du sous-continent indien et de l'Asie du Sud-Est, la mรฉdecine traditionnelle et les rituels religieux.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ghi[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un tipo di burro chiarificato, originario dell'antica India. ร comunemente usato nella cucina mediorientale, del subcontinente indiano e del sud-est asiatico, cosรฌ come nella medicina tradizionale e nei riti religiosi.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ghi[COLOR_REVERT], to rodzaj mas³a klarowanego, t³uszcz zwierzêcy wytwarzany przez d³ugotrwa³e gotowanie na ma³ym ogniu i usuwanie szumowin oraz osadów (klarowanie) mas³a[1]. TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Glassware[COLOR_REVERT] is a common term to define drinking vessels (drinkware) and tableware used to set a table for eating a meal, general glass items such as vases, and glasses used in the catering industry. Different styles of glassware exist for a number of reasons: they may reflect national traditions, folk art or legislation regarding serving measures, or being more practical in stacking, washing and avoiding breakage, or other practical uses.
TEXT.French: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]verrerie[COLOR_REVERT] est un terme courant pour dรฉfinir les rรฉcipients ร boire (verres) et la vaisselle utilisรฉe pour dresser une table pour manger un repas, les articles en verre gรฉnรฉraux tels que les vases, et les verres utilisรฉs dans l'industrie de la restauration. Diffรฉrents styles de verrerie existent pour plusieurs raisons : ils peuvent reflรฉter des traditions nationales, de l'art populaire ou une lรฉgislation concernant les mesures de service, ou รชtre plus pratiques pour l'empilage, le lavage et รฉviter les bris, ou d'autres utilisations pratiques.
TEXT.Italian: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Vetreria[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un termine generico per definire recipienti per bere (bicchieri) e stoviglie usate per apparecchiare una tavola, oggetti in vetro comuni come vasi e bicchieri usati nel settore della ristorazione. Esistono diversi stili di oggetti in vetro per una serie di motivi: possono riflettere le tradizioni nazionali, l'arte popolare o la legislazione in materia di misure di servizio, o essere piรน pratici nell'impilarli, lavarli ed evitare rotture o altri usi pratici.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wyroby szklane[COLOR_REVERT], to zbiorcze okreœlenie na wszelkiego rodzaju produkty wytworzone ze szk³a takie jak: szkalnki, kubki, talerze, wazy itp. Istniej¹ ró¿ne style wyrobów szklanych z wielu powodów: mog¹ odzwierciedlaæ tradycje narodowe, sztukê ludow¹ lub ustawodawstwo dotycz¹ce sposobu podawania, byæ bardziej praktycznym w uk³adaniu w stosy, myciu i unikaniu st³uczenia lub w innych praktycznych zastosowaniach.

ััะตะบะปะฐ ัะพะดะตัะถะฐั ะดะธะพะบัะธะด ะบัะตะผะฝะธั ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะณะพ ะบะพะผะฟะพะฝะตะฝัะฐ ะธ ััะตะบะปะพะพะฑัะฐะทะพะฒะฐัะตะปั.[PARAGRAPH:1]ะะฑััะฝะพะต ััะตะบะปะพ ะฟะตัะตั ะพะดะฝัั ัะพััะพัะฝะธะน ะฒ ะฒะธะดะธะผะพะผ ะดะธะฐะฟะฐะทะพะฝะต ัะฒะตัะฐ. ะะดะฝะพัะพะดะฝะพััั ััะตะบะปะฐ ะฝะฐ ะผะฐัััะฐะฑะฐั , ะฟัะตะฒััะฐััะธั ะดะปะธะฝั ะฒะพะปะฝั ะฒะธะดะธะผะพะณะพ ัะฒะตัะฐ, ัะฐะบะถะต ัะฟะพัะพะฑััะฒัะตั ะตะณะพ ะฟัะพะทัะฐัะฝะพััะธ, ะฟะพัะบะพะปัะบั ะฝะตะพะดะฝะพัะพะดะฝะพััะธ ะฒัะทัะฒะฐัั ัะฐััะตัะฝะธะต ัะฒะตัะฐ, ะฝะฐัััะฐั ะฟะตัะตะดะฐัั ะบะพะณะตัะตะฝัะฝะพะณะพ ะธะทะพะฑัะฐะถะตะฝะธั. ะะฝะพะณะธะต ะฟัะตะดะผะตัั ะดะพะผะฐัะฝะตะณะพ ะพะฑะธั ะพะดะฐ ัะดะตะปะฐะฝั ะธะท ััะตะบะปะฐ. ะกัะฐะบะฐะฝั, ะผะธัะบะธ ะธ ะฑัััะปะบะธ ัะฐััะพ ะดะตะปะฐัััั ะธะท ััะตะบะปะฐ, ัะฐะฒะฝะพ ะบะฐะบ ะธ ะปะฐะผะฟะพัะบะธ, ะทะตัะบะฐะปะฐ, ะฐะบะฒะฐัะธัะผั, ัะปะตะบััะพะฝะฝะพ-ะปััะตะฒัะต ัััะฑะบะธ, ะบะพะผะฟัััะตัะฝัะต ะฟะปะพัะบะธะต ะดะธัะฟะปะตะธ ะธ ะพะบะฝะฐ.[PARAGRAPH:1]ะ ะธััะปะตะดะพะฒะฐัะตะปััะบะธั ะปะฐะฑะพัะฐัะพัะธัั ะบะพะปะฑั, ะฟัะพะฑะธัะบะธ ะธ ะดััะณะพะต ัะฐััั ะปะฐะฑะพัะฐัะพัะฝะพะน ะฟะพััะดั ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดะธััั ัะตัะธะนะฝะพ, ะฝะพ ะฒ ะบััะฟะฝัั ะปะฐะฑะพัะฐัะพัะธัั ัะฐะบะถะต ะตััั ััะตะบะปะพะดัะฒ ะดะปั ััะตะบะปะฐยป ะฑะพะปะตะต 2300 ะณัะฐะดััะพะฒ ะฟะพ ะฆะตะปััะธั (4200 ะฟะพ ะคะฐัะตะฝะณะตะนัั). ะ ัะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะบะฐะบ ะธะท ัะธััะพะณะพ ะดะธะพะบัะธะดะฐ ะบัะตะผะฝะธั ะผะพะถะฝะพ ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดะธัั ััะตะบะปะพ ะดะปั ัะฟะตัะธะฐะปัะฝัั ะฟัะธะผะตะฝะตะฝะธะน, ะฒ ะพะฑััะฝะพะต ััะตะบะปะพ ะดะพะฑะฐะฒะปััั ะดััะณะธะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ ะดะปั ัะฟัะพัะตะฝะธั ะพะฑัะฐะฑะพัะบะธ. ะะดะธะฝ ะธะท ะฝะธั - ะบะฐัะฑะพะฝะฐั ะฝะฐััะธั (Na2CO3), ะบะพัะพััะน ะฟะพะฝะธะถะฐะตั ัะตะผะฟะตัะฐัััั ะฟะปะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธั ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ ะดะพ 1500 ะฟะพ ะฆะตะปััะธั (2700 ะฟะพ ะคะฐัะตะฝะณะตะนัั) ะฒ ะฝะฐััะธะตะฒะพ-ะธะทะฒะตััะบะพะฒะพะผ ััะตะบะปะต; ยซัะพะดะฐยป ะพัะฝะพัะธััั ะบ ะธัั ะพะดะฝะพะผั ะธััะพัะฝะธะบั ะบะฐัะฑะพะฝะฐัะฐ (ะพะบัะธะด ะบะฐะปััะธั (CaO), ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะฟะพะปััะฐะตะผัะน ะธะท ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัะบะฐ), ะฝะตะบะพัะพัะพะต ะบะพะปะธัะตััะฒะพ ะพะบัะธะดะฐ ะผะฐะณะฝะธั (MgO) ะธ ะพะบัะธะดะฐ TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Gold[COLOR_REVERT] is a bright, slightly reddish yellow, dense, soft, malleable, and ductile metal in its purest form. It often occurs in free elemental form, as nuggets or grains, in rocks, in veins, and in alluvial deposits. A relatively rare element, gold's high malleability, ductility, resistance to corrosion and most other chemical reactions, and conductivity of electricity have led to its use for coinage, jewelry, and other arts throughout recorded history, as well as in computer electrical connectors, infrared shielding, colored-glass production, gold leafing, and tooth restoration.
TEXT.German: Gold zรคhlt seit jeher zu den begehrtesten und wertvollsten Metallen der Welt. Seine Verwendungszwecke reichen von der Schmuckfertigung bis hin zur Elektronik, und sogar das weltweite Wรคhrungssystem basiert auf diesem Edelmetall. Die Ursache fรผr den hohen Wert von Gold ist seine Seltenheit. Zwar gibt es in vielen Regionen der Welt Goldvorkommen, allerdings sind die wertvollsten groรen Goldadern tief im Inneren der Berge. Das Auffinden eines groรen Vorkommens bedeutet fรผr die Siedlungen und Stรคdte in der Nรคhe der Mine einen bedeutenden wirtschaftlichen Aufschwung. Ein historisches Beispiel fรผr dieses Phรคnomen sind die vielen Ortschaften, die wรคhrend des kalifornischen Goldrauschs entstanden und eine kurze Blรผtezeit erlebten.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]oro[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un metallo brillante, leggermente rossastro, denso, morbido, malleabile e duttile nella sua forma piรน pura. Si presenta spesso in forma elementare libera, sotto forma di pepite o granaglie, nelle rocce, nelle vene e nei depositi alluvionali. Elemento relativamente raro, l'alta malleabilitร , la duttilitร , la resistenza alla corrosione e la maggior parte delle altre reazioni chimiche e la conduttivitร dell'elettricitร dell'oro hanno portato al suo uso per monete, gioielli e altre opere d'arte nel corso della storia, cosรฌ come nei connettori elettrici dei computer, nelle schermature a infrarossi, nella produzione di vetri colorati e foglie d'oro e nel restauro dei denti.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]grain[COLOR_REVERT] is a small, hard, dry seed, with or without an attached hull or fruit layer, harvested for human or animal consumption. The two main types of commercial grain crops are cereals and legumes. Because of their properties, they can be stored, measured, and transported more readily than can other kinds of food crops such as fresh fruits, roots and tubers. The development of grain agriculture allowed excess food to be produced and stored easily which could have led to the creation of the first permanent settlements and the division of society into classes.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]grano[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un seme piccolo, duro, secco, con o senza guscio o buccia, che viene raccolto per essere consumato da umani o animali. I due principali tipi di colture commerciali sono cereali e legumi. Per le loro proprietร , possono essere immagazzinati, misurati e trasportati piรน facilmente rispetto ad altri tipi di colture alimentari come frutta fresca, radici e tuberi. Lo sviluppo dell'agricoltura cerealicola ha permesso di produrre e conservare facilmente il cibo in eccesso, il che avrebbe potuto portare alla creazione dei primi insediamenti permanenti e a una primordiale divisione della societร in classi.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ziarniak (ziarno)[COLOR_REVERT], to suchy, niepêkaj¹cy owoc traw bêd¹cy jednonasienn¹ nie³upk¹. Ziarna zbó¿ stanowi¹ podstawowe Ÿród³o pokarmu roœlinnego dla ludzi. Ziarna zbó¿ (z wyj¹tkiem zbó¿ rzekomych โ gryki i komosy ry¿owej) maj¹ 25% udzia³ w Ÿród³ach energii w po¿ywieniu ludzi w krajach wysoko rozwiniêtych i a¿ do 85% w krajach s³abo rozwiniêtych. Po przetworzeniu uzyskuje siê z nich: skrobiê, bia³ka, m¹ki (a z nich chleb i makaron), kasze, p³atki, otrêby, oleje (g³. olej kukurydziany), piwo, spirytus, poza tym tak¿e paszê dla zwierz¹t.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตัะฒัะต ะทะตัะฝะฐ ะทะปะฐะบะพะฒ ะฑัะปะธ ะพะดะพะผะฐัะฝะตะฝั ะพะบะพะปะพ 12000 ะปะตั ะฝะฐะทะฐะด ะดัะตะฒะฝะธะผะธ ะทะตะผะปะตะดะตะปััะตัะบะธะผะธ ัะพะพะฑัะตััะฒะฐะผะธ ะฒ ัะตะณะธะพะฝะต ะะปะพะดะพัะพะดะฝะพะณะพ ะฟะพะปัะผะตัััะฐ ะฝะฐ ัะณะพ-ะทะฐะฟะฐะดะต ะะทะธะธ. ะัะตะฝะธัะฐ ะญะผะผะตัะฐ, ะพะดะฝะพะทะปะฐัะฝะฐั ะฟัะตะฝะธัะฐ ะธ ััะผะตะฝั ะฑัะปะธ ััะตะผั ะธะท ัะฐะบ ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐะตะผัั ะฝะตะพะปะธัะธัะตัะบะธั ะบัะปัััั-ะพัะฝะพะฒะพะฟะพะปะพะถะฝะธะบะพะฒ ะฒ ัะฐะทะฒะธัะธะธ ัะตะปััะบะพะณะพ ั ะพะทัะนััะฒะฐ.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]grape[COLOR_REVERT] is a berry of the deciduous woody vines of the flowering plant genus Vitis. Grapes row in clusters of 15 to 300, and can be crimson, black, dark blue, yellow, green, orange, and pink. The cultivation of the domesticated grape began 6,000-8,000 years ago in the Near East, and they can be eaten fresh as table grapes or they can be used for making wine, jam, grape juice, jelly, grape seed extract, raisins, vinegar, and grape seed oil.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]raisin[COLOR_REVERT] est une baie des vignes ligneuses caduques du genre de plantes ร fleurs Vitis. Les raisins poussent en grappes de 15 ร 300, et peuvent รชtre cramoisis, noirs, bleu foncรฉ, jaunes, verts, oranges et roses. La culture du raisin domestiquรฉ a commencรฉ il y a 6 000 ร 8 000 ans au Proche-Orient, et ils peuvent รชtre mangรฉs frais comme raisins de table ou utilisรฉs pour faire du vin, de la confiture, du jus de raisin, de la gelรฉe, de l'extrait de pรฉpins de raisin, des raisins secs, du vinaigre et de l'huile de pรฉpins de raisin.
TEXT.German: Die Edle Weinrebe (Vitis vinifera subsp. vinifera), meist einfach nur Wein genannt, gehรถrt zur Gattung der Weinreben (Vitis). Es handelt sich biologisch um eine Lianenpflanze, die bis zu 10 m hoch werden kann. Nach der Blรผte bildet der Rebstock Fruchtansรคtze aus, aus denen sich dann die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Weintrauben[COLOR_REVERT] entwickeln.[NEWLINE]Die Weinrebe gehรถrt zu den รคltesten Kulturpflanzen. Schon 5800 v.Chr. ist Weinanbau im Sรผdkaukasus nachgewiesen. รber Sumer breitete sie sich รผber den gesamten Nahen Osten aus. Ab dem 4. Jahrtausend v.Chr. bauten die Alten รgypter Wein an, um 1700 v.Chr. kultivierten die Minoer auf Kreta die ersten Reben. Mit den Rรถmern breitete sich der Weinbau in Spanien, Gallien und Nordafrika, etwas spรคter auch an Rhein und Mosel, aus.[NEWLINE]Die Edle Weinrebe zรคhlt zu den wichtigsten landwirtschaftlichen Kulturpflanzen weltweit. Sie werden in nahezu allen Lรคndern mit gemรครigtem und subtropischem Klima angebaut, vor allem zur Produktion von Wein, Tafeltrauben und Rosinen.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]uva[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una bacca delle viti legnose decidue del genere di pianta fiorita Vitis. L'uva cresce in grappoli da 15 a 300 chicchi l'uno e puรฒ essere di colore cremisi, neri, blu scuro, gialli, verdi, arancioni o rosa. La coltivazione dell'uva domestica รจ iniziata 6.000-8.000 anni fa nel Vicino Oriente e puรฒ essere consumata fresca come uva da tavola o puรฒ essere usata per produrre vino, marmellata, succhi, gelatina, estratto di semi, uva passa, aceto e olio di semi d'uva.

G³ówne produkty spo¿ywcze uzyskiwane z winogron to: d¿em, sok, galaretka, wino, wyci¹g z pestek winogron, rodzynki, ocet winny i olej z pestek winogron. Winogrona s¹ owocami, które pochodz¹ od owoców dzikiej winoroœli z regionu Morza Kaspijskiego. Pocz¹tki produkcji wina datowane s¹ na 8 tys. lat i mia³y miejsce na terenach obecnej Gruzji.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Graphene[COLOR_REVERT] is an allotrope of carbon that constitute the basic structural element of other carbon allotropes, including graphite, charcoal, carbon nanotubes and fullerenes. It also has a special set of properties which set it apart from other allotropes: is about 100 times stronger than the strongest steel, yet its density is dramatically lower; it conducts heat and electricity very efficiently and is nearly transparent. It is used for various material/device applications, including solar cells, light-emitting diodes (LED), touch panels, and smart windows or phones.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]grafene[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un allotropo del carbonio che costituisce l'elemento strutturale di base di altri allotropi carboniosi, tra cui grafite, carbone, nanotubi di carbonio e fullereni. Ha anche un serie di proprietร particolari che lo distinguono dagli altri allotropi: รจ circa 100 volte piรน forte dell'acciaio piรน resistente, ma la sua densitร รจ notevolmente inferiore; conduce calore ed elettricitร in modo molto efficiente ed รจ quasi trasparente. ร utilizzato per varie applicazioni di materiali e dispositivi, tra cui celle solari, diodi a emissione di luce (LED), pannelli a sfioramento e finestre o telefoni intelligenti.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Grafen[COLOR_REVERT], to p³aska struktura z³o¿ona z atomów wêgla, po³¹czonych w szeœciok¹ty. Testy przeprowadzone przez IBM wykaza³y, ¿e tranzystor wytworzony w procesie technologicznym 240 nm jest w stanie osi¹gn¹æ czêstotliwoœæ do 100 GHz. Przezroczystoœæ i znakomite przewodnictwo sprawiaj¹, ¿e grafen nadaje siê do wytwarzania przejrzystych, zwijanych w rolkê wyœwietlaczy dotykowych oraz do produkcji energii odnawialnej z modu³ów fotowoltaicznych i magazynowania jej w wysokowydajnych akumulatorach b¹dŸ tak¿e w superkondensatorach. TEXT.Russian: รรฐร รดรฅรญ รฏรฐรฅรครฑรฒร รขรซรฟรฅรฒ รฑรฎรกรฎรฉ รฎรครญรณ รจรง รชรฐรจรฑรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรต รดรฎรฐรฌ รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร , รญร รฐรฟรครณ รฑ ร รซรฌร รงรฎรฌ, รฃรฐร รดรจรฒรฎรฌ, รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรครญรปรฌรจ รญร รญรฎรฒรฐรณรกรชร รฌรจ รจ รดรณรซรซรฅรฐรฅรญร รฌรจ. ร รฝรฒรฎรฌ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรฅ ร รฒรฎรฌรป รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร รฐร รฑรฏรฎรซรฎรฆรฅรญรป รข รฏรฐร รขรจรซรผรญรฎรฉ รธรฅรฑรฒรจรณรฃรฎรซรผรญรฎรฉ รชร รฐรฒรจรญรฎรฉ. รรฐร รดรฅรญ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รกรปรฒรผ รฎรฏรจรฑร รญ รชร รช รฑรซรฎรจรฑรฒรปรฉ รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซ รฃรฐร รดรจรฒร รฒรฎรซรนรจรญรฎรฉ รฑรซรฎรฟ รข รฎรครจรญ ร รฒรฎรฌ. รรง รถรฅรญรญรปรต รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฎ รฃรฐร รดรฅรญร รฟรขรซรฟรพรฒรฑรฟ รกรฎรซรผรธร รฟ รฏรฐรฎรทรญรฎรฑรฒรผ, รซรฅรฃรชรฎรฑรฒรผ, รฏรฎรทรฒรจ รฏรฎรซรญร รฟ รฏรฐรฎรงรฐร รทรญรฎรฑรฒรผ, รฎรฒรซรจรทรญร รฟ รฒรฅรฏรซรฎ- รจ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรฏรฐรฎรขรฎรครจรฌรฎรฑรฒรผ. รรง รฅรฃรฎ รขรงร รจรฌรฎรครฅรฉรฑรฒรขรจรฟ รฑ รครฐรณรฃรจรฌรจ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซร รฌรจ รจ รฑรฎ รฑรขรฅรฒรฎรฌ, รจ รฅรฃรฎ รฏรฎ รฑรณรฒรจ รฑรขรฎรฅรฉ รครขรณรฌรฅรฐรญรฎรฉ รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครป, รฏรฐรฎรจรฑรตรฎรครฟรฒ รญรฅรชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รณรญรจรชร รซรผรญรปรฅ รฑรขรฎรฉรฑรฒรขร .

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Graphite[COLOR_REVERT] is a crystalline form of carbon, with its atoms arranged in a hexagonal structure. It occurs naturally in this form and is the most stable form of carbon under standard conditions. Under high pressures and temperatures, it converts to diamond. Graphite is used in pencils and lubricants. It is a good conductor of heat and electricity, and its high conductivity makes it useful in electronic products, such as electrodes, batteries, and solar panels.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]graphite[COLOR_REVERT] est une forme cristalline du carbone, avec ses atomes disposรฉs dans une structure hexagonale. Il se prรฉsente naturellement sous cette forme et est la forme la plus stable du carbone dans des conditions standard. Sous haute pression et tempรฉrature, il se transforme en diamant. Le graphite est utilisรฉ dans les crayons et les lubrifiants. C'est un bon conducteur de chaleur et d'รฉlectricitรฉ, et sa haute conductivitรฉ le rend utile dans les produits รฉlectroniques, tels que les รฉlectrodes, les batteries et les panneaux solaires.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]grafite[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una forma cristallina di carbonio, con i suoi atomi disposti in una struttura esagonale. Si presenta naturalmente in questa forma ed รจ la forma piรน stabile di carbonio in condizioni standard. Sotto alte pressioni e temperature, si converte in diamante. La grafite viene utilizzata in matite e lubrificanti. ร un buon conduttore di calore ed elettricitร e la sua elevata conduttivitร lo rende utile in prodotti elettronici come elettrodi, batterie e pannelli solari.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Grafit[COLOR_REVERT], to pospolity i szeroko rozpowszechniony minera³ z gromady pierwiastków rodzimych. Stosowany jako naturalny suchy smar. Jest โ obok diamentu i fulerytu โ odmian¹ alotropow¹ wêgla. Nazwa pochodzi od gr. graphein = pisaæ, nawi¹zuje do tradycyjnego zastosowania tego minera³u.
TEXT.Russian: รรจรญรฅรฐร รซรผรญรปรฉ รฃรฐร รดรจรฒ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ ร รซรซรฎรฒรฐรฎรฏรฎรฌ รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร . รร รซ รฅรฌรณ รฒร รชรฎรฅ รญร รงรขร รญรจรฅ รรขรฐร ร รฌ รรฎรฒรฒรซรฎรก รรฅรฐรญรฅรฐ รข 1789 รฎรฒ รครฐรฅรขรญรฅรฃรฎ รฃรฐรฅรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฃรฐร รดรฎ, "รฐรจรฑรฎรขร รฒรผ/รฏรจรฑร รฒรผ", รครซรฟ รฅรฃรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรฟ รข รชร รฐร รญรคร รธร รต, รฃรครฅ รฎรญ รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑรขรจรญรถรฎรฌ (รญรฅ รฏรณรฒร รฒรผ รฑ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฌ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒรฎรฌ รฑรขรจรญรฅรถ). ร รฎรฒรซรจรทรจรฅ รฎรฒ ร รซรฌร รงร (รครฐรณรฃรฎรฉ ร รซรซรฎรฒรฐรฎรฏ รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร ), รฃรฐร รดรจรฒ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฌ รฏรฐรฎรขรฎรครญรจรชรฎรฌ, รฏรฎรซรณรฌรฅรฒร รซรซรฎรฌ. รรซรฅรครฎรขร รฒรฅรซรผรญรฎ, รฝรฒรฎ รฏรฎรซรฅรงรญรฎ รข รฒร รชรจรต รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขร รต, รชร รช รข รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรคร รต รครณรฃรฎรขรฎรฉ รซร รฌรฏรป. รรฐร รดรจรฒ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รญร รจรกรฎรซรฅรฅ รฑรฒร รกรจรซรผรญรฎรฉ รดรฎรฐรฌรฎรฉ รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร รฏรฐรจ รฑรฒร รญรคร รฐรฒรญรปรต รณรฑรซรฎรขรจรฟรต. รรฎรฝรฒรฎรฌรณ รฅรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒ รข รฒรฅรฐรฌรฎรตรจรฌรจรจ รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รฑรฒร รญรคร รฐรฒรญรฎรฃรฎ รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรญรจรฟ รครซรฟ รฎรฏรฐรฅรครฅรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฒรฅรฏรซรฎรฒรป รฎรกรฐร รงรฎรขร รญรจรฟ รฑรฎรฅรครจรญรฅรญรจรฉ รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร . รรฐร รดรจรฒ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รฐร รฑรฑรฌร รฒรฐรจรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รขรปรฑรธรจรฌ รฑรฎรฐรฒรฎรฌ รณรฃรซรฟ, รทรณรฒรผ รขรปรธรฅ ร รญรฒรฐร รถรจรฒร รจ รจรญร รทรฅ รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รชร รช รฌรฅรฒร -ร รญรฒรฐร รถรจรฒ, รตรฎรฒรฟ รฎรญ รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ รญรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขร , รฏรฎรฑรชรฎรซรผรชรณ รฑ รฒรฐรณรครฎรฌ รขรฎรฑรฏรซร รฌรฅรญรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ.
รรฐร รขร

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Grass[COLOR_REVERT] is a large and nearly ubiquitous family of flowering plants, that includes cereal grasses, bamboos and the grasses of natural grassland and cultivated lawns and pasture. Grasses became widespread toward the end of the Cretaceous period, and are currently the most widespread plant type on every continent, including Antarctica. It is also a valuable source of food and energy for all sorts of wildlife and organics: many types of animals eat grass as their main source of food (and are called graminivores), but it is also eaten by omnivorous or even occasionally by primarily carnivorous animals.
TEXT.German: Unter dem Begriff [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Gras[COLOR_REVERT] werden krautige Pflanzen mit langen, schmalen Blรคttern, die an runden, hohlen Halmen oder Stรคngeln sitzen, zusammengefasst. Gras ist Lebensgrundlage vieler Tiere, insbesondere der Wiederkรคuer und Einhufer. Der Mensch hingegen nutzt nur verschiedene Sรผรgrรคser (Getreide) als Nahrung. Andere Grรคser werden heute รผberwiegend als Tierfutter, als Tiereinstreu, als Gebrauchs- oder Zierrasen oder auch als Substrat bei der Produktion von Biogas verwendet. Traditionell wird Gras immer noch zur Dachabdeckung verwendet, die modern auch in Form einer Dachbegrรผnung erfolgen kann. Nicht zuletzt wird Gras auch in der Papierherstellung verwendet.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]erba[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una grande e quasi onnipresente famiglia di piante da fiore, che comprende cereali, bambรน e l'erba dei prati naturali e dei pascoli coltivati. Le erbe si sono diffuse verso la fine del periodo Cretaceo e sono attualmente il tipo di pianta piรน diffuso in tutti i continenti, compresa l'Antartide. ร anche una preziosa fonte di cibo ed energia per tutti i tipi di fauna selvatica e organica: molti tipi di animali mangiano erba come principale fonte di cibo (e sono chiamati graminivori), ma รจ anche mangiata dagli onnivori o anche occasionalmente da animali principalmente carnivori.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Trawy (wiechlinowate)[COLOR_REVERT], to rodzina roœlin nale¿¹ca do rzêdu wiechlinowców. Liczy ok. 11 tys. gatunków. Roœliny te stanowi¹ g³ówny komponent roœlinnoœci formacji trawiastych, ³¹k i pastwisk.
TEXT.Russian: รร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฟ รฝรฒรฎรฃรฎ รฒรจรฏร รขรฑรฅรฃรคร รกรปรซรจ รขร รฆรญรป รครซรฟ รทรฅรซรฎรขรฅรชร . รรญรจ รขรปรฐร รนรจรขร รซรจรฑรผ รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รชรฎรฐรฌร รครซรฟ รครฎรฌร รธรญรจรต รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรปรต รข รฒรฅรทรฅรญรจรฅ 6000 รซรฅรฒ.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]gravity generator[COLOR_REVERT] is a machinery that creates an inertial force that mimics the effects of a gravitational force. Artificial gravity is used in simulations to help astronauts train for extreme conditions, and has been proposed as a solution in human spaceflight to the adverse health effects caused by prolonged weightlessness.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]gรฉnรฉrateur de gravitรฉ[COLOR_REVERT] est une machine qui crรฉe une force inertielle qui imite les effets d'une force gravitationnelle. La gravitรฉ artificielle est utilisรฉe dans des simulations pour aider les astronautes ร s'entraรฎner pour des conditions extrรชmes, et a รฉtรฉ proposรฉe comme solution dans les vols spatiaux humains aux effets nรฉfastes sur la santรฉ causรฉs par l'apesanteur prolongรฉe.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]generatore di gravitร [COLOR_REVERT] รจ una macchina che crea una forza inerziale che imita gli effetti della forza di gravitร . La gravitร artificiale รจ utilizzata nelle simulazioni per aiutare gli astronauti ad allenarsi in condizioni estreme ed รจ stata proposta come soluzione agli effetti negativi sulla salute causati dalla prolungata assenza di gravitร nei voli spaziali umani.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Guava[COLOR_REVERT] is a common tropical fruit cultivated in many tropical and subtropical regions. Guavas originated from Central America and were distributed throughout tropical America and the Caribbean region, and then later adopted as a crop in other subtropical and tropical areas. Guava fruits, usually 4 to 12 cm (1.6 to 4.7 in) long, are round or oval depending on the species. They have a pronounced and typical fragrance and are usually eaten raw, with salt or other spices, or made into juices, candies, preserves, jellies, jams, and marmalades.
TEXT.German: Die (Echte) [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Guave[COLOR_REVERT] (Psidium guajava) aus der Familie der Myrtengewรคchse ist gleichermaรen Obst und Heilpflanze. Die Frรผchte wachsen an einem bis zu 13 m hohen Baum mit glatter, grauer Borke, die durch sich ablรถsende Streifen auffรคllt. Die ursprรผnglich aus Sรผdamerika stammende Pflanze hat sich unter Einfluss des Menschen auf die gesamten Tropen ausgebreitet. An einigen Standorten verdrรคngt sie inzwischen sogar die heimischen Spezies und wird zum Problem, so zum Beispiel auf dem Hawaii-Archipel, wo sie vor knapp 200 Jahren eingefรผhrt wurde. Viele der Regenwรคlder dieser Inseln bestehen heute buchstรคblich nur noch aus Guavenbรคumen. Dies ist zum groรen Teil auch verwilderten Hausschweinen geschuldet, die die Frรผchte gern verzehren und die Samen mit ihrem Kot in weitem Umkreis verteilen.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]guava[COLOR_REVERT] (o guaiava) รจ un frutto tropicale, coltivato in molte regioni tropicali e subtropicali. Le guaiave provengono dall'America centrale, si sono diffuse in tutta l'America tropicale e nella regione dei Caraibi, per poi essere adottate come coltura in altre aree subtropicali e tropicali. I frutti di guaiava, di solito lunghi da 4 a 12 centimetri, sono rotondi o ovali a seconda della specie. Hanno un profumo pronunciato e di solito vengono consumati crudi, con sale o altre spezie, o trasformati in succhi, caramelle, conserve, gelatine o marmellate.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Guawa[COLOR_REVERT], to to tropikalny owoc pochodz¹cy z centralnej Ameryki i po³udniowej czêœci Meksyku. Psiudium guajava, bo tak nazwiemy j¹ fachowo, to owoc o kszta³cie gruszki. Jej mi¹¿sz przypomina ten pomidora, tylko o arbuzowym kolorze. Ma zaskakuj¹cy, s³odko-ostry smak. Ze wzglêdu na bogactwo substancji od¿ywczych zaliczana jest do grona superowoców. Skórka owocu ma zielonkawy kolor, który wraz z dojrzewaniem przechodzi w ¿ó³ty. Mi¹¿sz przybiera barwy ró¿ow¹, bia³¹ lub ¿ó³taw¹ i zawiera liczne nasiona, o nerkowatym kszta³cie.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Guinea pig[COLOR_REVERT], also known as cavy, is a species of rodent. They originated in the Andes of South America, and scientific studies suggest they are descendants of a closely related species of cavy, that was domesticated as livestock and as a source of food, but also used in folk medicine and in community religious ceremonies. In Western society, the domestic guinea pig has enjoyed widespread popularity as a household pet since 16th century, but they were also frequently used as model organisms in biological experimentation, so that "guinea pig" came into use to describe any kind of test subject, including humans.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Meerschweinchen[COLOR_REVERT] stammen ursprรผnglich aus Sรผdamerika und wurden dort bereits um 5000 bis 2000 v.Chr. zur Fleisch- und Pelzproduktion domestiziert. Auch heute noch werden in der Andenregion Meerschweinchen gegessen, aktuell werden sogar grรถรere und fettere Rassen gezรผchtet. Sie eignen sich auch gut als Futtertiere fรผr Zoos oder als Versuchstiere fรผr die Forschung. Als Haustiere werden Meerschweinchen erst seit Beginn des 20. Jahrhunderts gezรผchtet. Heute sind sie besonders bei Kindern beliebt, werden aber leider hรคufig einzeln und nicht in der Gruppe gehalten. Das zรคhlt als nicht artgerecht im Sinne des Tierschutzgesetzes.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]porcellino d'India[COLOR_REVERT], noto anche come cavia, รจ una specie di roditore. I porcellini d'India avuto origine nelle Ande del Sud America; studi scientifici suggeriscono che discendono da una specie selvatica strettamente correlata, che รจ stata nel tempo addomesticata come bestiame e come fonte di cibo, ma utilizzata anche nella medicina popolare e nelle cerimonie religiose. Nella societร occidentale, hanno goduto di una diffusa popolaritร come animali domestici dal XVI secolo, ma sono state anche frequentemente utilizzati come organismi modello nella sperimentazione biologica, al punto che il termine "cavia" viene comunemente utilizzato per descrivere qualsiasi tipo di soggetto di prova, compresi gli umani.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Œwinka morska (kawia domowa)[COLOR_REVERT], to gatunek udomowionego gryzonia z rodziny kawiowatych (Caviidae). Kawia domowa jest gryzoniem o krêpym ciele, krótkiej szyi, bardzo krótkich koñczynach, krótkim pysku. Nie ma ogona lub jest on jedynie w formie szcz¹tkowej. Sierœæ jest krótka, gruba, z delikatnym podszerstkiem. Odmiany d³ugow³ose wystêpuj¹ stosunkowo rzadko. Kawia domowa wykazuje aktywnoœæ dobow¹ zarówno w ci¹gu dnia, jak i w nocy. Jest zwierzêciem stadnym, silnie terytorialnym, spo³ecznym. Kawie ¿yj¹ w ma³ych grupach po 5โ10 osobników, tworz¹c zwi¹zki monogamiczne lub poligamiczne.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hematite[COLOR_REVERT], also spelled as haematite, is a common iron oxide that is widespread in rocks and soils. It is colored black to steel or silver-gray, brown to reddish-brown, or red. It is mined as the main ore of iron and is present in the waste tailings of iron mines. Hematite's popularity in jewelry rose in England during the Victorian era, due to its use in mourning jewelry. Certain types of hematite- or iron-oxide-rich clay have been used in gilding. Hematite is also used in the creation of intaglio engraved gems.
TEXT.French: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hรฉmatite[COLOR_REVERT], รฉgalement orthographiรฉe hรฉmatite, est un oxyde de fer courant que l'on trouve largement dans les roches et les sols. Elle est de couleur noire ร gris acier ou gris argentรฉ, brune ร brun rougeรขtre, ou rouge. Elle est extraite comme principal minerai de fer et est prรฉsente dans les rรฉsidus miniers des mines de fer. La popularitรฉ de l'hรฉmatite dans la bijouterie a augmentรฉ en Angleterre pendant l'รฉpoque victorienne, en raison de son utilisation dans les bijoux de deuil. Certains types d'argile riche en hรฉmatite ou en oxyde de fer ont รฉtรฉ utilisรฉs pour la dorure. L'hรฉmatite est รฉgalement utilisรฉe dans la crรฉation de gemmes gravรฉes en intaille.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ematite[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un ossido di ferro comune diffuso nelle rocce e nel suolo. Puรฒ essere di colore nero, acciaio o grigio-argento, marrone, rossastro-marrone o rosso. ร uno dei principali minerali ferrosi estratti ed รจ presente negli scarti delle miniere di ferro. La popolaritร dell'ematite crebbe in Inghilterra durante l'era vittoriana perchรฉ veniva utilizzato per realizzare gioielli da usare per funerali e occasioni di lutto. Alcuni tipi di argilla ricca di ematite o ossido di ferro sono stati anche usati nella doratura. L'ematite รจ anche usata per la creazione di gemme incise a intaglio.

Jest rozpowszechniony we wszystkich g³ównych typach ska³, w których tworzy wiêksze nagromadzenia lub jako minera³ poboczny barwi je na ró¿owo lub czerwono. Nie jest najbardziej bogat¹, ale bardzo powszechn¹ rud¹ ¿elaza. Bywa u¿ywany jako naturalny pigment do wyrobu czerwonej farby (czerwona ochra). Ma du¿e znaczenie kolekcjonerskie. Wykorzystywany jako kamieñ ozdobny i jubilerski. U¿ywany do wyrobu drobnej galanterii artystycznej i ¿a³obnej.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hat[COLOR_REVERT] is a head covering which is worn for various reasons, including protection against weather conditions, ceremonial or religious reasons, safety, or as a fashion accessory. In the past, hats were an indicator of social status of a person. In the military, hats may denote nationality, branch of service, rank or regiment.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chapeau[COLOR_REVERT] est un couvre-chef qui est portรฉ pour diverses raisons, notamment la protection contre les conditions mรฉtรฉorologiques, des raisons cรฉrรฉmonielles ou religieuses, la sรฉcuritรฉ, ou comme accessoire de mode. Dans le passรฉ, les chapeaux รฉtaient un indicateur du statut social d'une personne. Dans l'armรฉe, les chapeaux peuvent indiquer la nationalitรฉ, la branche de service, le rang ou le rรฉgiment.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cappello[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un copricapo che viene indossato per vari motivi, inclusa la protezione da condizioni meteorologiche o da incidenti, motivi cerimoniali o religiosi oppure come accessorio di moda. In passato, i cappelli erano un indicatore dello status sociale di una persona. In campo militare, i cappelli possono indicare nazionalitร , ramo del servizio, grado o reggimento.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]surfboard[COLOR_REVERT] is an elongated platform used in surfing. Surfboards are relatively light, but are strong enough to support an individual standing on them while riding an ocean wave. They were invented in ancient Hawaii, where they were known as "papa he'e nalu" in the Hawaiian language: they were usually made of wood from local trees, such as koa, and were often over 460 cm (15 ft) in length and extremely heavy. Major advances over the years include the addition of one or more fins (skegs) on the bottom rear of the board, to improve directional stability, and numerous improvements in materials and shape.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]tavola da surf[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una piattaforma allungata utilizzata nel surf. Le tavole da surf sono relativamente leggere, ma abbastanza forti da supportare un individuo in piedi su di loro mentre cavalcano un'onda oceanica. Furono inventati nelle Hawaii, dove erano chiamate "papa he'e nalu" in lingua hawaiana: di solito erano fatte in legno di alberi locali, come il koa, e spesso erano lunghe oltre 460 cm ed estremamente pesanti. I maggiori progressi nel corso degli anni includono l'aggiunta di una o piรน pinne (skeg) nella parte inferiore posteriore della tavola, per migliorarne la stabilitร direzionale, e numerosi miglioramenti nei materiali e nella forma.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Deska surfingowa[COLOR_REVERT], to to wyd³u¿ona platforma u¿ywana w surfingu. Zosta³y wynalezione w staro¿ytnoœci na Hawajach, gdzie w jêzyku hawajskim by³y znane jako โpapa he'e naluโ; zwykle by³y wykonane z drewna pochodz¹cego z lokalnych drzew, takich jak koa, i czêsto mia³y ponad 460 cm (15 stóp) d³ugoœci i by³y niezwykle ciê¿kie.
TEXT.Russian: ะัะบััััะฒะพ ัะตััะธะฝะณะฐ, ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐะตะผะพะต ะฝะฐ ะณะฐะฒะฐะนัะบะพะผ ัะทัะบะต he'enalu, ะฑัะปะพ ะฒะฟะตัะฒัะต ะพะฟะธัะฐะฝะพ ะฒ 1779 ะณะพะดั ะะถะพะทะตัะพะผ ะัะฝะบัะพะผ ะฝะฐ ะฑะฐัะบะต ยซะะฝะดะตะฒะพัยป ะฒะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะฟะตัะฒะพะณะพ ะฟััะตัะตััะฒะธั ะบะฐะฟะธัะฐะฝะฐ ะะถะตะนะผัะฐ ะัะบะฐ.[PARAGRAPH:1]ะกะตััะธะฝะณ ะฑัะป ัะตะฝััะฐะปัะฝะพะน ัะฐัััั ะดัะตะฒะฝะตะน ะฟะพะปะธะฝะตะทะธะนัะบะพะน ะบัะปััััั ะธ ะทะฐะดะพะปะณะพ ะดะพ ะบะพะฝัะฐะบัะพะฒ ั ะตะฒัะพะฟะตะนัะฐะผะธ. ะะพะถะดั (ะะปะธ'ะธ) ะฑัะป ัะฐะผัะผ ะพะฟััะฝัะผ ะฝะฐะตะทะดะฝะธะบะพะผ ะฝะฐ ะฒะพะปะฝะฐั ะฒ ัะพะพะฑัะตััะฒะต ั ะปัััะตะน ะดะพัะบะพะน, ัะดะตะปะฐะฝะฝะพะน ะธะท ะปัััะตะณะพ ะดะตัะตะฒะฐ. ะฃ ะฟัะฐะฒััะตะณะพ ะบะปะฐััะฐ ะฑัะปะธ ะปัััะธะต ะฟะปัะถะธ ะธ ะปัััะธะต ะดะพัะบะธ, ะธ ะฟัะพัััะผ ะปัะดัะผ ะฝะต ัะฐะทัะตัะฐะปะพัั ะฟะพัะตัะฐัั ะพะดะฝะธ ะธ ัะต ะถะต ะฟะปัะถะธ, ะฝะพ ะพะฝะธ ะผะพะณะปะธ ะฟะพะปััะธัั ะฟัะตััะธะถ ะฑะปะฐะณะพะดะฐัั ัะฒะพะตะน ัะฟะพัะพะฑะฝะพััะธ ะบะฐัะฐัััั ะฝะฐ ัะตััะต ะฝะฐ ัะฒะพะธั ััะตะทะฒััะฐะนะฝะพ ััะถะตะปัั ะดะพัะบะฐั .

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Heavy water[COLOR_REVERT] (or deuterium oxide) is a form of water that contains a larger than normal amount of the hydrogen isotope deuterium (also known as heavy hydrogen). In its pure form, it has a density about 11% greater than water, and is not radioactive. The presence of deuterium gives the water different nuclear properties, and the increase of mass gives it slightly different physical and chemical properties when compared to normal water. Heavy water was first produced in 1932. With the discovery of nuclear fission in late 1938, and the need for a neutron moderator that captured few neutrons, heavy water became an essential component in some types of reactors, both those that generate power and those designed to produce isotopes for nuclear weapons.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]acqua pesante[COLOR_REVERT] (o ossido di deuterio) รจ una forma di acqua che contiene una quantitร maggiore del normale di deuterio (un isotopo dell'idrogeno, noto anche come idrogeno pesante). Nella sua forma pura, ha una densitร maggiore dell'acqua di circa l'11% e non รจ radioattiva. La presenza di deuterio conferisce all'acqua proprietร nucleari diverse e l'aumento di massa le conferisce proprietร fisiche e chimiche leggermente diverse rispetto all'acqua normale. L'acqua pesante fu prodotta per la prima volta nel 1932. Con la scoperta della fissione nucleare alla fine del 1938 e la necessitร di un moderatore di neutroni che ne catturasse pochi, l'acqua pesante divenne un componente essenziale in alcuni tipi di reattori, sia quelli che generano energia, sia quelli progettati per produrre isotopi per le armi nucleari.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ciê¿ka woda[COLOR_REVERT], to zwi¹zek chemiczny D2O, czyli tlenek deuteru. Jest stosowana jako moderator w reaktorach j¹drowych, gdy¿ ma zdolnoœæ do spowalniania neutronów prêdkich. Zwyk³a woda te¿ ma tak¹ zdolnoœæ, jednak poch³ania znacznie wiêcej neutronów termicznych (optymalnych do wywo³ywania reakcji rozszczepienia j¹der U-235). Oprócz tego woda deuterowana jest stosowana w chemii, jako jeden z rozpuszczalników stosowanych w analitycznej technice NMR oraz tanie Ÿród³o deuteru, przy badaniu mechanizmów reakcji, poprzez znakowanie izotopowe i obserwacjê tzw. efektu izotopowego.

ััะพะณะพ, ะฒ ัะฒะพั ะพัะตัะตะดั, ะดะพััะฐัะพัะฝะพ, ััะพะฑั ะฒัะทะฒะฐัั ัะฐะทะปะธัะธั, ะฒะฐะถะฝัะต ะดะปั ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ะฑะธะพั ะธะผะธัะตัะบะธั ัะตะฐะบัะธะน. ะ TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]helicopter[COLOR_REVERT] is a type of rotorcraft in which lift and thrust are supplied by rotors. This allows the helicopter to take off and land vertically, to hover, and to fly forward, backward, and laterally. Helicopters were developed and built during first half of 20th century, but it was not until 1942 that helicopters reached full-scale production. Due to its operating characteristics, it has been chosen to conduct tasks that were previously not possible with other aircrafts, or were time- or work-intensive to accomplish on the ground. Today, helicopter uses include transportation of people and cargo, military uses, construction, firefighting, search and rescue, tourism, medical transport, law enforcement, agriculture, and aerial observation.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]elicottero[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un tipo di veicolo a elica, a cui la forza di ascensione e spinta sono fornite da dei rotori. Ciรฒ consente all'elicottero di decollare e atterrare in verticale, librarsi e volare in avanti, indietro e lateralmente. Gli elicotteri furono sviluppati e costruiti nella prima metร del XX secolo, ma fu solo nel 1942 che gli elicotteri raggiunsero la produzione su vasta scala. A causa delle sue caratteristiche operative, un elicottero viene impiegato per compiti che prima non era possibile svolgere con altri velivoli o che richiedevano molto tempo o lavoro da svolgere a terra. Gli elicotteri vengono utilizzati per il trasporto di persone e merci o per usi militari o relativi alle forze dell'ordine, all'edilizia, alla lotta agli incendi, alla ricerca e salvataggio, al turismo, al trasporto medico, all'agricoltura e all'osservazione aerea.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Œmig³owiec[COLOR_REVERT], to statek powietrzny ciê¿szy od powietrza (aerodyna), rodzaj wirop³atu, wytwarzaj¹cy si³ê noœn¹ w wyniku oddzia³ywania powietrza na jeden lub wiêcej wirników noœnych, napêdzanych silnikiem. Œmig³owce wesz³y do u¿ytku na du¿¹ skalê w latach 40. i 50. XX wieku. TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฐรฒรฎรซรฅรฒ - รฐร รงรญรฎรขรจรครญรฎรฑรฒรผ รฐรฎรฒรฎรฐรญรปรต รซรฅรฒร รฒรฅรซรผรญรปรต ร รฏรฏร รฐร รฒรฎรข, รข รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรต รฏรฎรครบรฅรฌ รจ รจ รฒรฟรฃร รฎรกรฅรฑรฏรฅรทรฅรญรป รซรฎรฏร รฑรฒรฟรฌรจ. รรฒรฎ รฏรฎรงรขรฎรซรฟรฅรฒ รขรฅรฐรฒรฎรซรฅรฒรณ รขรงรซรฅรฒร รฒรผ รจ รฑร รครจรฒรผรฑรฟ รขรฅรฐรฒรจรชร รซรผรญรฎ, รงร รขรจรฑร รฒรผ รจ รซรฅรฒร รฒรผ รจ รขรฏรฅรฐรฅรค, รจ รญร รงร รค รจ รขรกรฎรช. รรฒรจ ร รฒรฐรจรกรณรฒรป รฏรฎรงรขรฎรซรฟรพรฒ รขรฅรฐรฒรฎรซรฅรฒร รฌ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รข รฏรฅรฐรฅรญร รฑรฅรซรฅรญรญรปรต รจรซรจ รจรงรฎรซรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรปรต รฐร รฉรฎรญร รต, รฃรครฅ รฑร รฌรฎรซรฅรฒร รฌ รญรฅรครฎรฑรฒร รฅรฒ รฌรฅรฑรฒร รครซรฟ รขรงรซรฅรฒร รจรซรจ รฏรฎรฑร รครชรจ. รรฎรงรฌรฎรฆรญรฎรฑรฒรผ รฏร รฐรจรฒรผ รฝรดรดรฅรชรฒรจรขรญรฎ รข รฒรฅรทรฅรญรจรฅ รครซรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรปรต รฏรฅรฐรจรฎรครฎรข รขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรจ รฏรฎรงรขรฎรซรฟรฅรฒ รขรฅรฐรฒรฎรซรฅรฒรณ รณรฑรฏรฅรธรญรฎ รขรปรฏรฎรซรญรฟรฒรผ รงร รคร รทรจ รฑร รฌรฎรซรฅรฒรฎรข รจ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รขรจรครฎรข รซรฅรฒร รฒรฅรซรผรญรปรต ร รฏรฏร รฐร รฒรฎรข, รญรฅ รฑรฏรฎรฑรฎรกรญรปรต รช รจรต รจรฑรฏรฎรซรญรฅรญรจรพ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Helium-3[COLOR_REVERT] is a light, non-radioactive isotope of helium with two protons and one neutron discovered in 1939. Other than protium (ordinary hydrogen), Helium-3 is the only stable isotope of any element with more protons than neutrons. Much speculation has been made over the possibility of helium-3 as a future energy source: the fusion of helium-3 atoms releases large amounts of energy without causing the surrounding material to become radioactive, although the temperatures required are much higher than in traditional fusion reactions. The abundance of Helium-3 is greater on the Moon and, on a larger scale, on Jupiter and Saturn.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]elio-3[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un isotopo leggero e non radioattivo di elio con due protoni e un neutrone scoperto nel 1939. Oltre al protio (idrogeno ordinario), l'elio-3 รจ l'unico isotopo stabile di qualsiasi elemento con piรน protoni dei neutroni. Si รจ discusso molto sul rendere l'elio-3 come una futura fonte di energia: la fusione degli atomi di elio-3 libera grandi quantitร di energia senza far diventare radioattivo il materiale circostante, sebbene le temperature richieste siano molto piรน elevate rispetto alla fusione tradizionale. L'abbondanza di elio-3 รจ maggiore sulla Luna e, su scala piรน ampia, su Giove e Saturno.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hel-3[COLOR_REVERT], to lekki, nieradioaktywny izotop helu z dwoma protonami i jednym neutronem, odkryty w 1939 roku. Oprócz wodoru hel-3 jest jedynym stabilnym izotopem dowolnego pierwiastka maj¹cym wiêcej protonów ni¿ neutronów. Isniteje wiele spekulacji na temat mo¿liwoœci wykorzystania helu-3 jako przysz³ego Ÿród³a energii; fuzja atomów helu-3 uwalnia du¿e iloœci energii, nie powoduj¹c przy tym powstawania niepo¿¹danych odpadów promieniotwórczych, chocia¿ wymagane temperatury s¹ znacznie wy¿sze ni¿ w przypadku tradycyjnej fuzji. Na Ziemi wystêpuje jedynie œladowo, natomiast znaczne iloœci mog¹ wystêpowaæ w gruncie ksiê¿ycowym, w zwi¹zku z czym rozwa¿ane s¹ projekty jego wydobycia i transportu z Ksiê¿yca na Ziemiê.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตะปะธะน-3 - ะปะตะณะบะธะน, ะฝะต ัะฐะดะธะพะฐะบัะธะฒะฝัะน ะธะทะพัะพะฟ ะณะตะปะธั ั ะดะฒัะผั ะฟัะพัะพะฝะฐะผะธ ะธ ะพะดะฝะธะผ ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะพะผ, ะพัะบััััะน ะฒ 1939 ะณะพะดั. ะะพะผะธะผะพ ะฟัะพัะธั (ะพะฑััะฝะพะณะพ ะฒะพะดะพัะพะดะฐ), ะณะตะปะธะน-3 ัะฒะปัะตััั ะตะดะธะฝััะฒะตะฝะฝัะผ ััะฐะฑะธะปัะฝัะผ ะธะทะพัะพะฟะพะผ ะปัะฑะพะณะพ ัะปะตะผะตะฝัะฐ, ะธะผะตััะตะณะพ ะฑะพะปะตัะต ะฟัะพัะพะฝะพะฒ, ัะตะผ ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะพะฒ. ะัะปะพ ัะดะตะปะฐะฝะพ ะผะฝะพะณะพ ะฟัะตะดะฟะพะปะพะถะตะฝะธะน ะพ ะฒะพะทะผะพะถะฝะพััะธ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะฝะธั ะณะตะปะธั-3 ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ะธััะพัะฝะธะบะฐ ัะฝะตัะณะธะธ ะฒ ะฑัะดััะตะผ: ัะธะฝัะตะท ะฐัะพะผะพะฒ ะณะตะปะธั-3 ะฒััะฒะพะฑะพะถะดะฐะตั ะฑะพะปััะพะต ะบะพะปะธัะตััะฒะพ ัะฝะตัะณะธะธ, ะฝะต ะฒัะทัะฒะฐั ะฟัะตะฒัะฐัะตะฝะธั ะพะบััะถะฐััะตะณะพ ะผะฐัะตัะธะฐะปะฐ ะฒ ัะฐะดะธะพะฐะบัะธะฒะฝัะน, ั ะพัั ััะตะฑัะตะผัะต ัะตะผะฟะตัะฐัััั ะฝะฐะผะฝะพะณะพ ะฒััะต, ัะตะผ ะฟัะธ ััะฐะดะธัะธะพะฝะฝะพะน ัะตะฐะบัะธะธ ัะธะฝัะตะทะฐ. ะะพะณะฐััั ะผะตััะพัะพะถะดะตะฝะธะน ะณะตะปะธั-3 ะพัะพะฑะตะฝะฝะพ ะผะฝะพะณะพ ะฝะฐ ะัะฝะต ะธ, ะฒ ะฑะพะปััะตะผ ะผะฐัััะฐะฑะต, ะฝะฐ ะฎะฟะธัะตัะต ะธ ะกะฐัััะฝะต.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hemp fiber[COLOR_REVERT] has been extensively used throughout history for items ranging from rope to fabrics. Pure hemp has a texture similar to linen and, because of its versatility, today it is used in a number of consumer goods, including clothing, shoes, accessories, dog collars, and home wares.
TEXT.French: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fibre de chanvre[COLOR_REVERT] a รฉtรฉ largement utilisรฉe tout au long de l'histoire pour des articles allant de la corde aux tissus. Le chanvre pur a une texture similaire au lin et, en raison de sa polyvalence, il est aujourd'hui utilisรฉ dans un certain nombre de biens de consommation, y compris les vรชtements, les chaussures, les accessoires, les colliers pour chiens et les articles mรฉnagers.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]fibra di canapa[COLOR_REVERT] รจ stata ampiamente utilizzata nel corso della storia per oggetti che vanno dalla corda ai tessuti. La canapa pura ha una consistenza simile al lino e, per la sua versatilitร d'uso, viene utilizzata in numerosi beni di consumo, tra cui abbigliamento, scarpe, accessori, collari per cani e articoli per la casa.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]W³ókno konopne[COLOR_REVERT] jest u¿ywane od wieków do produkcji tekstyliów, ubrañ, przêdzy, lin, sznurów, materia³ów budowlanych oraz papieru. Charakterystyczne cechy tego surowca to wytrzyma³oœæ i wielofunkcyjnoœæ.
TEXT.Russian: รรฎรญรฎรฏรซรฟรญรฎรฅ รขรฎรซรฎรชรญรฎ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รซรฎรฑรผ รญร รฏรฐรฎรฒรฟรฆรฅรญรจรจ รขรฑรฅรฉ รจรฑรฒรฎรฐรจรจ. รรญรฎรฃรจรฅ รจรงรครฅรซรจรฟ, รญร รทรจรญร รฟ รฎรฒ รขรฅรฐรฅรขรฎรช, รฒรชร รญรฅรฉ, รฏรฐรฎรฌรปรธรซรฅรญรญรปรต รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรฎรข, รกรปรซรจ รจรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญรป รจรง รชรฎรญรฎรฏรซรฟรญรฎรฃรฎ รขรฎรซรฎรชรญร . รรฎรญรฎรฏรซรฟ รทร รฑรฒรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฐรจ รจรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญรจรจ รฏร รฐรณรฑรจรญรป รจ รฏรจรฑรทรฅรฉ รกรณรฌร รฃรจ. ร รญร รฑรฒรฎรฟรนรฅรฅรข รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รชรฎรญรฎรฏรซรฟรญรปรฅ รขรฎรซรฎรชรญร รฌรฎรฃรณรฒ รกรปรฒรผ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรป รข รจรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญรจรจ รฎรครฅรฆรครป. รรฅรชรฑรฒรณรฐร รทรจรฑรฒรฎรฉ รชรฎรญรฎรฏรซรจ รฏรฎรตรฎรฆร รญร รซรฅรญ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hemp[COLOR_REVERT] is a phenotype of the Cannabis sativa plant species. It is grown for industrial uses of its derived products, as well as for various phytochemicals other than THC. It is one of the fastest growing plants and was one of the first plants to be spun into usable fiber 10,000 years ago. It can be refined into a variety of commercial items, including paper, textiles, clothing, biodegradable plastics, paint, insulation, biofuel, food, and animal feed.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]chanvre[COLOR_REVERT] est un phรฉnotype de l'espรจce vรฉgรฉtale Cannabis sativa. Il est cultivรฉ pour les usages industriels de ses produits dรฉrivรฉs, ainsi que pour divers phytochimiques autres que le THC. C'est l'une des plantes ร croissance la plus rapide et a รฉtรฉ l'une des premiรจres plantes ร รชtre filรฉe en fibre utilisable il y a 10 000 ans. Il peut รชtre raffinรฉ en une variรฉtรฉ d'articles commerciaux, y compris le papier, les textiles, les vรชtements, les plastiques biodรฉgradables, la peinture, l'isolation, les biocarburants, la nourriture et l'alimentation animale.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hanf[COLOR_REVERT] (Cannabis sativa L.) ist eine der รคltesten Kulturpflanzen der Welt. Markant fรผr die Pflanze sind ihre handfรถrmigen Blรคtter mit den gesรคgten Rรคndern.[NEWLINE]In China wurde er schon vor mindestens 10000 Jahren genutzt. Die nahrhaften, wohlschmeckenden Samen wurden gern verzehrt, aber auch die Stรคngel mit ihren besonders langen und nahezu unverwรผstlichen Fasern wusste man schon frรผh zu schรคtzen. Auรerdem wurde Hanf als Heilmittel gegen Malaria, Rheuma und viele andere Krankheiten eingesetzt. รber Indien und die antiken Hochkulturen im heutigen Irak verbreitete sich diese universelle Pflanze um die ganze Welt. Kleidung aus Hanfgewebe war schon bei den alten Griechen und รgyptern beliebt. Die mittelalterlichen Langbรถgen wรคren ohne robuste und widerstandsfรคhige Hanffasern nicht mรถglich gewesen. Ab dem 13. Jahrhundert wurde Hanf der wichtigste Rohstoff fรผr die Papierherstellung. Hanfseile und Segeltuch aus Hanf waren in der Schifffahrt wichtig, da die Faser sehr widerstandsfรคhig gegenรผber Salzwasser ist und weniger Wasser aufnimmt als beispielsweise Baumwolle.[NEWLINE]Bestimmte Pflanzenteile der weiblichen Hanfpflanze (hauptsรคchlich die Blรผten und blรผtennahen Blรคtter) kรถnnen eine berauschende Wirkung hervorrufen. Man nennt diese umgangssprachlich Marihuana (oder auch Gras oder Weed). Haschisch hingegen ist das daraus gewonnene und gepresste Harz. Entscheidend fรผr die berauschende Wirkung ist der Wirkstoff Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), der aber nicht in allen Hanfarten in entsprechender Menge enthalten ist. Besitz und Weitergabe von Cannabis oder Cannabisprodukten ist in den meisten Lรคndern verboten.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]canapa[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un fenotipo delle specie di piante di Cannabis sativa. Viene coltivata per gli usi industriali dei suoi prodotti derivati, nonchรฉ per vari prodotti fitochimici diversi dal THC. ร una delle piante a piรน rapida crescita ed รจ stata una delle prime piante a essere trasformata in fibre utilizzabili 10.000 anni fa. Puรฒ essere raffinata in una serie di articoli commerciali, tra cui carta, tessuti, abbigliamento, plastica biodegradabile, vernici, isolanti, biocarburanti, alimenti per umani e per animali.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Konopie (konopie siewne)[COLOR_REVERT], to gatunek roœliny z rodziny konopiowatych. Wystêpuje w górach A³taj, Tienszan oraz na Zakaukaziu i w Afganistanie. Jako roœlina uprawna rozpowszechniona w ró¿nych czêœciach œwiata. Roœliny wykorzystywane s¹ jako surowiec do pozyskiwania w³ókien. Maj¹ te¿ szerokie dzia³anie farmakologiczne. W przemyœle spo¿ywczym wykorzystywany jest olej konopny. Owoce bywaj¹ stosowane jako karma dla ptaków. Nasiona s¹ Ÿród³em pe³nowartoœciowych bia³ek.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Henna[COLOR_REVERT] is a dye prepared from the plant Lawsonia inermis, also known as hina or henna tree. Henna can also refer to the temporary body art resulting from the staining of the skin from the dyes. Henna has been used since antiquity to dye skin, hair and fingernails, as well as fabrics including silk, wool and leather. Historically, henna was used in the Arabian Peninsula, Indian subcontinent, Near and Middle East, Carthage, other parts of North Africa and the Horn of Africa. The name is used in other skin and hair dyes, such as black henna and neutral henna, neither of which is derived from the henna plant.
TEXT.German: Der [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hennastrauch[COLOR_REVERT] (Lawsonia inermis), der bis zu 8 m hoch wachsen kann, gehรถrt zur Familie der Weiderichgewรคchse. Er bildet silbrig-grรผne, ledrige Laubblรคtter, die er im Winter vollstรคndig abwirft, und duftende, gelblich-weiรe, in Rispenform angeordnete Blรผten aus. Aus den Blรคttern wird Hennapulver gewonnen, das hauptsรคchlich zur Fรคrbung der Haare dient. Es ist aber auch mรถglich, die Haut oder Stoffe wie Wolle oder Seide damit einzufรคrben.[NEWLINE]Schon den รgyptern und Griechen war der Hennastrauch bekannt. Die Griechen und Rรถmer nutzten die Blรผten fรผr ein wohlriechendes รl. Die alten รgypter nutzten Henna bereits fรผr Bemalungen zu rituellen, aber auch kosmetischen Zwecken, was anhand von Mumienfunden belegt werden konnte. In Indien, dem Vorderen Orient und Nordafrika wird Henna auch heute noch bei Festlichkeiten fรผr Kรถrperverzierungen an Hรคnden und Fรผรen benutzt. Durch Stars wie Madonna wurden in den spรคten 1990ern Henna-Tattoos auch in der westlichen Welt populรคr.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hennรจ[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un colorante derivato dalla pianta Lawsonia inermis, nota anche come hina o albero di hennรจ. L'hennรจ puรฒ anche riferirsi alla tradizione di dipingere parti del corpo con il colorante. L'hennรจ รจ stato utilizzato fin dall'antichitร per tingere la pelle, i capelli e le unghie, nonchรฉ tessuti come seta, lana e pelle. Storicamente, l'hennรจ veniva usato nella Penisola arabica, nel Subcontinente indiano, nel Vicino e Medio Oriente, a Cartagine, in altre parti del Nord Africa e nel Corno d'Africa. Il nome รจ usato in altre tinture per pelle e capelli, come l'hennรจ nero e l'hennรจ neutro, che non derivano dalla pianta di hennรจ.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Henna[COLOR_REVERT], to barwnik roœlinny produkowany z liœci i pêdów roœliny zwanej lawsoni¹ bezbronn¹. Henna s³u¿y do malowania skóry od bardzo dawna. TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hide[COLOR_REVERT] is an animal skin treated for human use. Although they are usually from large animals such as cows or buffalos, it can also refer to skins of smaller animals, such as goats, sheeps, deers, pigs, fishes, alligators, snakes, etc. All are used for shoes, clothes, leather bags, belts and other fashion accessories. Leather is also used in cars, upholstery, interior decorating, horse tack and harnesses. Skins are sometimes still gathered from hunting and processed at a domestic or artisanal level, but most leather making is now industrialized and large-scale.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]pelle trattata[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una pelle di animale trattata per uso umano. Sebbene di solito provengano da grandi animali come mucche o bufali, puรฒ anche riferirsi a pelli di animali piรน piccoli, come capre, pecore, cervi, maiali, pesci, alligatori, serpenti, ecc. Tutti questi tipi di pelle sono usati per produrre scarpe, vestiti, borse, cinture e altri accessori, ma anche per tappezzerie e decorazioni d'interni o di automobili, finimenti per cavalli e imbracature. Talvolta, alcune pelli possono provenire da animali cacciati e lavorate a livello domestico o artigianale, ma la maggior parte della produzione di pelletteria รจ ora industrializzata e su larga scala.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Skóra[COLOR_REVERT] to skóra zwierzêca przeznaczona do u¿ytku przez ludzi. Chocia¿ zwykle pochodzi od du¿ych zwierz¹t, takich jak krowy czy bawo³y, mo¿e równie¿ odnosiæ siê do skór mniejszych zwierz¹t, takich jak kozy, owce, jelenie, œwinie, ryby, aligatory, wê¿e itp. Ze skór wytwarza siê obuwie, odzie¿, skórzane torby, paski i inne modne dodatki. Skórê wykorzystuje siê tak¿e do produkcji samochodów, tapicerki i dekoracji wnêtrz. TEXT.English: The [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hieracosphinx[COLOR_REVERT] is a mythical beast found in Egyptian sculpture and European heraldry, with the head of a hawk and the body of a lion. Sometimes it is depicted with a headdress. The combination of the two animals makes the hieracosphinx a symbol of royalty.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hiรฉracosphinx[COLOR_REVERT] est une crรฉature mythique que l'on trouve dans la sculpture รฉgyptienne et l'hรฉraldique europรฉenne, avec une tรชte de faucon et un corps de lion. Il est parfois reprรฉsentรฉ avec une coiffe. La combinaison des deux animaux fait du hiรฉracosphinx un symbole de royautรฉ.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ieracosfinge[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una bestia mitica, spesso presente nella scultura egizia e nell'araldica europea, con la testa di un falco e il corpo di un leone. A volte รจ raffigurata con un copricapo. La combinazione dei due animali rende la ieracosfinge un simbolo di regalitร .
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hierakosfinks[COLOR_REVERT], to mityczna bestia wystêpuj¹ca w egipskiej rzeŸbie i europejskiej heraldyce, z g³ow¹ jastrzêbia i cia³em lwa. Czasami jest przedstawiany z nakryciem g³owy. Po³¹czenie tych dwóch zwierz¹t sprawia, ¿e hierakosfinks jest symbolem rodziny królewskiej.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hit movie[COLOR_REVERT], also called blockbuster, is a feature film that is highly popular and financially successful. The term has also come to refer to any large-budget production intended for "blockbuster" status, aimed at mass markets with associated merchandising, sometimes on a scale that meant the financial fortunes of a film studio or a distributor could depend on it. The first use of the term was in 1943, but only by mid-1970s the term reached widespread popularity.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]film di successo[COLOR_REVERT], chiamato anche blockbuster, รจ un film molto popolare e di grande successo al botteghino. Il termine comprende, piรน in generale, qualsiasi produzione cinematografica con un grande budget rivolta al grande pubblico, con un merchandising associato, talvolta prodotto su una scala talmente ampia da poter determinare la sorte di uno studio cinematografico o di un distributore. Il primo uso del termine fu nel 1943, ma solo a metร degli anni settanta il termine raggiunse una popolaritร diffusa.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Musical theatre[COLOR_REVERT] is a form of theatrical performance that combines songs, spoken dialogue, acting and dance. The story and emotional content of a musical โ humor, pathos, love, anger โ are communicated through words, music, movement and technical aspects of the entertainment as an integrated whole. Since the early 20th century, musical theatre stage works have generally been called, simply, "musicals". Although music has been a part of dramatic presentations since ancient times, modern Western musical theatre emerged during 19th century.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]teatro musicale[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una forma di spettacolo teatrale che combina canzoni, dialoghi parlati, recitazione e danza. La storia e il contenuto emotivo di un musical - umorismo, pathos, amore, rabbia - sono comunicati attraverso parole, musica, movimento e aspetti tecnici dell'intrattenimento come un tutt'uno integrato. Dall'inizio del XX secolo, le opere teatrali di teatro musicale sono state generalmente chiamate semplicemente "musical". Sebbene la musica abbia fatto parte di rappresentazioni drammatiche fin dai tempi antichi, il canone dei moderni musical occidentali ha iniziato a definirsi nel corso del XIX secolo.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hit single[COLOR_REVERT], also known as hit song, hit record, or simply hit, is a recorded song or instrumental that becomes broadly popular or well-known. Although hit song means any widely played or big-selling song, the specific term usually refers to a single that has appeared in an official music chart, through repeated radio airplay or significant commercial sales.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]tube[COLOR_REVERT], รฉgalement connu sous le nom de chanson ร succรจs, disque ร succรจs, ou simplement hit, est une chanson enregistrรฉe ou un instrumental qui devient largement populaire ou connu. Bien que le terme chanson ร succรจs dรฉsigne toute chanson largement diffusรฉe ou vendue, le terme spรฉcifique fait gรฉnรฉralement rรฉfรฉrence ร un single qui est apparu dans un classement musical officiel, grรขce ร une diffusion radio rรฉpรฉtรฉe ou ร des ventes commerciales importantes.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]canzone di successo[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un brano registrato o strumentale che diventa ampiamente popolare o noto. Sebbene il termine "canzone di successo" sia comunemente usato per indicare qualsiasi canzone ampiamente suonata o conosciuta, il termine specifico di solito si riferisce a un singolo che รจ apparso in una classifica musicale ufficiale, attraverso ripetute trasmissioni radiofoniche o vendite commerciali significative.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hitowy singiel[COLOR_REVERT], znany równie¿ jako hit, to nagrana piosenka lub utwór instrumentalny, który staje siê powszechnie popularny lub dobrze znany. Chocia¿ hit oznacza dowoln¹ powszechnie odtwarzan¹ lub dobrze sprzedaj¹c¹ siê piosenkê, konkretny termin zwykle odnosi siê do singla, który pojawi³ siê na oficjalnych listach przebojów dziêki wielokrotnym emisjom radiowym lub znacz¹cym sprzeda¿om komercyjnym.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]holographic projector[COLOR_REVERT] is a type of projector that utilizes light diffraction to create a virtual three-dimensional image of an object. Holographic projectors are distinguished from other forms of 3D imaging in that they do not require the aid of any special glasses or external equipment for a viewer to see the image.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]projecteur holographique[COLOR_REVERT] est un type de projecteur qui utilise la diffraction de la lumiรจre pour crรฉer une image virtuelle tridimensionnelle d'un objet. Les projecteurs holographiques se distinguent des autres formes d'imagerie 3D en ce qu'ils ne nรฉcessitent pas l'aide de lunettes spรฉciales ou d'รฉquipement externe pour qu'un spectateur puisse voir l'image.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]proiettore olografico[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un tipo di proiettore che utilizza la diffrazione della luce per creare un'immagine tridimensionale virtuale di un oggetto. I proiettori olografici si distinguono da altre forme di apparecchiature tridimensionali perchรฉ non richiedono l'ausilio di occhiali speciali o apparecchiature esterne affinchรฉ uno spettatore possa vedere l'immagine.

Projektory holograficzne ró¿ni¹ siê od innych form obrazowania 3D tym, ¿e nie wymagaj¹ pomocy ¿adnych specjalnych okularów ani sprzêtu zewnêtrznego, aby widz móg³ zobaczyæ obraz.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Honey[COLOR_REVERT] is a sweet, viscous food substance, made by bees from floral nectar or from secretions of other insects (such as honeydew), by regurgitation, enzymatic activity, and water evaporation. Bees store honey in wax structures called honeycombs. The variety of honey produced by honey bees is the best-known, due to its worldwide commercial production and human consumption. Honey is collected from wild bee colonies, or from hives of domesticated bees, a practice known as beekeeping or apiculture. Honey use and production have a long and varied history as an ancient activity. Several cave paintings in Spain depict humans foraging for honey at least 8,000 years ago.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]miele[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una sostanza alimentare dolce e viscosa, prodotta dalle api dal nettare floreale o dalle secrezioni di altri insetti (come la melata), tramite rigurgito, attivitร enzimatica ed evaporazione dell'acqua. Le api immagazzinano il miele in strutture di cera chiamate favi. La varietร di miele prodotta dalle api รจ la piรน nota, grazie alla sua produzione commerciale mondiale e al consumo umano. Il miele viene raccolto da colonie di api selvatiche o da alveari di api domestiche - pratica nota come apicoltura. L'uso e la produzione del miele hanno una storia lunga e variegata come attivitร antica. Diverse pitture rupestri in Spagna raffigurano umani che cercano miele risalenti ad almeno 8.000 anni fa.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตะดะพะฝะพัะฝัะต ะฟัะตะปั ะฟัะตะฒัะฐัะฐัั ะฝะตะบัะฐั ะฒ ะผะตะด ะฒ ะฟัะพัะตััะต ัััะณะธะฒะฐะฝะธั ะธ ั ัะฐะฝัั ะตะณะพ ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะณะพ ะธััะพัะฝะธะบะฐ ะฟะธัะธ ะฒ ะฒะพัะบะพะฒัั ัะพัะฐั ะฒะฝัััะธ ัะปัั. ะัะฐะบัะธะบะฐ ะฟัะตะปะพะฒะพะดััะฒะฐ ัะฟะพัะพะฑััะฒัะตั ะฟะตัะตะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดััะฒั ะผะตะดะฐ, ััะพะฑั ะธะทะปะธัะบะธ ะผะพะถะฝะพ ะฑัะปะพ ะทะฐะฑัะฐัั ะธะท ะบะพะปะพะฝะธะธ.[PARAGRAPH:2]ะะตะด - ัะปะฐะดะบะฐั ะฟะธัะฐ, ะบะพัะพััั ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดัั ะผะตะดะพะฝะพัะฝัะต ะฟัะตะปั (ะธ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ะดััะณะธะต ะฒะธะดั) ะธ ะฟะพะปััะฐัั ะธะท ะฝะตะบัะฐัะฐ ัะฒะตัะพะฒ. ะกะพะณะปะฐัะฝะพ ะะฐัะธะพะฝะฐะปัะฝะพะผั ัะพะฒะตัั ะกะจะ ะฟะพ ะผะตะดั ะธ ัะฐะทะปะธัะฝัะผ ะผะตะถะดัะฝะฐัะพะดะฝัะผ ะฝะพัะผะฐะผ ะฒ ะพะฑะปะฐััะธ ะฟะธัะตะฒัั ะฟัะพะดัะบัะพะฒ, ยซะผะตะด - ััะพ ัะธัััะน ะฟัะพะดัะบั, ะบะพัะพััะน ะฝะต ะฟะพะทะฒะพะปัะตั ะดะพะฑะฐะฒะปััั ะบะฐะบะธะต-ะปะธะฑะพ ะดััะณะธะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ, ะฒะบะปััะฐั, ะฟะพะผะธะผะพ ะฟัะพัะตะณะพ, ะฒะพะดั ะธะปะธ ะดััะณะธะต ะฟะพะดัะปะฐััะธัะตะปะธยป. ะะตะด ะฟะพะปััะฐะตั ัะฒะพั ัะปะฐะดะพััั ะพั ะผะพะฝะพัะฐั ะฐัะธะดะพะฒ ัััะบัะพะทั ะธ ะณะปัะบะพะทั ะธ ะธะผะตะตั ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ ัะฐะบัั ะถะต ะพัะฝะพัะธัะตะปัะฝัั ัะปะฐะดะพััั, ะบะฐะบ ะธ ัะฐั ะฐั-ะฟะตัะพะบ (97% ัะปะฐะดะพััะธ ัะฐั ะฐัะพะทั, ะดะธัะฐั ะฐัะธะดะฐ). ะะตะด ะพะฑะปะฐะดะฐะตั ะฟัะธะฒะปะตะบะฐัะตะปัะฝัะผะธ ั ะธะผะธัะตัะบะธะผะธ ัะฒะพะนััะฒะฐะผะธ ะดะปั ะฒัะฟะตัะบะธ ะธ ั ะฐัะฐะบัะตัะฝัะผ ะฐัะพะผะฐัะพะผ, ะธะท-ะทะฐ ัะตะณะพ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ะปัะดะธ ะฟัะตะดะฟะพัะธัะฐัั ะตะณะพ ัะฐั ะฐัั ะธ ะดััะณะธะผ ะฟะพะดัะปะฐััะธัะตะปัะผ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Miód[COLOR_REVERT] jest s³odk¹, lepk¹ substancj¹ spo¿ywcz¹ wytwarzan¹ przez pszczo³y z nektaru kwiatowego lub wydzielin innych owadów (takich jak spadŸ) w wyniku regurgitacji, aktywnoœci enzymatycznej i odparowania wody. Pszczo³y przechowuj¹ miód w strukturach woskowych zwanych plastrami miodu. Odmiana miodu produkowanego przez pszczo³y miodne jest najbardziej znana ze wzglêdu na ogólnoœwiatow¹ produkcjê komercyjn¹ i spo¿ycie przez ludzi. Miód zbiera siê z koloni dzikich pszczó³ lub z uli pszczó³ udomowionych, co jest praktyk¹ znan¹ jako pszczelarstwo. Wykorzystanie i produkcja miodu maj¹ d³ug¹ historiê siêgaj¹c¹ a¿ do staro¿ytnoœci. Kilka malowide³ jaskiniowych w Hiszpanii przedstawia ludzi poszukuj¹cych miodu co najmniej 8 000 lat temu.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]horse[COLOR_REVERT] is an odd-toed ungulate mammal, belonging to the taxonomic family Equidae. Horses are adapted to run, allowing them to quickly escape predators, possessing an excellent sense of balance and a strong fight-or-flight response. Humans began domesticating horses around 4000 BC, and their domestication is believed to have been widespread by 3000 BC, being historically used in warfare. With time, horses were also employed in working activities such as police work, agriculture, entertainment, sporting, and therapy. Many products are derived from horses, including meat, milk, hide, hair, bone, and pharmaceuticals extracted from the urine of pregnant mares.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pferde[COLOR_REVERT] gibt es seit mehr als 50 Millionen Jahren. Sie sind die wohl nรผtzlichsten domestizierten Tiere in der Geschichte der Menschheit. Obwohl sich ihr รuรeres im Laufe der Jahre kaum verรคndert hat, haben die Pferde eine bedeutende Evolution durchlaufen. Immerhin waren ihre frรผhzeitlichen Artgenossen nur etwa 30 cm groร. Ursprรผnglich jagten die Menschen Pferde zu Nahrungszwecken. Erst um 2500 vor Christus tauchten die ersten domestizierten Zรผchtungen in Asien auf. Diese Idee wurde so gut angenommen, dass sie sich schnell in andere Lรคnder ausbreitete. Ihr Beitrag zur Ausbreitung der Vรถlker war enorm, da erst die Pferde die gewaltigen Feldzรผge Dschingis Khans und Alexanders des Groรen ermรถglichten. Im 16. Jahrhundert brachten die Spanier die heutigen Pferderassen nach Nordamerika. Oft lieรen sie dort ihre Herden zurรผck, um auf ihren Schiffen mehr Platz fรผr Gold und andere erbeutete Schรคtze zu haben. Obwohl die Bedeutung des Pferdes seit der Erfindung des Verbrennungsmotors deutlich rรผcklรคufig ist, hat es in der Landwirtschaft und im Sport bis heute einen festen Platz.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cavallo[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un mammifero ungulato, appartenente alla famiglia tassonomica degli equidi. I cavalli sono naturalmente portati alla corsa, cosa che gli permette di sfuggire rapidamente ai predatori, e possiedono un eccellente senso di equilibrio e una forte attitudine alla lotta e alla fuga. Gli umani iniziarono ad addomesticare i cavalli intorno al 4000 a.C. e si ritiene che la loro domesticazione fosse diffusa nel 3000 a.C., venendo storicamente impiegati anche in guerra. Con il tempo, i cavalli sono stati impiegati dalle forze di polizia e anche in attivitร lavorative come l'agricoltura, l'intrattenimento, lo sport e la terapia. I cavalli sono utilizzati anche per la carne, il latte, la pelle, i peli, le ossa e per prodotti farmaceutici estratti dall'urina delle cavalle in gravidanza.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Koñ[COLOR_REVERT], to ssak kopytny, nale¿¹cy do taksonomicznej rodziny koniowatych. Konie s¹ przystosowane do biegania, co pozwala im szybko uciekaæ przed drapie¿nikami, posiadaj¹ doskona³e poczucie równowagi i siln¹ reakcjê walki lub ucieczki. Ludzie zaczêli udomawiaæ konie oko³o 4000 roku p.n.e. i uwa¿a siê, ¿e ich udomowienie by³o powszechne ju¿ oko³o 3000 roku p.n.e. Historycznie konie wykorzystywano w dzia³aniach wojennych. TEXT.German: Luftkissenfahrzeuge
TEXT.Russian: รรณรคร รญร รข/รฏ

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hovercraft[COLOR_REVERT], also known as an air-cushion vehicle or ACV, is an amphibious craft capable of travelling over land, water, mud, ice, and other surfaces. Hovercraft use blowers to produce a large volume of air below the hull, or air cushion, that is slightly above atmospheric pressure. The pressure difference produces a lift, which causes the hull to float above the running surface. For stability reasons, the air is typically blown through slots or holes around the outside of a disk- or oval-shaped platform, giving most hovercraft a characteristic rounded-rectangle shape. Typically this cushion is contained within a flexible "skirt", which allows the vehicle to travel over small obstructions without damage.
TEXT.German: Ein Luftkissenfahrzeug ist ein Amphibienfahrzeug, das durch Luft getragen wird, die eine Art Kissen bildet. Es wird meist auf dem Wasser eingesetzt und dient dem Gรผter- und Personentransport, als Expeditionsfahrzeug oder als Hilfsfahrzeug bei Rettungsdiensten und Feuerwehren. Zum Fรผhren ist eine der Fahrzeuggrรถรe entsprechende Fahrerlaubnis fรผr ein Wasserfahrzeug notwendig.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hovercraft[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un velivolo anfibio in grado di viaggiare su terra, acqua, fango, ghiaccio e altre superfici. Un hovercraft utilizza i soffiatori per produrre un "cuscino d'aria" sotto lo scafo, di pressione leggermente superiore a quella atmosferica. La differenza di pressione produce un sollevamento, che fa galleggiare lo scafo sopra la superficie. Per motivi di stabilitร , l'aria viene generalmente soffiata attraverso fessure o fori praticate in una piattaforma ovale o discoidale, conferendo alla maggior parte degli hovercraft una caratteristica forma rettangolare. In genere, questo cuscino รจ contenuto in una "gonna" flessibile, che consente al veicolo di spostarsi su piccoli ostacoli senza danni.
TEXT.Russian: รรณรครญรฎ รญร รขรฎรงรครณรธรญรฎรฉ รฏรฎรครณรธรชรฅ รจรซรจ รรร - รฝรฒรฎ รชรฎรฐร รกรซรผ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฑรฏรฎรฑรฎรกรฅรญ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฌรฅรนร รฒรผรฑรฟ รฏรฎ รงรฅรฌรซรฅ, รขรฎรครฅ, รฃรฐรฟรงรจ รจรซรจ รซรผรครณ รจ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รฏรฎรขรฅรฐรตรญรฎรฑรฒรฟรต รฒร รชรฆรฅ รชร รช รจ รฎรฑรฒร รญร รขรซรจรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รญร รญรจรต. รรร รชร รช รฃรจรกรฐรจรครญรฎรฅ รฑรณรครญรฎ, รกรฎรซรผรธรฅ รณรฏรฐร รขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรจรซรฎรฒรฎรฌ, ร รญรฅ รชร รฏรจรฒร รญรฎรฌ, รชร รช รญร รฌรฎรฐรฑรชรจรต รฑรณรคร รต. รรญรจ รฐร รกรฎรฒร รพรฒ, รฑรฎรงรคร รขร รฟ รงรฎรญรณ รญรจรงรชรฎรฃรฎ รคร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ, รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรฃรฎ รฎรกรผรฅรฌร รขรฎรงรครณรตร รฌรฅรฆรครณ รชรฎรฐรฏรณรฑรฎรฌ รฑรฎรฑรณรคร รจ รฏรฎรครฑรฒรจรซร รพรนรฅรฉ รฏรฎรขรฅรฐรตรญรฎรฑรฒรผรพ. รรกรปรทรญรฎ รฝรฒร รฏรฎรครณรธรชร รฏรฐรจรขรฎรครจรฒรฑรฟ รข รฃรจรกรชรจรฉ "รฏรฎรครฎรซ". รรญรจ รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฏร รฐรฟรฒ รญร รขรปรฑรฎรฒร รต รฎรฒ 200 รฌรฌ (7.9 ร) รซรจรกรฎ 600 รฌรฌ (24 รครพรฉรฌร ) รขรปรธรฅ รซรพรกรฎรฉ รฏรฎรขรฅรฐรตรญรฎรฑรฒรจ รจ รครขรจรฃร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฑรฎ TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hydrogen[COLOR_REVERT] is a colorless, odorless, tasteless, non-toxic, nonmetallic, highly combustible gas, and it is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass. The most common isotope of hydrogen, protium, has one proton and no neutrons; since hydrogen readily forms covalent compounds with most nonmetallic elements, study of the energetics and bonding of the hydrogen atom has played a key role in the development of quantum mechanics. Industrial production is mainly from steam reforming natural gas, and less often from more energy-intensive methods such as the electrolysis of water.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]idrogeno[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un gas incolore, inodore, insapore, non tossico, non metallico, altamente combustibile ed รจ la sostanza chimica piรน abbondante nell'Universo, costituendo il 75% circa di tutta la massa barionica. L'isotopo piรน comune dell'idrogeno, il protio, ha un protone e nessun neutrone; poichรฉ l'idrogeno forma prontamente composti covalenti con la maggior parte degli elementi non metallici, lo studio dell'energia e dei legami degli atomi di idrogeno ha svolto un ruolo chiave nello sviluppo della meccanica quantistica. La produzione industriale proviene principalmente dai vapori di gas naturale e, meno spesso, da metodi a piรน alta intensitร energetica, come l'elettrolisi dell'acqua.
TEXT.Russian: ร รฑรฅรฐรฅรครจรญรฅ XXI รขรฅรชร รซรพรครจ รญร รณรทรจรซรจรฑรผ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรผ รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรพ รขรฎรครฎรฐรฎรคร . รรฎรขรปรฅ รครขรจรฃร รฒรฅรซรจ รกรปรฑรฒรฐรฎ รญร รธรซรจ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรญรฅรญรจรฅ รข ร รขรฒรฎรฌรฎรกรจรซรผรญรฎรฉ รฏรฐรฎรฌรปรธรซรฅรญรญรฎรฑรฒรจ. รรฎรฒรฟ รขรฎ รรฑรฅรซรฅรญรญรฎรฉ รฝรฒรฎรฒ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฉ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฅรครขร รซรจ รญรฅ รฑร รฌรปรฌ รฐร รฑรฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฐร รญรฅรญรญรปรฌ, รครฎรกรปรฒรผ รฅรฃรฎ รญรฅ รฒร รช-รฒรฎ รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฎ, รฏรฎรฝรฒรฎรฌรณ รชรฎรฑรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรจ รฝรฒรฎรฃรฎ รฐรฅรฑรณรฐรฑร รฎรฑรฎรกรฅรญรญรฎ รถรฅรญรญรป.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wodór[COLOR_REVERT], bezbarwny, bezwonny, pozbawiony smaku, nietoksyczny, niemetaliczny i wysoce palny gaz, bêd¹cy najobficiej wystêpuj¹c¹ substancj¹ chemiczn¹ we Wszechœwiecie, stanowi¹c¹ oko³o 75% ca³ej masy. Najpopularniejszy izotop wodoru, prot, ma jeden proton i nie ma neutronów; poniewa¿ wodór ³atwo tworzy zwi¹zki kowalencyjne z wiêkszoœci¹ pierwiastków niemetalicznych, badanie energetyki i wi¹zania atomu wodoru odegra³o kluczow¹ rolê w rozwoju mechaniki kwantowej. Produkcja przemys³owa odbywa siê g³ównie z gazu ziemnego poddanego reformingowi parowemu, rzadziej z bardziej energoch³onnych metod, takich jak elektroliza wody.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hydrothermal vent[COLOR_REVERT] is a fissure on the seafloor from which geothermally heated water issues. They are commonly found near volcanically or geologically active places, ocean basins, and hotspots. Under the sea, hydrothermal vents may form features called black smokers or white smokers. Relative to the majority of the deep sea, the areas around submarine hydrothermal vents are biologically more productive, often hosting complex communities of lifeforms, fueled by the chemicals dissolved in the vent fluids. Active hydrothermal vents are thought to exist on Europa and Enceladus, and it is speculated that ancient hydrothermal vents once existed on Mars.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]sorgente idrotermale[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una fessura sul fondo del mare da cui vengono emesse colonne d'acqua calda. Si trovano spesso in luoghi vulcanicamente o geologicamente attivi, bacini oceanici e punti caldi. Sotto il mare, le sorgenti possono formare delle fumarole di colore nero o bianco. Rispetto al resto del mare profondo, le aree intorno alle sorgenti sono biologicamente piรน produttive, ospitando spesso comunitร complesse di forme di vita, alimentate dalle sostanze chimiche disciolte nei fluidi di sfiato. Si pensa che esistano sorgenti idrotermali attive su Europa ed Encelado, e si ipotizza che in passato esistessero anche su Marte.

Pod powierzchni¹ morza kominy hydrotermalne mog¹ tworzyæ elementy zwane czarnymi lub bia³ymi palaczami. W porównaniu z wiêkszoœci¹ g³êbin morskich obszary wokó³ podwodnych kominów hydrotermalnych s¹ biologicznie bardziej produktywne i czêsto zamieszkuj¹ je z³o¿one zbiorowiska form ¿ycia, zasilane chemikaliami rozpuszczonymi w p³ynach wylotowych.
