รรจรฏรฎรฑรฏรฐรฅรจ

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]hypospray[COLOR_REVERT] is a jet injector that delivers medications intramuscularly, if applied at the top of the arm, or intravenously or intra-arterially, if applied on the neck. It can also be applied through clothing. The hypospray is extremely versatile as the medicine vials can be quickly swapped out from the bottom of the hypospray. As the hypospray is bloodless, it is not contaminated by use. This allows it to be used on many people until the supply of medicine runs out.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ipospray[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un iniettore a getto che eroga farmaci per via intramuscolare, se applicato nella parte superiore del braccio, oppure per via endovenosa o intra-arteriosa, se applicato sul collo. Puรฒ anche essere applicato attraverso l'abbigliamento. L'ipospray รจ estremamente versatile, poichรฉ le fiale del medicinale possono essere rapidamente sostituite dal fondo. Poichรฉ privo di un ago, non viene contaminato dall'uso. Ciรฒ consente di utilizzarlo su molte persone fino all'esaurimento della fornitura di medicinali.
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรครจรถรจรญรฑรชรจรฉ รจรญรฑรฒรฐรณรฌรฅรญรฒ, รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฌรปรฉ รครซรฟ รขรขรฅรครฅรญรจรฟ รซรฅรชร รฐรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรปรต รขรฅรนรฅรฑรฒรข รฏรฎรครชรฎรฆรญรฎ, รขรญรณรฒรฐรจรฌรปรธรฅรทรญรฎ, รขรญรณรฒรฐรจรขรฅรญรญรฎ, รข รณรฑรซรฎรขรจรฟรต รฒรนร รฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รชรฎรญรฒรฐรฎรซรฟ รงร รครฎรงรจรฐรฎรขรชรฎรฉ รฏรฐรฅรฏร รฐร รฒรฎรข. รรฐรฅรฏร รฐร รฒรป รขรขรฎรครฟรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฎรฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฎรฌ รฒรฎรทรฅรทรญรฎรฃรฎ, รญร รตรฎรครฟรนรฅรฃรฎรฑรฟ รฏรฎรค รขรปรฑรฎรชรจรฌ รคร รขรซรฅรญรจรฅรฌ, รฌร รซรฎรฎรกรบรฅรฌรญรฎรฃรฎ, รฌรจรชรฐรฎรฑรชรฎรฏรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฎรฒรฎรชร รขรฎรงรครณรธรญรฎรฉ รฑรณรฑรฏรฅรญรงรจรจ. รรฒรฎ รฏรฎรงรขรฎรซรฟรฅรฒ รฏรฐรฅรฏร รฐร รฒร รฌ รฏรฐรฎรญรจรชร รฒรผ รทรฅรฐรฅรง รชรฎรฆรณ รกรฅรง รฅรฅ รฌรฅรตร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฎรขรฐรฅรฆรครฅรญรจรฟ. รรฅรซรจรทรจรญร รฏรซรฎรนร รครจ รขรฎรงรครฅรฉรฑรฒรขรจรฟ รจ รฃรซรณรกรจรญร รฏรฐรฎรญรจรชรญรฎรขรฅรญรจรฟ รฌรฎรฃรณรฒ รขร รฐรผรจรฐรฎรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Hiposprej[COLOR_REVERT], to wtryskiwacz strumieniowy, który podaje leki domiêœniowo, jeœli zostanie zastosowany w górnej czêœci ramienia, lub do¿ylnie lub dotêtniczo, jeœli zostanie zastosowany na szyi. TEXT.Russian: รรฑรซร รญรครถรป
รรฎรฐรฎรฆรฅรญรฎรฅ

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ice cream[COLOR_REVERT] is a sweetened, smooth, semi-solid food, typically eaten as a snack or dessert. It may be made from dairy milk or cream and is flavoured with a sweetener (either sugar or an alternative) and any spice, such as cocoa or vanilla. Colourings are usually added, in addition to stabilizers. The mixture is stirred to incorporate air spaces and cooled below the freezing point of water to prevent detectable ice crystals from forming. Ice cream may be served in dishes, for eating with a spoon, or licked from edible cones. Ice cream may be served with other desserts, such as apple pie, or as an ingredient in ice cream floats, sundaes, milkshakes, ice cream cakes and even baked items.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]gelato[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un alimento zuccherato semi-solido, tipicamente consumato come spuntino o dessert. Puรฒ essere fatto con latte o crema di latte ed รจ aromatizzato con un dolcificante (come lo zucchero o simili) e qualsiasi spezia, come il cacao o la vaniglia. Di solito vengono aggiunti coloranti, oltre agli stabilizzanti. La miscela viene agitata per incorporare aria e raffreddata sotto la temperatura di congelamento dell'acqua per impedire la formazione di cristalli di ghiaccio rilevabili. Il gelato puรฒ essere servito in un piatto, mangiato con un cucchiaio o leccato su un cono commestibile. Il gelato puรฒ essere servito con altri dessert, come la torta di mele, o come ingrediente in gelati, coppe, frappรจ, torte gelato e persino prodotti da forno.
TEXT.Russian: ะะพัะพะถะตะฝะพะต - ััะพ ะพั ะปะฐะถะดะตะฝะฝัะน ะดะตัะตัั, ะบะพัะพััะน ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะณะพัะพะฒัั ะธะท ะผะพะปะพัะฝัั ะฟัะพะดัะบัะพะฒ, ัะฐะบะธั ะบะฐะบ ะผะพะปะพะบะพ ะธ ัะปะธะฒะบะธ, ะธ ัะฐััะพ ัะพัะตัะฐัั ั ัััะบัะฐะผะธ ะธะปะธ ะดััะณะธะผะธ ะธะฝะณัะตะดะธะตะฝัะฐะผะธ ะธ ะฐัะพะผะฐัะธะทะฐัะพัะฐะผะธ. ะะพะปััะธะฝััะฒะพ ัะพััะพะฒ ัะพะดะตัะถะฐั ัะฐั ะฐั, ั ะพัั ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ะธะท ะฝะธั ัะดะตะปะฐะฝั ั ะดััะณะธะผะธ ะฟะพะดัะปะฐััะธัะตะปัะผะธ. ะ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ัะปััะฐัั ะฒ ะดะพะฟะพะปะฝะตะฝะธะต ะธะปะธ ะฒะผะตััะพ ะฝะฐัััะฐะปัะฝัั ะธะฝะณัะตะดะธะตะฝัะพะฒ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ะธัะบััััะฒะตะฝะฝัะต ะฐัะพะผะฐัะธะทะฐัะพัั ะธ ะบัะฐัะธัะตะปะธ. ะะพัะพะถะตะฝะพะต ะผะพะถะฝะพ ะฟะพะดะฐะฒะฐัั ะฒ ะฟะพััะดะต, ะตััั ะปะพะถะบะพะน ะธะปะธ ัะปะธะทัะฒะฐัั ะธะท ััะตะดะพะฑะฝัั ััะฐะบะฐะฝัะธะบะพะฒ. ะะพัะพะถะตะฝะพะต ะผะพะถะฝะพ ะฟะพะดะฐะฒะฐัั ั ะดััะณะธะผะธ ะดะตัะตััะฐะผะธ, ัะฐะบะธะผะธ ะบะฐะบ ัะฑะปะพัะฝัะน ะฟะธัะพะณ, ะธะปะธ ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ะธะฝะณัะตะดะธะตะฝัะฐ ะดะปั ะผะพัะพะถะตะฝะพะณะพ, ะผะพัะพะถะตะฝะพะณะพ, ะผะพะปะพัะฝัั ะบะพะบัะตะนะปะตะน, ัะพััะพะฒ ั ะผะพัะพะถะตะฝัะผ ะธ ะดะฐะถะต ะฒัะฟะตัะบะธ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Immunity drugs[COLOR_REVERT], better known as immunosuppressive drugs or agents, are drugs that inhibit or prevent activity of the immune system. They are mainly used to prevent the rejection of transplanted organs and tissues, to treat autoimmune diseases or diseases that are most likely of autoimmune origin (e.g. rheumatoid arthritis, multiple sclerosis, psoriasis, Crohn's disease, Behcet's Disease, etc.), and to treat other non-autoimmune inflammatory diseases (e.g. long-term allergic asthma control).
TEXT.Italian: I [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]farmaci immunitari[COLOR_REVERT], meglio noti come farmaci o agenti immunosoppressori, sono farmaci che inibiscono o impediscono l'attivitร del sistema immunitario. Sono principalmente utilizzati per prevenire il rigetto di organi e tessuti trapiantati, per il trattamento di malattie autoimmuni o patologie che sono molto probabilmente di origine autoimmune (ad es. artrite reumatoide, sclerosi multipla, psoriasi, morbo di Crohn, malattia di Behcet, ecc.) e per il trattamento altre malattie infiammatorie non autoimmuni (ad es. controllo allergico dell'asma a lungo termine).
TEXT.Russian: ะะผะผัะฝะฝะฐั ัะธััะตะผะฐ - ััะพ ัะธััะตะผะฐ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ะฑะธะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะธั ััััะบััั ะธ ะฟัะพัะตััะพะฒ ะฒะฝัััะธ ะพัะณะฐะฝะธะทะผะฐ, ะบะพัะพัะฐั ะทะฐัะธัะฐะตั ะพั ะฑะพะปะตะทะฝะตะน. ะะปั ะฟัะฐะฒะธะปัะฝะพะณะพ ััะฝะบัะธะพะฝะธัะพะฒะฐะฝะธั ะธะผะผัะฝะฝะฐั ัะธััะตะผะฐ ะดะพะปะถะฝะฐ ะพะฑะฝะฐััะถะธะฒะฐัั ัะธัะพะบะธะน ัะฟะตะบัั ะฐะณะตะฝัะพะฒ, ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัั ะบะฐะบ ะฟะฐัะพะณะตะฝั, ะพั ะฒะธัััะพะฒ ะดะพ ะฟะฐัะฐะทะธัะธัะตัะบะธั ัะตัะฒะตะน, ะธ ะพัะปะธัะฐัั ะธั ะพั ัะพะฑััะฒะตะฝะฝะพะน ะทะดะพัะพะฒะพะน ัะบะฐะฝะธ ะพัะณะฐะฝะธะทะผะฐ. ะฃ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ะฒะธะดะพะฒ ะธะผะผัะฝะฝัั ัะธััะตะผั ะผะพะถะฝะพ ัะฐะทะดะตะปะธัั ะฝะฐ ะฟะพะดัะธััะตะผั, ัะฐะบะธะต ะบะฐะบ ะฒัะพะถะดะตะฝะฝะฐั ะธะผะผัะฝะฝะฐั ัะธััะตะผะฐ ะฟะพ ััะฐะฒะฝะตะฝะธั ั ะฐะดะฐะฟัะธะฒะฝะพะน ะธะผะผัะฝะฝะพะน ัะธััะตะผะพะน ะธะปะธ ะณัะผะพัะฐะปัะฝัะน ะธะผะผัะฝะธัะตั ะฟะพ ััะฐะฒะฝะตะฝะธั ั ะบะปะตัะพัะฝะพ-ะพะฟะพััะตะดะพะฒะฐะฝะฝัะผ ะธะผะผัะฝะธัะตัะพะผ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Incense[COLOR_REVERT] is aromatic biotic material that releases fragrant smoke when burned. The term is used for either the material or the aroma. Incense is composed of aromatic plant materials, often combined with essential oils and is used for aesthetic reasons, aromatherapy, meditation, and ceremony. It may also be used as a simple deodorant or insect repellent. The forms taken by incense differ with the underlying culture, and have changed with advances in technology and increasing number of uses.
TEXT.German: Bei [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Weihrauch[COLOR_REVERT] handelt es sich um das getrocknete Gummiharz des Weihrauchbaumes (Boswellia sacra), der nur in Trockengebieten wรคchst und heute hauptsรคchlich in Somalia, dem Oman und Jemen angebaut wird.[NEWLINE]Bei der Verbrennung von Weihrauch entsteht ein geruchsintensiver Dampf. Dieser kommt oft bei religiรถsen Zeremonien zum Einsatz und manche glauben, dass er auch eine therapeutische Wirkung habe. Die meisten Rรคucherdรผfte setzen sich aus pflanzlichen Bestandteilen wie Nelke, Zedernholz, Weihrauch oder Muskatnuss zusammen, sie kรถnnen jedoch auch aus essenziellen รlen oder Tierprodukten wie Moschus oder Ambra erzeugt werden. Viele alte Kulturen haben Rรคucherdรผfte verwendet, darunter die Chinesen, Japaner, รgypter, Babylonier und die Assyrer. In Indien kommen sie seit mindestens 5000 v.Chr. bei religiรถsen Zeremonien zum Einsatz.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]incenso[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un materiale biotico aromatico che rilascia fumo profumato quando viene bruciato. Il termine viene usato sia per il materiale, sia per l'aroma. L'incenso รจ composto da materiali vegetali aromatici, spesso combinati con oli essenziali e viene utilizzato per motivi estetici, aromaterapia, meditazione e usi cerimoniali. Puรฒ anche essere usato come semplice deodorante o repellente per insetti. Le forme adottate dall'incenso differiscono con le culture e sono cambiate con i progressi della tecnologia e il numero crescente di usi.
TEXT.Russian: ะะปะฐะณะพะฒะพะฝะธะต - ััะพ ัะพะตะดะธะฝะตะฝะธะต, ะบะพัะพัะพะต ะฟัะธ ัะถะธะณะฐะฝะธะธ ะฒัะดะตะปัะตั ะฐัะพะผะฐัะฝัะน ะดัะผ. ะะณะพ ัะฐััะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััั ะฒ ัะตะปะธะณะธะพะทะฝัั ัะตัะตะผะพะฝะธัั , ะธ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ััะธัะฐัั, ััะพ ะพะฝ ัะฐะบะถะต ะพะฑะปะฐะดะฐะตั ะปะตัะตะฑะฝัะผะธ ัะฒะพะนััะฒะฐะผะธ. ะะปะฐะณะพะฒะพะฝะธั ะพะฑััะฝะพ ัะพััะพัั ะธะท ัะฐััะธัะตะปัะฝัั ะผะฐัะตัะธะฐะปะพะฒ, ัะฐะบะธั ะบะฐะบ ะณะฒะพะทะดะธะบะฐ, ะบะตะดั, ะปะฐะดะฐะฝ ะธะปะธ ะผััะบะฐัะฝัะน ะพัะตั , ะฝะพ ะตะณะพ ะผะพะถะฝะพ ัะพะทะดะฐัั ะธะท ััะธัะฝัั ะผะฐัะตะป ะธะปะธ ะฟัะพะดัะบัะพะฒ ะถะธะฒะพัะฝะพะณะพ ะฟัะพะธัั ะพะถะดะตะฝะธั - ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั, ะผััะบััะฐ ะธะปะธ ะฐะผะฑัั. ะะพ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ัะฐะฝะฝะธั ะบัะปััััะฐั , ะฒะบะปััะฐั ะบะธัะฐะนัะบัั, ัะฟะพะฝัะบัั, ะตะณะธะฟะตััะบัั, ะฒะฐะฒะธะปะพะฝัะบัั ะธ ะฐััะธัะธะนัะบัั ัะธะฒะธะปะธะทะฐัะธะธ, ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปะธ ะปะฐะดะฐะฝ. ะะฝะดะตะนัั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปะธ ะปะฐะดะฐะฝ ะฒ ัะตะปะธะณะธะพะทะฝัั ัะตัะตะผะพะฝะธัั ะฟะพ ะบัะฐะนะฝะตะน ะผะตัะต ั 5000 ะณ. ะดะพ ะฝ. ั.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kadzid³o[COLOR_REVERT] to aromatyczny materia³, który po spaleniu wydziela pachn¹cy dym. Termin ten jest u¿ywany w odniesieniu do materia³u lub aromatu. Kadzid³o sk³ada siê z aromatycznych materia³ów roœlinnych, czêsto ³¹czonych z olejkami eterycznymi i jest u¿ywane ze wzglêdów estetycznych, lub do aromaterapii, medytacji albo w czsie ceremonii. Mo¿e byæ równie¿ stosowany jako prosty dezodorant lub œrodek odstraszaj¹cy owady. Formy przybierane przez kadzid³o ró¿ni¹ siê w zale¿noœci od podstawowej kultury i zmienia³y siê wraz z postêpem technologii i rosn¹c¹ liczb¹ zastosowañ.

TEXT.Russian: รรญรครจรฉรถรป
รรญรครจรฃรฎ

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Indigo dye[COLOR_REVERT] is an organic compound with a distinctive blue color. Historically, indigo was a natural dye extracted from the leaves of certain plants or from certain mollusks, but this process was economically expensive and its use was extremely rare. Only by the end of 19th century, there was an established process for industrial-scale synthesis. The primary use for indigo is as a dye for cotton yarn, which is mainly for the production of denim cloth for blue jeans.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Indigo[COLOR_REVERT] wird aus den Blรคttern der tropischen Indigopflanze (Indigofera tinctoria) gewonnen, die in Indien (wie der Name schon andeutet), China und Afrika heimisch ist. Es handelt sich um einen tiefblauen Farbstoff von hoher Farbstรคrke, der in Wasser nur schwer lรถslich ist.[NEWLINE]Indigo ist eines der verbreitetsten und am hรคufigsten verwendeten Fรคrbemittel vom Altertum bis heute. Bereits vor 6000 Jahren wurde Indigo in Peru zum Fรคrben von Baumwollstoffen eingesetzt. Auch in den frรผhen mesopotamischen, persischen und afrikanischen Kulturen war der Farbstoff bereits bekannt. In รgypten wurden im Alten Reich bereits Mumien in indigofarbene Bandagen eingewickelt. Von dort gelangte er als Luxusartikel รผber Griechenland und Italien nach Europa. In Europa blieb der Farbstoff aus der Indigopflanze bis zum 12. Jahrhundert selten, er wurde nur in kleinen Mengen aus Indien importiert. Bis zum 17. Jahrhundert wurde stattdessen Fรคrberwaid zur Indigogewinnung in England, Frankreich und Deutschland angebaut. Nach dem Ersten Weltkrieg verlor Indigo durch die Entwicklung synthetischer Farbstoffe zunehmend an Bedeutung. Die zunehmende Popularitรคt der Jeans kurbelte aber in den 1960er-Jahren die Nachfrage wieder an.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]indaco[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un composto organico con un caratteristico colore blu. Storicamente, l'indaco era un colorante naturale estratto dalle foglie di alcune piante o da alcuni molluschi, ma questo processo era economicamente costoso e il suo uso era estremamente limitato. Solo alla fine del XIX secolo, vi fu un processo consolidato per la sintesi su scala industriale. L'uso principale dell'indaco รจ come tintura per filati di cotone, che sono a loro volta usati per la produzione di tessuti denim per i jeans.
TEXT.Russian: ะะฝะดะธะณะพ - ััะพ ัะฒะตั, ะฝะฐะทะฒะฐะฝะฝัะน ะฒ ัะตััั ัะธะฝะตะณะพ ะบัะฐัะธัะตะปั, ะฟะพะปััะตะฝะฝะพะณะพ ะธะท ัะฐััะตะฝะธั Indigofera tinctoria ะธ ัะพะดััะฒะตะฝะฝัั ะตะผั ะฒะธะดะพะฒ. ะฆะฒะตั ะฟะพะผะตัะฐะตััั ะฒ ัะปะตะบััะพะผะฐะณะฝะธัะฝัะน ัะฟะตะบัั ั ะดะปะธะฝะพะน ะฒะพะปะฝั ะพะบะพะปะพ 420-450 ะฝะผ, ะฟะพะผะตัะฐั ะตะณะพ ะผะตะถะดั ัะธะฝะธะผ ะธ ัะธะพะปะตัะพะฒัะผ. ะฅะพัั ะพะฝ ััะฐะดะธัะธะพะฝะฝะพ ััะธัะฐะตััั ะพะดะฝะธะผ ะธะท ัะตะผะธ ัะฒะตัะพะฒ ัะฐะดัะณะธ ะธะปะธ ะพะฟัะธัะตัะบะพะณะพ ัะฟะตะบััะฐ, ัะพะฒัะตะผะตะฝะฝัะต ัะฟะตัะธะฐะปะธััั ะฟะพ ัะฒะตัั ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะฝะต ะฒัะดะตะปััั ะธะฝะดะธะณะพ ะบะฐะบ ะพัะดะตะปัะฝัะน ัะฐะทะดะตะป ะธ ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะบะปะฐััะธัะธัะธัััั ะดะปะธะฝั ะฒะพะปะฝ ะบะพัะพัะต ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ 450 ะฝะผ ะบะฐะบ ัะธะพะปะตัะพะฒัะน. ะฃัะตะฝัะต-ะพะฟัะธะบะธ ะฅะฐัะดะธ ะธ ะะตััะธะฝ ัะบะฐะทัะฒะฐัั, ััะพ ะดะปะธะฝะฐ ะฒะพะปะฝั ะธะฝะดะธะณะพ ัะพััะฐะฒะปัะตั ะพั 446 ะดะพ 464 ะฝะผ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Barwnik indygo[COLOR_REVERT] to zwi¹zek organiczny o charakterystycznej niebieskiej barwie. Historycznie rzecz bior¹c, indygo by³o naturalnym barwnikiem wydobywanym z liœci niektórych roœlin lub niektórych miêczaków, ale proces ten by³ kosztowny ekonomicznie, a jego stosowanie by³o niezwykle rzadkie. Dopiero pod koniec XIX wieku powsta³ proces syntezy na skalê przemys³ow¹. Podstawowym zastosowaniem indygo jest barwnik do przêdzy bawe³nianej, która s³u¿y g³ównie do produkcji tkaniny d¿insowej na niebieskie d¿insy.

TEXT.English: An [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ingot[COLOR_REVERT] is a material, usually metal, that is cast into a shape suitable for further processing. Non-metallic and semiconductor materials prepared in bulk form may also be referred to as ingots, particularly when cast by mold based methods.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]lingot[COLOR_REVERT] est un matรฉriau, gรฉnรฉralement mรฉtallique, qui est coulรฉ dans une forme adaptรฉe ร un traitement ultรฉrieur. Les matรฉriaux non mรฉtalliques et semi-conducteurs prรฉparรฉs en vrac peuvent รฉgalement รชtre appelรฉs lingots, en particulier lorsqu'ils sont moulรฉs par des mรฉthodes basรฉes sur des moules.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]lingotto[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una barra di metallo fusa in una forma utile per essere ulteriormente lavorata. Si puรฒ parlare di lingotti anche per i materiali non-metallici e semiconduttori, specie quando vengono prodotti per fusione.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wlewek, sztabka[COLOR_REVERT], to materia³, zwykle metal, który jest odlany w kszta³t odpowiedni do dalszej obróbki. Materia³y niemetaliczne i pó³przewodnikowe przygotowane luzem mo¿na równie¿ nazwaæ wlewkami, szczególnie w przypadku odlewania metodami opartymi na formach.
TEXT.Russian: รรซรจรฒรฎรช รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซร , รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฎรฒรซรจรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รดรฎรฐรฌรณ, รฏรฐรจรฃรฎรครญรณรพ รครซรฟ รคร รซรผรญรฅรฉรธรฅรฉ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒรชรจ. รรฅรฌรฅรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รจ รฏรฎรซรณรฏรฐรฎรขรฎรครญรจรชรฎรขรปรฅ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรป รข รฎรกรผรฅรฌรญรฎรฌ รขรจรครฅ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รฌรฎรฃรณรฒ รฎรฒรซรจรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รข รชร รช รทรณรธรชรจ, รฎรฑรฎรกรฅรญรญรฎ รชรฎรฃรคร รญร รฎรฑรญรฎรขรฅ รฑรฒร รญรคร รฐรฒรญรปรต รฌรฅรฒรฎรครฎรข รฎรญรจ รฎรฒรซรจรขร รพรฒรฑรฟ รข รฏรฐรฅรฑรฑรดรฎรฐรฌรณ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Iron[COLOR_REVERT] is the most common element on Earth, forming much of Earth's outer and inner core, and the fourth most common element in the Earth's crust, mostly in the form of ores. Humans started to master iron smelting process in Eurasia only about 2000 BCE, and the use of iron tools and weapons began to displace copper alloys, in some regions, only around 1200 BCE, triggering the transition from the Bronze Age to the Iron Age. In the modern world, iron alloys, such as steel, inox, cast iron and special steels are by far the most common industrial metals, because of their high mechanical properties and low cost.
TEXT.German: Als die Zivilisationen der Frรผhzeit begannen, metallene Werkzeuge und Waffen anzufertigen, war Bronze das am meisten verwendete Material. Es hatte den Vorteil, dass es leicht erhรคltlich war und gut bearbeitet werden konnte. Leider war es zu weich, als dass man scharfe Klingen daraus hรคtte formen kรถnnen. Mitte des 14. Jahrhunderts vor Christus, zu Beginn der so genannten Eisenzeit, lรถste Eisen die Bronze als bevorzugtes Metall in Europa ab. Seit dieser Zeit ist Eisen ein wertvoller Gebrauchsartikel. Eisen- und Eisenerzablagerungen, die in Bergen zu finden sind, werden abgebaut und in Rohform oder bei der Produktion von Stahl verwendet.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ferro[COLOR_REVERT] รจ l'elemento piรน comune sulla Terra, costituendo gran parte del nucleo esterno e interno della Terra, e il quarto elemento piรน comune nella crosta terrestre, principalmente sotto forma di minerali. Gli umani iniziarono a dominare il processo di fusione del ferro in Eurasia solo verso il 2000 a.C. e l'uso di strumenti e armi di ferro iniziรฒ a sostituire le leghe di rame, in alcune regioni, solo verso il 1200 a.C., innescando il passaggio dall'etร del bronzo all'etร del ferro. Nel mondo moderno, le leghe di ferro, quali acciaio, inox, ghisa e acciai speciali, sono di gran lunga i metalli industriali piรน comuni, a causa delle loro elevate proprietร meccaniche e del basso costo.
TEXT.Russian: ะะพะณะดะฐ ะฟะตัะฒัะต ัะธะฒะธะปะธะทะฐัะธะธ ะฝะฐัะฐะปะธ ัะพะทะดะฐะฒะฐัั ะฟะตัะฒัะต ะผะตัะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะธะต ะธะฝััััะผะตะฝัั ะธ ะพััะถะธะต, ะดะปั ััะพะน ัะตะปะธ ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปะฐัั ะฑัะพะฝะทะฐ: ะพะฝะฐ ะดะพัััะฟะฝะฐ ะธ ะฟัะพััะฐ ะฒ ะพะฑัะฐะฑะพัะบะต. ะะดะฝะฐะบะพ ััะพั ะผะตัะฐะปะป ัะปะธัะบะพะผ ะผัะณะพะบ, ััะพะฑั ะพััะดะธั ะธะท ะฝะตะณะพ ะผะพะถะฝะพ ะฑัะปะพ ัััะตะบัะธะฒะฝะพ ะทะฐัะพัะธัั. ะ ะฆะตะฝััะฐะปัะฝะพะน ะะฒัะพะฟะต ะถะตะปะตะทะพ ัะผะตะฝะธะปะพ ะฑัะพะฝะทั ะฒ ัะพะปะธ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะณะพ ะผะตัะฐะปะปะฐ ะบ ัะตัะตะดะธะฝะต XIV ะฒ. ะดะพ ะฝ.ั., ะธ ั ััะพะณะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะธ ะฝะฐัะฐะปัั ะะตะปะตะทะฝัะน ะฒะตะบ. ะก ัะตั ะฟะพั ะถะตะปะตะทะพ ััะธัะฐะตััั ัะตะฝะฝัะผ ัะตััััะพะผ. ะะตะปะตะทะพ ะธ ะถะตะปะตะทะฝัั ััะดั ะธะท ะณะพัะฝัั ะผะตััะพัะพะถะดะตะฝะธะน ะดะพะฑัะฒะฐัั ะธ ะฟะตัะตัะฐะฑะฐััะฒะฐัั, ััะพะฑั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐัั ะฒ ัะธััะพะผ ะฒะธะดะต ะธ ะฟัะธ ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดััะฒะต ััะฐะปะธ.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ivory[COLOR_REVERT] is a hard, white material from the tusks and teeth of animals, traditionally elephants, even if trade in certain teeth and tusks other than elephant is well established and widespread; therefore, "ivory" can correctly be used to describe any mammalian teeth or tusks of commercial interest which are large enough to be carved or scrimshawed. Ivory has been valued since ancient times in art or manufacturing for making a range of items from ivory carvings to false teeth, piano keys, fans, dominoes and joint tubes. Since the end of 20th century, national and international trade in ivory of threatened species has been declared illegal.
TEXT.German: Elfenbein (mittelhochdeutsch fรผr Elefantenknochen) bezeichnet das Zahnbein der Stoรzรคhne von Elefant, Walross und Mammut und der Eckzรคhne verschiedener Sรคugetiere (Pottwal, Narwal, Flusspferd), das als Werkstoff zur Herstellung von Gebrauchs- und Schmuckgegenstรคnden verwendet werden kann. Aus Elfenbein wurden neben kunstvollen Schnitzereien und Intarsien auch so profane Dinge wie falsche Zรคhne, Dominosteine, Klaviertasten und Billardkugeln gefertigt. Heutzutage greift man bei derartigen Gebrauchsgรผtern zwar fast nur noch auf Kunstharze zurรผck, trotzdem ist der weltweite Bedarf an Elfenbein immer noch groร und besonders der afrikanische Elefant durch Wilderei stark bedroht.
TEXT.Italian: L'[COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]avorio[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un materiale duro e bianco proveniente dalle zanne e dai denti degli animali, tradizionalmente elefanti, anche se il commercio di denti e zanne di altri animali รจ ben consolidato e diffuso; pertanto, "avorio" puรฒ essere correttamente utilizzato per descrivere eventuali denti o zanne di mammiferi di interesse commerciale che sono abbastanza grandi da poter essere scolpiti o lavorati. L'avorio รจ stato apprezzato fin dall'antichitร nell'arte e per la realizzazione di una gamma di sculture in avorio, come denti finti, tasti di pianoforte, ventole, pedine del domino e tubi articolati. A partire dalla fine del XX secolo, il commercio nazionale e internazionale di avorio di specie minacciate รจ stato dichiarato illegale.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]jackalope[COLOR_REVERT] is a mythical animal from North American folklore, described as a jackrabbit with antelope horns. The jackalope has appeared in published stories, poems, television shows, video games, and a low-budget mockumentary film. Researchers suggest that at least some of the tales of horned hares were inspired by sightings of rabbits infected with the Shope papilloma virus, that causes horn- and antler-like tumors to grow in various places on a rabbit's head and body.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]jackalope[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un animale mitico del folklore nordamericano, descritto come un coniglio selvatico con corna di antilope. Il jackalope รจ apparso in numerose storie, poesie, spettacoli televisivi, videogiochi e un finto documentario a basso budget. I ricercatori suggeriscono che alcuni degli avvistamenti di lepri cornute siano dovuti ad avvistamenti di conigli infetti dal papilloma virus di Shope, che causa la crescita di tumori a forma di corna (anche ramificate) in varie parti della testa o del corpo di un coniglio.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]jade death mask[COLOR_REVERT] is an impression of a person's face after their death, usually made by putting together several pieces of jade. Death masks may be mementos of the dead, or be used for creation of portraits. It is sometimes possible to identify portraits that have been painted from death masks, because of the characteristic slight distortions of the face features.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]masque mortuaire en jade[COLOR_REVERT] est une empreinte du visage d'une personne aprรจs sa mort, gรฉnรฉralement rรฉalisรฉe en assemblant plusieurs morceaux de jade. Les masques mortuaires peuvent รชtre des souvenirs des dรฉfunts, ou รชtre utilisรฉs pour la crรฉation de portraits. Il est parfois possible d'identifier des portraits qui ont รฉtรฉ peints ร partir de masques mortuaires, en raison des lรฉgรจres distorsions caractรฉristiques des traits du visage.
TEXT.Italian: Una [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]maschera mortuaria di giada[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un'impressione del volto di una persona dopo la sua morte, di solito realizzata mettendo insieme diversi pezzi di giada. Le maschere mortuarie possono essere ricordi dei morti o essere utilizzate per la creazione di ritratti. A volte รจ possibile identificare i ritratti che sono stati dipinti dalle maschere, a causa delle lievi distorsioni caratteristiche dei lineamenti del viso.

Czasem udaje siê rozpoznaæ portrety namalowane z masek poœmiertnych, ze wzglêdu na charakterystyczne lekkie zniekszta³cenia rysów twarzy.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Jade[COLOR_REVERT] is an ornamental mineral, mostly known for its green varieties. It can refer to either of two different minerals: nephrite, a silicate of calcium and magnesium, or jadeite, a silicate of sodium and aluminium. The former can be found in a creamy white form, as well as in a variety of light green colours; the latter is rarer and shows more colour variations, including blue, brown, red, black, dark green, lavender and white. Both materials were used from prehistoric periods for ornaments and decorative objects: among the earliest known jade artifacts excavated from prehistoric sites are simple ornaments with bead, button, and tubular shapes. Additionally, jade was used for adze heads, knives, and other weapons, which can be delicately shaped.
TEXT.German: Ein grรผnliches Mineral, Jade ist extrem hart und robust und ist im Gebrauch fรผr viele Tausenden Jahre gewesen. Jade wurde ursprรผnglich in den Werkzeugen und in den Waffen benutzt, aber, wรคhrend andere wirkungsvollere Substanzen (wie Bronze und Eisen) entdeckt wurden, wรผrde Jade groร fรผr dekorative Zwecke verwendet werden. Jade war erste allgemein verwendet in China wรคhrend der neolithischen Periode. รberzeit als die zugรคnglichsten Quellen wurden verbraucht, es wurde wertvoller als Gold. Heute wird Jade noch in den Schmucksachen benutzt und in einer Vielzahl von Trinkets, obwohl es nicht mehr das Prestige trรคgt oder bewertet, sie einmal.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]giada[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un minerale ornamentale, noto soprattutto per le sue varietร verdi. Il termine puรฒ riferirsi a due diversi minerali: la nefrite, un silicato di calcio e magnesio, e la giadeite, un silicato di sodio e alluminio. La prima va dal bianco crema a una varietร di sfumature verdi chiare; l'altra รจ piรน rara e mostra piรน variazioni di colore, tra cui blu, marrone, rosso, nero, verde scuro, lavanda e bianco. Entrambi i materiali sono stati utilizzati dalla preistoria per ornamenti e oggetti decorativi: tra i primi manufatti di giada noti, vi sono semplici ornamenti con perline, bottoni e forme tubolari. Inoltre, la giada veniva utilizzata per asce, coltelli e altre armi che possono essere modellate delicatamente.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตะปะตะฝะพะฒะฐััะน ะผะธะฝะตัะฐะป, ะฝะตััะธั ััะตะทะฒััะฐะนะฝะพ ัะฒะตัะดัะน ะธ ะฟัะพัะฝัะน, ะตะณะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปะธ ะผะฝะพะณะธะต ัััััะธ ะปะตั. ะะทะฝะฐัะฐะปัะฝะพ ะฝะตััะธั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปัั ะฒ ะธะฝััััะผะตะฝัะฐั ะธ ะพััะถะธะธ, ะฝะพ ะบะพะณะดะฐ ะฑัะปะธ ะพะฑะฝะฐััะถะตะฝั ะดััะณะธะต ะฑะพะปะตะต ัััะตะบัะธะฒะฝัะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ (ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั, ะฑัะพะฝะทะฐ ะธ ะถะตะปะตะทะพ), ะพะฝ ััะฐะป ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐัััั ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ะฒ ะดะตะบะพัะฐัะธะฒะฝัั ัะตะปัั . ะะฟะตัะฒัะต ะฝะตััะธั ะฝะฐัะฐะปะธ ัะธัะพะบะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐัั ะฒ ะะธัะฐะต ะฒ ะฟะตัะธะพะด ะฝะตะพะปะธัะฐ. ะกะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะตะผ, ะบะพะณะดะฐ ะฝะฐะธะฑะพะปะตะต ะดะพัััะฟะฝัะต ะธััะพัะฝะธะบะธ ะธััะพัะธะปะธัั, ะพะฝ ััะฐะป ะดะพัะพะถะต ะทะพะปะพัะฐ. ะกะตะณะพะดะฝั ะฝะตััะธั ะฟะพ-ะฟัะตะถะฝะตะผั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะฒ ัะฒะตะปะธัะฝัั ะธะทะดะตะปะธัั ะธ ะฒ ัะฐะทะปะธัะฝัั ะฑะตะทะดะตะปััะบะฐั , ั ะพัั ะพะฝ ะฑะพะปััะต ะฝะต ะธะผะตะตั ัะพะณะพ ะฟัะตััะธะถะฐ ะธะปะธ ัะตะฝะฝะพััะธ, ะบะฐะบ ัะฐะฝััะต.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Jadeit[COLOR_REVERT], to minera³ ozdobny, znany g³ównie ze swoich zielonych odmian. Mo¿e odnosiæ siê do jednego z dwóch ró¿nych minera³ów: nefrytu: krzemianu wapnia i magnezu lub jadeitu: krzemianu sodu i glinu. Te pierwsze wystêpuj¹ w kremowo-bia³ej formie, a tak¿e w ró¿nych jasnozielonych kolorach; ten ostatni jest rzadszy i wykazuje wiêcej odmian kolorystycznych, w tym niebieski, br¹zowy, czerwony, czarny, ciemnozielony, lawendowy i bia³y. Obydwa materia³y by³y u¿ywane od okresów prehistorycznych do ozdób i przedmiotów dekoracyjnych: wœród najwczeœniejszych znanych artefaktów z jadeitu wydobytych z miejsc prehistorycznych znajduj¹ siê proste ozdoby w kszta³cie koralików, guzików i rurek. Dodatkowo z jadeitu wytwarzano g³ówki toporów, no¿e i inn¹ broñ, któr¹ mo¿na delikatnie kszta³towaæ.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]jet engine[COLOR_REVERT] is a type of reaction combustion engine discharging a fast-moving jet that generates thrust by jet propulsion. Jet engines usually power jet aircraft, cruise missiles and unmanned aerial vehicles, while the term typically refers to an airbreathing jet engine such as a turbojet, turbofan, ramjet, or pulse jet, that typically feature a rotating air compressor powered by a turbine, with the leftover power providing thrust through the propelling nozzle. Initially developed in 1930s, only by 1970s jet engine efficiency was enough to par and surpass piston and propeller engines.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]motore a reazione[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un tipo di motore a combustione che genera spinta mediante propulsione a getto. I motori a reazione di solito alimentano aeromobili a reazione, missili da crociera e velivoli aerei senza equipaggio, mentre il termine si riferisce in genere a un motore a getto d'aria che tipicamente presenta un compressore d'aria rotante alimentato da una turbina, con la potenza rimanente che fornisce la spinta attraverso l'ugello di propulsione. Sviluppato inizialmente negli anni trenta del XX secolo, solo negli anni settanta l'efficienza del motore a reazione era sufficiente per raggiungere e superare quella dei motori a pistoni e a elica.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Silnik odrzutowy[COLOR_REVERT], to rodzaj silnika spalinowego, który wytwarza ci¹g poprzez wyrzucanie szybko poruszaj¹cego siê strumienia. Silniki odrzutowe zazwyczaj napêdzaj¹ samoloty odrzutowe, rakiety manewruj¹ce i bezza³ogowe statki powietrzne, natomiast termin ten zazwyczaj odnosi siê do silnika odrzutowego oddychaj¹cego powietrzem, takiego jak silnik turboodrzutowy, turbowentylatorowy, strumieniowy lub impulsowy, który zazwyczaj jest wyposa¿ony w obrotow¹ sprê¿arkê powietrza napêdzan¹ turbin¹. TEXT.Russian: รรขรฅรซรจรฐรญรปรฅ รจรงรครฅรซรจรฟ

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Jewelry[COLOR_REVERT] are decorative items worn for personal adornment, such as brooches, rings, necklaces, earrings, pendants, bracelets, and cufflinks. Jewelry may be attached to the body, to adorn nearly every body part, or to clothes. For many centuries metal, often combined with gemstones, amber or coral, has been the normal material used, but other materials such as shells and other plant materials may be used. It is one of the oldest type of archaeological artefact ever found. The basic forms of jewelry vary between cultures but are often extremely long-lived. The patterns of wearing jewelry can vary greatly between cultures, but adult women have been the most consistent wearers of jewelry. In most cultures jewelry can be understood as a status symbol, for its material properties, its patterns, or for meaningful symbols.
TEXT.Italian: I [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]gioielli[COLOR_REVERT] sono oggetti decorativi indossati per ornamento personale, come spille, anelli, collane, orecchini, pendenti, bracciali e gemelli. I gioielli possono essere attaccati al corpo, per adornare quasi ogni parte del corpo, o ai vestiti. Per molti secoli il metallo, spesso combinato con pietre preziose, ambra o corallo, รจ stato il normale materiale utilizzato, ma possono essere utilizzati altri materiali come conchiglie e altri materiali vegetali. ร uno dei piรน antichi manufatti archeologici mai trovati. Le forme di base dei gioielli variano tra le culture, ma sono spesso estremamente longevi. La tradizione di indossare gioielli puรฒ variare notevolmente tra le culture, ma le donne adulte sono le principali indossatrici di gioielli. Nella maggior parte delle culture, la gioielleria puรฒ essere intesa come uno status symbol, per il materiale usato, la sua fattura o per il simbolismo rivestito.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Bi¿uteria[COLOR_REVERT], to przedmioty dekoracyjne noszone w celu ozdoby osobistej, takie jak broszki, pierœcionki, naszyjniki, kolczyki, wisiorki, bransoletki i spinki do mankietów. Bi¿uteriê mo¿na przymocowaæ do cia³a, ozdobiæ niemal ka¿d¹ jego czêœæ lub do ubrania. Przez wiele stuleci metal, czêsto ³¹czony z kamieniami szlachetnymi, bursztynem lub koralem, by³ najczêœciej u¿ywanym materia³em, ale mo¿na równie¿ u¿ywaæ innych materia³ów, takich jak muszle i materia³y roœlinne. Jest to jeden z najstarszych typów artefaktów archeologicznych, jakie kiedykolwiek znaleziono. Podstawowe formy bi¿uterii ró¿ni¹ siê w zale¿noœci od kultury, ale czêsto s¹ niezwykle trwa³e. TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]kangaroo[COLOR_REVERT] is a marsupial animal from the Macropodidae family, indigenous to Australia. Depending on their size, they can be called kangaroos, wallaroo, and wallaby, from biggest to smallest. Kangaroos have large, powerful hind legs, large feet adapted for leaping, a long muscular tail for balance, and a small head. Like most marsupials, female kangaroos have a pouch called a marsupium, in which joeys complete postnatal development. Kangaroo is a symbol of Australia, due to its importance to Australian culture and national image. Nonetheless, wild kangaroos are shot for meat, leather hides, and to protect grazing land.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kรคngurus[COLOR_REVERT] wurden schon lange vor der Ankunft der Europรคer von den Aborigines bejagt. Diese nutzten nicht nur das nahrhafte Fleisch, sondern verarbeiteten auch das Fell der Beuteltiere. Heute ist das Kรคngurufleisch seltsamerweise eher im Ausland beliebt und wird von den Australiern vorwiegend zu Tierfutter verarbeitet. Das sehr reiรfeste Kรคnguruleder hingegen wird auch heute noch fรผr die Herstellung von Handschuhen, Stiefeln und Schuhen verwendet.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]canguro[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un animale marsupiale della famiglia Macropodidae, originario dell'Australia. A seconda delle loro dimensioni, possono essere chiamati canguri, wallaroo e wallaby (dal piรน grande al piรน piccolo). I canguri hanno zampe posteriori grandi e potenti, piedi larghi adatti per saltare, una lunga coda muscolare che usano per mantenere l'equilibrio e una testa piccola. Come la maggior parte dei marsupiali, i canguri femmina hanno una sacca anteriore chiamata marsupio, in cui i cuccioli completano il proprio sviluppo postnatale. Il canguro รจ un simbolo dell'Australia, per la sua importanza per la cultura e l'immagine nazionale australiana. Tuttavia, i canguri selvatici vengono cacciati per le loro carni e pelli e per proteggere i pascoli.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kangur[COLOR_REVERT],to torbacz z rodziny Macropodidae, zamieszkuj¹cy Australiê. W zale¿noœci od wielkoœci mo¿na je nazwaæ kangurami, wallaroo i wallaby, od najwiêkszego do najmniejszego. Kangury maj¹ du¿e, mocne tylne nogi, du¿e stopy przystosowane do skakania, d³ugi, muskularny ogon zapewniaj¹cy równowagê i ma³¹ g³owê. Podobnie jak wiêkszoœæ torbaczy, samice kangurów maj¹ worek zwany torb¹, w którym m³ode koñcz¹ rozwój poporodowy. Kangur jest symbolem Australii ze wzglêdu na jego znaczenie dla australijskiej kultury i wizerunku narodowego. Niemniej jednak dzikie kangury odstrzeliwane s¹ w celu zdobycia miêsa, skór i ochrony pastwisk.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kava[COLOR_REVERT], or kava kava, is a crop of the Pacific Islands. The name comes from Tongan and Marquesan, meaning "bitter", and is consumed for its sedating effects throughout the Pacific Ocean cultures. To a lesser extent, it is consumed in nations where it is exported as an herbal medicine. The root of the plant is used to produce a drink with sedative, anesthetic, and euphoriant properties. Moderate consumption of kava in its traditional form, i.e. as a water-based suspension of kava roots, has been deemed as presenting an "acceptably low level of health risk", while consumption of kava extracts produced with organic solvents, or excessive amounts of poor quality kava products, may be linked to an increased risk of adverse health outcomes, including potential liver injury.
TEXT.German: Die immergrรผne [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kava[COLOR_REVERT] oder Kava-Kava (Piper methysticum) gehรถrt zur Familie der Pfeffergewรคchse. Sie stammt aus dem westpazifischen Raum und wird wegen ihrer euphorisierenden Wirkung auch Rauschpfeffer genannt. Es handelt sich um einen 1,5 bis 5 m hohen Strauch mit stark verzweigtem Wurzelstock.[NEWLINE]Die ursprรผngliche Heimat der Pflanze ist nicht durch Fakten gesichert, man geht aber davon aus, dass sie aus dem sรผdlichen Pazifikraum stammt. Die Kava wurde wohl anfรคnglich zum Fischfang genutzt, weil die Fische aufgrund der entspannenden Wirkung nicht wegschwammen. Die niederlรคndischen Entdecker Le Maire und Schouten berichteten von der Pflanze nach einer Reise auf die Sรผdseeinseln Wallis und Futuna im Jahr 1616. Ihre Beobachtungen fรผhrten aber zu der irrigen Annahme, dass Kava als Getrรคnk berauschende Wirkung habe, weshalb ihr der Name Rauschpfeffer gegeben wurde. Die Kava-Pflanze wurde spรคter auch vom deutschen Botaniker Johann Georg Forster beschrieben, der James Cook auf dessen zweiter wissenschaftlicher Erdumseglung 1772-1775 begleitete. Etwa seit 1820 wird Kava-Kava in Europa fรผr medizinische und therapeutische Zwecke angewendet, aufgrund mรถglicher leberschรคdigender Wirkung ist sie aber in letzter Zeit wieder in Verruf gekommen.
TEXT.Italian: La [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]kava[COLOR_REVERT], o kava kava, รจ una coltura delle isole del Pacifico. Il nome deriva dal tongano e dal marquesano (e significa "amaro") ed รจ consumato per i suoi effetti sedativi nelle culture dell'Oceano Pacifico. In misura minore, viene consumato nelle nazioni in cui viene esportato come fitoterapia. La radice della pianta viene utilizzata per produrre una bevanda con proprietร sedative, anestetiche ed euforizzanti. Il consumo moderato di kava nella sua forma tradizionale, cioรจ come sospensione a base acquosa di radici di kava, presenta un "livello accettabilmente basso di rischio per la salute", mentre il consumo di estratti di kava prodotti con solventi organici o quantitร eccessive di kava di bassa qualitร possono essere collegati ad un aumentato rischio per la salute, tra cui potenziali danni al fegato.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kava[COLOR_REVERT] lub kava kava, to roœlina uprawna porastaj¹ca wyspy Pacyfiku. Nazwa pochodzi z jêzyka Tonga i Marquesan, i oznacza ŒgorzkiŒ. W kulturach oceanu spokojnego jest spo¿ywana ze wzglêdu na dzia³anie uspokajaj¹ce . W mniejszym stopniu jest spo¿ywany w krajach, do których jest eksportowana jako lek zio³owy. Z korzenia tej roœliny sporz¹dza siê napój o dzia³aniu uspokajaj¹cym, znieczulaj¹cym i euforycznym. Uznano, ¿e umiarkowane spo¿ycie kavy w jej tradycyjnej postaci, tj. w postaci wodnej zawiesiny korzeni kavy, stwarza Œakceptowalnie niski poziom zagro¿enia dla zdrowiaŒ, natomiast spo¿ywanie ekstraktów kavy wytwarzanych przy u¿yciu rozpuszczalników organicznych lub nadmiernych iloœci niskiej jakoœci produktów z kavy, mo¿e byæ powi¹zane ze zwiêkszonym ryzykiem niekorzystnych skutków zdrowotnych, w tym potencjalnego uszkodzenia w¹troby.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kerosene[COLOR_REVERT], also known as paraffin or lamp oil, is a combustible hydrocarbon liquid which is derived from petroleum. It is widely used as a fuel in aviation, as well as in households as fuel for lamps and stoves. It can be also used as a solvent and lubricant in industry applications, as fuel for small outboard motors or even motorcycles, or for entertainment with fire performances, such as fire breathing, fire juggling, and fire dancing. First distilled by mid-19th century, it rapidly substituted whale oil as fuel for lamps, only to be substituted in turn by gasoline as main commercial fuel by early 20th century.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cherosene[COLOR_REVERT], noto anche come cherosene o paraffina, รจ un liquido combustibile idrocarburico derivato dal petrolio. ร ampiamente usato come combustibile nell'aviazione, cosรฌ come combustibile per lampade e stufe. Puรฒ anche essere usato come solvente e lubrificante in applicazioni industriali, come combustibile per piccoli motori fuoribordo o persino motociclette, o per intrattenimento negli spettacoli di mangiafuoco e danza del fuoco. Distillato per la prima volta a metร del XIX secolo, sostituรฌ rapidamente l'olio di balena come combustibile per lampade, per poi essere a sua volta sostituito dalla benzina come principale combustibile commerciale all'inizio del XX secolo.
TEXT.Russian: รรฐรฅรครจ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รขรจรครฎรข รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขร รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รซรจรฑรผ รณรฃรฎรซรผ, รญรฅรดรฒรผ รจ รชรฅรฐรฎรฑรจรญ รข รชรฅรฐรฎรฑรจรญรฎรขรปรต รซร รฌรฏร รต.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nafta[COLOR_REVERT], znana równie¿ jako parafina lub olej do lamp, to palna ciecz wêglowodorowa otrzymywana z ropy naftowej. Ma szerokie zastosowanie jako paliwo w lotnictwie, a tak¿e w gospodarstwach domowych, jako paliwo do lamp i pieców. Mo¿e byæ równie¿ stosowana jako rozpuszczalnik i smar w zastosowaniach przemys³owych, jako paliwo do ma³ych silników zaburtowych, a nawet motocykli lub do celów rozrywkowych podczas wystêpów ogniowych, takich jak ziewanie ogniem, ¿onglowanie ogniem i taniec z ogniem. Wydestylowana po raz pierwszy w po³owie XIX wieku, szybko zast¹pi³a olej wielorybi jako paliwo do lamp, by na pocz¹tku XX wieku zostaæ zast¹piona przez benzynê, jako g³ówe paliwo komercyje.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kevlar[COLOR_REVERT] is a heat-resistant and strong synthetic fiber. Developed in 1965, this high-strength material was used first commercially in the early 1970s as a replacement for steel in racing tires. Typically it is spun into ropes or fabric sheets, that can be used as such or as an ingredient in composite material components. Kevlar has many applications, ranging from bicycle tires and racing sails to bulletproof vests, because of its high tensile strength-to-weight ratio that makes it five times stronger than steel. It also is used to make modern marching drumheads that withstand high impact. When used as a woven material, it is suitable for mooring lines and other underwater applications.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]kevlar[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una fibra sintetica resistente al calore e forte. Sviluppato nel 1965, รจ stato utilizzato per la prima volta commercialmente nei primi anni settanta del XX secolo in sostituzione dell'acciaio nei pneumatici da corsa. Tipicamente รจ filato in corde o fogli di tessuto, che possono essere usati come tali o come componenti in materiali compositi. Il kevlar ha molte applicazioni, che vanno dai pneumatici per biciclette alle vele da corsa, ai giubbotti antiproiettile, grazie al suo elevato rapporto resistenza-peso a trazione che lo rende cinque volte piรน resistente dell'acciaio. Inoltre, viene utilizzato nei tamburi da marcia perchรฉ resistano a forti impatti. Se utilizzato come materiale tessuto, รจ adatto per i cavi di ormeggio e altre applicazioni subacquee.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]kugelblitz[COLOR_REVERT] (German for "ball lightning") is a concentration of heat, light or radiation so intense that its energy forms an event horizon and becomes self-trapped: according to general relativity and the equivalence of mass and energy, if enough radiation is aimed into a region, the concentration of energy can warp space-time enough for the region to become a black hole. In simpler terms, a kugelblitz is a black hole formed from radiation as opposed to matter. Such a black hole would nonetheless have properties identical to one of equivalent mass and angular momentum formed in a more conventional way. Kugelblitz drives have been considered as possible future black hole starship engines.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]kugelblitz[COLOR_REVERT] (tedesco per "sfera di lampo") รจ una concentrazione di calore, luce o radiazione cosรฌ intensa che l'energia emessa forma un orizzonte degli eventi e si auto-intrappola: secondo la relativitร generale e l'equivalenza di massa ed energia, se la radiazione รจ sufficientemente concentrata in una regione, la concentrazione di energia puรฒ deformare lo spaziotempo al punto da rendere la regione un buco nero. In termini piรน semplici, un kugelblitz รจ un buco nero formato da radiazioni, piuttosto che da materia. Un simile buco nero avrebbe comunque proprietร identiche a uno di massa e momento angolare equivalente formatosi in un modo convenzionale. Un motore alimentato da kugelblitz รจ stato considerato come un potenziale motore per astronavi alimentato a buchi neri.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kugelblitz[COLOR_REVERT] (po niemiecku "b³yskawica kulista"), to koncentracja ciep³a, œwiat³a lub promieniowania tak intensywna, ¿e jego energia tworzy horyzont zdarzeñ i zostaje uwiêziona; zgodnie z ogóln¹ teori¹ wzglêdnoœci oraz równowa¿noœci¹ masy i energii, jeœli wystarczaj¹co silne promieniowanie jest skierowanw w jeden punkt, koncentracja energii mo¿e zakrzywiæ czasoprzestrzeñ na tyle, ¿e powstanie czarna dziura. Mówi¹c proœciej, kugelblitz to czarna dziura utworzona z promieniowania, a nie z materii. Taka czarna dziura mia³aby jednak w³aœciwoœci identyczne jak czarna dzura z równowa¿n¹ mas¹ i momentem pêdu utworzonyma w bardziej konwencjonalny sposób. Napêdy Kugelblitz uznano za mo¿liwe przysz³e silniki statków kosmicznych.

TEXT.English: Lacquerware are objects decoratively covered with lacquer. The lacquer is sometimes inlaid or carved. Lacquerware includes boxes, tableware, buttons and even coffins painted with lacquer in cultures mostly in the Eastern Asia.
TEXT.French: Les objets laquรฉs sont des objets dรฉcorativement recouverts de laque. La laque est parfois incrustรฉe ou sculptรฉe. Les objets laquรฉs comprennent des boรฎtes, de la vaisselle, des boutons et mรชme des cercueils peints avec de la laque dans des cultures principalement d'Asie orientale.
TEXT.German: Die Lackkunst ist eine kunsthandwerkliche Technik, deren Ursprรผnge vor 3500 Jahren in China lagen. Dabei werden Lacke zur Oberflรคchenveredelung und Dekoration auf Alltagsgegenstรคnde und Kunstgegenstรคnde aufgetragen.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wyroby lakierowane[COLOR_REVERT], to przedmioty dekoracyjnie pokryte lakierem. Lakier jest czasami inkrustowany lub rzeŸbiony. Wyroby lakierowane obejmuj¹ pude³ka, zastawê sto³ow¹, guziki, a nawet trumny malowane lakierem g³ównie w Azji Wschodniej.
TEXT.Russian: รร รชรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรปรฅ รจรงรครฅรซรจรฟ รทร รนรฅ รขรฑรฅรฃรฎ รฑรฎรฏรฐรฎรขรฎรฆรคร รพรฒรฑรฟ รจรญรชรฐรณรฑรฒร รถรจรฅรฉ รจ รครฅรชรฎรฐร รฒรจรขรญรฎรฉ รฐรฅรงรผรกรฎรฉ, ร รจรฌรฅรญรญรฎ: รซร รชรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรปรฅ รธรชร รฒรณรซรชรจ, รฏรฎรฑรณรคร , รฏรณรฃรฎรขรจรถรป รจ รคร รฆรฅ รฃรฐรฎรกรป รฎรชรฐร รธรจรขร รพรฒรฑรฟ รซร รชรฎรฌ รข รญรฅรชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรต รชรณรซรผรฒรณรฐร รต, รข รฎรฑรญรฎรขรญรฎรฌ รข รรฎรฑรฒรฎรทรญรฎรฉ รรงรจรจ.

TEXT.English: Lard is pig fat in both its rendered and unrendered forms. Lard was commonly used in many cuisines as a cooking fat or shortening, or as a spread similar to butter. Its use in contemporary cuisine has diminished; however, many contemporary cooks and bakers favor it over other fats for select uses. The culinary qualities of lard vary somewhat depending on the part of the pig from which the fat was taken and how the lard was processed.
TEXT.French: Le saindoux est la graisse de porc sous ses formes fondue et non fondue. Le saindoux รฉtait couramment utilisรฉ dans de nombreuses cuisines comme graisse de cuisson ou shortening, ou comme tartinade similaire au beurre. Son utilisation dans la cuisine contemporaine a diminuรฉ; cependant, de nombreux cuisiniers et boulangers contemporains le prรฉfรจrent ร d'autres graisses pour certaines utilisations spรฉcifiques. Les qualitรฉs culinaires du saindoux varient quelque peu en fonction de la partie du porc d'oรน provient la graisse et de la faรงon dont le saindoux a รฉtรฉ traitรฉ.
TEXT.Italian: Il lardo รจ grasso di maiale sia nella sua forma fusa che in quella non fusa. Il lardo era comunemente usato in molte cucine come grasso da cucina o accorciamento, o come crema da spalmare simile al burro. Il suo utilizzo nella cucina contemporanea รจ diminuito; tuttavia, molti cuochi e fornai contemporanei lo preferiscono ad altri grassi per usi selezionati. Le qualitร culinarie del lardo variano leggermente a seconda della parte del maiale da cui รจ stato prelevato il grasso e di come รจ stato lavorato il lardo.

TEXT.Russian: รร รซรฎ - รฝรฒรฎ รฑรขรจรญรฎรฉ รฆรจรฐ รฑ รญร รซรจรทรจรฅรฌ รจรซรจ รฎรฒรฑรณรฒรฑรฒรขรจรฅรฌ รฌรฟรฑรญรปรต รฏรฐรฎรฑรซรฎรฅรช. รรขรจรญรฎรฅ รฑร รซรฎ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รชรณรตรญรฟรต รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รญร รฐรฎรครฎรข รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รชรณรซรจรญร รฐรญรฎรฃรฎ รฆรจรฐร รจรซรจ รฌร รฑรซร , รฏรฎรครฎรกรญรฎรฃรฎ รฑรซรจรขรฎรทรญรฎรฌรณ. ร รฃรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรฅ รข รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรฎรฉ รชรณรตรญรฅ รณรฌรฅรญรผรธรจรซร รฑรผ; รฒรฅรฌ รญรฅ รฌรฅรญรฅรฅ, รฌรญรฎรฃรจรฅ รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรฅ รฏรฎรขร รฐร รจ รฏรฅรชร รฐรจ รฎรฒรฌรฅรทร รพรฒ รฅรฃรฎ รฏรฎรซรผรงรณ รฏรฎ รฑรฐร รขรญรฅรญรจรพ รฑ รครฐรณรฃรจรฌรจ รฆรจรฐร รฌรจ รฏรฐรจ รขรปรกรฎรฐรฅ รจรญรฃรฐรฅรครจรฅรญรฒรฎรข. รรณรซรจรญร รฐรญรปรฅ รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขร รฑร รซร รญรฅรฑรชรฎรซรผรชรฎ รฐร รงรซรจรทร รพรฒรฑรฟ รข รงร รขรจรฑรจรฌรฎรฑรฒรจ รฎรฒ รทร รฑรฒรฅรฉ รฑรขรจรญรผรจ, รจรง รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฉ รกรปรซ รจรงรขรซรฅรทรฅรญ รฆรจรฐ รจ รฑร รซรฎ, รจ รชร รชรจรฌ รฎรกรฐร รงรฎรฌ รฎรญ รกรปรซ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒร รญ.

TEXT.English: Lazulite is a blue, phosphate mineral containing magnesium, iron, and aluminium phosphate. Lazulite forms one endmember of a solid solution series with the darker iron rich scorzalite.
TEXT.French: La lazulite est un minรฉral phosphatรฉ bleu contenant du phosphate de magnรฉsium, de fer et d'aluminium. La lazulite forme un pรดle d'une sรฉrie de solution solide avec la scorzalite plus sombre riche en fer.
TEXT.Italian: La lazulite รจ un minerale fosfato blu contenente magnesio, ferro e fosfato di alluminio. La lazulite forma un membro terminale di una serie di soluzioni solide con la scorzalite piรน scura ricca di ferro

TEXT.English: Lead, symbol Pb (Latin plumbum, a lead weight), dense, bluish-grey metallic element that was one of the first known metals. The atomic number of lead is 82; the element is in group 14 (or IVa) of the periodic table. Lead is used in enormous quantities in storage batteries and in sheathing electric cables. Large quantities are used in industry for lining pipes, tanks, and X-ray apparatus. Because of its high density and nuclear properties, lead is used extensively as protective shielding for radioactive material. Among numerous alloys containing a high percentage of lead are solder, type metal, and various bearing metals. A considerable amount of lead is consumed in the form of its compounds, particularly in paints and pigments. Lead has been used by humans for at least 7000 years, because it is widespread, easy to extract and easy to work with. It is highly malleable and ductile as well as easy to melt. In the early bronze age lead was used with antimony and arsenic. Lead was mentioned in the Book of Exodus. Alchemists thought that lead was the oldest metal and associated it with the planet Saturn. Lead pipes that bear the insignia of Roman emperors are still in service and many Roman "pigs" (ingots) of lead figure in Derbyshire lead mining history and in the history of the industry in other English centres. Lead's symbol Pb is an abbreviation of its Latin name plumbum. The English word "plumbing" also derives from this Latin root. However, it is also toxic, and lead poisoning was recognized even by the ancients. Similarly, in the Twentieth Century, the use of lead in paint pigments was ended because of the danger of lead poisoning, especially to children. By the mid-1980s, a significant shift in lead end-use patterns had taken place. Much of this shift was a result of the U.S. lead consumers' compliance with environmental regulations that significantly reduced or eliminated the use of lead in nonbattery products, including gasoline, paints, solders, and water systems. Recently, lead use is being further curtailed by the RoHS directive.
TEXT.French: Plomb, Pb de symbole (plumbum latin, un poids de plomb), l'รฉlรฉment mรฉtallique dense et gris-bleuรขtre qui รฉtait l'un des premiers mรฉtaux connus. Le nombre atomique de plomb est 82 ; l'รฉlรฉment est dans le groupe 14 (ou IVa) de la table pรฉriodique. Le plomb est employรฉ en รฉnorme quantitรฉ dans des batteries de stockage et en engainant les cรขbles รฉlectriques. De grandes quantitรฉs sont employรฉes dans l'industrie pour des pipes de doublure, des rรฉservoirs, et l'appareillage de rayon X. En raison de ses propriรฉtรฉs ร haute densitรฉ et nuclรฉaires, le plomb est employรฉ intensivement comme l'armature protectrice pour le matรฉriel radioactif. Parmi de nombreux alliages contenant un pourcentage รฉlevรฉ de plomb soyez soudure, type mรฉtal, et divers mรฉtaux de roulement. Une quantitรฉ considรฉrable de plomb est consommรฉe sous forme de ses composรฉs, en particulier en peintures et colorants. Le plomb a รฉtรฉ employรฉ par des humains pendant au moins 7000 annรฉes, parce qu'il est rรฉpandu, facile d'extraire et facile ร travailler avec. Il est fortement mallรฉable et mallรฉable comme facile ร fondre. Dans l'รขge en bronze jeune le plomb a รฉtรฉ employรฉ avec de l'antimoine et l'arsenic. Le plomb a รฉtรฉ mentionnรฉ dans le livre de l'exode. Les alchimistes ont pensรฉ que menez รฉtait le mรฉtal le plus ancien et associรฉ lui ร la planรจte Saturne. Menez les pipes qui soutiennent les insignes des empereurs romains sont toujours en service et beaucoup de "porcs" romains (des lingots) de chiffre de plomb en plomb de Derbyshire extrayant l'histoire et dans l'histoire de l'industrie ร d'autres centres anglais. Le Pb du symbole du plomb est une abrรฉviation de son plumbum nommรฉ latin. Le mot anglais "tuyauterie" dรฉrive รฉgalement de cette racine latine. Cependant, il est รฉgalement toxique, et l'empoisonnement de plomb a รฉtรฉ identifiรฉ mรชme par les ancients. De mรชme, au vingtiรจme siรจcle, l'utilisation du plomb en colorants de peinture a รฉtรฉ finie en raison du danger du plomb empoisonnant, particuliรจrement aux enfants. Par le milieu des annรฉes 80, une variation significative dans l'usage final de plomb a modelรฉ avait eu lieu. Beaucoup de ce dรฉcalage รฉtait un rรฉsultat de la conformitรฉ des consommateurs de plomb des ETATS-UNIS aux rรจglements environnementaux qui ont rรฉduit ou ont รฉliminรฉ de maniรจre significative l'utilisation du plomb dans des produits de nonbattery, y compris l'essence, des peintures, des soudures, et des systรจmes de l'eau. Rรฉcemment, l'utilisation de plomb est encore raccourcie par la directive de RoHS.
TEXT.Italian: Il piombo, simbolo Pb (in latino plumbum, un'unitร di peso), รจ un elemento metallico denso e grigio bluastro che fu uno dei primi metalli conosciuti. Il numero atomico del piombo รจ 82; l'elemento รจ nel gruppo 14 della tavola periodica. Il piombo รจ usato in quantitร enorme nelle batterie di accumulo e nell'inguainamento dei cavi elettrici, per il rivestimento dei tubi, i carri armati e le apparecchiature a raggi X. A causa della sua alta densitร e delle sue proprietร nucleari, il piombo รจ usato estesamente come scudo protettivo dai materiali radioattivi. Una quantitร considerevole di piombo รจ consumata sotto forma dei suoi componenti, specialmente in vernici e pigmenti. Il piombo รจ stato usato dagli esseri umani per almeno 7000 anni, perchรฉ รจ diffuso, facile da estrarre e facile da lavorare. ร altamente malleabile e duttile e facile a fondersi. All'inizio dell'etร del bronzo il piombo รจ stato usato con antimonio ed arsenico. Il piombo รจ stato menzionato nel libro dell'Esodo. Gli alchimisti credevano che fosse il piรน vecchio metallo e lo associavano con il pianeta Saturno. Molti tubi di piombo con il simbolo degli imperatori romani sono ancora in servizio e molti lingotti di piombo figurano nella storia dell'industria in vari centri inglesi. Il simbolo Pb del piombo รจ un'abbreviazione del relativo plumbum latino. La parola inglese "plumbing" deriva anch'essa da questa radice latina. Comunque รจ tossico e l'avvelenamento da piombo era riconosciuto anche dagli antichi. Similmente, nel ventesimo secolo, l'uso di piombo nei pigmenti delle vernici รจ stato interrotto a causa del pericolo di avvelenamento, particolarmente per i bambini. Entro la metร degli anni 80, avvenne una variazione significativa nell'uso del piombo.

(ัะปะธัะบะธ) ัะฒะธะฝัะฐ ะฒ ะธััะพัะธะธ ะดะพะฑััะธ ัะฒะธะฝัะฐ ะะตัะฑะธัะธัะฐ ะธ ะฒ ะธััะพัะธะธ ะฟัะพะผััะปะตะฝะฝะพััะธ ะฒ ะดััะณะธั ะฐะฝะณะปะธะนัะบะธั ัะตะฝััะฐั . TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]O³ów[COLOR_REVERT], symbol Pb (³ac. plumbum, ciê¿arek o³owiany), gêsty, niebieskawo-szary pierwiastek metaliczny, bêd¹cy jednym z pierwszych znanych metali. Do licznych stopów zawieraj¹cych wysoki procent o³owiu nale¿y miêdzy innymi lut. Znaczna iloœæ o³owiu jest zu¿ywana w postaci jego zwi¹zków, szczególnie w farbach i pigmentach. O³ów jest u¿ywany przez cz³owieka od co najmniej 7000 lat, poniewa¿ jest szeroko rozpowszechniony, ³atwy do wydobycia i ³atwy w obróbce. Jest bardzo kowalny i ci¹gliwy, a tak¿e ³atwo siê topi. TEXT.English: Leather is a durable and flexible material created via the tanning of putrescible animal rawhide and skin, primarily cattlehide. It can be produced through different manufacturing processes, ranging from cottage industry to heavy industry.
TEXT.French: Le cuir est un matรฉriau durable et flexible crรฉรฉ par le tannage de peaux et cuirs bruts putrescibles d'animaux, principalement de bovins. Il peut รชtre produit par diffรฉrents processus de fabrication, allant de l'artisanat ร l'industrie lourde.
TEXT.Italian: Il cuoio รจ un materiale durevole e flessibile creato con la conciatura della pelle animale. Puรฒ essere prodotta in diversi modi da una produzione artigiana ad una industriale su grande scala.

รรจรฌรฎรญรป

TEXT.English: The lemon is a citrus tree, a hybrid of cultivated origin. The fruit are cultivated primarily for their juice, though the pulp and rind (zest) are also used, primarily in cooking or mixing. Lemon juice is about 5% citric acid, which gives lemons a sour taste. This is a small tree, grows to 6 m (20 ft) but usually smaller. The branches are thorny, and form an open crown. The leaves are elliptical-acuminate. Flowers are violet and streaked in the interior and white on the outside. On a lemon tree, flowers and ripe fruits can be found at the same time. The first description of the lemon, which had been introduced from India two centuries earlier, is found in Arabic writings from the 12th century. They were cultivated in Genoa in the mid-fifteenth century, and appeared in the Azores in 1494. More recent research has identified lemons in the ruins of Pompeii. Lemons were once used by the British Royal navy to combat scurvy, as they provided a large amount of vitamin C.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Zitronen[COLOR_REVERT] oder Limonen werden die Frรผchte des Zitronenbaums (Citrus ร limon) genannt. Sie sind lรคnglich-oval, etwa faustgroร und von gelber bis grรผn-gelber Farbe. Ihr saftiges, saures Fruchtfleisch enthรคlt viel Vitamin C.[NEWLINE]Die Gattung der Zitruspflanzen stammt aus Asien, vermutlich aus dem Gebiet von Nordostindien und Myanmar sowie Teilen von Sรผdchina. Von dort aus haben sie sich im Laufe der Jahrhunderte verbreitet. Die Zitronatzitrone war die erste Zitrusfrucht, die in Europa eingefรผhrt wurde. Die Nachfolger Alexanders des Groรen brachten sie von Persien nach Kleinasien mit. Etwa im 2. Jahrhundert n.Chr. wurde die Zitrone dann von jรผdischen Einwanderern in Spanien, Griechenland und Italien verbreitet. Gerade die Rรถmer wussten die Frucht, die sie Medischen Apfel nannten, zu schรคtzen. Von den arabischen Siedlern wurden im 9. Jahrhundert in den annektierten Gebieten dann auch echte Zitronen und Bitterorangen (Pomeranzen) angepflanzt. Sie wurden aber selbst zu Zeiten der Renaissance noch weitgehend als Zierpflanze genutzt und kaum in der Kรผche eingesetzt. Wichtig war die Entdeckung, dass Zitrusfrรผchte den Skorbut, der durch Vitaminmangel bei langen Seereisen ausgelรถst wurde, heilen konnten.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cytryna[COLOR_REVERT], to drzewo cytrusowe, hybryda pochodzenia uprawnego. Owoce uprawia siê g³ównie dla soku, chocia¿ wykorzystuje siê tak¿e mi¹¿sz i skórkê, g³ównie do gotowania. Sok cytrynowy zawiera oko³o 5% kwasu cytrynowego, który nadaje cytrynom kwaœny smak. To ma³e drzewo, dorasta do 6 m, ale zwykle jest mniejsze. Ga³êzie s¹ cierniste i tworz¹ otwart¹ koronê. Liœcie s¹ eliptyczno-szpiczaste. Kwiaty s¹ fioletowe z pr¹¿kami wewn¹trz i bia³e na zewn¹trz. Na drzewie cytrynowym mo¿na znaleŸæ jednoczeœnie kwiaty i dojrza³e owoce. Pierwszy opis cytryny, która zosta³a sprowadzona z Indii dwa wieki wczeœniej, znajduje siê w pismach arabskich z XII wieku. Uprawiano je w Genui w po³owie XV wieku, a na Azorach pojawi³y siê w 1494 roku. Nowsze badania zidentyfikowa³y cytryny w ruinach Pompejów. Cytryny by³y kiedyœ u¿ywane przez brytyjsk¹ marynarkê królewsk¹ do zwalczania szkorbutu, poniewa¿ dostarcza³y du¿ej iloœci witaminy C.

TEXT.English: While levitation trough usage of room superconductors, magnetics, electricity, acoustics and optics was theoretically possible earlier, it wasn't economically feasible to do as energy requirements were enormous. Now with fusion reactors it is possible to use levitation anywhere and everywhere. That is levitation is no longer limited to maglev trains.
TEXT.French: Bien que la lรฉvitation par l'utilisation de supraconducteurs ร tempรฉrature ambiante, de magnรฉtisme, d'รฉlectricitรฉ, d'acoustique et d'optique fรปt thรฉoriquement possible auparavant, ce n'รฉtait pas รฉconomiquement rรฉalisable car les besoins en รฉnergie รฉtaient รฉnormes. Maintenant, avec les rรฉacteurs ร fusion, il est possible d'utiliser la lรฉvitation n'importe oรน et partout. C'est-ร -dire que la lรฉvitation n'est plus limitรฉe aux trains ร sustentation magnรฉtique.

TEXT.English: The term "Liangzhu Jades" refers to finely worked large ritual jades, commonly incised with the taotie motif related to the Liangzhu culture. The most exemplary artifacts from the culture were its cong (cylinders), the largest of which weighed 3.5 kg (7.72 lb). Bi (discs), Yue axes (ceremonial axes), and pendants were also found. These artifacts are said to have been highly influential on the development of other neolithic cultures in China, and on production of ritual objects in later periods of Chinese culture.
TEXT.Italian: Il termine "Giada di Liangzhu" si riferisce a grandi oggetti rituali in giada, finemente lavorati e comunemente incisi con un taotie, un motivo rituale legato alla cultura di Liangzhu. I manufatti piรน esemplari di questa cultura furono i suoi cong (cilindri), il piรน grande dei quali pesava 3,5 kg. Sono stati trovati anche bi (dischi), assi yue (assi cerimoniali) e pendenti. Si dice che questi manufatti abbiano avuto un'influenza notevole sullo sviluppo di altre culture neolitiche in Cina e sulla produzione di oggetti rituali nei periodi successivi della cultura cinese.

TEXT.Polish: Termin [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Jadeity z Lianghzu[COLOR_REVERT] odnosi siê do drobno obrobionych, du¿ych jadeitów rytualnych, powszechnie z wyciêtych motywem ŒtaotieŒ zwi¹zanym z kultur¹ Liangzhu. Najbardziej widowiskowymi artefaktami by³y cong (cylindry), z których najwiêkszy wa¿y³ 3,5 kg. Znaleziono tak¿e Bi (dyski), topory Yue (osie ceremonialne) i wisiorki. Uwa¿a siê, ¿e te artefakty wywar³y du¿y wp³yw na rozwój innych kultur neolitycznych w Chinach oraz na produkcjê przedmiotów rytualnych w póŸniejszych okresach kultury chiñskiej.

TEXT.English: A liquid metal consists of alloys with very low melting points. The standard metal used to be mercury, but gallium-based alloys, which are lower both in their vapor pressure at room temperature and toxicity, are being used as a replacement in various applications. Alloy systems that are liquid at room temperature have thermal conductivity far superior to ordinary non-metallic liquids, and a higher electrical conductivity that allows the liquid to be pumped by more efficient electromagnetic pumps. Typical uses of liquid metals include thermostats, switches, barometers, heat transfer systems, and thermal cooling and heating designs. Starting from 21st century, also wearable devices and IoT applications made use of liquid metals.
TEXT.Italian: Un metallo liquido รจ costituito da leghe con punti di fusione molto bassi. Il metallo piรน usato inizialmente era il mercurio, ma le leghe a base di gallio, che hanno una pressione dei vapori a temperatura ambiente e una tossicitร inferiore, lo hanno gradualmente sostituito. Questo tipo di leghe liquide a temperatura ambiente hanno una conduttivitร termica di gran lunga superiore ai normali liquidi non metallici e una maggiore conduttivitร elettrica, che consente al liquido di essere pompato piรน efficientemente da pompe elettromagnetiche. Gli usi tipici dei metalli liquidi includono termostati, interruttori, barometri, sistemi di trasferimento di calore e impianti di raffreddamento e riscaldamento termici. A partire dal XXI secolo, anche i dispositivi indossabili e le applicazioni IoT hanno iniziato a utilizzarli.

TEXT.English: Lithium is a chemical element with symbol Li and atomic number 3. It is a soft, silver-white metal belonging to the alkali metal group of chemical elements. Under standard conditions it is the lightest metal and the least dense solid element. Like all alkali metals, lithium is highly reactive and flammable. For this reason, it is typically stored in mineral oil. When cut open, lithium exhibits a metallic luster, but contact with moist air corrodes the surface quickly to a dull silvery gray, then black tarnish. Because of its high reactivity, lithium never occurs freely in nature, and instead, only appears in compounds, which are usually ionic. Lithium occurs in a number of pegmatitic minerals, but due to its solubility as an ion is present in ocean water and is commonly obtained from brines and clays. On a commercial scale, lithium is isolated electrolytically from a mixture of lithium chloride and potassium chloride.
TEXT.Russian: รรจรฒรจรฉ (รฎรฒ รฃรฐรฅรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รรจรฒรฎรฑ 'รชร รฌรฅรญรผ') - รฝรฒรฎ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฉ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒ รฑ รฑรจรฌรขรฎรซรฎรฌ Li รจ ร รฒรฎรฌรญรปรฌ รญรฎรฌรฅรฐรฎรฌ 3. รรฒรฎ รฌรฟรฃรชรจรฉ, รฑรฅรฐรฅรกรฐรจรฑรฒรฎ-รกรฅรซรปรฉ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซ, รฎรฒรญรฎรฑรฟรนรจรฉรฑรฟ รช รฃรฐรณรฏรฏรฅ รนรฅรซรฎรทรญรปรต รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรฎรข รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรต รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒรฎรข. รรฐรจ รฑรฒร รญรคร รฐรฒรญรปรต รณรฑรซรฎรขรจรฟรต รฎรญ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑร รฌรปรฌ รซรฅรฃรชรจรฌ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรฎรฌ รจ รญร รจรฌรฅรญรฅรฅ รฏรซรฎรฒรญรปรฌ รจรง รฒรขรฅรฐรครปรต รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒรฎรข. รร รช รจ รขรฑรฅ รนรฅรซรฎรทรญรปรฅ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรป, รซรจรฒรจรฉ รฎรกรซร รคร รฅรฒ รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรฉ รฐรฅร รชรถรจรฎรญรญรฎรฉ รฑรฏรฎรฑรฎรกรญรฎรฑรฒรผรพ, รซรฅรฃรชรฎ รขรฎรฑรฏรซร รฌรฅรญรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ. รรฎ รฝรฒรฎรฉ รฏรฐรจรทรจรญรฅ, รฎรญร รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รตรฐร รญรจรฒรฑรฟ รข รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซรผรญรฎรฌ รฌร รฑรซรฅ. รรฐรจ รญร รครฐรฅรงรฅ, รซรจรฒรจรฉ รจรฌรฅรฅรฒ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฉ รกรซรฅรฑรช, รญรฎ รจ รชรฎรญรฒร รชรฒ รฑ รขรซร รฆรญรปรฌ รขรฎรงรครณรตรฎรฌ รกรปรฑรฒรฐรฎ รฐร รงรบรฅรคร รฅรฒ รฏรฎรขรฅรฐรตรญรฎรฑรฒรผ รครฎ รณรญรปรซรฎรฃรฎ รฑรฅรฐรฅรกรฐรจรฑรฒรฎ-รฑรฅรฐรฎรฃรฎ รถรขรฅรฒร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รงร รฒรฅรฌ รฒรณรฑรชรญรฅรฅรฒ รครฎรทรฅรฐรญร . รรง-รงร รฑรขรฎรฅรฉ รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรฉ รฐรฅร รชรฒรจรขรญรฎรฑรฒรจ, รซรจรฒรจรฉ รญรฅ รขรฑรฒรฐรฅรทร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รขรจรครฅ รฑร รฌรฎรฐรฎรครชรฎรข รข รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครฅ, รจ รฏรฎรฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รซรจรธรผ รข รฑรฎรฅรครจรญรฅรญรจรฟรต, รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ รข รจรงรฎรฒรฎรฏร รต. รรจรฒรจรฉ รขรฑรฒรฐรฅรทร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รฐรฟรครฅ รฏรฅรฃรฌร รฒรจรฒรฎรขรปรต รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซรฎรข, รญรฎ รจรง-รงร รฑรขรฎรฅรฉ รฐร รฑรฒรขรฎรฐรจรฌรฎรฑรฒรจ รข รขรจรครฅ รจรฎรญรฎรข รฏรฐรจรฑรณรฒรฑรฒรขรณรฅรฒ รข รฎรชรฅร รญรฑรชรฎรฉ รขรฎรครฅ รจ รทร รนรฅ รขรฑรฅรฃรฎ รจรงรขรซรฅรชร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รจรง รฐร รฑรฑรฎรซรฎรข รจ รฃรซรจรญ. ร รฏรฐรฎรฌรปรธรซรฅรญรญรปรต รฌร รฑรธรฒร รกร รต, รซรจรฒรจรฉ รขรปรครฅรซรฟรพรฒ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรซรจรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ รจรง รฑรฌรฅรฑรจ รตรซรฎรฐรจรคร รซรจรฒรจรฟ รจ รตรซรฎรฐรจรคร รชร รซรจรฟ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Lit[COLOR_REVERT], to pierwiastek chemiczny o symbolu Li i liczbie atomowej 3. Jest to miêkki, srebrzystobia³y metal nale¿¹cy do grupy pierwiastków chemicznych zawieraj¹cych metale alkaliczne. W normalnych warunkach jest to najl¿ejszy metal i najmniej gêsty pierwiastek sta³y. Podobnie jak wszystkie metale alkaliczne, lit jest wysoce reaktywny i ³atwopalny. Z tego powodu zazwyczaj przechowuje siê go w oleju mineralnym. Po rozciêciu lit wykazuje metaliczny po³ysk, ale kontakt z wilgotnym powietrzem szybko powoduje korozjê powierzchni, nadaj¹c jej matowy srebrzystoszary, a nastêpnie czarny nalot. Ze wzglêdu na swoj¹ wysok¹ reaktywnoœæ lit nigdy nie wystêpuje w przyrodzie w postaci wolnej, a zamiast tego wystêpuje jedynie w zwi¹zkach, które zwykle s¹ jonowe. Lit wystêpuje w wielu minera³ach pegmatytycznych, ale ze wzglêdu na swoj¹ rozpuszczalnoœæ jako jon jest obecny w wodzie oceanicznej i jest powszechnie otrzymywany z solanek i glin. Na skalê przemys³ow¹ lit jest izolowany elektrolitycznie z mieszaniny chlorku litu i chlorku potasu.

TEXT.English: Lizards are squamate reptiles that generally have four legs and external ears.

TEXT.Russian: รร รฌรป

TEXT.English: The llama (Lama glama) is a South American camelid, widely used as a meat and pack animal by Andean cultures since pre-Hispanic times. The Moche people frequently placed llamas and llama parts in the burials of important people, as offerings or provisions for the afterlife. The Moche culture of pre-Columbian Peru depicted llamas quite realistically in their ceramics. In the Inca empire, llamas were the only beasts of burden, and many of the peoples dominated by the Inca had long traditions of llama herding. For the Inca nobility, the llama was of symbolic significance, and llama figures were often buried with the dead. In South America, llamas are still used as beasts of burden, as well as for the production of fibre and meat.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Lamas[COLOR_REVERT] sind eine beliebte Nutztierart in den sรผdamerikanischen Anden, die zur Familie der Kamele zรคhlen, aber im Gegensatz zu ihren Altweltvettern รผber keine Hรถcker verfรผgen. Sie wurden bereits vor mehr als 5000 Jahren aus den wilden Guanakos domestiziert und waren lange Zeit das einzige verfรผgbare Lasttier auf dem amerikanischen Kontinent. Auรerdem wurden Wolle und Fleisch des Lamas genutzt und รคhnlich wie beim Kamel dienten die Exkremente als guter Brennstoff, wรคhrend die Haut fรผr Lederwaren und das Fett zur Kerzenherstellung verwendet wurde. Heute werdem Lamas hauptsรคchlich wegen ihrer Wolle und ihres Fleisches gezรผchtet, inzwischen sogar in Europa.

TEXT.English: Lobsters are economically important as seafood, forming the basis of a global industry that nets more than 1 billion dollars annually. In North America, the American lobster did not achieve popularity until the mid-19th century, when New Yorkers and Bostonians developed a taste for it; not until the invention of a special boat, the lobster smack, did a commercial fishery flourish. The American Lobster is fished from Canada to New Jersey. In Massachusetts, the near-shore lobster fishery consists of independent fishermen who operate their own vessels. Lobster is the most valuable species caught in Massachusetts waters. Lobsters can be air-shipped anywhere in the world and arrive alive, making them the most expensive.

TEXT.English: Lysergic acid diethylamide, abbreviated LSD or LSD-25, also known as lysergide (INN) and colloquially as acid, is a semisynthetic psychedelic drug of the ergoline family, well known for its psychological effects which can include altered thinking processes, closed and open eye visuals, synesthesia, an altered sense of time and spiritual experiences, as well as for its key role in 1960s counterculture. It is used mainly as an entheogen, recreational drug, and as an agent in psychedelic therapy. LSD is non-addictive, is not known to cause brain damage, and has extremely low toxicity relative to dose. However, adverse psychiatric reactions such as anxiety, paranoia, and delusions are possible.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dietyloamid kwasu D-lizergowego (LSD, LSD-25)[COLOR_REVERT], potocznie znany jako kwas, jest pó³syntetycznym lekiem psychedelicznym z rodziny ergolin, dobrze znanym ze swoich skutków psychologicznych, które mog¹ obejmowaæ zmienione procesy myœlenia, wizualizacje przy zamkniêtych i otwartych oczach, synestezjê, zmienione poczucie czasu i doœwiadczeñ duchowych. Stosowany jest g³ównie jako enteogen, narkotyk rekreacyjny oraz jako œrodek w terapii psychodelicznej. LSD nie powoduje uzale¿nienia, nie powoduje uszkodzeñ mózgu i ma wyj¹tkowo nisk¹ toksycznoœæ w stosunku do dawki. Jednak¿e mo¿liwe s¹ niepo¿¹dane reakcje psychiczne, takie jak lêk, paranoja i urojenia.

TEXT.English: Lye is a term that can refer to the liquid obtained by leaching ashes (containing largely potassium carbonate or "potash"), or to a strong alkali which is highly soluble in water producing caustic basic solutions. It is commonly the alternative name of sodium hydroxide or historically potassium hydroxide.
TEXT.French: La lessive est un terme qui peut dรฉsigner le liquide obtenu par lixiviation des cendres (contenant principalement du carbonate de potassium ou "potasse"), ou un alcali fort qui est hautement soluble dans l'eau produisant des solutions basiques caustiques. C'est communรฉment le nom alternatif de l'hydroxyde de sodium ou historiquement de l'hydroxyde de potassium.

TEXT.English: Machu Picchu is a 15th-century Inca citadel located in the Eastern Cordillera of southern Peru on a 2,430-meter (7,970 ft) mountain ridge. It is located in the Machupicchu District within Urubamba Province above the Sacred Valley, which is 80 kilometers (50 mi) northwest of Cuzco. The Urubamba River flows past it, cutting through the Cordillera and creating a canyon with a tropical mountain climate. For most speakers of English or Spanish, the first 'c' in Picchu is silent. The Incas, in contrast with the Mayans, had no written language, and no European visited the site until the 19th century, so far as is known. There are, therefore, no written records of the site while it was in use. The names of the buildings, their supposed uses, and their inhabitants are all the product of modern archeologists, on the basis of physical evidence, including tombs at the site.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Machu Picchu[COLOR_REVERT], to XV-wieczna cytadela Inków po³o¿ona we wschodniej Kordylierii w po³udniowym Peru, na grzbiecie górskim o wysokoœci 2430 metrów. Znajduje siê w dystrykcie Machupicchu w prowincji Urubamba, nad Œwiêt¹ Dolin¹, 80 kilometrów na pó³nocny zachód od Cuzco. Przep³ywa obok niej rzeka Urubamba, przecinaj¹c Kordylierê i tworz¹c kanion o tropikalnym, górskim klimacie. Dla wiêkszoœci osób mówi¹cych po angielsku lub hiszpañsku pierwsze "c" w jêzyku Picchu jest ciche. Inkowie, w przeciwieñstwie do Majów, nie znali jêzyka pisanego i, o ile wiadomo, ¿aden Europejczyk nie odwiedzi³ tego miejsca a¿ do XIX wieku. Nie ma zatem ¿adnych pisemnych wzmianek o tym miejscu w okresie jego u¿ytkowania. Nazwy budynków, ich rzekome przeznaczenie i mieszkañcy s¹ dzie³em wspó³czesnych archeologów na podstawie dowodów fizycznych, w tym grobowców.

TEXT.English: The free element (metal) is not found naturally on Earth, as it is highly reactive (though once produced, it is coated in a thin layer of oxide (see passivation), which partly masks this reactivity). Magnesium is the eighth-most-abundant element in the Earth's crust by mass and tied in seventh place with iron in terms of molarity. It is found in large deposits of magnesite, dolomite, and other minerals, and in mineral waters, where magnesium ion is soluble.
TEXT.French: L'รฉlรฉment libre (mรฉtal) ne se trouve pas naturellement sur Terre, car il est hautement rรฉactif (bien qu'une fois produit, il soit recouvert d'une fine couche d'oxyde (voir passivation), qui masque partiellement cette rรฉactivitรฉ). Le magnรฉsium est le huitiรจme รฉlรฉment le plus abondant dans la croรปte terrestre en masse et se classe au septiรจme rang avec le fer en termes de molaritรฉ. On le trouve dans de grands gisements de magnรฉsite, de dolomite et d'autres minรฉraux, ainsi que dans les eaux minรฉrales, oรน l'ion magnรฉsium est soluble.

TEXT.English: A magnetic monopole is a hypothetical elementary particle in particle physics that is an isolated magnet with only one magnetic pole (a north pole without a south pole or vice versa). In more technical terms, a magnetic monopole would have a net "magnetic charge". Modern interest in the concept stems from particle theories, notably the grand unified and superstring theories, which predict their existence.
TEXT.French: Un monopรดle magnรฉtique est une particule รฉlรฉmentaire hypothรฉtique en physique des particules qui est un aimant isolรฉ avec un seul pรดle magnรฉtique (un pรดle nord sans pรดle sud ou vice versa). En termes plus techniques, un monopรดle magnรฉtique aurait une "charge magnรฉtique" nette. L'intรฉrรชt moderne pour ce concept provient des thรฉories des particules, notamment les thรฉories de grande unification et des supercordes, qui prรฉdisent leur existence.

Wspó³czesne zainteresowanie t¹ koncepcj¹ wywodzi siê z teorii cz¹stek, zw³aszcza teorii wielkiej unifikacji i teorii superstrun, które przewiduj¹ ich istnienie.

TEXT.English: A mammoth is any species of the extinct genus Mammuthus. These proboscideans are members of Elephantidae, the family of elephants and mammoths, and close relatives of modern elephants. They were often equipped with long curved tusks and, in northern species, a covering of long hair. They lived from the Pliocene Epoch from around 4.8 million years ago, into the Holocene at about 4,500 years ago. The largest known species, Songhua River mammoth (Mammuthus sungari), reached heights of at least 5 metres (16 ft) at the shoulder. Mammoths would probably normally weigh in the region of 6 to 8 tons, but exceptionally large males may have exceeded 12 tons. However, most species of mammoth were only about as large as a modern Asian elephant. Fossils of species of dwarf mammoth have been found on the Californian Channel Islands (Mammuthus exilis) and the Mediterranean island of Sardinia (Mammuthus lamarmorae). There was also a race of dwarf woolly mammoths on Wrangel Island, north of Siberia, within the Arctic Circle.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Mammuts[COLOR_REVERT] sind eine ausgestorbene Gattung aus der Familie der Elefanten, von denen sich die meisten Arten durch eine gewaltige Kรถrpergrรถรe auszeichneten. Die frรผhen Menschen kamen hauptsรคchlich mit dem Wollhaarmammut in Kontakt, das sie bejagten oder zumindest schlachteten. Neben dem Fleisch wurden wie beim Elefanten auch Knochen und Elfenbein der Tiere verarbeitet. Mammutknochenhรคuser wurden z.B. bei Ausgrabungen an verschiedenen Stellen in der Ukraine entdeckt.
