ยซะผะฐะผะพะฝัยป ััะฐะปะพ ะฟัะธะปะฐะณะฐัะตะปัะฝัะผ, ะพะทะฝะฐัะฐััะธะผ ยซะฑะพะปััะพะนยป ะธะปะธ ยซะผะฐััะธะฒะฝัะนยป. ะกะฐะผัะน ะบััะฟะฝัะน ะธะท ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัั ะฒะธะดะพะฒ, ะผะฐะผะพะฝั ัะตะบะธ ะกัะฝะณะฐัะธ (Mammuthus sungari), ะดะพััะธะณะฐะป ะฒััะพัั ะฝะต ะผะตะฝะตะต 5 ะผะตััะพะฒ (16 ัััะพะฒ) ะฒ ะฟะปะตัะต. ะะฐะผะพะฝัั, ะฒะตัะพััะฝะพ, ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะฒะตัะธะปะธ ะฑั ะพั 6 ะดะพ 8 ัะพะฝะฝ, ะฝะพ ะธัะบะปััะธัะตะปัะฝะพ ะบััะฟะฝัะต ัะฐะผัั ะผะพะณะปะธ ะฟัะตะฒััะฐัั 12 ัะพะฝะฝ. ะะดะฝะฐะบะพ ะฑะพะปััะธะฝััะฒะพ ะฒะธะดะพะฒ Czêsto by³y wyposa¿one w d³ugie zakrzywione k³y, a u gatunków pó³nocnych w os³onê d³ugich w³osów. ¯y³y od epoki pliocenu sprzed oko³o 4,8 miliona lat do holocenu oko³o 4500 lat temu. Najwiêkszy znany gatunek, mamut rzeczny Songhua (Mammuthus sungari), osi¹ga³ wysokoœæ co najmniej 5 metrów w k³êbie. Mamuty zwykle wa¿y³y od 6 do 8 ton, ale wyj¹tkowo du¿e samce mog³y przekraczaæ 12 ton. Jednak wiêkszoœæ gatunków mamutów by³a mniej wiêcej tak du¿a jak wspó³czesny s³oñ azjatycki. TEXT.English: Manganese is a chemical element, designated by the symbol Mn. It has the atomic number 25. It is found as a free element in nature (often in combination with iron), and in many minerals. Manganese is a metal with important industrial metal alloy uses, particularly in stainless steels. Historically, manganese is named for various black minerals (such as pyrolusite) from the same region of Magnesia in Greece which gave names to similar-sounding magnesium, Mg, and magnetite, an ore of the element iron, Fe. By the mid-18th century, Swedish chemist Carl Wilhelm Scheele had used pyrolusite to produce chlorine. Scheele and others were aware that pyrolusite (now known to be manganese dioxide) contained a new element, but they were not able to isolate it. Johan Gottlieb Gahn was the first to isolate an impure sample of manganese metal in 1774, by reducing the dioxide with carbon.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Mangan[COLOR_REVERT], to pierwiastek chemiczny nale¿¹cy w uk³adzie okresowym do grupy metali przejœciowych. Ma 15 izotopów z przedzia³u mas 49-62 i izomery j¹drowe 51m, 52m, 54m. Trwa³y jest tylko izotop 55, który stanowi niemal 100% sk³adu izotopowego manganu wystêpuj¹cego w naturze.
TEXT.Russian: รร รฐรฃร รญรฅรถ - รฝรฒรฎ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฉ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒ, รฎรกรฎรงรญร รทรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฑรจรฌรขรฎรซรฎรฌ Mn. รรญ รจรฌรฅรฅรฒ ร รฒรฎรฌรญรปรฉ รญรฎรฌรฅรฐ 25. รรญ รขรฑรฒรฐรฅรทร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รชร รช รฑรขรฎรกรฎรครญรปรฉ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒ รข รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครฅ (รทร รฑรฒรฎ รข รฑรฎรทรฅรฒร รญรจรจ รฑ รฆรฅรซรฅรงรฎรฌ) รจ รขรฎ รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซร รต. รรฅรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฉ รฌร รฐรฃร รญรฅรถ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑ รขร รฆรญรปรฌรจ รฏรฐรฎรฌรปรธรซรฅรญรญรปรฌรจ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฌรจ รฑรฏรซร รขร รฌรจ, รข รทร รฑรฒรญรฎรฑรฒรจ รฑ รญรฅรฐรฆร รขรฅรพรนรจรฌรจ รฑรฒร รซรฟรฌรจ.รรฑรฒรฎรฐรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ, รฌร รฐรฃร รญรฅรถ รญร รงรขร รญ รข รทรฅรฑรฒรผ รทรฅรฐรญรปรต รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซรฎรข (รฒร รชรจรต รชร รช รฏรจรฐรฎรซรพรงรจรฒ) รจรง รฒรฎรฉ รฆรฅ รฎรกรซร รฑรฒรจ รร รฃรญรฅรงรจรฟ รข รรฐรฅรถรจรจ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รคร รซ รจรฌรฅรญร รกรซรจรงรชรจรฅ รฏรฎ รงรขรณรทร รญรจรพ รฌร รฃรญรจรฟ, Mg, รจ รฌร รฃรญรฅรฒรจรฒรณ, รฐรณรครป รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒร รฆรฅรซรฅรงร , FE. ร รฑรฅรฐรฅรครจรญรฅ 18 รขรฅรชร รธรขรฅรครฑรชรจรฉ รตรจรฌรจรช รร รฐรซ รรจรซรผรฃรฅรซรผรฌ รรฅรฅรซรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒ รฏรจรฐรฎรซรพรงรจรฒ รครซรฟ รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครฑรฒรขร รตรซรฎรฐร . รรฅรฅรซรฅ รจ รครฐรณรฃรจรฅ รตรจรฌรจรชรจ รกรปรซรจ รข รชรณรฐรฑรฅ, รทรฒรฎ รข รฏรจรฐรฎรซรพรงรจรฒรฅ (รฒรฅรฏรฅรฐรผ รจรงรขรฅรฑรฒรฅรญ, รชร รช รครจรฎรชรฑรจรค รฌร รฐรฃร รญรถร ), รฑรฎรครฅรฐรฆร รนรจรฒรฑรฟ รญรฎรขรปรฉ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒ, รญรฎ รฎรญรจ รกรปรซรจ รญรฅ รข รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรญรจรจ รขรปรครฅรซรจรฒรผ รฅรฃรฎ. รรฎรตร รญ รรฎรฒรฒรซรจรก รร รญ รกรปรซ รฏรฅรฐรขรปรฌ, รขรปรครฅรซรจรขรธรจรฉ รญรฅรฎรทรจรนรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฎรกรฐร รงรฅรถ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฌร รฐรฃร รญรถร รข 1774 รฃรฎรครณ รงร รฑรทรฅรฒ รขรปรฌรปรขร รญรจรฟ รครจรฎรชรฑรจรคร รณรฃรซรฅรฐรฎรคร .

TEXT.English: The mango is a fleshy stone fruit belonging to the genus Mangifera, consisting of numerous tropical fruiting trees in the flowering plant family Anacardiaceae. The mango is native to India from where it spread all over the world. It is one of the most cultivated fruits of the tropical world. While other Mangifera species are also grown on a more localized basis, Mangifera indica the common mango or Indian mango is the only mango tree commonly cultivated in many tropical and subtropical regions, and its fruit is distributed essentially worldwide. In several cultures, its fruit and leaves are ritually used as floral decorations at weddings, public celebrations and religious ceremonies.
TEXT.German: Die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Mango[COLOR_REVERT] (Mangifera indica) gehรถrt zur Familie der Sumachgewรคchse (Anacardiaceae). Der Mangobaum ist immergrรผn, schnellwรผchsig und langlebig und kann bis zu 300 der ei- bis birnenfรถrmigen Frรผchte tragen. Er stammt aus Borneo und einem Gebiet zwischen Indien und Myanmar und ist in tropischen Regenwรคldern heimisch. Die Mango wird bereits in den hinduistischen Veden (etwa 1200 v.Chr.) als Gรถtterspeise erwรคhnt und auch heute opfert man sie noch den Gรถttern. In Europa werden die Bรคume hauptsรคchlich in Spanien angebaut. Mangos werden nicht nur als Nahrungsmittel genutzt, das Pflanzenfett aus den Samen kann รคhnlich wie Kakaobutter zur Herstellung von Schokolade, Margarine und Pflegeprodukten verwendet werden. Auรerdem soll die Mango gegen Entzรผndungen, Diabetes und Reizdarm wirken und das Herz stรคrken.
TEXT.Russian: ะะฐะฝะณะพ - ััะพ ะผััะธัััะน ะบะพััะพัะบะพะฒัะน ะฟะปะพะด, ะฟัะธะฝะฐะดะปะตะถะฐัะธะน ะบ ัะพะดั Mangifera, ัะพััะพััะตะผั ะธะท ะผะฝะพะณะพัะธัะปะตะฝะฝัั ะฟะปะพะดะพะฝะพัััะธั ััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะธั ะดะตัะตะฒัะตะฒ ัะตะผะตะนััะฒะฐ ัะฒะตัะบะพะฒัั ัะฐััะตะฝะธะน Anacardiaceae. ะะฐะฝะณะพ ัะพะดะพะผ ะธะท ะะฝะดะธะธ, ะพัะบัะดะฐ ัะฐัะฟัะพัััะฐะฝะธะปะพัั ะฟะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะผะธัั. ะญัะพ ะพะดะธะฝ ะธะท ัะฐะผัั ะบัะปััะธะฒะธััะตะผัั ัััะบัะพะฒ ััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะพะณะพ ะผะธัะฐ. ะ ัะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะบะฐะบ ะดััะณะธะต ะฒะธะดั Mangifera ัะฐะบะถะต ะฒััะฐัะธะฒะฐัััั ะฝะฐ ะฑะพะปะตะต ะปะพะบะฐะปัะฝะพะน ะพัะฝะพะฒะต, Mangifera indica, ะพะฑััะฝะพะต ะผะฐะฝะณะพ ะธะปะธ ะธะฝะดะธะนัะบะพะต ะผะฐะฝะณะพ, ัะฒะปัะตััั ะตะดะธะฝััะฒะตะฝะฝัะผ ะผะฐะฝะณะพะฒัะผ ะดะตัะตะฒะพะผ, ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะบัะปััะธะฒะธััะตะผัะผ ะฒะพ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะธั ะธ ััะฑััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะธั ัะตะณะธะพะฝะฐั , ะฐ ะตะณะพ ะฟะปะพะดั ัะฐัะฟัะพัััะฐะฝะตะฝั ะฟะพ ะฒัะตะผั ะผะธัั.[PARAGRAPH:2]ะ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ะบัะปััััะฐั ะตะณะพ ะฟะปะพะดั ะธ ะปะธัััั ัะธััะฐะปัะฝะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ัะฒะตัะพัะฝัั ัะบัะฐัะตะฝะธะน ะฝะฐ ัะฒะฐะดัะฑะฐั , ะฟัะฑะปะธัะฝัั ัะพัะถะตััะฒะฐั ะธ ัะตะปะธะณะธะพะทะฝัั ัะตัะตะผะพะฝะธัั .
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Mango[COLOR_REVERT], to miêsisty owoc pestkowy nale¿¹cy do rodzaju Mangifera, sk³adaj¹cego siê z licznych tropikalnych drzew owocuj¹cych z rodziny roœlin kwitn¹cych Anacardiaceae. Mango pochodzi z Indii, sk¹d rozprzestrzeni³o siê na ca³y œwiat. Jest to jeden z najczêœciej uprawianych owoców tropikalnego œwiata. Podczas gdy inne gatunki Mangifera s¹ uprawiane bardziej lokalnie, Mangifera indica, mango pospolite lub mango indyjskie, jest jedynym drzewem mango powszechnie uprawianym w wielu regionach tropikalnych i subtropikalnych, a jego owoce s¹ rozprowadzane na ca³ym œwiecie. W kilku kulturach jej owoce i liœcie s¹ rytualnie u¿ywane jako dekoracje kwiatowe na weselach, uroczystoœciach publicznych i ceremoniach religijnych.

TEXT.English: Marble is a kind of limestone, used by man for millennia. It is a useful material for construction, especially in dry climates. Marble is an excellent material from which to make statues, as well. Marble surfaces can be polished to a beautiful sheen, but the rock remains attractive even when the surface is left rough. The Egyptians used marble blocks to line the outside of the Pyramid of Cheops. The Greeks used blocks of marble to construct some of their buildings; they also used the material to sculpt some of the world's great artworks. The Romans were the first to use slabs of marble as wall coverings. Marble remains one of the most popular construction and artistic materials extant.
TEXT.German: Marmor ist eine Kalksteinart, die schon seit Jahrtausenden genutzt wird. Er eignet sich vor allem in trockenen Regionen als Baumaterial und fรผr die Herstellung von Statuen. Marmoroberflรคchen kรถnnen poliert werden, werden aber auch naturbelassen eingesetzt. [PARAGRAPH:1]Die รgypter nutzten Marmorblรถcke, um die Auรenseite der Cheopspyramide zu verkleiden. Die Griechen konstruierten einige Gebรคude aus Marmorblรถcken und schufen aus diesem Material einige der schรถnsten Kunstwerke der Welt. Die Rรถmer verwendeten erstmals Marmorplatten als Wandbedeckungen. Marmor gehรถrt bis heute zu den beliebtesten Materialien fรผr Bau- und Kunstwerke.

TEXT.English: Algae are a group of single or multi-cellular plant-based organisms. They are found mostly near the water surface, and are made only of tissues (called "thallus") and have no roots, stems or leaves. Their presence on Mars is important to testify the success of the terraforming process.
TEXT.French: Les algues sont un groupe d'organismes vรฉgรฉtaux unicellulaires ou multicellulaires. On les trouve principalement prรจs de la surface de l'eau, et elles sont constituรฉes uniquement de tissus (appelรฉs "thalle") et n'ont ni racines, ni tiges, ni feuilles. Leur prรฉsence sur Mars est importante pour tรฉmoigner du succรจs du processus de terraformation.
TEXT.Italian: Le alghe sono un gruppo di organismi vegetali monocellulari o multicellulari. Si trovano principalmente vicino alla superficie dell'acqua e sono fatti solo di tessuti (chiamati "tallo") e non hanno radici, steli o foglie. La loro presenza su Marte รจ un'importante testimonianza del successo del processo di terraformazione.

TEXT.English: These are not edible blueberries. They are especially rich deposits of useful metals.

TEXT.Italian: Benchรฉ si chiamino "mirtilli", non sono frutti commestibili, ma depositi particolarmente ricchi di metalli utili.

TEXT.English: A crop is a plant or animal product that can be grown and harvested extensively for profit or subsistence. The term may refer either to the harvested parts or to the harvest in a more refined state. Crops growth on Mars is important to testify the success of the terraforming process.
TEXT.French: Une culture est un produit vรฉgรฉtal ou animal qui peut รชtre cultivรฉ et rรฉcoltรฉ de maniรจre extensive ร des fins lucratives ou de subsistance. Le terme peut dรฉsigner soit les parties rรฉcoltรฉes, soit la rรฉcolte dans un รฉtat plus raffinรฉ. La croissance des cultures sur Mars est importante pour tรฉmoigner du succรจs du processus de terraformation.
TEXT.Italian: Una coltura รจ un prodotto vegetale o animale che puรฒ essere coltivato e raccolto ampiamente a scopo di lucro o di sussistenza. Il termine puรฒ riferirsi alle singole parti raccolte o al raccolto in uno stato piรน raffinato. Lo sviluppo di colture su Marte รจ un'importante testimonianza del successo del processo di terraformazione.

TEXT.English: Consumer electronics or home electronics are analog or digital electronic equipment intended for everyday use, typically in private homes. Consumer electronics include devices used for entertainment, communications, and home-office activities. The development of such an industry on Mars is important to testify the thriving of the human colonies on the Red Planet.
TEXT.French: L'รฉlectronique grand public ou l'รฉlectronique domestique sont des รฉquipements รฉlectroniques analogiques ou numรฉriques destinรฉs ร un usage quotidien, gรฉnรฉralement dans les maisons privรฉes. L'รฉlectronique grand public comprend les appareils utilisรฉs pour le divertissement, les communications et les activitรฉs de bureau ร domicile. Le dรฉveloppement d'une telle industrie sur Mars est important pour tรฉmoigner de la prospรฉritรฉ des colonies humaines sur la Planรจte Rouge.
TEXT.Italian: L'elettronica di consumo comprende le apparecchiature elettroniche analogiche o digitali destinate all'uso quotidiano, in genere nelle case private. L'elettronica di consumo include dispositivi utilizzati per attivitร di intrattenimento, comunicazione e attivitร a casa e in ufficio. Lo sviluppo di tale industria su Marte รจ un'importante testimonianza della prosperitร delle colonie umane sul pianeta rosso.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Elektronika u¿ytkowa[COLOR_REVERT] lub elektronika domowa to analogowy lub cyfrowy sprzêt elektroniczny przeznaczony do codziennego u¿ytku, zazwyczaj w domach prywatnych. Elektronika u¿ytkowa obejmuje urz¹dzenia wykorzystywane do rozrywki, komunikacji i prowadzenia domowego biura. Rozwój takiego przemys³u na Marsie jest wa¿nym œwiadectwem rozkwitu kolonii ludzkich na Czerwonej Planecie.

TEXT.English: A fruit is the seed-bearing structure in flowering plants, and are the means by which angiosperms disseminate seeds. Edible fruits, in particular, have propagated with the movements of humans and animals in a symbiotic relationship as a means for seed dispersal and nutrition. Their growth on Mars is important to testify the success of the terraforming process.
TEXT.French: Un fruit est la structure porteuse de graines dans les plantes ร fleurs, et constitue le moyen par lequel les angiospermes dissรฉminent leurs graines. Les fruits comestibles, en particulier, se sont propagรฉs avec les mouvements des humains et des animaux dans une relation symbiotique comme moyen de dispersion des graines et de nutrition. Leur croissance sur Mars est importante pour tรฉmoigner du succรจs du processus de terraformation.
TEXT.Italian: Un frutto รจ la struttura portante del seme nelle piante da fiore e sono il mezzo con cui le angiosperme diffondono i semi. I frutti commestibili, in particolare, si sono propagati con i movimenti di esseri umani e animali, in una relazione simbiotica come mezzo per la dispersione e l'alimentazione dei semi. La loro crescita su Marte รจ un'importante testimonianza del successo del processo di terraformazione.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Owoc[COLOR_REVERT], jest struktur¹ zawieraj¹c¹ nasiona w roœlinach kwitn¹cych i jest œrodkiem, za pomoc¹ którego roœliny okrytozal¹¿kowe rozprzestrzeniaj¹ nasiona. W szczególnoœci owoce jadalne rozmna¿a³y siê wraz z przemieszczaniem siê ludzi i zwierz¹t w symbiozie, stanowi¹c sposób na rozsiewanie nasion i od¿ywianie. Ich rozwój na Marsie jest wa¿nym dowodem powodzenia procesu terraformowania.

TEXT.English: A manufactured good is a commodity that is used by a consumer to satisfy current wants or needs or to produce another, more complex good. The manufacturing process begins with the product design, and materials specification from which the product is made. These materials are then modified through manufacturing processes to become the required object. The development of such an industry on Mars is important to testify the thriving of the human colonies on the Red Planet.
TEXT.French: Un bien manufacturรฉ est une marchandise utilisรฉe par un consommateur pour satisfaire des dรฉsirs ou des besoins actuels ou pour produire un autre bien plus complexe. Le processus de fabrication commence par la conception du produit et la spรฉcification des matรฉriaux ร partir desquels le produit est fabriquรฉ. Ces matรฉriaux sont ensuite modifiรฉs par des processus de fabrication pour devenir l'objet requis. Le dรฉveloppement d'une telle industrie sur Mars est important pour tรฉmoigner de la prospรฉritรฉ des colonies humaines sur la Planรจte Rouge.
TEXT.Italian: Un manufatto รจ un prodotto che viene utilizzato da un consumatore per soddisfare i suoi desideri o necessitร oppure per produrre un altro bene piรน complesso. Il processo di fabbricazione inizia con la progettazione del prodotto e le specifiche dei materiali con cui il prodotto รจ realizzato. Questi materiali vengono quindi modificati attraverso i processi di produzione per diventare l'oggetto richiesto. Lo sviluppo di tale industria su Marte รจ un'importante testimonianza della prosperitร delle colonie umane sul pianeta rosso.

TEXT.English: A Martian rock is a rocky element found on Mars and taken for research or scientific purposes. The International Astronomical Union's official Martian naming system declares that objects smaller than 100 m (330 ft) are not to be given official names. Because of this, some less significant rocks have largely unofficial, and sometimes conflicting, designations.
TEXT.French: Une roche martienne est un รฉlรฉment rocheux trouvรฉ sur Mars et prรฉlevรฉ ร des fins de recherche ou scientifiques. Le systรจme officiel de dรฉnomination martienne de l'Union astronomique internationale dรฉclare que les objets de moins de 100 m (330 pieds) ne doivent pas recevoir de noms officiels. Pour cette raison, certaines roches moins importantes ont des dรฉsignations largement non officielles, et parfois contradictoires.
TEXT.Italian: Una roccia marziana รจ un elemento roccioso trovato su Marte e prelevato per scopi di ricerca o scientifici. Il sistema ufficiale di denominazione relativo a Marte dell'Unione Astronomica Internazionale dichiara che non devono essere assegnati nomi ufficiali agli oggetti di dimensioni inferiori a 100 m. Per questo motivo, alcune rocce meno significative hanno designazioni in gran parte non ufficiali e talvolta contrastanti.

Z tego powodu niektóre mniej znacz¹ce ska³y maj¹ w du¿ej mierze nieoficjalne, a czasem sprzeczne oznaczenia.

TEXT.English: Vegetables are parts of plants that are consumed by humans or other animals as food. The original meaning of the term is still used to refer to all edible plant matter, including the flowers, fruits, stems, leaves, roots, and seeds. Their growth on Mars is important to testify the success of the terraforming process.
TEXT.French: Les lรฉgumes sont des parties de plantes qui sont consommรฉes par les humains ou d'autres animaux comme nourriture. Le sens original du terme est encore utilisรฉ pour dรฉsigner toute matiรจre vรฉgรฉtale comestible, y compris les fleurs, les fruits, les tiges, les feuilles, les racines et les graines. Leur croissance sur Mars est importante pour tรฉmoigner du succรจs du processus de terraformation.
TEXT.Italian: Le verdure sono parti di piante che vengono consumate dall'uomo o da altri animali come cibo. Il significato originale del termine รจ ancora usato per indicare tutta la materia vegetale commestibile, inclusi fiori, frutti, steli, foglie, radici e semi. La loro crescita su Marte รจ un'importante testimonianza del successo del processo di terraformazione.

TEXT.English: Marzipan is a confection consisting primarily of sugar or honey and almond meal, sometimes augmented with almond oil or extract. It is often made into sweets; common uses are marzipan-filled chocolate and small marzipan imitations of fruits and vegetables. It is also rolled into thin sheets and glazed for icing cakes, primarily birthday and wedding cakes and Christmas cakes. This use is particularly common in England, on large fruitcakes. Marzipan (or almond paste) may also be used as a cake ingredient, as in stollen. In some countries, it is shaped into small figures of animals as a traditional treat for New Year's Day. Marzipan is also used in Tortell, and in some versions of king cake eaten during the Carnival season. Traditional Swedish Princess Cake is typically covered with a layer of marzipan that has been tinted pale green.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Marcepan[COLOR_REVERT], to s³odka masa cukiernicza utworzona g³ównie z pra¿onych i zmielonych migda³ów i cukru z dodatkiem olejku migda³owego. Pod wp³ywem ciep³a (wystarczy ogrzanie w d³oni) masa staje siê plastyczna, dlatego bywa u¿ywana do wyrobu ozdobnych, skomplikowanych kszta³tów.
TEXT.Russian: รร รฐรถรจรฏร รญ - รฝรฒรฎ รชรฎรญรครจรฒรฅรฐรฑรชรฎรฅ รจรงรครฅรซรจรฅ, รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรนรฅรฅ รข รฎรฑรญรฎรขรญรฎรฌ รจรง รฑร รตร รฐร รจรซรจ รฌรฅรคร รจ รฌรจรญรคร รซรผรญรฎรฉ รฌรณรชรจ, รจรญรฎรฃรคร รฏรฐรจรฏรฐร รขรซรฅรญรญรฎรฅ รฌรจรญรคร รซรผรญรปรฌ รฌร รฑรซรฎรฌ รจรซรจ รฝรชรฑรฒรฐร รชรฒรฎรฌ. รรฒรฎ รทร รฑรฒรฎ รครฅรซร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รฑรซร รครฎรฑรฒรฟรต; รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒรฑรฟ รฌร รฐรถรจรฏร รญรป รจ รธรฎรชรฎรซร รครญรปรฅ รฌร รซรฅรญรผรชรจรฅ รจรฌรจรฒร รถรจรจ รดรฐรณรชรฒรฎรข รจ รฎรขรฎรนรฅรฉ. รรต รฒร รชรฆรฅ รฐร รฑรชร รฒรปรขร รพรฒ รข รฒรฎรญรชรจรฅ รซรจรฑรฒรป รจรง รฃรซร รงรณรฐรจ รครซรฟ รฒรฎรฐรฒรฎรข, รข รฏรฅรฐรขรณรพ รฎรทรฅรฐรฅรครผ, รช รครญรฟรฌ รฐรฎรฆรครฅรญรจรฟ, รครซรฟ รฑรขร รครฅรกรญรปรต รฏรจรฐรฎรฃรฎรข รจ รรฎรฆรครฅรฑรฒรขรฅรญรฑรชรจรต รฒรฎรฐรฒรป. รรฒรฎ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรญรฅรญรจรฅ รฎรฑรฎรกรฅรญรญรฎ รฐร รฑรฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฐร รญรฅรญรฎ รข รรญรฃรซรจรจ รญร รกรฎรซรผรธรจรฅ รชรฅรชรฑรป. รร รฐรถรจรฏร รญ (รจรซรจ รฌรจรญรคร รซรผรญรฎรฉ รฏร รฑรฒร ) รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รชร รช รจรญรฃรฐรฅรครจรฅรญรฒ รครซรฟ รฒรฎรฐรฒร . ร รญรฅรชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรต รฑรฒรฐร รญร รต รฎรญรฎ รดรฎรฐรฌรจรฐรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รญรฅรกรฎรซรผรธรจรฅ รดรจรฃรณรฐรชรจ รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรปรต, รชร รช รฒรฐร รครจรถรจรฎรญรญรฎรฅ รซร รชรฎรฌรฑรฒรขรฎ รญร รรฎรขรปรฉ รรฎรค. รร รฐรถรจรฏร รญ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รฐรณรซรฅรฒร รต, รจ รข รญรฅรชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรต รขรฅรฐรฑรจรฟรต รชรฎรฐรฎรซรผ รฑรผรฅรคร รฅรฒ รฒรฎรฐรฒ รขรฎ รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รชร รฐรญร รขร รซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รฑรฅรงรฎรญร . รรฐร รครจรถรจรฎรญรญรปรฉ รฒรฎรฐรฒ รธรขรฅรครฑรชร รฟ รรฐรจรญรถรฅรฑรฑร รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฏรฎรชรฐรปรฒ รฑรซรฎรฅรฌ รฌร รฐรถรจรฏร รญร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฒรฎรญรจรฐรฎรขร รญ รข รกรซรฅรครญรฎ-รงรฅรซรฅรญรปรฉ รถรขรฅรฒ.

TEXT.English: Mascagnite is a rare ammonium sulfate mineral. It crystallizes in the orthorhombic system typically forming as stalactitic masses exhibiting good cleavage. It is soft (not higher than 2.5 on the Mohs Scale) and water soluble. Optical properties are variable; the purest form is transparent and colorless, but opaque gray or yellow deposits are also known.
TEXT.French: La mascagnite est un minรฉral rare de sulfate d'ammonium. Elle cristallise dans le systรจme orthorhombique, formant typiquement des masses stalactitiques prรฉsentant un bon clivage. Elle est tendre (pas plus de 2,5 sur l'รฉchelle de Mohs) et soluble dans l'eau. Les propriรฉtรฉs optiques sont variables ; la forme la plus pure est transparente et incolore, mais des dรฉpรดts opaques gris ou jaunes sont รฉgalement connus.

W³aœciwoœci optyczne s¹ zmienne; najczystsza postaæ jest przezroczysta i bezbarwna, ale znane s¹ równie¿ nieprzezroczyste szare lub ¿ó³te osady.
TEXT.Russian: รร รฑรชร รญรผรจรฒ - รฝรฒรฎ รฐรฅรครชรจรฉ รฌรจรญรฅรฐร รซ รฑรณรซรผรดร รฒร ร รฌรฌรฎรญรจรฟ. รรญ รชรฐรจรฑรฒร รซรซรจรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รฐรฎรฌรกรจรทรฅรฑรชรณรพ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌรณ, รฎรกรฐร รงรณรฟรฑรผ รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รข รฑรฒร รซร รชรฒรจรฒรฎรขรปรต รฌร รฑรฑร รต รขรปรคร รขร รฟ รตรฎรฐรฎรธรณรพ รฑรฏร รฉรญรฎรฑรฒรผ. รรญ รฌรฟรฃรฎรช (รญรฅ รขรปรธรฅ 2.5 รฏรฎ รธรชร รซรฅ รรฎรฎรฑร ) รจ รขรฎรครฎรฐร รฑรฒรขรฎรฐรจรฌ. รรฏรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รฑรขรฎรฉรฑรฒรขร รฟรขรซรฟรพรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรฌรจ; รข รทรจรฑรฒรฎรฌ รขรจรครฅ รฏรฐรฎรงรฐร รทรฅรญ รจ รกรฅรฑรถรขรฅรฒรฅรญ, รญรฎ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รจรงรขรฅรฑรฒรญรป รญรฅรฏรฐรฎรงรฐร รทรญรปรฅ รฑรฅรฐรปรฅ รจรซรจ รฆรฅรซรฒรปรฅ รฎรฒรซรฎรฆรฅรญรจรฟ.

TEXT.English: A mask is an object normally worn on the face, typically for protection, disguise, performance or entertainment. Masks have been used since antiquity for both ceremonial and practical purposes. They are usually worn on the face, although they may also be positioned for effect elsewhere on the wearer's body, so in parts of Australia giant totem masks cover the body, whilst Inuit women use finger masks during storytelling and dancing
TEXT.French: Un masque est un objet normalement portรฉ sur le visage, gรฉnรฉralement pour la protection, le dรฉguisement, la performance ou le divertissement. Les masques ont รฉtรฉ utilisรฉs depuis l'Antiquitรฉ ร des fins ร la fois cรฉrรฉmonielles et pratiques. Ils sont gรฉnรฉralement portรฉs sur le visage, bien qu'ils puissent รฉgalement รชtre positionnรฉs pour effet ailleurs sur le corps du porteur, ainsi dans certaines parties de l'Australie, des masques totรฉmiques gรฉants couvrent le corps, tandis que les femmes inuites utilisent des masques de doigts pendant la narration et la danse.

TEXT.English: The majority of Mechs are bipedal and can be classed as either humanoid or reverse-joint ("chicken walker"). Humanoid-type BattleMechs are the most common and include the iconic Atlas and Summoner (Thor). Reverse-joint 'Mechs are slightly less common on the field of battle, but include such famous models as the Supernova, Marauder, Catapult, Timber Wolf (Mad Cat) and the Dire Wolf (Daishi). There are a few, however, with digitigrade legs that have a raised ankle and stand on their toes, like the legs of a dog, cat, etc. Examples include the Thanatos and Nova Cat.

TEXT.English: A medical tricorder is a handheld portable scanning device to be used by consumers to self-diagnose medical conditions within seconds and take basic vital measurements, such as blood pressure, temperature, and flow in a noninvasive way. It can diagnose a person's state of health either as a standalone device or as a connection to medical databases via an Internet connection. The idea of a medical tricorder comes from an imaginary device on the science fiction TV show Star Trek.
TEXT.French: Un tricordeur mรฉdical est un appareil de balayage portable ร main destinรฉ ร รชtre utilisรฉ par les consommateurs pour auto-diagnostiquer des conditions mรฉdicales en quelques secondes et prendre des mesures vitales de base, comme la pression artรฉrielle, la tempรฉrature et le flux sanguin de maniรจre non invasive. Il peut diagnostiquer l'รฉtat de santรฉ d'une personne soit comme un appareil autonome, soit comme une connexion aux bases de donnรฉes mรฉdicales via une connexion Internet. L'idรฉe d'un tricordeur mรฉdical vient d'un appareil imaginaire de la sรฉrie tรฉlรฉvisรฉe de science-fiction Star Trek.
TEXT.Italian: Un tricorder medico รจ un dispositivo medico portatile che puรฒ essere utilizzato per auto-diagnosticare condizioni mediche in pochi secondi e per eseguire misurazioni vitali di base, come la pressione, la temperatura e il flusso sanguigno in modo non invasivo. Puรฒ diagnosticare lo stato di salute di una persona come dispositivo autonomo oppure connettendosi tramite Internet a dei database medici. L'idea di un tricorder medico proviene da un dispositivo immaginario dello show televisivo di fantascienza Star Trek.

รรฅรฃร รฏรฐรฎรทรญรปรฅ รฑรฏรซร รขรป

TEXT.English: A megastrong alloy is a material composed of two or more metals or a metal and a nonmetal. An alloy may be a solid solution of the elements (a single phase), a mixture of metallic phases (two or more solutions) or an intermetallic compound with no distinct boundary between the phases. Solid solution alloys give a single solid phase microstructure, while partial solutions exhibit two or more phases that may or may not be homogeneous in distribution, depending on the thermal (heat treatment) history of the material. An inter-metallic compound has one other alloy or pure metal embedded within another pure metal.
TEXT.Italian: Una lega megaresistente รจ un materiale composto da due o piรน metalli o un metallo e una sostanza non metallica. Una lega puรฒ essere una soluzione solida degli elementi (una singola fase), una miscela di fasi metalliche (due o piรน soluzioni) o un composto intermetallico senza confini distinti tra le fasi. Le leghe di soluzioni solide forniscono una microstruttura a singola fase solida, mentre le soluzioni parziali presentano due o piรน fasi che possono o meno essere omogenee nella distribuzione, a seconda della storia termica (trattamento termico) del materiale. Un composto intermetallico ha un'altra lega o metallo puro incorporato in un altro metallo puro.

TEXT.English: Melon is a name given to various members of the plant family Cucurbitaceae with sweet flavored, fleshy fruit e.g. gourds or cucurbits. Melon can be referred as a plant or a fruit. Many different cultivars have been produced, particularly of muskmelons. The plant grows as a vine. Although the melon is a fruit, some varieties may be considered "culinary vegetables".
TEXT.French: Le melon est un nom donnรฉ ร divers membres de la famille des plantes Cucurbitaceae avec des fruits charnus et sucrรฉs, par exemple les courges ou les cucurbitacรฉes. Le melon peut รชtre dรฉsignรฉ comme une plante ou un fruit. De nombreux cultivars diffรฉrents ont รฉtรฉ produits, en particulier de melons musquรฉs. La plante pousse comme une vigne. Bien que le melon soit un fruit, certaines variรฉtรฉs peuvent รชtre considรฉrรฉes comme des "lรฉgumes culinaires".
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Melonen[COLOR_REVERT] gehรถren zur Familie der Kรผrbisgewรคchse (Cucurbitaceae). Der deutsche Name leitet sich vom griechischen Wort melo (Apfel) ab. Melonen wachsen vor allem in subtropischen Gebieten und bilden groรe Beerenfrรผchte aus. Diese werden aufgrund ihres hohen Wassergehalts in heiรen Lรคndern vorwiegend zum Durststillen verwendet.[NEWLINE]Es wird hauptsรคchlich zwischen Zucker- und Wassermelonen unterschieden. Die Frรผchte wurden zunรคchst wegen der nahrhaften Samen gesammelt, da das Fruchtfleisch der Wildpflanzen bitter ist. Man geht heute davon aus, dass Melonen etwa 3000 v.Chr. domestiziert wurden. Ab 2000 v.Chr. sind kultivierte Melonen aus dem Alten รgypten und Westasien bekannt. Sie wurde lange nur in heiรen und trockenen Gebieten angebaut und erst relativ spรคt in Europa eingefรผhrt. Heute wird vermutet, dass sie die Araber wรคhrend der ottomanischen Expansion mitbrachten.

Wyprodukowano wiele ró¿nych odmian, szczególnie muskmelonów. Roœlina roœnie jak winoroœl. Chocia¿ melon jest owocem, niektóre odmiany mo¿na uznaæ za "warzywa kulinarne".
TEXT.Russian: ะะฐั ัะตะฒัะต - ััะพ ะฝะฐะทะฒะฐะฝะธะต, ะดะฐะฝะฝะพะต ัะฐะทะปะธัะฝัะผ ัะปะตะฝะฐะผ ัะตะผะตะนััะฒะฐ ัะฐััะตะฝะธะน Cucurbitaceae ัะพ ัะปะฐะดะบะธะผะธ ะฐัะพะผะฐัะฝัะผะธ, ะผััะธัััะผะธ ัััะบัะฐะผะธ, ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั: ะดัะฝะธ, ะฐัะฑัะทั ะธะปะธ ััะบะฒั. ะ ะฐััะตะฝะธั ะธะท ัะพะดะฐ ะขัะบะฒะตะฝะฝัั ะผะพะถะฝะพ ะฝะฐะทะฒะฐัั ัะณะพะดะพะน ะธะปะธ ัััะบัะพะผ. ะัะปะพ ะบัะปััะธะฒะธัะพะฒะฐะฝะพ ะผะฝะพะถะตััะฒะพ ัะฐะทะฝัั ัะพััะพะฒ, ะพัะพะฑะตะฝะฝะพ ะธะท ะผััะบััะฝะพะน ะดัะฝะธ. ะะฐั ัะตะฒัะต ัะฐัััั ะบะฐะบ ะปะพะทะฐ. ะฅะพัั ะพะฝะธ ัะฒะปััััั ัััะบัะพะผ, ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ัะพััะฐ ะผะพะถะฝะพ ััะธัะฐัั ยซะบัะปะธะฝะฐัะฝัะผะธ ะพะฒะพัะฐะผะธยป. ะกะปะพะฒะพ ยซะดัะฝัยป ะฟัะพะธัั ะพะดะธั ะพั ะปะฐัะธะฝัะบะพะณะพ ยซะผะตะปะพะฟะตะฟะพยป, ะบะพัะพัะพะต ะพะทะฝะฐัะฐะตั ะปะฐัะธะฝะธะทะฐัะธั ะณัะตัะตัะบะพะณะพ ัะทัะบะฐ (ะผะตะปะพะฟะตะฟะพะฝ), ััะพ ะพะทะฝะฐัะฐะตั ยซะดัะฝัยป, ััะพ ัะฐะผะพ ะฟะพ ัะตะฑะต ัะฒะปัะตััั ัะพะตะดะธะฝะตะฝะธะตะผ (ะดัะฝั), ยซัะฑะปะพะบะพยป (pepon), ััะตะดะธ ะฟัะพัะธั ยซัะฐะทะฝะพะฒะธะดะฝะพััะตะน ััะบะฒั ะธะปะธ ะดัะฝะธยป.

TEXT.English: Metallic hydrogen is a kind of degenerate matter, a phase of hydrogen in which it behaves as an electrical conductor. This phase was predicted theoretically in 1935. It has yet to be unambiguously observed, but some observations consistent with metallic behaviour have been reported such as the possible observation of some new phases of solid hydrogen under static conditions, and, in dense liquid deuterium, electrical insulator-to-conductor transitions associated with an increase in optical reflectivity. At high pressure, on the order of hundreds of gigapascals, hydrogen might exist as a liquid rather than a gas. Liquid metallic hydrogen is thought to be present in large amounts in the gravitationally compressed interiors of Jupiter, Saturn, and in some extrasolar planets.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตัะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะธะน ะฒะพะดะพัะพะด - ััะพ ัะฐะทะฝะพะฒะธะดะฝะพััั ะฒััะพะถะดะตะฝะฝะพะณะพ ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ, ัะฐะทะฐ ะฒะพะดะพัะพะดะฐ, ะฒ ะบะพัะพัะพะน ะพะฝ ะฒะตะดะตั ัะตะฑั ะบะฐะบ ัะปะตะบััะธัะตัะบะธะน ะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฝะธะบ. ะญัะฐ ัะฐะทะฐ ะฑัะปะฐ ะฟัะตะดัะบะฐะทะฐะฝะฐ ัะตะพัะตัะธัะตัะบะธ ะฒ 1935 ะณะพะดั. ะะต ะตัะต ะฟัะตะดััะพะธั ะพะดะฝะพะทะฝะฐัะฝะพ ะฝะฐะฑะปัะดะฐัั, ะฝะพ ัะพะพะฑัะฐะปะพัั ะพ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ะฝะฐะฑะปัะดะตะฝะธัั , ัะพะณะปะฐััััะธั ัั ั ะฟะพะฒะตะดะตะฝะธะตะผ ะผะตัะฐะปะปะพะฒ, ัะฐะบะธั ะบะฐะบ ะฒะพะทะผะพะถะฝะพะต ะฝะฐะฑะปัะดะตะฝะธะต ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ะฝะพะฒัั ัะฐะท ัะฒะตัะดะพะณะพ ะฒะพะดะพัะพะดะฐ ะฒ ััะฐัะธัะตัะบะธั ััะปะพะฒะธัั , ะฐ ะฒ ะฟะปะพัะฝะพะผ ะถะธะดะบะพะผ ะดะตะนัะตัะธะธ - ัะปะตะบััะธัะตัะบะพะต. ะฟะตัะตั ะพะดั ะดะธัะปะตะบััะธะบ-ะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฝะธะบ ัะฒัะทะฐะฝั ั ัะฒะตะปะธัะตะฝะธะตะผ ะพะฟัะธัะตัะบะพะน ะพััะฐะถะฐัะตะปัะฝะพะน ัะฟะพัะพะฑะฝะพััะธ. ะัะธ ะฒััะพะบะพะผ ะดะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธะธ ะฟะพััะดะบะฐ ัะพัะตะฝ ะณะธะณะฐะฟะฐัะบะฐะปะตะน ะฒะพะดะพัะพะด ะผะพะถะตั ัััะตััะฒะพะฒะฐัั ะฒ ะฒะธะดะต ะถะธะดะบะพััะธ, ะฐ ะฝะต ะณะฐะทะฐ. ะกัะธัะฐะตััั, ััะพ ะถะธะดะบะธะน ะผะตัะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะธะน ะฒะพะดะพัะพะด ะฟัะธัััััะฒัะตั ะฒ ะฑะพะปััะธั ะบะพะปะธัะตััะฒะฐั ะฒ ะณัะฐะฒะธัะฐัะธะพะฝะฝะพ ัะถะฐััั ะฝะตะดัะฐั ะฎะฟะธัะตัะฐ, ะกะฐัััะฝะฐ ะธ ะฝะฐ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ะฒะฝะตัะพะปะฝะตัะฝัั ะฟะปะฐะฝะตัะฐั .
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wodór metaliczny[COLOR_REVERT], jest rodzajem materii zdegenerowanej, faz¹ wodoru, która zachowuje siê jak przewodnik elektryczny. TEXT.English: A metal foam is a cellular structure consisting of a solid metal, frequently aluminium, as well as a large volume fraction of gas-filled pores. The pores can be sealed (closed-cell foam), or they can form an interconnected network (open-cell foam). The defining characteristic of metal foams is a very high porosity: typically 75-95% of the volume consists of void spaces making these ultralight materials. The strength of foamed metal possesses a power law relationship to its density; i.e., a 20% dense material is more than twice as strong as a 10% dense material.

TEXT.English: A metamaterial is any material engineered to have properties that are not found in naturally occurring materials. They are made from assemblies of multiple elements, fashioned from composite materials such as metals and plastics, and then arranged in repeating patterns, at scales that are smaller than the wavelengths of the phenomena they influence, that give them their smart properties. Applications of metamaterials include optical filters and lenses, medical devices, and a wide range of remote-based interactions and monitoring infrastructures.
TEXT.Italian: Un metamateriale รจ un qualsiasi materiale progettato per avere proprietร che non si trovano nei materiali naturali. Sono costituiti da insiemi di piรน elementi, realizzati con materiali compositi come metalli e materie plastiche, e quindi disposti secondo schemi ripetitivi, su scale piรน piccole delle lunghezze d'onda dei fenomeni che influenzano, che conferiscono loro le loro proprietร peculiari. Le applicazioni dei metamateriali includono filtri e lenti ottici, dispositivi medici e una vasta gamma di infrastrutture di monitoraggio e di interazioni da remoto.

TEXT.English: N-methylamphetamine, commonly named methamphetamine or meth, is a potent central nervous system stimulant that is mainly used as a recreational drug, and less commonly as a second-line treatment for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and obesity. It is rarely prescribed over concerns involving human neurotoxicity, high addiction and dependence liability, as well as the availability of safer substitute drugs with comparable treatment efficacy. Internationally, the production, distribution, sale, and possession of methamphetamine is restricted or banned in many countries.
TEXT.Italian: La N-metilamfetamina, comunemente chiamata metanfetamina, รจ un potente stimolante del sistema nervoso centrale che viene utilizzato principalmente come farmaco ricreativo e, meno comunemente, come trattamento di seconda linea per il disturbo da deficit di attenzione e iperattivitร e l'obesitร . Viene raramente prescritta come medicinale a causa delle controindicazioni (come neurotossicitร e alto rischio di dipendenza), nonchรฉ dalla disponibilitร di farmaci sostitutivi piรน sicuri. A livello internazionale, la produzione, la distribuzione, la vendita e il possesso di metanfetamine sono limitati o vietati in molti paesi.

TEXT.German: Methanhydrat
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฒร รญรฎรขรปรฉ รซรฅรค

TEXT.English: Methane clathrate, also called methane hydrate or methane ice, is a solid clathrate compound (more specifically, a clathrate hydrate) in which a large amount of methane is trapped within the crystal structure of water ice. Originally thought to occur only in the outer regions of the Solar System where temperatures are low and water ice is common, significant deposits of methane clathrate have been found under sediments on the ocean floors of Earth. Methane clathrates are common constituents of the shallow marine geosphere, and they occur both in deep sedimentary structures, and as outcrops on the ocean floor.

TEXT.English: An integrated circuit or monolithic integrated circuit (also referred to as an IC, a chip, or a microchip) is a set of electronic circuits on one small flat piece (or "chip") of semiconductor material that is normally silicon. The integration of large numbers of tiny MOS transistors into a small chip results in circuits that are orders of magnitude smaller, faster, and less expensive than those constructed of discrete electronic components. The IC's mass production capability, reliability, and building-block approach to circuit design has ensured the rapid adoption of standardized ICs in place of designs using discrete transistors. ICs are now used in virtually all electronic equipment and have revolutionized the world of electronics. Computers, mobile phones, and other digital home appliances are now inextricable parts of the structure of modern societies, made possible by the small size and low cost of ICs.

TEXT.English: Micropower describes the work that researchers at several universities are doing to develop very small electric generators and prime movers or devices to convert heat or motion to electricity, for use close to the generator. These devices offer the promise of a power source for portable electronic devices which is lighter weight and has a longer operating time than batteries. Micropower differs from microgeneration in being concerned with universities, mobility and milliwatts or watts rather than individuals, homes and kilowatts.

TEXT.English: A microwave oven, often colloquially shortened to microwave, is a kitchen appliance that heats food by bombarding it with electromagnetic radiation in the microwave spectrum causing polarized molecules in the food to rotate and build up thermal energy in a process known as dielectric heating. Microwave ovens heat foods quickly and efficiently because excitation is fairly uniform in the outer 25 - 38 mm of a dense (high water content) food item; food is more evenly heated throughout (except in thick, dense objects) than generally occurs in other cooking techniques.

TEXT.English: Milk is a white liquid produced by the mammary glands of mammals. It provides the primary source of nutrition for young mammals before they are able to digest other types of food.
TEXT.French: Le lait est un liquide blanc produit par les glandes mammaires des mammifรจres. Il constitue la principale source de nutrition pour les jeunes mammifรจres avant qu'ils ne soient capables de digรฉrer d'autres types d'aliments.
TEXT.Italian: Il latte รจ un liquido bianco prodotto dalle ghiandole mammarie dei mammiferi; รจ la fonte principale di nutrimento dei mammiferi prima che essi siano in grado di digerire altri tipi di cibo.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ming vase[COLOR_REVERT] is an example of Chinese ceramic manufacture from 14th to 17th century, that was also the result of an extraordinary period of innovation. Manufacturers explored and investigated new techniques in design and shapes, showing a predilection for colour and painted design, and an openness to foreign forms (especially Islamic ones, during the first part of the 15th century). Late Ming period vessels underwent a dramatic shift towards a market economy, exporting porcelain around the world on an unprecedented scale, thus becoming valuable and highly prized possessions.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]vaso Ming[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un esempio di manifattura ceramica cinese dal XIV al XVII secolo, frutto anche di uno straordinario periodo di innovazione. I produttori esplorarono e investigarono nuove tecniche nel design e nelle forme, mostrando una predilezione per il colore e il design dipinto, e un'apertura alle forme straniere (specialmente quelle islamiche, durante la prima parte del XV secolo). La qualitร dei vasi del tardo periodo Ming subรฌ una marcata transizione verso l'economia di mercato, quando la porcellana cinese venne esportata in tutto il mondo su una scala senza precedenti, diventando cosรฌ un bene di valore molto apprezzato.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wazon Mingijski[COLOR_REVERT] jest przyk³adem chiñskiej produkcji ceramiki od XIV do XVII wieku, która by³a wynikiem niezwyk³ego okresu innowacji. Producenci badali i odkrywali nowe techniki projektowania i kszta³cenia, wykazuj¹c upodobanie do koloru i malarstwa oraz otwartoœæ na formy obce (zw³aszcza islamskie w pierwszej po³owie XV wieku). Naczynia z póŸnego okresu Ming przesz³y dramatyczn¹ zmianê w kierunku gospodarki rynkowej, eksportuj¹c porcelanê na ca³y œwiat na niespotykan¹ dot¹d skalê, staj¹c siê w ten sposób wysoko cenionym nabytkiem.

TEXT.English: Mithril is an artisan material that is manufactured out of lunar regolith, designed specifically to have the properties described about in fantasy literature: highly malleable when pure, resembling silver in appearance, and stronger and lighter than steel. In actuality, it is a composite material treated with high-tech (and highly secret) methods.
TEXT.French: Le mithril est un matรฉriau artisanal fabriquรฉ ร partir de rรฉgolithe lunaire, conรงu spรฉcifiquement pour avoir les propriรฉtรฉs dรฉcrites dans la littรฉrature fantastique : hautement mallรฉable ร l'รฉtat pur, ressemblant ร l'argent en apparence, et plus rรฉsistant et plus lรฉger que l'acier. En rรฉalitรฉ, c'est un matรฉriau composite traitรฉ avec des mรฉthodes de haute technologie (et hautement secrรจtes).
TEXT.Italian: Il mithril รจ un materiale artificiale fabbricato con regolite lunare, progettato specificamente per avere le proprietร descritte nella letteratura fantasy: altamente malleabile se puro, simile all'argento in apparenza e piรน forte e leggero dell'acciaio. In realtร , รจ un materiale composito trattato con metodi altamente tecnologici (e altamente segreti).

TEXT.English: A lunar rock, or Moon rock, is a rocky element found on Earth's Moon and taken for research or scientific purposes, or that has been ejected naturally from the Moon's surface (and which has then landed on the Earth as meteorite). Analyzed elements have been dated from about 3.16 billion years old (for the rocks coming from the lunar maria) up to about 4.44 billion years old (for those coming from the highlands). Maria basalts are classified in direct relation to their titanium content, while highlands rocks are dominantly mafic plutonic rocks.
TEXT.Italian: Una roccia lunare รจ un elemento roccioso trovato sulla Luna e preso per scopi di ricerca o scientifici, o che รจ stato espulso naturalmente dalla superficie della Luna (e che รจ poi atterrato sulla Terra come meteorite). Gli elementi analizzati sono stati datati da circa 3,16 miliardi di anni (per le rocce provenienti dai mari lunari) fino a circa 4,44 miliardi di anni (per quelli provenienti dagli altopiani). I basalti provenienti dai mari sono classificati in relazione diretta con il loro contenuto di titanio, mentre le rocce degli altipiani sono prevalentemente rocce plutoniche femiche.

TEXT.English: Murex is a genus of medium to large sized predatory tropical sea snails. These are carnivorous marine gastropod molluscs in the family Muricidae, commonly called "murexes" or "rock snails". The common name murex is still used for a large number of species in the family Muricidae which were originally given the Latin generic name Murex in the past, but have more recently been regrouped into different newer genera. The word murex was used by Aristotle in reference to these kinds of snails, thus making it one of the oldest classical seashell names still in use by the scientific community.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Rozkolcowate[COLOR_REVERT], to rodzina œlimaków morskich z rzêdu Neogastropoda. Rozkolcowate to bardzo liczna rodzina skupiaj¹ca (w³¹cznie z taksonami kopalnymi) ponad 2600 gatunków podzielonych na ponad 230 rodzajów[2]. Zamieszkuj¹ wody morskie ca³ego œwiata, od równika po umiarkowan¹ strefê klimatyczn¹ (g³ównie dzielnica Indo-Pacyfiku i Panamy). Istnieje du¿a rozpiêtoœæ kszta³tów i wielkoœci. Muszle s¹ przewa¿nie gruboœcienne, ze spiczast¹ skrêtk¹. Powierzchnia zewnêtrzna jest misternie pokryta kolcami, palmiastymi wyrostkami, guzami. Z regu³y na powierzchni skrêtki widaæ rozmieszczone w regularnych odstêpach zgrubienia kolabralne (zgodne z kszta³tem linii wzrostu muszli) โ œlady istnienia wczeœniejszych ujœæ. TEXT.English: A mushroom (or toadstool) is the fleshy, spore-bearing fruiting body of a fungus, typically produced above ground on soil or on its food source. The standard for the name "mushroom" is the cultivated white button mushroom, Agaricus bisporus; hence the word "mushroom" is most often applied to those fungi (Basidiomycota, Agaricomycetes) that have a stem (stipe), a cap (pileus), and gills (lamellae, sing. lamella) on the underside of the cap. These gills produce microscopic spores that help the fungus spread across the ground or its occupant surface.
TEXT.German: Die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pilze[COLOR_REVERT] (Fungi) sind in einer heute noch gebrรคuchlichen, aber veralteten Klassifikation neben den Tieren und Pflanzen das dritte groรe Reich der Lebewesen. Zwar zรคhlen auch viele Einzeller zu den Pilzen, aber im engeren Sinne wird der Begriff fรผr die Stรคnder- und Schlauchpilze verwendet, von denen viele auch als Speisepilze Verwendung finden. Dazu gehรถren nicht kultivierbare Arten wie Steinpilz und Pfifferling, aber auch Kulturarten und -sorten von Champignon, Shiitake und Austernpilz. Beim Sammeln von Wildpilzen ist allerdings immer grรถรte Sorgfalt geboten, um nicht durch versehentlich geerntete Giftpilze eine unter Umstรคnden tรถdliche Vergiftung zu riskieren.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Grzyb[COLOR_REVERT], to miêsisty, zawieraj¹cy zarodniki owocnik grzyba, zwykle wytwarzany nad ziemi¹ na glebie lub w Ÿródle po¿ywienia. Standardem nazwy "grzyb" jest uprawiana pieczarka bia³a Agaricus bisporus; st¹d s³owo "grzyb" jest najczêœciej stosowane w odniesieniu do grzybów (Basidiomycota, Agaricomycetes), które maj¹ ³odygê (trzon), kapelusz (pileus) i skrzela (lamellae, sing. lamella) na spodniej stronie kapelusza. TEXT.English: An actuator is a type of motor for moving or controlling a mechanism or system. It is operated by a source of energy, typically electric current, hydraulic fluid pressure, or pneumatic pressure, and converts that energy into motion. An actuator is the mechanism by which a control system acts upon an environment. The control system can be simple (a fixed mechanical or electronic system), software-based (e.g. a printer driver, robot control system), or a human or other agent. Nanoactuators do basically the same - just in nanoscale.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Si³ownik[COLOR_REVERT], to rodzaj silnika s³u¿¹cy do poruszania lub sterowania mechanizmem lub systemem. Si³ownik to mechanizm, za pomoc¹ którego system sterowania oddzia³uje na otoczenie. System sterowania mo¿e byæ prosty (sta³y system mechaniczny lub elektroniczny), oparty na oprogramowaniu (np. sterownik drukarki, system sterowania robotem) lub mo¿e byæ oparty na cz³owieku lub innym czynniku. Nanosi³owniki robi¹ w zasadzie to samo โ tylko w nanoskali.
TEXT.Russian: รร รญรฎร รชรฒรจรขร รฒรฎรฐ - รฒรจรฏ รฌรฎรฒรฎรฐร รครซรฟ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฌรฅรนรฅรญรจรฟ รจรซรจ รณรฏรฐร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฌรฅรตร รญรจรงรฌรฎรฌ รจรซรจ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌรฎรฉ. รรญ รดรณรญรชรถรจรฎรญรจรฐรณรฅรฒ รฑ รฏรฎรฌรฎรนรผรพ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชร รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรจ, รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ, รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฒรฎรชร , รคร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฃรจรครฐร รขรซรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฉ รฆรจรครชรฎรฑรฒรจ, รจรซรจ รฏรญรฅรขรฌร รฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รคร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ, รฏรฐรฅรฎรกรฐร รงรณรฟ รฝรฒรณ รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรพ รข รครขรจรฆรฅรญรจรฅ. รรชรฒรจรขร รฒรฎรฐ - รฝรฒรฎ รฌรฅรตร รญรจรงรฌ, รฏรฎรฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฎรฌ รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฃรฎ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌร รณรฏรฐร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รขรฎรงรครฅรฉรฑรฒรขรณรฅรฒ รญร รฑรฐรฅรครณ. รรจรฑรฒรฅรฌร รณรฏรฐร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รกรปรฒรผ รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฎรฉ (รดรจรชรฑรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรปรฉ รฌรฅรตร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รจรซรจ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรปรฅ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌรป), รญร รฎรฑรญรฎรขรฅ รฏรฐรฎรฃรฐร รฌรฌรญรฎรฃรฎ รฎรกรฅรฑรฏรฅรทรฅรญรจรฟ (รญร รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรฐ, รครฐร รฉรขรฅรฐ รฏรฐรจรญรฒรฅรฐร , รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌร รณรฏรฐร รขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฐรฎรกรฎรฒรฎรฌ), รจรซรจ รทรฅรซรฎรขรฅรชร รจรซรจ รครฐรณรฃรฎรฃรฎ ร รฃรฅรญรฒร . รร รญรฎร รชรฒรจรขร รฒรฎรฐรป รครฅรซร รพรฒ รข รฏรฐรจรญรถรจรฏรฅ รฒรฎ รฆรฅ รฑร รฌรฎรฅ - รฒรฎรซรผรชรฎ รข รญร รญรฎรฌร รฑรธรฒร รกรฅ.
รร รญรฎรกรฎรฒรป

TEXT.English: Nanorobotics is the emerging technology field creating machines or robots whose components are at or close to the scale of a nanometer (10^-9 meters). More specifically, nanorobotics refers to the nanotechnology engineering discipline of designing and building nanorobots, with devices ranging in size from 0.1-10 micrometers and constructed of nanoscale or molecular components. The names nanobots, nanoids, nanites, nanomachines, or nanomites have also been used to describe these devices currently under research and development.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nanoroboty[COLOR_REVERT], s¹ hipotetycznymi maszynami, skonstruowanymi dziêki nanotechnologii w nanoskali z komponentów nanometrycznych. Bardzo rzadko stosuje siê inn¹ definicjê nanorobota. Jest to urz¹dzenie, które pozwala na precyzyjn¹ interakcjê z obiektami w nanoskali. Tak¹ definicjê mo¿na przypisaæ zarówno ma³ym robotom poruszaj¹cym siê z nanometrow¹ precyzj¹, jak i du¿ym urz¹dzeniem, np. mikroskopowi si³ atomowych. W niedalekiej przysz³oœci prawdopodobnie nanoboty bêd¹ mieæ wiele zastosowañ. W medycynie, nanoroboty mog¹ byæ u¿yteczne do identyfikowania komórek rakowych i ich niszczenia oraz prawdopodobnie, wprowadzone do organizmu by³yby wstanie znacznie wyd³u¿yæ czas ¿ycia naszego gatunku likwiduj¹c inne zagro¿enia ¿ycia (choroby genetyczne) a nawet zapewniæ nam nieœmiertelnoœæ (nieustanie "naprawiaj¹c" DNA- lecz to jedynie plotki). Innym potencjalnym zastosowaniem, jest detekcja toksycznych chemikaliów i pomiar ich koncentracji w œrodowisku.
TEXT.Russian: ะะฐะฝะพัะพะฑะพัะพัะตั ะฝะธะบะฐ - ััะพ ัะฐะทะฒะธะฒะฐััะฐััั ะพะฑะปะฐััั ัะตั ะฝะพะปะพะณะธะน, ะฒ ะบะพัะพัะพะน ัะพะทะดะฐัััั ะผะฐัะธะฝั ะธะปะธ ัะพะฑะพัั, ะบะพะผะฟะพะฝะตะฝัั ะบะพัะพััั ะธะผะตัั ะผะฐัััะฐะฑ ะฝะฐะฝะพะผะตััะฐ (10^-9 ะผะตััะพะฒ) ะธะปะธ ะฑะปะธะทะบะธ ะบ ะฝะตะผั. ะะพะปะตะต ะบะพะฝะบัะตัะฝะพ, ะฝะฐะฝะพัะพะฑะพัะพัะตั ะฝะธะบะฐ ะพัะฝะพัะธััั ะบ ะดะธััะธะฟะปะธะฝะต ะฝะฐะฝะพัะตั ะฝะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะพะน ะธะฝะถะตะฝะตัะธะธ, ัะฒัะทะฐะฝะฝะพะน ั ะฟัะพะตะบัะธัะพะฒะฐะฝะธะตะผ ะธ ัะพะทะดะฐะฝะธะตะผ ะฝะฐะฝะพัะพะฑะพัะพะฒ, ั ััััะพะนััะฒะฐะผะธ ัะฐะทะผะตัะพะผ ะพั 0,1 ะดะพ 10 ะผะธะบัะพะผะตััะพะฒ ะธ ะฟะพัััะพะตะฝะฝัะผะธ ะธะท ะฝะฐะฝะพัะฐะทะผะตัะฝัั ะธะปะธ ะผะพะปะตะบัะปััะฝัั ะบะพะผะฟะพะฝะตะฝัะพะฒ. ะะฐะทะฒะฐะฝะธั ะฝะฐะฝะพะฑะพัะพะฒ, ะฝะฐะฝะพะธะดะพะฒ, ะฝะฐะฝะธัะพะฒ, ะฝะฐะฝะพะผะฐัะธะฝ ะธะปะธ ะฝะฐะฝะพะผะธัะพะฒ ัะฐะบะถะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปะธัั ะดะปั ะพะฟะธัะฐะฝะธั ััะธั ััััะพะนััะฒ, ะบะพัะพััะต ะฒ ะฝะฐััะพััะตะต ะฒัะตะผั ะฝะฐั ะพะดัััั ะฒ ััะฐะดะธะธ ะธััะปะตะดะพะฒะฐะฝะธะน ะธ ัะฐะทัะฐะฑะพัะพะบ.
รร รญรฎรฃรฅรญรฅรฐร รฒรฎรฐรป

TEXT.English: Nanogenerator is a technology that converts mechanical/thermal energy as produced by small-scale physical change into electricity. Nanogenerator has three typical approaches: piezoelectric, triboelectric, and pyroelectric nanogenerators. Both the piezoelectric and triboelectric nanogenerators can convert the mechanical energy into electricity. However, the pyroelectric nanogenerators can be used to harvest thermal energy from a time-dependent temperature fluctuation.
TEXT.French: Un nanogรฉnรฉrateur est une technologie qui convertit l'รฉnergie mรฉcanique/thermique produite par un changement physique ร petite รฉchelle en รฉlectricitรฉ. Le nanogรฉnรฉrateur a trois approches typiques : les nanogรฉnรฉrateurs piรฉzoรฉlectriques, triboรฉlectriques et pyroรฉlectriques. Les nanogรฉnรฉrateurs piรฉzoรฉlectriques et triboรฉlectriques peuvent convertir l'รฉnergie mรฉcanique en รฉlectricitรฉ. Cependant, les nanogรฉnรฉrateurs pyroรฉlectriques peuvent รชtre utilisรฉs pour rรฉcolter l'รฉnergie thermique ร partir d'une fluctuation de tempรฉrature dรฉpendante du temps.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nanogenerator[COLOR_REVERT], to technologia przekszta³caj¹ca energiê mechaniczn¹/termiczn¹ wytwarzan¹ w wyniku przemian fizycznych na ma³¹ skalê w energiê elektryczn¹. TEXT.English: Nanopunk refers to an emerging subgenre of science fiction that is still very much in its infancy in comparison to its ancestor-genre, cyberpunk, and some of its other derivatives. The genre is especially similar to biopunk, but describes a world where nanites and bio-nanotechnologies are widely in use and nanotechnologies are the predominant technological forces in society. Currently the genre is mainly concerned with the artistic, psychological, and societal impact of nanotechnology, rather than aspects of the technology which itself is still in its infancy. Unlike cyberpunk, which can be distinguished by a gritty and low-life yet technologically advanced character, nanopunk can have a darker dystopian character that might examine potential risks by nanotechnology as well as a more optimistic outlook that might emphasize potential uses of nanotechnology.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nanopunk[COLOR_REVERT] odnosi siê do wy³aniaj¹cego siê podgatunku science fiction, który jest wci¹¿ w powijakach w porównaniu do swojego gatunku-przodka, cyberpunku i niektórych innych jego pochodnych. Gatunek ten jest szczególnie podobny do biopunku, ale opisuje œwiat, w którym nanity i bionanotechnologie s¹ szeroko stosowane, a nanotechnologie stanowi¹ dominuj¹c¹ si³ê technologiczn¹ w spo³eczeñstwie. Obecnie gatunek ten dotyczy g³ównie artystycznego, psychologicznego i spo³ecznego wp³ywu nanotechnologii, a nie aspektów samej technologii, która sama w sobie jest wci¹¿ w powijakach. W przeciwieñstwie do cyberpunku, który wyró¿nia siê szorstkim i p³ytkim, ale zaawansowanym technologicznie charakterem, nanopunk mo¿e mieæ mroczniejszy, dystopijny charakter, który mo¿e badaæ potencjalne ryzyko zwi¹zane z nanotechnologi¹, a tak¿e bardziej optymistyczn¹ perspektywê, która mo¿e podkreœlaæ potencjalne zastosowania nanotechnologii.

TEXT.English: A nanotruss is a carefully architected nanostructure made up of members that are as thin as five nanometers. They are constructed in such a way that it is possible to tune the material properties of the used materials in order to make the whole structure extra-resistant and extremely lightweight at the same time.

Elles sont construites de maniรจre ร pouvoir ajuster les propriรฉtรฉs matรฉrielles des matรฉriaux utilisรฉs afin de rendre l'ensemble de la structure ร la fois ultra-rรฉsistante et extrรชmement lรฉgรจre.
TEXT.Italian: Un nanotraliccio รจ una nanostruttura attentamente progettata, i cui componenti sono spessi fino a cinque nanometri. Sono costruiti in modo tale che sia possibile adattare le proprietร del materiale utilizzato per rendere l'intera struttura extra-resistente ed estremamente leggera allo stesso tempo.

TEXT.English: Carbon nanotubes (CNTs) are allotropes of carbon with a cylindrical nanostructure. Nanotubes have been constructed with length-to-diameter ratio of up to 28,000,000:1, which is significantly larger than any other material. These cylindrical carbon molecules have novel properties that make them potentially useful in many applications in nanotechnology, electronics, optics and other fields of materials science, as well as potential uses in architectural fields. They exhibit extraordinary strength and unique electrical properties, and are efficient conductors of heat. Their final usage, however, may be limited by their potential toxicity. Nanotubes are members of the fullerene structural family, which also includes the spherical buckyballs. The ends of a nanotube might be capped with a hemisphere of the buckyball structure. Their name is derived from their size, since the diameter of a nanotube is on the order of a few nanometers (approximately 1/50,000th of the width of a human hair), while they can be up to several millimeters in length (as of 2008). Nanotubes are categorized as single-walled nanotubes (SWNTs) and multi-walled nanotubes (MWNTs). Carbon nanotubes are the strongest and stiffest materials yet discovered in terms of tensile strength and elastic modulus respectively. In 2000, a multi-walled carbon nanotube was tested to have a tensile strength of 63 gigapascals (GPa). This, for illustration, translates into the ability to endure weight of 6300 kg on a cable with cross-section of 1 square millimeter.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nanorurki wêglowe (CNT)[COLOR_REVERT], to alotropy wêgla o cylindrycznej nanostrukturze. Ich ostateczne zastosowanie mo¿e byæ jednak ograniczone ze wzglêdu na ich potencjaln¹ toksycznoœæ. Nanorurki nale¿¹ do rodziny strukturalnej fulerenów, do której zaliczaj¹ siê tak¿e kuliste kulki. Naonorurki mog¹ byæ zakoñczone pó³kul¹ o strukturze buckyballa. Ich nazwa wywodzi siê od ich wielkoœci, poniewa¿ œrednica nanorurki jest rzêdu kilku nanometrów (oko³o 1/50000 szerokoœci ludzkiego w³osa), podczas gdy d³ugoœæ nanorurki mo¿e dochodziæ do kilku milimetrów (wed³ug stanu wiedzy z 2008 roku). Nanorurki dziel¹ siê na nanorurki jednoœcienne (SWNT) i nanorurki wieloœcienne (MWNT). Nanorurki wêglowe to najmocniejsze i najsztywniejsze materia³y, jakie dotychczas odkryto, odpowiednio pod wzglêdem wytrzyma³oœci na rozci¹ganie i modu³u sprê¿ystoœci. W 2000 r. przetestowano wieloœcienn¹ nanorurkê wêglow¹, która uzyska³a wytrzyma³oœæ na rozci¹ganie 63 gigapaskali (GPa). Przek³ada siê to dla przyk³adu na mo¿liwoœæ wytrzymania ciê¿aru 6300 kg na kablu o przekroju 1 milimetra kwadratowego.

TEXT.English: Natron is a naturally occurring mixture of sodium carbonate decahydrate and about 17% sodium bicarbonate (also called nahcolite or baking soda, NaHCO3) along with small quantities of sodium chloride and sodium sulfate. Natron is white to colourless when pure, varying to gray or yellow with impurities. Natron deposits are sometimes found in saline lake beds which arose in arid environments. Throughout history natron has had many practical applications which continue in the wide range of modern uses of its constituent mineral components. In modern mineralogy the term natron has come to mean only the sodium carbonate decahydrate (hydrated soda ash) which makes up most of the historical salt.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Natron[COLOR_REVERT] to naturalnie wystêpuj¹ca mieszanina dekahydratu wêglanu sodu i oko³o 17% wodorowêglanu sodu (zwanego tak¿e nahkolitem lub sod¹ oczyszczon¹, NaHCO3) wraz z niewielkimi iloœciami chlorku sodu i siarczanu sodu. Natron jest bia³y lub bezbarwny w stanie czystym, przechodz¹cy w szary lub ¿ó³ty z zanieczyszczeniami. Z³o¿a natronu czasami znajduj¹ siê w s³onych jeziorach, które powsta³y w suchym œrodowisku. W ca³ej historii natron mia³ wiele praktycznych zastosowañ, które s¹ kontynuowane w szerokim zakresie w nowoczesnych zastosowaniach jego sk³adowych sk³adników mineralnych. We wspó³czesnej mineralogii termin natron zacz¹³ oznaczaæ wy³¹cznie dekahydrat wêglanu sodu (uwodniona soda kalcynowana), który stanowi wiêkszoœæ historycznej soli.

TEXT.German: Natural Gas
TEXT.Russian: รรฐรจรฐรฎรครญรปรฉ รฃร รง

TEXT.English: Natural gas is a fossil fuel formed when layers of buried plants, gases, and animals are exposed to intense heat and pressure over thousands of years. The energy that the plants originally obtained from the sun is stored in the form of chemical bonds in natural gas. Natural gas is a nonrenewable resource because it cannot be replenished on a human time frame. Natural gas is a hydrocarbon gas mixture consisting primarily of methane, but commonly includes varying amounts of other higher alkanes and sometimes a usually lesser percentage of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and/or hydrogen sulfide. Natural gas is an energy source often used for heating, cooking, and electricity generation. It is also used as fuel for vehicles and as a chemical feedstock in the manufacture of plastics and other commercially important organic chemicals.
TEXT.Russian: รรฐรจรฐรฎรครญรปรฉ รฃร รง - รฃร รงรฎรฎรกรฐร รงรญรฎรฅ รจรฑรชรฎรฏร รฅรฌรฎรฅ รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขรฎ, รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรนรฅรฅ รฃรซร รขรญรปรฌ รฎรกรฐร รงรฎรฌ รจรง รฌรฅรฒร รญร , รญรฎ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รขรชรซรพรทร รฅรฒ รกรฎรซรผรธรฎรฅ รชรฎรซรจรทรฅรฑรฒรขรฎ รฝรฒร รญร , รกรณรฒร รญร , รฏรฐรฎรฏร รญร , รณรฃรซรฅรชรจรฑรซรฎรฃรฎ รฃร รงร , ร รงรฎรฒร , รฃรฅรซรจรฟ รจ รฑรณรซรผรดรจรคร รขรฎรครฎรฐรฎรคร . ร รฃรฎ รญร รตรฎรครฟรฒ รข รฌรฅรฑรฒรฎรฐรฎรฆรครฅรญรจรฟรต รญรฅรดรฒรจ รจ รฏรฎรซรฟรต รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครญรฎรฃรฎ รฃร รงร รจ รข รณรฃรฎรซรผรญรปรต รฏรซร รฑรฒร รต (รชร รช รฌรฅรฒร รญ รณรฃรฎรซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รฏรซร รฑรฒร ). รรฎรฃรคร รกรฎรฃร รฒรปรฅ รฌรฅรฒร รญรฎรฌ รฃร รงรป รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฅรครฅรญรป ร รญร รฝรฐรฎรกรญรปรฌ รฃรญรจรฅรญรจรฅรฌ รญรฅรฎรชร รฌรฅรญรฅรซรฎรฃรฎ รฎรฐรฃร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซร , รฎรญรจ รฎรฒรญรฎรฑรฟรฒรฑรฟ รช รกรจรฎรฃร รงรณ. รรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรจ รกรจรฎรฃร รงร รขรชรซรพรทร รพรฒ รฒรฐรฟรฑรจรญรป, รกรฎรซรฎรฒร , รจ รฑรขร รซรชรจ รฌรณรฑรฎรฐร , ร รฒร รช รฆรฅ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฐร รกรฎรฒรชร รฎรฒรฑรฒรฎรฟ รฑรฒรฎรทรญรปรต รขรฎรค รจ รญร รขรฎรงร ร รญร รฝรฐรฎรกรญรปรฌรจ รกร รชรฒรฅรฐรจรฟรฌรจ, รข รครฎรฏรฎรซรญรฅรญรจรฅ รช รชรจรธรฅรทรญรฎรฌรณ รกรฐรฎรฆรฅรญรจรพ รข รฐรฎรฃร รฒรฎรฌ รฑรชรฎรฒรฅ. รรฐรจรฐรฎรครญรปรฉ รฃร รง รทร รฑรฒรฎ รณรฏรฎรฌรจรญร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฎ รชร รช รฃร รง, รฎรฑรฎรกรฅรญรญรฎ รชรฎรฃรคร รฑรฐร รขรญรจรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑ รครฐรณรฃรจรฌรจ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชร รฌรจ รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรจ, รฒรจรฏร รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรจรทรฅรฑรฒรขร . รรฎ รฒรฎรฃรฎ, รชร รช รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครญรปรฉ รฃร รง รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รชร รช รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขรฎ, รฎรญ รครฎรซรฆรฅรญ รฏรฎรครขรฅรฐรฃรญรณรฒรผรฑรฟ รฑรซรฎรฆรญรฎรฉ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒรชรฅ, รทรฒรฎรกรป รณรคร รซรจรฒรผ รจรง รญรฅรฃรฎ รฏรฎรทรฒรจ รขรฑรฅ รขรฅรนรฅรฑรฒรขร รชรฐรฎรฌรฅ รฌรฅรฒร รญร . รรฎรกรฎรทรญรปรฅ รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒรป รฝรฒรฎรฉ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒรชรจ รขรชรซรพรทร รพรฒ รฝรฒร รญ, รฏรฐรฎรฏร รญ, รกรณรฒร รญรป, รฏรฅรญรฒร รญรป รจ รณรฃรซรฅรขรฎรครฎรฐรฎรครป รกรฎรซรฅรฅ รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรฉ รฌรฎรซรฅรชรณรซรฟรฐรญรฎรฉ รฌร รฑรฑรป, รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒร รฐรญรณรพ รฑรฅรฐรณ, รจ รจรญรฎรฃรคร รฃรฅรซรจรฉ รจ ร รงรฎรฒ. - Wikipedia

TEXT.English: A fishing net or fishnet is a net that is used for fishing. Fishing nets are meshes usually formed by knotting a relatively thin thread. Modern nets are usually made of artificial polyamides like nylon, although nets of organic polyamides such as wool or silk thread were common until recently and are still used.
TEXT.French: Un filet de pรชche est un filet utilisรฉ pour la pรชche. Les filets de pรชche sont des mailles gรฉnรฉralement formรฉes en nouant un fil relativement fin. Les filets modernes sont gรฉnรฉralement fabriquรฉs en polyamides artificiels comme le nylon, bien que les filets en polyamides organiques comme la laine ou la soie รฉtaient courants jusqu'ร rรฉcemment et sont encore utilisรฉs.
TEXT.Italian: Una rete da pesca รจ un dispositivo a maglie utilizzato per catturare i pesci; vengono realizzate legando in modo piรน o meno fitto i fili. Le reti moderne sono fatte in materiali sintetici come il nylon, anche se vengono ancora usate reti in lana o in seta.

TEXT.English: Brain implants, often referred to as neural implants, are technological devices that connect directly to a biological subject's brain - usually placed on the surface of the brain, or attached to the brain's cortex. A common purpose of modern brain implants and the focus of much current research is establishing a biomedical prosthesis circumventing areas in the brain that have become dysfunctional after a stroke or other head injuries. This includes sensory substitution, e.g., in vision. Other brain implants are used in animal experiments simply to record brain activity for scientific reasons. Some brain implants involve creating interfaces between neural systems and computer chips. This work is part of a wider research field called brain-computer interfaces. (Brain-computer interface research also includes technology such as EEG arrays that allow interface between mind and machine but do not require direct implantation of a device.)

Obejmuje to tak¿e zast¹pienie czynnoœci uszkodzonych uk³adów czuciowych, np. wzrokowego (proteza wzroku). TEXT.Russian: รรฎรงรฃรฎรขรปรฅ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒร รฒรป, รทร รฑรฒรฎ รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฌรปรฅ รญรฅรฉรฐรฎรญรญรปรฌรจ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒร รฒร รฌรจ, รฟรขรซรฟรพรฒรฑรฟ รฒรฅรตรญรฎรซรฎรฃรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฌรจ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขร รฌรจ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฏรฎรครชรซรพรทร รพรฒรฑรฟ รญรฅรฏรฎรฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรฎ รช รกรจรฎรซรฎรฃรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฉ รฒรชร รญรจ รฌรฎรงรฃร - รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฐร รงรฌรฅรนร รพรฒรฑรฟ รญร รชรฎรฐรฅ รฃรฎรซรฎรขรญรฎรฃรฎ รฌรฎรงรฃร , รจรซรจ รฏรฐรจรชรฐรฅรฏรซรฟรพรฒรฑรฟ รช รฝรญรครฎรชรฐร รญรณ. รรกรนรฅรฉ รถรฅรซรผรพ รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรฎรฃรฎ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒร รญรฒร รฌรฎรงรฃร รจ รฑรฎรฑรฐรฅรครฎรฒรฎรทรฅรญรจรฅรฌ รขรญรจรฌร รญรจรฟ รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรต รจรฑรฑรซรฅรครฎรขร รญรจรฉ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑรฎรงรคร รญรจรฅ รกรจรฎรฌรฅรครจรถรจรญรฑรชรจรต รฏรฐรฎรฒรฅรงรฎรข รฎรกรตรฎรค รงรฎรญ รข รฌรฎรงรฃรฅ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฑรฒร รซรจ รดรณรญรชรถรจรฎรญรจรฐรฎรขร รฒรผ รฏรฎรฑรซรฅ รจรญรฑรณรซรผรฒร รจรซรจ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รฒรฐร รขรฌ รฃรฎรซรฎรขรป. รรฒรฎ รขรชรซรพรทร รฅรฒ รจ รฑรฅรญรฑรฎรฐรญรฎรฅ รงร รฌรฅรนรฅรญรจรฟ, รญร รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรฐ, รข รงรฐรฅรญรจรจ. รรฐรณรฃรจรฅ รฌรฎรงรฃรฎรขรปรฅ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒรป รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒรฑรฟ รข รฎรฏรปรฒร รต รญร รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรปรต รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฎ รครซรฟ รงร รฏรจรฑรจ ร รชรฒรจรขรญรฎรฑรฒรจ รฌรฎรงรฃร รครซรฟ รญร รณรทรญรปรต รถรฅรซรฅรฉ. รรฅรชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฌรฎรงรฃรฎรขรปรฅ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒรป รขรชรซรพรทร รฒรผ รข รฑรฅรกรฟ รฑรฎรงรคร รญรจรฅ รจรญรฒรฅรฐรดรฅรฉรฑรฎรข รฌรฅรฆรครณ รญรฅรฉรฐรฎรญรญรปรฌรจ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌร รฌรจ รจ รชรฎรฌรฏรผรพรฒรฅรฐรญรปรฌรจ รทรจรฏร รฌรจ. รรฒร รฐร รกรฎรฒร รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รทร รฑรฒรผรพ รกรฎรซรฅรฅ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎรฃรฎ รจรฑรฑรซรฅรครฎรขร รฒรฅรซรผรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฎรซรฟ รฌรฎรงรฃรฎ-รชรฎรฌรฏรผรพรฒรฅรฐรญรปรต รจรญรฒรฅรฐรดรฅรฉรฑรฎรข. (รรฑรฑรซรฅรครฎรขร รญรจรฅ รจรฎรงรฃรฎ-รชรฎรฌรฏรผรพรฒรฅรฐรญรปรต รจรญรฒรฅรฐรดรฅรฉรฑรฎรข รฒร รชรฆรฅ รขรชรซรพรทร รฅรฒ รข รฑรฅรกรฟ รฒรฅรตรญรฎรซรฎรฃรจรจ, รฒร รชรจรฅ รชร รช รรร รฌร รฑรฑรจรขรป, รฏรฎรงรขรฎรซรฟรพรนรจรฅ รดรณรญรชรถรจรฎรญรจรฐรฎรขร รฒรผ รจรญรฒรฅรฐรดรฅรฉรฑรณ รฌรฅรฆรครณ รฐร รงรณรฌรฎรฌ รจ รฌร รธรจรญรฎรฉ, รญรฎ รญรฅ รฒรฐรฅรกรณรพรฒ รฏรฐรฟรฌรฎรฉ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒร รถรจรจ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขร .)
รรฅรฉรฒรฐรฎรญรจรฉ

TEXT.English: Neutronium (sometimes shortened to neutrium) is a proposed name for a substance composed purely of neutrons. The word was coined by scientist Andreas von Antropoff in 1926 (before the discovery of the neutron) for the conjectured 'element of atomic number zero' that he placed at the head of the periodic table. However, the meaning of the term has changed over time, and from the last half of the 20th century onward it has been also used legitimately to refer to extremely dense substances resembling the neutron-degenerate matter theorized to exist in the cores of neutron stars; henceforth 'degenerate neutronium' will refer to this. Science fiction and popular literature frequently use the term 'neutronium' to refer to a highly dense phase of matter composed primarily of neutrons.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตะนััะพะฝะธะน (ะธะฝะพะณะดะฐ ัะพะบัะฐัะตะฝะฝะพ ะดะพ ะฝะตะนััะธัะผ) - ััะพ ะฟัะตะดะปะพะถะตะฝะฝะพะต ะฝะฐะทะฒะฐะฝะธะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ, ัะพััะพััะตะณะพ ะธัะบะปััะธัะตะปัะฝะพ ะธะท ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะพะฒ. ะกะปะพะฒะพ ะฑัะปะพ ะฟัะธะดัะผะฐะฝะพ ััะตะฝัะผ ะะฝะดัะตะฐัะพะผ ัะพะฝ ะะฝััะพะฟะพััะพะผ ะฒ 1926 ะณะพะดั (ะดะพ ะพัะบัััะธั ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะฐ) ะดะปั ะฟัะตะดะฟะพะปะฐะณะฐะตะผะพะณะพ ยซัะปะตะผะตะฝัะฐ ั ะฐัะพะผะฝัะผ ะฝะพะผะตัะพะผ ะฝะพะปัยป, ะบะพัะพััะน ะพะฝ ะฟะพะผะตััะธะป ะฒะพ ะณะปะฐะฒะต ะฟะตัะธะพะดะธัะตัะบะพะน ัะฐะฑะปะธัั. ะะดะฝะฐะบะพ ะทะฝะฐัะตะฝะธะต ััะพะณะพ ัะตัะผะธะฝะฐ ัะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะตะผ ะผะตะฝัะปะพัั, ะธ ัะพ ะฒัะพัะพะน ะฟะพะปะพะฒะธะฝั 20-ะณะพ ะฒะตะบะฐ ะพะฝ ัะฐะบะถะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปัั ะฝะฐ ะทะฐะบะพะฝะฝัั ะพัะฝะพะฒะฐะฝะธัั ะดะปั ะพะฑะพะทะฝะฐัะตะฝะธั ััะตะทะฒััะฐะนะฝะพ ะฟะปะพัะฝัั ะฒะตัะตััะฒ, ะฝะฐะฟะพะผะธะฝะฐััะธั ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะฝะพ-ะฒััะพะถะดะตะฝะฝะพะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะพ, ัะตะพัะตัะธัะตัะบะธ ัััะตััะฒัััะตะต ะฒ ัะดัะฐั ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะฝัั ะทะฒะตะทะด; ะฒะฟัะตะดั ะบ ััะพะผั ะฑัะดะตั ะพัะฝะพัะธัััั ยซะฒััะพะถะดะตะฝะฝัะน ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะธะนยป. ะะฐััะฝะฐั ัะฐะฝัะฐััะธะบะฐ ะธ ะฟะพะฟัะปััะฝะฐั ะปะธัะตัะฐัััะฐ ัะฐััะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััั ัะตัะผะธะฝ ยซะฝะตะนััะพะฝะธะนยป ะดะปั ะพะฑะพะทะฝะฐัะตะฝะธั ะพัะตะฝั ะฟะปะพัะฝะพะน ัะฐะทั ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ, ัะพััะพััะตะน ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ะธะท ะฝะตะนััะพะฝะพะฒ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Neutronium[COLOR_REVERT] (czasami skracany do neutrium), to proponowana nazwa substancji sk³adaj¹cej siê wy³¹cznie z neutronów. S³owo to zosta³o ukute przez naukowca Andreasa von Antropoffa w 1926 r. (przed odkryciem neutronu) na okreœlenie przypuszczalnego pierwiastka o liczbie atomowej zero, który umieœci³ na czele uk³adu okresowego. Jednak¿e znaczenie tego terminu zmienia³o siê z biegiem czasu i od drugiej po³owy XX wieku zaczêto go legalnie u¿ywaæ w odniesieniu do niezwykle gêstych substancji przypominaj¹cych zdegenerowan¹ materiê neutronow¹, która wed³ug teorii istnieje w j¹drach gwiazd neutronowych. W fantastyce naukowej i literaturze popularnej czêsto u¿ywa siê terminu "neutronium" w odniesieniu do bardzo gêstej fazy materii sk³adaj¹cej siê g³ównie z neutronów.

TEXT.German: Zeitungen
TEXT.Russian: รร รงรฅรฒรป

TEXT.English: A newspaper is a periodical publication containing news regarding current events, informative articles, diverse features, editorials, and advertising. It usually is printed on relatively inexpensive, low-grade paper such as newsprint. By 2007, there were 6,580 daily newspapers in the world selling 395 million copies a day. The late 2000s-early 2010s global recession, combined with the rapid growth of web-based alternatives, caused a serious decline in advertising and circulation, as many papers closed or sharply retrenched operations.
TEXT.German: Das Wort Zeitung war ursprรผnglich der Begriff fรผr eine beliebige Nachricht; die Bedeutung hat sich jedoch im Laufe des 18. Jahrhunderts geรคndert. Heute versteht man darunter ein periodisch erscheinendes Druckerzeugnis mit aktuellem und universellem Inhalt. Dieser besteht aus mehreren inhaltlich in sich abgeschlossenen Texten, die Zeitungsartikel genannt werden und bei deren Abfassung verschiedene journalistische Stilmittel angewandt werden.
TEXT.Russian: รร รงรฅรฒร - รฝรฒรฎ รฏรฅรฐรจรฎรครจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฅ รจรงรคร รญรจรฅ, รฑรฎรครฅรฐรฆร รนรฅรฅ รญรฎรขรฎรฑรฒรจ รฎ รฒรฅรชรณรนรจรต รฑรฎรกรปรฒรจรฟรต, รฏรฎรงรญร รขร รฒรฅรซรผรญรปรฅ รฑรฒร รฒรผรจ, รฐร รงรซรจรทรญรปรฅ รงร รฌรฅรฒรชรจ, รฐรฅรคร รชรถรจรฎรญรญรปรฅ รฑรฒร รฒรผรจ รจ รฐรฅรชรซร รฌรณ. รรญรจ รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฏรฅรทร รฒร รพรฒรฑรฟ รญร รฑรฐร รขรญรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎ รญรฅรครฎรฐรฎรฃรฎรฉ, รญรจรงรชรฎรฑรฎรฐรฒรญรฎรฉ รกรณรฌร รฃรฅ, รญร รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรฐ, รฃร รงรฅรฒรญรฎรฉ รกรณรฌร รฃรฅ. ร 2007 รฃรฎรครณ, รข รฌรจรฐรฅ รฑรณรนรฅรฑรฒรขรฎรขร รซรฎ 6,580 รฅรฆรฅรครญรฅรขรญรปรต รฃร รงรฅรฒ รฏรฐรฎรคร รขร รซรฎรฑรผ รฅรฆรฅรครญรฅรขรญรฎ รครฎ 395 รฌรซรญ. รฝรชรงรฅรฌรฏรซรฟรฐรฎรข. ร รชรฎรญรถรฅ 2000-รต - รญร รทร รซรฅ 2010-รต รฃรฃ. รจรง-รงร รฃรซรฎรกร รซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รฝรชรฎรญรฎรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฑรฏร รคร , รข รฑรฎรทรฅรฒร รญรจรจ รฑ รกรปรฑรฒรฐรปรฌ รฐรฎรฑรฒรฎรฌ รฑรฅรฒรฅรขรปรต ร รซรผรฒรฅรฐรญร รฒรจรข, รขรปรงรขร รซรฎ รฑรฅรฐรผรฅรงรญรปรฉ รฑรฏร รค รข รฐรฅรชรซร รฌรฅ รจ รฒรจรฐร รฆร รต, รฏรฎรฝรฒรฎรฌรณ รฐรฟรค รจรงรคร รญรจรฉ รงร รชรฐรปรซรฎรฑรผ รจรซรจ รณรฌรฅรญรผรธรจรซรฎ รฒรจรฐร รฆ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Gazeta[COLOR_REVERT] jest publikacj¹ okresow¹ zawieraj¹c¹ wiadomoœci dotycz¹ce bie¿¹cych wydarzeñ, artyku³y informacyjne, ró¿norodne felietony, artyku³y redakcyjne i reklamy. TEXT.English: Because of nickel's slow rate of oxidation at room temperature, it is considered corrosion-resistant. Historically, this has led to its use for plating metals such as iron and brass, in chemical apparatus, and in certain alloys that retain a high silvery polish, such as German silver.
TEXT.French: En raison de la lente vitesse d'oxydation du nickel ร tempรฉrature ambiante, il est considรฉrรฉ comme rรฉsistant ร la corrosion. Historiquement, cela a conduit ร son utilisation pour le placage de mรฉtaux comme le fer et le laiton, dans les appareils chimiques, et dans certains alliages qui conservent un poli argentรฉ รฉlevรฉ, comme l'argent allemand.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Nikiel[COLOR_REVERT] (Ni, ³ac. niccolum) - pierwiastek chemiczny z grupy metali przejœciowych w uk³adzie okresowym. Zosta³ odkryty w roku 1751 przez szwedzkiego chemika, Axela Cronstedta. W 1804 r. otrzymano go po raz pierwszy w stanie czystym. Przed nasz¹ er¹ by³ u¿ywany w stopach z miedzi¹ i cynkiem.
TEXT.Russian: รรง-รงร รฌรฅรครซรฅรญรญรฎรฉ รฑรชรฎรฐรฎรฑรฒรจ รฎรชรจรฑรซรฅรญรจรฟ รญรจรชรฅรซรฟ รฏรฐรจ รชรฎรฌรญร รฒรญรฎรฉ รฒรฅรฌรฏรฅรฐร รฒรณรฐรฅ, รฎรญ รฑรทรจรฒร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รชรฎรฐรฐรฎรงรจรฎรญรญรฎรฑรฒรฎรฉรชรจรฌ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรฎรฌ. รรฑรฒรฎรฐรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ รฑรซรฎรฆรจรซรฎรฑรผ รฒร รช, รทรฒรฎ รฝรฒรฎ รฏรฐรจรขรฅรซรฎ รช รฅรฃรฎ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรญรฅรญรจรพ รครซรฟ รฎรกรธรจรขรชรจ รฌรฅรฒร รซรซรฎรข, รฒร รชรจรต รชร รช รฆรฅรซรฅรงรฎ รจ รฌรฅรครผ, รข รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฉ ร รฏรฏร รฐร รฒรณรฐรฅ, รจ รข รฎรฏรฐรฅรครฅรซรฅรญรญรปรต รฑรฏรซร รขร รต, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฑรฎรตรฐร รญรฟรพรฒ รขรปรฑรฎรชรณรพ รฑรฅรฐรฅรกรฐรจรฑรฒรฎ-รฏรฎรซรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรณรพ รฏรฎรขรฅรฐรตรญรฎรฑรฒรผ, รฒร รชรจรฅ รชร รช รรฅรฌรฅรถรชรฎรฅ รรฅรฐรฅรกรฐรฎ.

รรฅรฐรฐร รชรฎรฒ. รฑรชรณรซรผรฏรฒรณรฐรป

TEXT.English: The Nok culture appeared in Nigeria around 1000 BC and vanished under unknown circumstances around 300 AD in the region of West Africa. This region lies in Northern and Central Nigeria. Its social system is thought to have been highly advanced. The Nok culture was considered to be the earliest sub-Saharan producer of life-sized Terracotta. It has been suggested that the Nok culture eventually evolved into the later Yoruba Culture of Ife based on similarities seen in the artwork from these two cultures.
TEXT.Russian: ร รชรณรซรผรฒรณรฐรฅ รรฎรช, รฏรฎรฟรขรจรขรธรฅรฉรฑรฟ รข รรจรฃรฅรฐรจรจ รฎรชรฎรซรฎ 1000 รฃ. รครฎ รญ. รฝ. รจ รจรฑรทรฅรงรญรณรขรธรฅรฉ รข รฐรฅรฃรจรฎรญรฅ รร รฏร รครญรฎรฉ รรดรฐรจรชรจ รฏรฐรจ รญรฅรจรงรขรฅรฑรฒรญรปรต รฎรกรฑรฒรฎรฟรฒรฅรซรผรฑรฒรขร รต รฎรชรฎรซรฎ 300 รฃ. รญ. รฝ. รรฒร รฎรกรซร รฑรฒรผ รซรฅรฆรจรฒ รข รรฅรขรฅรฐรญรฎรฉ รจ รรฅรญรฒรฐร รซรผรญรฎรฉ รรจรฃรฅรฐรจรจ. ร รฃรฎ รฑรฎรถรจร รซรผรญร รฟ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌร , รชร รช รฏรฎรซร รฃร รพรฒ, รกรปรซร รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรฐร รงรขรจรฒรฎรฉ. ร รชรณรซรผรฒรณรฐรฅ รรฎรช รฐร รญรฅรฅ รขรฑรฅรฎ รช รพรฃรณ รฎรฒ รร รตร รฐรป รญร รทร รซรฎรฑรผ รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครฑรฒรขรฎ รฒรฅรฐรฐร รชรฎรฒรฎรขรปรต รฑรชรณรซรผรฏรฒรณรฐ. รรปรซรฎ รขรปรฑรชร รงร รญรฎ รฏรฐรฅรครฏรฎรซรฎรฆรฅรญรจรฅ, รทรฒรฎ รชรณรซรผรฒรณรฐร รรฎรช รข รชรฎรญรฅรทรญรฎรฌ รฑรทรฅรฒรฅ รฐร รงรขรจรขร รซร รฑรผ รข รกรฎรซรฅรฅ รฏรฎรงรครญรจรต รชรณรซรผรฒรณรฐร รต รรฎรฐรณรกร รข รรดรฅ รญร รฎรฑรญรฎรขรฅ รฑรตรฎรครฑรฒรขร รฏรฐรจ รฑรฐร รขรญรฅรญรจรจ รฝรฒรจรต รครขรณรต รชรณรซรผรฒรณรฐ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kultura Nok[COLOR_REVERT] pojawi³a siê w Nigerii oko³o 1000 roku p.n.e. i zniknê³a w nieznanych okolicznoœciach oko³o 300 roku naszej ery w rejonie Afryki Zachodniej. TEXT.English: It is becoming increasingly clear that a large number of chemical companies have failed to manage and nurture their business models well enough. Research and development funds have dried up, and new molecular discoveries are increasingly rare. Few specialty chemical companies have demonstrated the ability or nimbleness to adjust to the new, much more difficult realities in the industry. How well these companies adopt one of three business models will determine success or failure for individual outfits and the industry as a whole.

TEXT.English: Nutella is the brand name of a hazelnut chocolate spread. Manufactured by the Italian company Ferrero, it was introduced to the market in 1964. Nutella is made from sugar, modified palm oil, hazelnuts, cocoa, skimmed milk powder, whey powder, lecithin, and vanillin. Its name is a portmanteau of the English word "nut" and the suffix "-ella", a meaningless suffix added to get a better sounding name.
TEXT.French: Nutella est le nom de marque d'une pรขte ร tartiner au chocolat et aux noisettes. Fabriquรฉe par l'entreprise italienne Ferrero, elle a รฉtรฉ introduite sur le marchรฉ en 1964. Le Nutella est fabriquรฉ ร partir de sucre, d'huile de palme modifiรฉe, de noisettes, de cacao, de lait รฉcrรฉmรฉ en poudre, de lactosรฉrum en poudre, de lรฉcithine et de vanilline. Son nom est un mot-valise formรฉ du mot anglais "nut" (noisette) et du suffixe "-ella", un suffixe sans signification ajoutรฉ pour obtenir un nom plus agrรฉable ร l'oreille.
TEXT.German: Nutella ist ein sรผรer Haselnussbrotaufstrich des italienischen Herstellers Ferrero, der in Konsistenz und Geschmack an Nougat erinnert. Er besteht aus Zucker, Pflanzenรถl, gerรถsteten Haselnรผssen, Kakao, Milchpulver, Sojalecithin und Vanillin.
TEXT.Italian: Nutella รจ il marchio di una crema di nocciole spalmabile. Prodotta dall'azienda italiana Ferrero, fu introdotta sul mercato nel 1964. Gli ingredienti sono zucchero, olio di palma, nocciole, cacao, latte scremato in polvere, siero di latte in polvere, lecitina e vanillina. Il suo nome deriva dalla parola inglese "nut" ("nocciola") e dal suffisso "-ella", un suffisso senza alcun significato, aggiunto per avere un nome orecchiabile.

Nutella wytwarzana jest z cukru, modyfikowanego oleju palmowego, orzechów laskowych, kakao, odt³uszczonego mleka w proszku, serwatki w proszku, lecytyny i waniliny. Jej nazwa jest po³¹czeniem angielskiego s³owa "nut-orzech" i przyrostka "-ella", bezsensownego przyrostka dodanego w celu uzyskania lepiej brzmi¹cej nazwy.
TEXT.Russian: รรณรฒรฅรซรซร - รฝรฒรฎ รดรจรฐรฌรฅรญรญรฎรฅ รญร รงรขร รญรจรฅ รธรฎรชรฎรซร รครญรฎรฉ รฏร รฑรฒรป รฑ รดรณรญรครณรชรฎรฌ . รรงรฃรฎรฒรฎรขรซรฅรญร รจรฒร รซรผรฟรญรฑรชรฎรฉ รชรฎรฌรฏร รญรจรฅรฉ รรฅรฐรฐรฅรฐรฎ, รขรปรธรฅรครธรฅรฉ รญร รฐรปรญรฎรช รข 1964 รฃรฎรครณ. รรณรฒรฅรซรซร รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครจรฒรฑรฟ รจรง รฑร รตร รฐร , รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒร รญรญรฎรฃรฎ รฏร รซรผรฌรฎรขรฎรฃรฎ รฌร รฑรซร , รฎรฐรฅรตรฎรข, รชร รชร รฎ, รฑรณรตรฎรฃรฎ รฎรกรฅรงรฆรจรฐรฅรญรญรฎรฃรฎ รฌรฎรซรฎรชร , รฑรณรตรฎรฉ รฌรฎรซรฎรทรญรฎรฉ รฑรปรขรฎรฐรฎรฒรชรจ, รซรฅรถรจรฒรจรญร รจ รขร รญรจรซรจรญร .
