TEXT.English: The pistachio a member of the cashew family, is a small tree originating from Central Asia and the Middle East. Pistachio trees can be found in regions of Iran, Syria, Lebanon, Turkey, Greece, Xinjiang (China), Tunisia, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, India, Egypt, Italy (Sicily), Uzbekistan, Afghanistan (especially in the provinces of Samangan and Badghis), and the United States, specifically in California. It is indigenous to the eastern Mediterranean (Cyprus and Turkey to Israel and Syria), Central Asia (Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, Afghanistan, Tajikistan, and Kyrgyzstan) and Xinjiang. The tree produces seeds that are widely consumed as food.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pistazien[COLOR_REVERT] (Pistacia vera) sind nahe Verwandte der Cashews aus der Familie der Sumachgewรคchse. Ihre Heimat ist der Nahe Osten. Sie kรถnnen bis zu 12 m hoch und bis zu 300 Jahre alt werden. Die ovalen Steinfrรผchte beherbergen in einer harten Schale die essbaren hellgrรผnen Pistaziensamen.[NEWLINE]Pistazien gehรถren zu den รคltesten blรผhenden Kulturpflanzen. Sie wurden bereits im Epipalรคolithikum (vor etwa 15000 Jahren) gesammelt und spรคtestens seit der Antike gezielt angebaut. Im 1. Jahrhundert n.Chr. wurden sie nahezu zeitgleich in Italien und Spanien eingefรผhrt. Nach Amerika gelangten sie mit Einwanderern erst 1880, umso erstaunlicher ist es, dass die USA heute neben dem Iran, der Tรผrkei und China zu den weltweit fรผhrenden Pistazienproduzenten zรคhlen.[NEWLINE]Pistazien werden meist gerรถstet und gesalzen als Knabberei verzehrt. Auรerdem sind sie Zutaten diverser Sรผรwaren wie Mozartkugeln, Pralinen und Baklava, von Wurst (z.B. Mortadella) und Speiseeis. Wegen รberweidung und รผbermรครiger Fruchtnutzung steht der Pistazienbaum als potenziell gefรคhrdet auf der Roten Liste der gefรคhrdeten Arten.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pistacje[COLOR_REVERT] nale¿¹ce do rodziny nerkowców to ma³e drzewo pochodz¹ce z Azji Œrodkowej i Bliskiego Wschodu. TEXT.English: Plasma is one of the four fundamental states of matter, and was first described by chemist Irving Langmuir in the 1920s. It consists of a gas of ions - atoms which have some of their orbital electrons removed โ and free electrons. Plasma can be artificially generated by heating or subjecting a neutral gas to a strong electromagnetic field to the point where an ionized gaseous substance becomes increasingly electrically conductive. The resulting charged ions and electrons become influenced by long-range electromagnetic fields, making the plasma dynamics more sensitive to these fields than a neutral gas.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Plazma[COLOR_REVERT] jest jednym z czterech podstawowych stanów materii i zosta³a po raz pierwszy opisana przez chemika Irvinga Langmuira w latach dwudziestych XX wieku. Sk³ada siê z gazu jonów atomów, z których usuniêto czêœæ elektronów z orbity, oraz wolnych elektronów. Plazmê mo¿na wytworzyæ sztucznie, podgrzewaj¹c lub poddaj¹c gaz obojêtny dzia³aniu silnego pola elektromagnetycznego do momentu, w którym zjonizowana substancja gazowa staje siê coraz bardziej przewodz¹ca pr¹d elektryczny. Powsta³e w ten sposób na³adowane jony i elektrony ulegaj¹ wp³ywowi pól elektromagnetycznych dalekiego zasiêgu, przez co dynamika plazmy jest bardziej wra¿liwa na te pola ni¿ gaz obojêtny.

TEXT.English: A plastic material is any of a wide range of synthetic or semi-synthetic organic solids that are moldeable. Plastics are typically organic polymers of high molecular mass, but they often contain other substances. They are usually synthetic, most commonly derived from petrochemicals, but many are partially natural.
TEXT.French: Un matรฉriau plastique est l'un des nombreux solides organiques synthรฉtiques ou semi-synthรฉtiques qui sont moulables. Les plastiques sont gรฉnรฉralement des polymรจres organiques de masse molรฉculaire รฉlevรฉe, mais ils contiennent souvent d'autres substances. Ils sont gรฉnรฉralement synthรฉtiques, le plus souvent dรฉrivรฉs de produits pรฉtrochimiques, mais beaucoup sont partiellement naturels.
TEXT.Italian: Un materiale plastico รจ un qualsiasi materiale sintetico o semi-sintetico che ha la capacitร di piegarsi allo stato solido. La plastica รจ solitamente un polimero organico con elevata massa molecolare, ma spesso contiene altre sostanze; รจ solitamente sintetica, derivata dai prodotti petrolchimici, ma alcune varietร hanno anche componenti naturali.

TEXT.English: Platinum is a precious metal. Platinum was well-known to many ancient cultures, but most textbooks date the discovery of the metal to the eighteenth century when it was finally isolated in pure form. Archaeologists have discovered platinum artefacts originating from Egypt and Pre-Columbian America. The word platinum was derived from the Spanish platina or "little silver". Europeans became aware of platinum after Spanish Conquistadors brought it back with plundered gold and silver from the New World. Platinum became a very fashionable jewellery material during the nineteenth century. Many famous royal jewels including the Hope Diamond and the Star of Africa are mounted in platinum settings. During World War II, platinum use was banned in the US for any non-military purpose. This event suppressed consumer demand for platinum for decades, but it has made a strong resurgence in recent years as a premium jewellery material.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Platyna[COLOR_REVERT] jest metalem szlachetnym. Archeolodzy odkryli artefakty platynowe pochodz¹ce z Egiptu i Ameryki Prekolumbijskiej. S³owo platyna pochodzi od hiszpañskiego s³owa platina, czyli "ma³ego srebra". Platyna sta³a siê bardzo modnym materia³em jubilerskim w XIX wieku. Wiele s³ynnych klejnotów królewskich, w tym Diament Nadziei i Gwiazda Afryki, jest oprawionych w platynowe oprawy. TEXT.English: A pocket universe is a naturally or artificially-created universe, that exist within the bounds of another universe. The term is derived from its illustration in the actual universe, where it is usually portrayed as an orb which would fit in a pocket. In the inflationary theory, the concept defines a realm, like the one that contains the observable universe, as only one of many inflationary zones.
TEXT.French: Un univers de poche est un univers naturel ou crรฉรฉ artificiellement, qui existe dans les limites d'un autre univers. Le terme est dรฉrivรฉ de son illustration dans l'univers rรฉel, oรน il est gรฉnรฉralement reprรฉsentรฉ comme un orbe qui tiendrait dans une poche. Dans la thรฉorie de l'inflation, le concept dรฉfinit un royaume, comme celui qui contient l'univers observable, comme un seul parmi de nombreuses zones inflationnistes.
TEXT.Italian: Un universo tascabile รจ un universo creato naturalmente o artificialmente, che esiste all'interno dei limiti di un altro universo. Il termine deriva dalla sua illustrazione nell'universo reale, dove di solito รจ rappresentato come un globo di grandezza tascabile. Nella teoria inflazionistica, il concetto definisce la realtร , come quella che contiene l'universo osservabile, come una di numerose zone inflazionistiche.

W teorii inflacji koncepcja ta definiuje dziedzinê, tak¹ jak ta, która zawiera obserwowalny wszechœwiat, jako tylko jedn¹ z wielu stref inflacyjnych.

TEXT.English: A pomegranate is a fruit-bearing deciduous shrub or small tree growing between 5 and 8 m tall. The pomegranate is native to the Caucasus, the Himalayas in north Pakistan and Northern India. It has been cultivated in the Caucasus since ancient times, and today, is widely cultivated throughout Iran, Azerbaijan, Armenia, Afghanistan, India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Iraq, Egypt, China, Burma, Saudi Arabia, Israel, Jordan, the drier parts of southeast Asia, the Mediterranean region of Southern Europe, and tropical Africa. Introduced into Latin America and California by Spanish settlers in 1769, pomegranate is now cultivated in parts of California and Arizona for juice production
TEXT.German: Der [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Granatapfel[COLOR_REVERT] (Punica granatum) ist ein sommergrรผner, kleiner Baum, der meist als Strauch kultiviert wird. Er kommt ursprรผnglich aus West- bis Mittelasien, wird aber heute hauptsรคchlich im Mittelmeerraum angebaut. Begehrt sind seine kugeligen, apfelรคhnlichen Frรผchte, die ganz unterschiedliche Farben haben kรถnnen von Grรผn-Gelb รผber verschiedene Rot- und Brauntรถne bis hin zu Schwarz-Violett. Granatรคpfel sind Scheinfrรผchte, deren bittere Schale die verzehrbaren Samen umschlieรt. Diese befinden sich in einzelnen Kammern, die durch Membranen voneinander getrennt sind. Der Granatapfel ist ein Symbol fรผr Leben und Fruchtbarkeit, aber als Reichsapfel auch fรผr die Macht.[NEWLINE]Es gibt kaum frรผhe archรคologische Funde von Granatรคpfeln, da diese meist direkt verzehrt wurden. Bewiesen ist, dass sie in der frรผhen Bronzezeit in Mesopotamien bekannt waren und als Nahrung genutzt wurden. Sie hatten auch religiรถse Bedeutung, denn man nutzte die Frรผchte als Opfer- oder Grabbeigaben. In Deutschland ist der Granatapfel seit dem Mittelalter nachgewiesen.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Granat[COLOR_REVERT], to owocuj¹cy krzew liœciasty lub ma³e drzewo osi¹gaj¹ce wysokoœæ od 5 do 8 m. TEXT.English: Porcelain is a ceramic material made by heating materials in a kiln to temperatures between 1,200 and 1,400 ยฐC (2,200 and 2,600 ยฐF). Properties associated with porcelain include low permeability and elasticity, considerable strength, hardness, toughness, whiteness, translucency and resonance, and a high resistance to chemical attack and thermal shock. Its manufacturing process is more demanding than that for earthenware and stoneware, and this is the reason it has usually been regarded as the most prestigious type of pottery. It combines well with both glazes and paint, and can be modelled very well, allowing a huge range of decorative treatments in tablewares, vessels and figurines. It also has many uses in technology and industry.
TEXT.Italian: La porcellana รจ un materiale ceramico costituito da materiali riscaldanti in un forno a temperature comprese tra 1.200 e 1.400 ยฐC. Le proprietร associate alla porcellana comprendono bassa permeabilitร ed elasticitร , notevole resistenza, durezza, tenacitร , bianchezza, traslucenza e risonanza e un'alta resistenza agli attacchi chimici e agli shock termici. Il suo processo di produzione รจ piรน impegnativo di quello per terracotta e gres, ed รจ per questo che la porcellana viene generalmente considerata il tipo piรน prestigioso di ceramica. Si combina bene con smalti e vernici e puรฒ essere modellata molto bene, consentendo una vasta gamma di trattamenti decorativi in stoviglie, vasi e figurine. Ha anche molti usi nella tecnologia e nell'industria.

TEXT.English: Potash is any of various mined and manufactured salts that contain potassium in water-soluble form. The name derives from "pot ash", which refers to plant ashes soaked in water in a pot, the primary means of manufacturing the product before the industrial era.
TEXT.French: La potasse est l'un des divers sels extraits et fabriquรฉs qui contiennent du potassium sous forme soluble dans l'eau. Le nom dรฉrive de "cendre de pot", qui fait rรฉfรฉrence aux cendres de plantes trempรฉes dans l'eau dans un pot, le principal moyen de fabrication du produit avant l'รจre industrielle.

TEXT.English: A potato is a root vegetable and a starchy tuber. Potatoes were domesticated approximately 7,000-10,000 years ago in an area comprised between southern Peru and extreme northwestern Bolivia. Then they spread throughout the Americas, and were introduced to Europe in the second half of the 16th century. Today they are a staple food in many parts of the world (especially Northern and Eastern Europe), and an integral part of much of the world's food supply.
TEXT.French: La pomme de terre est un lรฉgume-racine et un tubercule fรฉculent. Les pommes de terre ont รฉtรฉ domestiquรฉes il y a environ 7 000 ร 10 000 ans dans une zone comprise entre le sud du Pรฉrou et l'extrรชme nord-ouest de la Bolivie. Puis elles se sont rรฉpandues dans toutes les Amรฉriques, et ont รฉtรฉ introduites en Europe dans la seconde moitiรฉ du 16e siรจcle. Aujourd'hui, elles sont un aliment de base dans de nombreuses parties du monde (en particulier en Europe du Nord et de l'Est), et font partie intรฉgrante de l'approvisionnement alimentaire mondial.
TEXT.German: Die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kartoffel[COLOR_REVERT] (Solanum tuberosum), auch Erdapfel oder Erd-/Grundbirne genannt, ist eine Pflanzenart aus der Familie der Nachtschattengewรคchse (Solanaceae). Weltweit werden jรคhrlich รผber 300 Millionen Tonnen Kartoffeln geerntet. Die Kartoffel ist damit in groรen Teilen der Welt ein wichtiges Grundnahrungsmittel. In Mitteleuropa ist sie sogar das Hauptgrundnahrungsmittel.[NEWLINE]Die Kartoffel stammt aus den sรผdamerikanischen Anden. Dort lernten die Spanier in der ersten Hรคlfte des 16. Jahrhunderts von den Inka die Frucht Papa (aus Quechua: die Knolle) kennen. In Europa angekommen, zunรคchst auf den Kanarischen Inseln und in Andalusien, wurde sie Patata genannt - der englische Name potato oder der franzรถsische Spitzname patate erinnern noch heute daran, ebenso wie die alte deutsche Bezeichnung Batate, die heute die Sรผรkartoffel bezeichnet. Die Kartoffel wurde zunรคchst wegen der schรถnen Blรผte und des รผppigen Laubes als reine Zierpflanze betrachtet und in botanischen Gรคrten ausgestellt. Die Europรคer gingen zunรคchst fรคlschlicherweise davon aus, dass die oberirdischen Beeren mit den Samen die Frรผchte der Pflanze wรคren. Alle oberirdischen Teile der Kartoffelpflanze sind fรผr Menschen aber leicht giftig und fรผhren zu Bauchschmerzen und anderen Vergiftungserscheinungen. Deshalb dauerte es einige Generationen, bis die Kartoffel eine Hauptnahrungsquelle wurde.[NEWLINE]Die รคltesten Spuren der Wildkartoffel wurden auf der patagonischen Insel Chiloรฉ gefunden, man schรคtzt ihr Alter auf 13000 Jahre v.Chr.
TEXT.Italian: Una patata รจ un ortaggio a radice e un tubero amidaceo. Le patate sono state addomesticate circa 7.000-10.000 anni fa in un'area compresa tra il Perรน meridionale e la Bolivia nord-occidentale. Si diffusero successivamente in tutte le Americhe e, nella seconda metร del XVI secolo, furono introdotte in Europa. Oggi sono un alimento base in molte parti del mondo (specialmente in Europa settentrionale e orientale) e parte integrante di gran parte dell'offerta alimentare mondiale.

TEXT.German: Kartoffelchips
TEXT.Russian: รรจรฏรฑรป

TEXT.English: A potato chip (known as a crisp in British English and Hiberno-English, and as either a chip or wafer in Indian English) is a thin slice of potato that is deep fried or baked until crunchy. Potato chips are commonly served as an appetizer, side dish, or snack. The basic chips are cooked and salted; additional varieties are manufactured using various flavorings and ingredients including seasonings, herbs, spices, cheeses, and artificial additives.
TEXT.French: Une chips de pomme de terre (connue sous le nom de crisp en anglais britannique et hiberno-anglais, et comme chip ou wafer en anglais indien) est une fine tranche de pomme de terre qui est frite ou cuite au four jusqu'ร ce qu'elle soit croustillante. Les chips de pomme de terre sont gรฉnรฉralement servies comme apรฉritif, accompagnement ou collation. Les chips de base sont cuites et salรฉes ; des variรฉtรฉs supplรฉmentaires sont fabriquรฉes en utilisant divers arรดmes et ingrรฉdients, notamment des assaisonnements, des herbes, des รฉpices, des fromages et des additifs artificiels.
TEXT.German: Kartoffelchips, kurz Chips, in der Schweiz auch Pommes-Chips, in (Ost-)รsterreich frรผher, heute nur noch gelegentlich, Rohscheiben genannt, sind in dรผnne Scheiben geschnittene und frittierte oder gebackene Kartoffeln. Sie dienen als Beilage oder Snack. Kartoffelchips gehรถren zu den beliebtesten Snacks und werden weltweit in vielen Geschmacksvarianten angeboten. In den USA werden sie Chips genannt, in Groรbritannien und Irland jedoch Crisps, da dort Pommes frites Chips genannt werden (vgl. "Fish and Chips").

Poszczególne smaki uzyskuje siê dziêki doborowi przypraw i aromatów, rzadziej zió³.

TEXT.German: Keramik
TEXT.Russian: รรฎรฑรณรคร

TEXT.English: The invention of pottery was essential to the development of agricultural societies. To get the most out of seasonal crops and domesticated animals, a tribe needed sturdy, waterproof containers in which to store and protect surplus food. The discovery of the properties of clay, and the invention of the kiln and the potter's wheel made it possible to build such containers. The earliest examples of Western pottery date back to as early as 6500 BC. Most of the examples from this period were strictly utilitarian. Although use continued to be primarily practical, decorative pottery soon became a ritualistic and aesthetic art form in cultures all over the world. Some of the most impressive examples of artistic pottery comes from the pre-Columbian cultures of South America, and from the Ming dynasty in 14th century China.

TEXT.Polish: Wynalezienie [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]ceramiki[COLOR_REVERT] odegra³o kluczow¹ rolê w rozwoju spo³eczeñstw rolniczych. Aby jak najlepiej wykorzystaæ sezonowe uprawy i udomowione zwierzêta, plemiê potrzebowa³o solidnych, wodoodpornych pojemników do przechowywania i ochrony nadwy¿ek ¿ywnoœci. Odkrycie w³aœciwoœci gliny oraz wynalezienie pieca i ko³a garncarskiego umo¿liwi³o budowanie takich pojemników. Najwczeœniejsze przyk³ady ceramiki zachodniej pochodz¹ ju¿ z 6500 roku p.n.e. Chocia¿ ceramika dekoracyjna nadal mia³a przede wszystkim charakter praktyczny, szybko sta³a siê rytualn¹ i estetyczn¹ form¹ sztuki w kulturach na ca³ym œwiecie. Niektóre z najbardziej imponuj¹cych przyk³adów ceramiki artystycznej pochodz¹ z kultur prekolumbijskich Ameryki Po³udniowej oraz z dynastii Ming w XIV-wiecznych Chinach.

TEXT.Russian: รรฒรจรถรป

TEXT.English: Poultry are domesticated birds kept by humans for the eggs they produce, their meat, their feathers, or sometimes as pets. These birds are most typically members of the superorder Galloanserae (fowl), especially the order Galliformes (which includes chickens, quails and turkeys) and the family Anatidae, in order Anseriformes, commonly known as "waterfowl" and including domestic ducks and domestic geese. Poultry also includes other birds that are killed for their meat, such as the young of pigeons (known as squabs) but does not include similar wild birds hunted for sport or food and known as game. The word "poultry" comes from the French/Norman word poule, itself derived from the Latin word pullus, which means small animal.
TEXT.German: Als [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Geflรผgel[COLOR_REVERT] bezeichnet man alle Vogelarten, die als Nutz- oder Haustiere gezรผchtet werden. Da alle diese Vogelarten zunรคchst als Nahrung gejagt wurden, ist Geflรผgel grundsรคtzlich zum Verzehr geeignet. Zu Geflรผgel zรคhlen Hรผhner, Puten, Gรคnse, Enten, Tauben und diverse Wildvogelarten wie Wachteln, Fasane, Reb-, Perl- und Birkhรผhner und sogar Strauรe. Neben dem Fleisch werden besonders von Hรผhnern, Wachteln und Strauรen auch die Eier verwertet. Hรผhnereier dienen heutzutage auch zur Gewinnung von Impfstoffen.

TEXT.English: A power tool is a tool that is actuated by an additional power source and mechanism other than the solely manual labour used with hand tools. The most common types of power tools use electric motors. Internal combustion engines and compressed air are also commonly used. Other power sources include steam engines, direct burning of fuels and propellants, or even natural power sources like wind or moving water. Tools directly driven by animal power are not generally considered power tools.
TEXT.French: Un outil รฉlectrique est un outil qui est actionnรฉ par une source d'รฉnergie et un mรฉcanisme supplรฉmentaires autres que le travail manuel utilisรฉ avec les outils ร main. Les types les plus courants d'outils รฉlectriques utilisent des moteurs รฉlectriques. Les moteurs ร combustion interne et l'air comprimรฉ sont รฉgalement couramment utilisรฉs. D'autres sources d'รฉnergie comprennent les moteurs ร vapeur, la combustion directe de combustibles et de propergols, ou mรชme des sources d'รฉnergie naturelles comme le vent ou l'eau en mouvement. Les outils directement entraรฎnรฉs par la force animale ne sont gรฉnรฉralement pas considรฉrรฉs comme des outils รฉlectriques.

TEXT.Russian: รรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรจรญรฑรฒรฐรณรฌรฅรญรฒ - รฝรฒรฎ รจรญรฑรฒรฐรณรฌรฅรญรฒ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฏรฐรจรขรฎรครจรฒรฑรฟ รข รครฅรฉรฑรฒรขรจรฅ รครฎรฏรฎรซรญรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรปรฌ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรฎรฌ รฏรจรฒร รญรจรฟ รจ รครฐ. รฌรฅรตร รญรจรงรฌรฎรฌ, รฒรฅรฌ รฑร รฌรปรฌ รจรฑรชรซรพรทร รฟ รฐรณรทรญรฎรฉ รฒรฐรณรค. รร รจรกรฎรซรฅรฅ รฐร รฑรฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฐร รญรฅรญรญรปรฅ รฒรจรฏรป รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรจรญรฑรฒรฐรณรฌรฅรญรฒรฎรข รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒรฑรฟ รฑ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรครขรจรฃร รฒรฅรซรฟรฌรจ. รร รชรฆรฅ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒรฑรฟ รครขรจรฃร รฒรฅรซรจ รขรญรณรฒรฐรฅรญรญรฅรฃรฎ รฑรฃรฎรฐร รญรจรฟ รจ รฑรฆร รฒรฎรฃรฎ รขรฎรงรครณรตร . รรฐรณรฃรจรฅ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรจ รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรจ รขรชรซรพรทร รพรฒ รฏร รฐรฎรขรปรฅ รครขรจรฃร รฒรฅรซรจ, รญรฅรฏรฎรฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรฎรฅ รฑรฆรจรฃร รญรจรฅ รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขร รจ รฃรฎรฐรพรทรจรต รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรฎรข รจรซรจ รคร รฆรฅ รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครญรปรฅ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรจ รฝรญรฅรฐรฃรจรจ, รฒร รชรจรฅ รชร รช รขรฅรฒรฅรฐ รจรซรจ รครขรจรฆรณรนร รฟรฑรฟ รขรฎรคร . รรฃรฐรฅรฃร รฒรป, รครขรจรฆรจรฌรปรฅ รฌรณรฑรชรณรซรผรญรฎรฉ รฑรจรซรฎรฉ รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรปรต, รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ, รญรฅ รฑรทรจรฒร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรจรญรฑรฒรฐรณรฌรฅรญรฒร รฌรจ.

TEXT.English: The most common culinary species is the Indian fig opuntia (O. ficus-indica). Most culinary uses of the term "prickly pear" refer to this species. Prickly pears are also known as tuna (fruit) or nopal (paddle, plural nopales) from the Nahuatl word nopalli for the pads, or nostle, from the Nahuatl word nochtli for the fruit; or paddle cactus. Prickly pears typically grow with flat, rounded cladodes (also called platyclades) armed with two kinds of spines; large, smooth, fixed spines and small, hairlike prickles called glochids, that easily penetrate skin and detach from the plant. Many types of prickly pears grow into dense, tangled structures.
TEXT.German: Als [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kaktusfeigen[COLOR_REVERT] werden die Frรผchte der Kakteenart Opuntia ficus-indica bezeichnet. Es handelt sich um etwa 6 bis 10 cm lange, eifรถrmige oder lรคngliche Frรผchte von gelbgrรผner, oranger oder roter Farbe. Sie werden zusammen mit ihren essbaren Kernen verzehrt und haben einen sรผร-sรคuerlichen Geschmack. Man ist Kaktusfeigen entweder roh oder verarbeitet sie zu Marmelade oder Likรถr. Der Feigenkaktus stammt vermutlich aus Mexiko. Er ist die domestizierte Form von Opuntia megacantha, die nur noch รผber harmlose Dornen verfรผgt. Er war eine traditionelle Heilpflanze der Azteken und wird auch heute noch in der Volksmedizin verwendet, z.B. gegen Diabetes.
TEXT.Russian: ะกะฐะผัะน ัะฐัะฟัะพัััะฐะฝะตะฝะฝัะน ะบัะปะธะฝะฐัะฝัะน ะฒะธะด - ะพะฟัะฝัะธั ะธะฝะดะธะนัะบะพะณะพ ะธะฝะถะธัะฐ (O. ficus-indica). ะะพะปััะธะฝััะฒะพ ะบัะปะธะฝะฐัะฝัั ะฟัะธะผะตะฝะตะฝะธะน ัะตัะผะธะฝะฐ ยซะพะฟัะฝัะธัยป ะพัะฝะพััััั ะบ ััะพะผั ะฒะธะดั. ะะฟัะฝัะธั ัะฐะบะถะต ะธะทะฒะตััะฝะฐ ะบะฐะบ ััะฝะตั (ัััะบั) ะธะปะธ nopal (ะฒะตัะปะพ, ะผะฝะพะถะตััะฒะตะฝะฝะพะต ัะธัะปะพ nopales) ะพั ัะปะพะฒะฐ ะฝะฐ ะฝะฐัะฐัะปั nopalli, ะพะฑะพะทะฝะฐัะฐััะตะณะพ ะฟะพะดััะตัะบะธ, ะธะปะธ ะฝะพััะฐะปั, ะพั ัะปะพะฒะฐ nochtli, ะพะฑะพะทะฝะฐัะฐััะตะณะพ ัััะบั ะฝะฐ ะฝะฐัะฐัะปะต; ะธะปะธ ะฒะตัะปะพ ะบะฐะบััั. ะะฟัะฝัะธั ะพะฑััะฝะพ ัะฐััะตั ั ะฟะปะพัะบะธะผะธ ะทะฐะบััะณะปะตะฝะฝัะผะธ ะบะปะฐะดะพะดะฐะผะธ (ัะฐะบะถะต ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐะตะผัะผะธ ะฟะปะฐััะธะฝัะฐััะผะธ), ะฒะพะพััะถะตะฝะฝัะผะธ ะดะฒัะผั ะฒะธะดะฐะผะธ ัะธะฟะพะฒ; ะฑะพะปััะธะต, ะณะปะฐะดะบะธะต, ะฝะตะฟะพะดะฒะธะถะฝัะต ะบะพะปััะบะธ ะธ ะผะฐะปะตะฝัะบะธะต, ะฟะพั ะพะถะธะต ะฝะฐ ะฒะพะปะพัั ะบะพะปััะบะธ, ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐะตะผัะต ะณะปะพั ะธะดะตัะผะธ, ะบะพัะพััะต ะปะตะณะบะพ ะฟัะพะฝะธะบะฐัั ะฒ ะบะพะถั ะธ ะพัะดะตะปััััั ะพั ัะฐััะตะฝะธั. ะะฝะพะณะธะต ะฒะธะดั ะพะฟัะฝัะธะธ ะฟัะตะฒัะฐัะฐัััั ะฒ ะฟะปะพัะฝัะต ัะฟััะฐะฝะฝัะต ััััะบัััั.
TEXT.Polish: Najpopularniejszym gatunkiem kulinarnym jest [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]opuncja figowa indyjska[COLOR_REVERT] (O. ficus-indica). Wiêkszoœæ kulinarnych zastosowañ terminu "opuncja" odnosi siê do tego gatunku. Opuncje s¹ równie¿ znane jako tuñczyk (owoce) lub nopal (wios³o, liczba mnoga nopales) od s³owa nopalli w jêzyku nahuatl oznaczaj¹cego podk³adki lub nostle od s³owa nochtli w jêzyku nahuatl oznaczaj¹cego owoc. Opuncje zazwyczaj wyrastaj¹ z p³askimi, zaokr¹glonymi kladodami (zwanymi tak¿e platykladami) uzbrojonymi w dwa rodzaje kolców; du¿e, g³adkie, nieruchome kolce i ma³e, w³oskowate kolce zwane glochidami, które ³atwo wnikaj¹ w skórê i oddzielaj¹ siê od roœliny. Wiele rodzajów opuncji wyrasta w gêste, spl¹tane struktury.

TEXT.English: Lumber or timber is wood that is used in any of its stages from felling through readiness for use as structural material for construction, or wood pulp for paper production. Lumber is supplied either rough or finished. Besides pulpwood, rough lumber is the raw material for furniture-making and other items requiring additional cutting and shaping. It is available in many species, usually hardwoods. Finished lumber is supplied in standard sizes, mostly for the construction industry, primarily softwood from coniferous species including pine, fir and spruce (collectively known as Spruce-pine-fir), cedar, hemlock, but also some hardwood, for high-grade flooring.
TEXT.German: Unter [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Edelholz[COLOR_REVERT] versteht man Holzsorten, die sich durch bestimmte Eigenschaften von weit verbreiteten Hรถlzern wie Birke oder Buche unterscheiden. Dazu zรคhlen eine ausdrucksvolle Farbgebung, markante Kontraste, schรถne Maserungen und andere Auffรคlligkeiten, aber auch seltenes Vorkommen und dementsprechend hoher Wert oder eine besondere Resistenz gegen Schรคdlingsbefall. Letzten Endes kann die Einklassifizierung als Edelholz auch von einer rein subjektiven Wertschรคtzung herrรผhren. Einige bekannte Beispiele fรผr Edelhรถlzer sind Mahagoni, Palisander, Teak und Ebenholz. Edelholz wird gern fรผr Kunstwerke und Musikinstrumente verwendet. Auรerdem werden hรคufig Furniere und Intarsien fรผr Mรถbel und Automobilausstattung aus Edelhรถlzern hergestellt.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Drewno[COLOR_REVERT] - surowiec drzewny otrzymywany ze œciêtych drzew i formowany przez obróbkê w ró¿nego rodzaju sortymenty. Pod wzglêdem technicznym drewno jest naturalnym materia³em kompozytowym o osnowie polimerowej wzmacniany ci¹g³ymi w³óknami polimerowymi, którymi s¹ pod³u¿ne komórki zorientowane jednoosiowo.
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฑรฎรฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซ รจรซรจ รฏรจรซรฎรฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซ - รฒรฅรฐรฌรจรญ, รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฌรปรฉ, รทรฒรฎรกรป รฎรฏรจรฑร รฒรผ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญรณ, รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒร รญรญรณรพ รครซรฟ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรฟ รจรง รขรปรครฅรฐรฆร รญรปรต รฑรฐรณรกรซรฅรญรญรปรต รครฅรฐรฅรขรผรฅรข, รครซรฟ รชรฎรญรฅรทรญรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒร รชร รช รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซร , รฏรฎรครตรฎรครฟรนรฅรฃรฎ รครซรฟ รจรญรครณรฑรฒรฐรจร รซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรฟ รชร รช รชรฎรญรฑรฒรฐรณรชรฒรจรขรญรฎรฃรฎ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซ รครซรฟ รฑรฒรฐรฎรจรฒรฅรซรผรฑรฒรขร รจรซรจ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรญร รฟ รฌร รฑรฑร รครซรฟ รกรณรฌร รฆรญรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครฑรฒรขร . ร รรฅรซรจรชรฎรกรฐรจรฒร รญรจรจ รจ รรขรฑรฒรฐร รซรจรจ "รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญร " - รฒรฅรฐรฌรจรญ, รฒร รชรฆรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฌรปรฉ รครซรฟ รฐร รฑรฏรจรซรฅรญรญรปรต รครฅรฐรฅรขรฟรญรญรปรต รจรงรครฅรซรจรฉ (รฒรฎ รฅรฑรฒรผ, รครฎรฑรฎรช), รฒรฎรฃรคร รชร รช รขรฎรฎรกรนรฅ รข รรฎรฅรครจรญรฅรญรญรปรต รรฒร รฒร รต รจ รร รญร รครฅ, รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒ รฎรกรฐรฅรงรชรจ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญรป รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญร . ร รรฎรฅรครจรญรฅรญรญรปรต รรฒร รฒร รต รจ รร รญร รครฅ รฐร รฑรฏรจรซรฅรญรญรปรฅ รครฅรฐรฅรขรฟรญรญรปรฅ รจรงรครฅรซรจรฟ รฏรฟรฒรจ รครพรฉรฌรฎรข (127 รฌรจรซรซรจรฌรฅรฒรฐรฎรข) (รญรฎรฌรจรญร รซรผรญรปรฉ รฐร รงรฌรฅรฐ) รครจร รฌรฅรฒรฐร รจรซรจ รกรฎรซรผรธรฅ รจรญรฎรฃรคร รญร รงรปรขร รพรฒรฑรฟ "รฏรจรซรฎรฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซ". รรฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญร รฏรฎรฑรฒร รขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รจรซรจ รญรฅรฑรฒรฐรฎรฃร รญรฎรฉ รจรซรจ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒร รญรญรฎรฉ. รรฐรณรกร รฟ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญร - รฑรปรฐรผรฅ รครซรฟ รฑรฎรงรคร รญรจรฟ รฌรฅรกรฅรซรจ รจ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รจรงรครฅรซรจรฉ, รฒรฐรฅรกรณรพรนรจรต รครฎรฏรฎรซรญรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฉ รฐรฅรงรชรจ รจ รฑรฒรฐรฎรฃร รญรจรฟ. รรญร รครฎรฑรฒรณรฏรญร รขรฎ รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รฐร รงรญรฎรขรจรครญรฎรฑรฒรฟรต, รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฒรขรฅรฐรครฎรฉ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญรป. รรกรฐร รกรฎรฒร รญรญร รฟ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญร รฏรฎรฑรฒร รขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รฑรฒร รญรคร รฐรฒรญรปรต รฐร รงรฌรฅรฐร รต, รฃรซร รขรญรปรฌ รฎรกรฐร รงรฎรฌ รครซรฟ รฑรฒรฐรฎรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฉ รฏรฐรฎรฌรปรธรซรฅรญรญรฎรฑรฒรจ, รจ รฏรฐรฅรฆรครฅ รขรฑรฅรฃรฎ รตรขรฎรฉรญรปรฅ (รจรฌรฅรพรนรจรฅ รจรฃรซรป) รฐร รงรญรฎรขรจรครญรฎรฑรฒรจ, รฒรจรฏร รฑรฎรฑรญรป, รชรฅรครฐร , รฏรจรตรฒรป รจรซรจ รฅรซรจ. - TEXT.English: Pumice, called pumicite in its powdered or dust form, is a volcanic rock that consists of highly vesicular rough textured volcanic glass, which may or may not contain crystals. It is typically light colored. Scoria is another vesicular volcanic rock that differs from pumice in having larger vesicles and thicker vesicle walls and being dark colored and denser.
TEXT.French: La pierre ponce, appelรฉe pumicite sous sa forme en poudre ou en poussiรจre, est une roche volcanique qui consiste en un verre volcanique trรจs vรฉsiculaire ร texture rugueuse, qui peut ou non contenir des cristaux. Elle est gรฉnรฉralement de couleur claire. La scorie est une autre roche volcanique vรฉsiculaire qui diffรจre de la pierre ponce en ayant des vรฉsicules plus grandes et des parois de vรฉsicules plus รฉpaisses, et en รฉtant de couleur foncรฉe et plus dense.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Bimsstein[COLOR_REVERT] oder kurz Bims ist ein stark porรถses vulkanisches Gestein, das entsteht, wenn zรคhflรผssige Lava bei Eruptionen durch Gase aufgeschรคumt wird. Seine Farbe kann je nach Lavaart und Einschlรผssen stark variieren. Es gibt nahezu schwarzen und weiรen Bims, aber auch sรคmtliche Zwischentรถne. Bimsstein geht nicht unter, sondern schwimmt auf Wasser, was an seinen vielen Poren und der daraus resultierenden sehr geringen Dichte liegt.[NEWLINE]Bims wurde bereits seit Vorzeiten in Kreta als Baumaterial, Opfergabe, Reinigungs- und Heilmittel und fรผr diverse andere Zwecke eingesetzt. Das Gestein wird dort hรคufig รผber die Strรถmung von den vulkanischen Nachbarinseln angespรผlt. In der Antike nutzte man den Bimsstein bereits zur Kรถrperpflege, zum Schรคrfen von Schreibfedern und zum Glรคtten von Pergamentleder. Auch heute wird das Material noch vielseitig eingesetzt, z.B. fรผr die Herstellung von Leichtbetonsteinen und Dรคmmstoffen und zum Filtern von Wasser. Bimsstein ist fรผr den Stone-washed-Effekt von Jeans verantwortlich und stellt einen Grundstoff fรผr viele gรคrtnerische Substrate dar.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pumeks[COLOR_REVERT] (z ³ac. pumex), dawniej pemza - to wulkaniczna ska³a magmowa zbudowana z porowatego (ponad 50% porów) szkliwa wulkanicznego powsta³ego z silnie gazuj¹cej, pienistej lawy. Ma szklist¹, drobnoziarnist¹ strukturê. Powstaje g³ównie z kwaœnych odmian lawy (riolitowej).
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฌรงร , รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฌร รฟ รฏรฎรฐรจรฑรฒรปรฌ รฏรณรฌรจรถรจรฒรฎรฌ, รฝรฒรฎ รขรณรซรชร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชร รฟ รฏรฎรฐรฎรคร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐร รฟ รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรจรฒ รจรง รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรขรฅรงรจรชรณรซรฟรฐรญรฎรฃรฎ รฃรฐรณรกรฎรฃรฎ รฒรฅรชรฑรฒรณรฐรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรฎรฃรฎ รขรณรซรชร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฑรฒรฅรชรซร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รฑรฎรครฅรฐรฆร รฒรผ รจรซรจ รญรฅ รฑรฎรครฅรฐรฆร รฒรผ รชรฐรจรฑรฒร รซรซรป. รรญรจ, รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ, รฑรขรฅรฒรซรฎรฃรฎ รถรขรฅรฒร . รรซร รช - รฝรฒรฎ รครฐรณรฃร รฟ รขรฅรงรจรชรณรซรฟรฐรญร รขรณรซรชร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชร รฟ รฏรฎรฐรฎรคร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐร รฟ รฎรฒรซรจรทร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รฎรฒ รฏรฅรฌรงรป รญร รซรจรทรจรฅรฌ รชรฐรณรฏรญรปรต รฏรณรงรปรฐรผรชรฎรข รจ รขรฅรงรจรชรณรซ รข รฒรฎรซรนรฅ รฑรฒรฅรญ, รฒรฅรฌรญรปรฌ รถรขรฅรฒรฎรฌ รจ รกรฎรซรผรธรฅรฉ รฏรซรฎรฒรญรฎรฑรฒรผรพ.

TEXT.English: A pumpkin is a gourd-like squash of the genus Cucurbita and the family Cucurbitaceae (which also includes gourds). It commonly refers to cultivars of any one of the species Cucurbita pepo, Cucurbita mixta, Cucurbita maxima, and Cucurbita moschata, and is native to North America. They typically have a thick, orange or yellow shell, creased from the stem to the bottom, containing the seeds and pulp.
TEXT.French: Une citrouille est une courge de type calebasse du genre Cucurbita et de la famille des Cucurbitaceae (qui comprend รฉgalement les gourdes). Elle fait gรฉnรฉralement rรฉfรฉrence aux cultivars de l'une des espรจces Cucurbita pepo, Cucurbita mixta, Cucurbita maxima et Cucurbita moschata, et est originaire d'Amรฉrique du Nord. Elles ont gรฉnรฉralement une coque รฉpaisse, orange ou jaune, plissรฉe de la tige au fond, contenant les graines et la pulpe.
TEXT.German: Die [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kรผrbisse[COLOR_REVERT] (Cucurbita) sind die namensgebende Pflanzengattung aus der Familie der Kรผrbisgewรคchse (Cucurbitaceae). Es handelt sich um krautige Pflanzen mit meist sehr groรen, auffรคlligen Frรผchten (Panzerbeeren), die in Form und Farbe sehr stark variieren.[NEWLINE]Die Frรผchte der Wildform verfรผgen รผber eine sehr harte, holzige Schale und sind voll von Bitterstoffen, sodass ursprรผnglich nur die nahrhaften Samen verwendet wurden. Erst durch Auslese im Rahmen der Domestizierung wurde die Nutzung als Gemรผse mรถglich. Anhand von Funden gilt heute als sicher, dass Kรผrbisse bereits zwischen 8000 und 10000 v.Chr. domestiziert wurden. Der Gartenkรผrbis hat seinen Ursprung im sรผdlichen Nordamerika, wรคhrend der Riesenkรผrbis aus Sรผd- und der Moschuskรผrbis aus Mittelamerika stammt.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Dynia[COLOR_REVERT] to podobna do tykwy dynia z rodzaju Cucurbita i rodziny Cucurbitaceae (która obejmuje równie¿ tykwy). Powszechnie odnosi siê do dowolenj odmiany gatunku Cucurbita pepo, Cucurbita mixta, Cucurbita maxima i Cucurbita moschata. Pochodzi z Ameryki Pó³nocnej. Dynie zwykle maj¹ grub¹, pomarañczow¹ lub ¿ó³t¹ skorupê, pomarszczon¹ od ³odygi do do³u, zawieraj¹c¹ nasiona i mi¹¿sz.

TEXT.English: A punched card or punch card is a piece of stiff paper that can be used to contain digital data represented by the presence or absence of holes in predefined positions. Digital data can be used for data processing applications or, in earlier examples, used to directly control automated machinery. Punched cards were widely used through much of the 20th century in the data processing industry, where specialized and increasingly complex unit record machines, organized into semiautomatic data processing systems, used punched cards for data input, output, and storage. Many early digital computers used punched cards, often prepared using keypunch machines, as the primary medium for input of both computer programs and data. While punched cards are now obsolete as a storage medium, as of 2012, some voting machines still use punched cards to record votes.

TEXT.English: The mineral pyrite, or iron pyrite, also known as fool's gold, is an iron sulfide with the formula FeS2. This mineral's metallic luster and pale brass-yellow hue give it a superficial resemblance to gold, hence the well-known nickname of fool's gold. The color has also led to the nicknames brass, brazzle, and Brazil, primarily used to refer to pyrite found in coal.
TEXT.French: Le minรฉral pyrite, ou pyrite de fer, รฉgalement connu sous le nom d'or des fous, est un sulfure de fer de formule FeS2. L'รฉclat mรฉtallique et la teinte jaune laiton pรขle de ce minรฉral lui donnent une ressemblance superficielle avec l'or, d'oรน le surnom bien connu d'or des fous. La couleur a รฉgalement conduit aux surnoms laiton, brazzle et Brรฉsil, principalement utilisรฉs pour dรฉsigner la pyrite trouvรฉe dans le charbon.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Piryt[COLOR_REVERT] - minera³ ¿elaza z gromady siarczków, nadsiarczek ¿elaza(II), FeS2. Nazwa pochodzi od gr. pyr = ogieñ oraz pyrites = iskrz¹cy, gdy¿ minera³ ten iskrzy siê pod wp³ywem uderzeñ krzesiwa (krzemienia, twardego metalu).

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]qilin[COLOR_REVERT] is a mythical chimerical creature known in Chinese and other East Asian cultures, said to appear with the imminent arrival or passing of a sage or illustrious ruler. Qilin generally have Chinese dragon-like features, most notably their heads, eyes with thick eyelashes, manes that always flow upward and beards. The body is fully or partially scaled and often shaped like an ox, deer, or horse. They are always shown with cloven hooves. Qilin are thought to be a symbol of luck, good omens, protection, prosperity, success, and longevity by the Chinese; they are also a symbol of fertility, and often depicted in decorations as bringing a baby to a family.
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]qilin[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una creatura mitica chimerica, nota nelle culture cinese e dell'Asia orientale, che si dice appaia all'arrivo o al passaggio imminente di un sovrano saggio o illustre. Generalmente, i lineamenti di un qilin sono simili a quelli di un dragone cinese, in particolare la testa, gli occhi con ciglia folte, le criniere che scorrono sempre verso l'alto e la barba. Il corpo รจ completamente o parzialmente coperto da scaglie, spesso a forma di bue, cervo o cavallo, e hanno sempre gli zoccoli. Si pensa che un qilin sia un simbolo di fortuna, buoni auspici, protezione, prosperitร , successo e longevitร da parte dei cinesi; sono anche un simbolo di fertilitร e spesso raffigurati nelle decorazioni mentre portano un bimbo a una famiglia.

TEXT.English: A quantonium crystal is a powerful radioactive energy crystal that can fuel spaceships. It also has the significant contraindication of turning humans into giants.
TEXT.French: Un cristal de quantonium est un puissant cristal d'รฉnergie radioactive qui peut alimenter des vaisseaux spatiaux. Il prรฉsente รฉgalement la contre-indication significative de transformer les humains en gรฉants.
TEXT.Italian: Un cristallo di quantonio รจ un potente cristallo di energia radioattiva che puรฒ alimentare astronavi. Ha anche la significativa controindicazione di trasformare gli umani in giganti.

TEXT.English: Quantum dots are tiny semiconductor particles, a few nanometers in size, having optical and electronic properties that differ from larger particles due to quantum mechanics. Quantum dots are sometimes referred to as artificial atoms, emphasizing their singularity, having bound, discrete electronic states, like naturally occurring atoms or molecules. Potential applications of quantum dots include single-electron transistors, solar cells, LEDs, lasers, single-photon sources, second-harmonic generation, quantum computing, and medical imaging.
TEXT.Italian: I punti quantici sono minuscole particelle semiconduttrici, di dimensioni di alcuni nanometri, con proprietร ottiche ed elettroniche che differiscono dalle particelle piรน grandi a causa della meccanica quantistica. I punti quantici sono talvolta chiamati "atomi artificiali", per enfatizzare la loro singolaritร , avendo stati elettronici legati e discreti, come atomi o molecole presenti in natura. Le potenziali applicazioni dei punti quantici includono transistor a singolo elettrone, celle solari, LED, laser, sorgenti a singolo fotone, generazione di seconda armonica, calcolo quantico e imaging medico.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Quetzalcoatl[COLOR_REVERT] is a deity in Aztec culture and literature, whose name comes from the Nahuatl language and means "Precious serpent" or "Quetzal-feathered Serpent". According to Ixtlilxรณchitl, a descendant of Aztec royalty and historian of the Nahua people, the name in its literal sense means "serpent of precious feathers", but in the allegorical sense means "wisest of men". Quetzalcoatl was related to gods of the wind, of the planet Venus, of the dawn, of merchants and of arts, crafts and knowledge. He was also the patron god of the Aztec priesthood, of learning and knowledge, and wears around his neck the "wind breastplate" ehฤcacลzcatl, "the spirally voluted wind jewel" made of a conch shell (possibly symbolizing patterns witnessed in hurricanes, dust devils, seashells, and whirlpools).
TEXT.Italian: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Quetzalcoatl[COLOR_REVERT] รจ una divinitร della cultura e della letteratura azteca, il cui nome deriva dalla lingua nahuatl e significa "Serpente prezioso" o "Serpente dalle piume di Quetzal". Secondo Ixtlilxรณchitl, discendente della famiglia reale azteca e storico del popolo Nahua, il nome nel suo senso letterale significa "serpente di piume preziose", ma in senso allegorico significa "il piรน saggio degli uomini". Quetzalcoatl viene accostato agli dei del vento, del pianeta Venere, dell'alba, dei mercanti e delle arti, dei mestieri e della conoscenza. Era anche il dio protettore del sacerdozio azteco, dell'apprendimento e della conoscenza, e indossa al collo la "corazza del vento" ehฤcacลzcatl, "il gioiello del vento a spirale" fatto di una conchiglia (e che forse simboleggia i motivi degli uragani, dei "diavoli della polvere", delle conchiglie e dei vortici).
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Quetzalcoatl[COLOR_REVERT], to bóstwo w kulturze i literaturze Azteków, którego imiê pochodzi z jêzyka nahuatl i oznacza "Drogocenny w¹¿" lub "W¹¿ opierzony Quetzalem". Wed³ug Ixtlilxóchitla, potomka azteckiej rodziny królewskiej i historyka ludu Nahua, imiê to w dos³ownym znaczeniu oznacza "w¹¿ o cennych piórach", ale w sensie alegorycznym oznacza "najm¹drzejszy z ludzi". Quetzalcoatl by³ spokrewniony z bogami wiatru, planety Wenus, œwitu, kupców oraz sztuki, rzemios³a i wiedzy. By³ tak¿e bogiem patronem azteckiego kap³añstwa, nauki i wiedzy, i nosi³ na szyi "napierœnik wiatru" ehฤcacลzcatl, "spiralnie skrêcony klejnot wiatru" wykonany z muszli (prawdopodobnie symbolizuj¹cy wzory widziane w huraganach, diab³y py³owe, muszle i wiry).

TEXT.English: A rabbit is a small mammal, found in several parts of the world. There are currently 305 known domestic breeds; the non-domesticated rabbits, known as hares, are generally larger and have more elongated ears and larger and longer hind legs. Domestication started during the Middle Ages, when rabbits were kept as livestock for their meat and fur, generating an increasingly wider selection. Many of these breeds were, starting with 19th century, also kept as pets. Rabbits are also often used as a symbol of fertility or rebirth in religion, and of vulnerability, sympathy and innocence in folklore and modern children's stories.
TEXT.German: Bei [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kaninchen[COLOR_REVERT] handelt es sich um spezielle, meist kleinere Vertreter der Familie der Hasen, die sich durch kรผrzere Ohren und weniger starke Hinterlรคufe auszeichnen. Sie wurden schon von den frรผhen Menschen wegen ihres schmackhaften Fleischs und ihres Fells gejagt. Seit antiker Zeit ist eine Zucht von Kaninchen zur Mast belegt, heutzutage werden Rassekaninchen aber auch zu Schauzwecken gezรผchtet.
TEXT.Italian: Un coniglio รจ un piccolo mammifero, presenti in diverse parti del mondo. Attualmente ci sono 305 razze domestiche conosciute; le specie non addomesticate, note come lepri, sono generalmente piรน grandi e hanno orecchie piรน allungate e zampe posteriori piรน grandi e piรน lunghe. L'addomesticamento รจ iniziato durante il Medioevo, quando i conigli venivano allevati per la loro carne e la loro pelliccia, generando una selezione sempre piรน ampia. Molte di queste razze, a partire dal XIX secolo, iniziarono a essere allevate anche come animali domestici. I conigli sono spesso usati anche come simbolo di fertilitร o rinascita nella religione e di vulnerabilitร , simpatia e innocenza nel folklore e nelle moderne storie per bambini.

TEXT.Russian: รร รครจรฎรฏรฐรจรฅรฌรญรจรชรจ

TEXT.English: A radio receiver, also known as radio, is an electronic device that receives radio waves and converts the information carried by them to a usable form. It is used with an antenna, that intercepts radio electromagnetic waves and converts them in such a way to allow the receiver to extract the desired information. The receiver uses electronic filters to separate the desired radio frequency signal from all the other signals picked up by the antenna, an electronic amplifier to increase the power of the signal for further processing, and finally recovers the desired information through demodulation. The most familiar type of radio receiver for most people is a broadcast radio receiver, which reproduces sound transmitted by radio broadcasting stations; however radio receivers are very widely used in televisions, cell phones, wireless modems and other components of communications, remote control, and wireless networking systems.
TEXT.Italian: Un ricevitore radio, noto anche come radio, รจ un dispositivo elettronico che riceve le onde radio e converte le informazioni trasportate da loro in una forma utilizzabile. Viene utilizzato con un'antenna, che intercetta le onde radio elettromagnetiche e le converte in modo che il ricevitore possa estrarre le informazioni desiderate. Il ricevitore utilizza filtri elettronici per separare il segnale di radiofrequenza desiderato da tutti gli altri segnali raccolti dall'antenna, un amplificatore elettronico per aumentare la potenza del segnale per un'ulteriore elaborazione e infine recuperare le informazioni desiderate attraverso la demodulazione. Il tipo piรน familiare di ricevitore radio per la maggior parte delle persone รจ un ricevitore radio che riproduce il suono trasmesso dalle stazioni radio; tuttavia i radioricevitori sono ampiamente utilizzati in televisori, telefoni cellulari, modem wireless e altri componenti di sistemi di comunicazione, controllo remoto e rete wireless.
TEXT.Russian: ร รฐร รครจรฎรฑรขรฟรงรจ, รฐร รครจรฎรฏรฐรจรฅรฌรญรจรช - รฝรฒรฎ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรฎรฅ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รฏรฐรจรญรจรฌร รฅรฒ รฐร รครจรฎรขรฎรซรญรป รจ รฏรฐรฅรฎรกรฐร รงรณรฅรฒ รจรญรดรฎรฐรฌร รถรจรพ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรณรพ รฎรญรจ รญรฅรฑรณรฒ รข รฑรซรปรธรจรฌรฎรฌ รครจร รฏรฎรงรฎรญรฅ. รรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑ ร รญรฒรฅรญรญรฎรฉ. รรญรฒรฅรญรญร รฏรฅรฐรฅรตรขร รฒรปรขร รฅรฒ รฐร รครจรฎรขรฎรซรญรป (รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรฌร รฃรญรจรฒรญรปรฅ รขรฎรซรญรป) รจ รฏรฐรฅรฎรกรฐร รงรณรฅรฒ รจรต รข รฑรซร รกรปรฉ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฒรฎรช, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรญรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รช รฏรฐรจรฅรฌรญรจรชรณ, ร รฎรญ รจรงรขรซรฅรชร รฅรฒ รญรณรฆรญรณรพ รจรญรดรฎรฐรฌร รถรจรพ. รรฐรจรฅรฌรญรจรช รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรปรฅ รดรจรซรผรฒรฐรป, รทรฒรฎรกรป รฎรฒรครฅรซรจรฒรผ รญรณรฆรญรปรฅ รฐร รครจรฎรทร รฑรฒรฎรฒรป รจรง รขรฑรฅรต รครฐรณรฃรจรต รฑรจรฃรญร รซรฎรข รฏรฎรฉรฌร รญรญรปรต ร รญรฒรฅรญรญรฎรฉ, รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรปรฉ รณรฑรจรซรจรฒรฅรซรผ รครซรฟ รณรขรฅรซรจรทรฅรญรจรฟ รฌรฎรนรญรฎรฑรฒรจ รฑรจรฃรญร รซร รครซรฟ รคร รซรผรญรฅรฉรธรฅรฉ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒรชรจ, รจ, รญร รชรฎรญรฅรถ, รขรฎรฑรฑรฒร รญร รขรซรจรขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รญรณรฆรญรฎรฉ รจรญรดรฎรฐรฌร รถรจรจ รฏรฎรฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฎรฌ รครฅรฌรฎรครณรซรฟรถรจรจ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Odbiornik radiowy[COLOR_REVERT], zwany tak¿e radiem, to urz¹dzenie elektroniczne odbieraj¹ce fale radiowe i przetwarzaj¹ce przenoszone przez nie informacje na u¿yteczn¹ formê. Stosowany jest wraz z anten¹, która przechwytuje fale radiowe elektromagnetyczne i przetwarza je w taki sposób, aby umo¿liwiæ odbiornikowi wydobycie ¿¹danej informacji. Odbiornik wykorzystuje filtry elektroniczne do oddzielenia ¿¹danego sygna³u o czêstotliwoœci radiowej od wszystkich innych sygna³ów odbieranych przez antenê, wzmacniacz elektroniczny w celu zwiêkszenia mocy sygna³u do dalszego przetwarzania, a na koniec odzyskuje ¿¹dan¹ informacjê poprzez demodulacjê. Najbardziej znanym typem odbiornika radiowego dla wiêkszoœci ludzi jest odbiornik radiowy, który odtwarza dŸwiêk transmitowany przez stacje radiowe; jednak¿e odbiorniki radiowe s¹ bardzo szeroko stosowane w telewizorach, telefonach komórkowych, modemach bezprzewodowych i innych elementach komunikacji, zdalnym sterowaniu i systemach sieci bezprzewodowych.

TEXT.Russian: รร รครณรฆรญรปรฉ รงรฌรฅรฉ

TEXT.English: The Rainbow Serpent (or Rainbow Snake) is a common deity, often a creator god, in the mythology and a common motif in the art of Aboriginal Australia. It is named for the obvious identification between the shape of a rainbow and that of a snake. Some scholars have suggested that the link between snake and rainbow suggests the cycle of the seasons and the importance of water in human life. When the rainbow is seen in the sky, it is said to be the Rainbow Serpent moving from one waterhole to another, and the divine concept explained why some waterholes never dried up when drought struck. There are innumerable names and stories associated with the serpent, all of which communicate the significance and power of this being within Aboriginal traditions. It is viewed as a giver of life, through its association with water, but can be a destructive force if angry. The Rainbow Serpent is one of the oldest continuing religious beliefs in the world and continues to be a cultural influence today.
TEXT.Italian: Il serpente arcobaleno รจ una divinitร comune, spesso un dio creatore, nella mitologia e un motivo comune nell'arte degli aborigeni australiani. Trae il nome dalla somiglianza tra la forma di un arcobaleno e quella di un serpente. Alcuni studiosi hanno ipotizzato che il legame tra serpente e arcobaleno rappresenti il ciclo delle stagioni e l'importanza dell'acqua nella vita umana. Quando un arcobaleno appare in cielo, si dice che sia il serpente arcobaleno che si muove da una pozza d'acqua all'altra - questo concetto contribuirebbe a spiegare anche perchรฉ alcune pozze d'acqua non si fossero seccate durante i periodi di siccitร . Ci sono innumerevoli nomi e storie associati al serpente, che comunicano il significato e il potere attribuito a questo essere all'interno delle tradizioni aborigene. ร visto come un donatore di vita, attraverso la sua associazione con l'acqua, ma puรฒ essere una forza distruttiva se irato. Il serpente arcobaleno รจ una delle piรน antiche credenze religiose ancora esistenti al mondo e continua a esercitare la sua influenza culturale ancora oggi.

TEXT.English: A rare-earth element (REE) or rare-earth metal (REM) is one of a set of seventeen chemical elements in the periodic table, i.e. cerium, dysprosium, erbium, europium, gadolinium, holmium, lanthanum, lutetium, neodymium, praseodymium, promethium, samarium, scandium, terbium, thulium, ytterbium, and yttrium. Despite their name, rare-earth elements are โ with the exception of the radioactive promethium โ relatively plentiful in Earth's crust. However, because of their geochemical properties, rare-earth elements are typically dispersed, therefore economically exploitable ore deposits are less common. Most of them are used for catalysts, high-performance magnets, alloys, glasses, and electronics. Some are also used to increase plant growth, productivity, and stress resistance seemingly without negative effects for human and animal consumption.
TEXT.Italian: Una terra rara o un metallo raro รจ uno di diciassette elementi chimici nella tavola periodica, ovvero cerio, disprosio, erbio, europeo, gadolinio, itterbio, ittrio, lantanio, lutezio, neodimio, olmio, praseodimio, promezio, samario, scandio, terbio e tulio. Nonostante il loro nome, le terre rare sono - con l'eccezione del prometio, che รจ radioattivo - relativamente abbondanti nella crosta terrestre. Tuttavia, a causa delle loro proprietร geochimiche, gli elementi delle terre rare sono in genere dispersi, quindi i depositi economicamente sfruttabili sono meno comuni. La maggior parte di essi viene utilizzata per catalizzatori, magneti ad alte prestazioni, leghe, vetri ed elettronica. Alcuni sono anche usati per aumentare la crescita, la produttivitร e la resistenza allo stress delle piante apparentemente senza effetti negativi per il consumo umano e animale.

Pomimo swojej nazwy pierwiastki ziem rzadkich, z wyj¹tkiem radioaktywnego prometu wystêpuj¹ stosunkowo powszechnie w skorupie ziemskiej. Wiêkszoœæ z nich jest stosowana w katalizatorach, magnesach o wysokiej wydajnoœci, stopach, szk³ach i elektronice. Niektóre z nich s¹ równie¿ stosowane w celu zwiêkszenia wzrostu roœlin, produktywnoœci i odpornoœci na stres, pozornie bez negatywnych skutków dla spo¿ycia przez ludzi i zwierzêta.

TEXT.English: Raw fish generally refers to any type of uncooked muscle tissue of aquatic creatures used for food. Raw fish present a higher risk of disease from ingesting pathogens than cooked fish.
TEXT.French: Le poisson cru dรฉsigne gรฉnรฉralement tout type de tissu musculaire non cuit de crรฉatures aquatiques utilisรฉ comme nourriture. Le poisson cru prรฉsente un risque plus รฉlevรฉ de maladie due ร l'ingestion de pathogรจnes que le poisson cuit.

TEXT.English: Raw meat generally refers to any type of uncooked muscle tissue of an animal used for food. This term refers specifically to mammalian flesh, or to any animal which is not a bird (in this case, the preferred term is "poultry") or an aquatic creature (in this case, the preferred term is "seafood"). Raw meat present a higher risk of disease from ingesting pathogens than cooked meat.
TEXT.French: La viande crue dรฉsigne gรฉnรฉralement tout type de tissu musculaire non cuit d'un animal utilisรฉ comme nourriture. Ce terme fait spรฉcifiquement rรฉfรฉrence ร la chair de mammifรจre, ou ร tout animal qui n'est pas un oiseau (dans ce cas, le terme prรฉfรฉrรฉ est "volaille") ou une crรฉature aquatique (dans ce cas, le terme prรฉfรฉrรฉ est "fruits de mer"). La viande crue prรฉsente un risque plus รฉlevรฉ de maladie due ร l'ingestion de pathogรจnes que la viande cuite.
TEXT.Italian: La carne cruda รจ qualsiasi tipo di tessuto muscolare crudo di un animale usato per il cibo. Questo termine si riferisce specificamente alla carne di mammiferi o di qualsiasi animale che non sia un uccello (in questo caso, il termine preferito รจ "pollame") o una creatura acquatica (in questo caso, il termine preferito รจ "pescato"). La carne cruda presenta un rischio piรน elevato di malattie dovute ad agenti patogeni rispetto alla carne cotta.

Surowe miêso wi¹¿e siê z wiêkszym ryzykiem chorób w wyniku spo¿ycia patogenów ni¿ miêso gotowane.

TEXT.English: Red and black ceramics were one of the most important styles of figural Greek vase painting. This process was developed in Athens around 520 BC, remained in use until the late 3rd century BC, and replaced the previously dominant style of black-figure vase painting within a few decades. For a long time, they dominated the market for fine ceramics in much of the Mediterranean basin, with Etruria being the only non-Greek area to develop an autonomous "school". Their images provide evidence for the exploration of Greek and Etruscan cultural history, everyday life, iconography, and mythology.
TEXT.Italian: Le ceramiche rosse e nere hanno rappresentato uno degli stili piรน importanti della pittura figurale greca su vaso. Questo processo si sviluppรฒ ad Atene intorno al 520 a.C., rimase in uso fino alla fine del III secolo a.C. e rimpiazzรฒ lo stile precedentemente dominante della pittura a figure nere nel giro di pochi decenni. Per molto tempo, hanno dominato il mercato delle ceramiche di pregio in gran parte del bacino del Mediterraneo; l'Etruria fu l'unica area non greca a sviluppare una "scuola" autonoma. Le loro immagini forniscono prove dell'esplorazione della storia culturale, della vita quotidiana, dell'iconografia e della mitologia greca ed etrusca.
TEXT.Russian: ะัะฐัะฝะพัะธะณััะฝะฐั ะบะตัะฐะผะธะบะฐ ะฑัะปะฐ ะพะดะฝะธะผ ะธะท ะฒะฐะถะฝะตะนัะธั ััะธะปะตะน ัะธะณััะฝะพะน ัะพัะฟะธัะธ ะณัะตัะตัะบะธั ะฒะฐะท. ะญัะพั ะฟัะพัะตัั ะฑัะป ัะฐะทัะฐะฑะพัะฐะฝ ะฒ ะัะธะฝะฐั ะพะบะพะปะพ 520 ะณ. ะดะพ ะฝ.ั., ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปัั ะดะพ ะบะพะฝัะฐ III-ะณะพ ะฒะตะบะฐ ะดะพ ะฝ.ั. ะธ ะฒ ัะตัะตะฝะธะต ะฝะตัะบะพะปัะบะธั ะดะตัััะธะปะตัะธะน ะทะฐะผะตะฝะธะป ะดะพะผะธะฝะธััััะธะน ััะธะปั ัะตัะฝะพัะธะณััะฝะพะน ัะพัะฟะธัะธ ะฒะฐะท. ะ ัะตัะตะฝะธะต ะดะพะปะณะพะณะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะธ ะพะฝะธ ะดะพะผะธะฝะธัะพะฒะฐะปะธ ะฝะฐ ััะฝะบะต ะฟัะตะบัะฐัะฝะพะน ะบะตัะฐะผะธะบะธ ะฒ ะฑะพะปััะตะน ัะฐััะธ ะกัะตะดะธะทะตะผะฝะพะผะพััะบะพะณะพ ะฑะฐััะตะนะฝะฐ, ะฟัะธ ััะพะผ ะญััััะธั ะฑัะปะฐ ะตะดะธะฝััะฒะตะฝะฝะพะน ะฝะตะณัะตัะตัะบะพะน ัะตััะธัะพัะธะตะน, ะณะดะต ะฒะพะทะฝะธะบะปะฐ ะฐะฒัะพะฝะพะผะฝะฐั ยซัะบะพะปะฐยป. ะั ะธะทะพะฑัะฐะถะตะฝะธั ัะปัะถะฐั ัะฒะธะดะตัะตะปัััะฒะพะผ ะธะทััะตะฝะธั ะณัะตัะตัะบะพะน ะธ ััััััะบะพะน ะบัะปััััะฝะพะน ะธััะพัะธะธ, ะฟะพะฒัะตะดะฝะตะฒะฝะพะน ะถะธะทะฝะธ, ะธะบะพะฝะพะณัะฐัะธะธ ะธ ะผะธัะพะปะพะณะธะธ.

TEXT.English: In polyphyletic botany, a reed is a type of tall, grass-like plant of wet places, it is also very flammable; the namesake vegetation of reed beds.
TEXT.French: En botanique polyphylรฉtique, un roseau est un type de plante haute, semblable ร l'herbe, des lieux humides, il est รฉgalement trรจs inflammable ; la vรฉgรฉtation รฉponyme des roseliรจres.
TEXT.German: Schilfrohr (Phragmites australis oder Phragmites communis) oder kurz [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Schilf[COLOR_REVERT] ist eine weltweit verbreitete Sumpfpflanze, die zu den Sรผรgrรคsern gehรถrt. Schilfhalme kรถnnen bis zu 4 m hoch werden. Sie bilden rispenfรถrmige Blรผtenstรคnde aus, die bis zu 50 cm groร sein kรถnnen. Schilf verdrรคngt bei gรผnstigen Nรคhrstoffangebot andere Wildgrรคser und -krรคuter, weshalb an stehenden und langsam flieรenden Gewรคssern in der Regel natรผrliche Monokulturen entstehen. Schilf kann vielseitig verwendet werden. Als Naturbaustoff eignet es sich besonders in Form von Reet als Dachabdeckung, es kam aber auch beim Bau frรผher Schiffe zum Einsatz. Von Vorteil war hierbei der hohe Gehalt an Kieselsรคure, durch den es eine brandhemmende Wirkung hatte. Schilf kann als Gemรผse verzehrt und aus seinen getrockneten Wurzeln kann ein Mehl zum Brotbacken gewonnen werden. In der Antike war es als Schreibrohr jahrhundertelang das wichtigste Schreibgerรคt. Heute wird Schilf als Dรคmmstoff, in Pflanzenklรคranlagen und als natรผrlicher Bodenfilter verwendet.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Trzcina[COLOR_REVERT] (Phragmites L.) - rodzaj roœlin nale¿¹cy do rodziny wiechlinowatych. Nale¿y do niego 4-5 gatunków. W Polsce w œrodowisku naturalnym roœnie jeden tylko gatunek - trzcina pospolita (Phragmites australis), jest to zarazem gatunek typowy rodzaju.
TEXT.Russian: ะ ะฟะพะปะธัะธะปะตัะธัะตัะบะพะน ะฑะพัะฐะฝะธะบะต ััะพััะฝะธะบ - ััะพ ัะธะฟ ะฒััะพะบะพะณะพ ััะฐะฒะพะฟะพะดะพะฑะฝะพะณะพ ัะฐััะตะฝะธั ะฒะปะฐะถะฝัั ะผะตัั, ะพะฝ ัะฐะบะถะต ะพัะตะฝั ะปะตะณะบะพ ะฒะพัะฟะปะฐะผะตะฝัะตััั; ะพะดะฝะพะธะผะตะฝะฝะฐั ัะฐััะธัะตะปัะฝะพััั ััะพััะฝะธะบะพะฒัั ะทะฐัะพัะปะตะน.

TEXT.German: Kรผhlschrรคnke
TEXT.Russian: รรฎรซรฎรครจรซรผรญรจรชรจ

TEXT.English: A refrigerator (colloquially fridge) is a common household appliance that consists of a thermally insulated compartment and a heat pump (mechanical, electronic, or chemical) that transfers heat from the inside of the fridge to its external environment so that the inside of the fridge is cooled to a temperature below the ambient temperature of the room. Refrigeration is an essential food storage technique in developed countries. Lower temperatures in a confined volume lowers the reproduction rate of bacteria, so the refrigerator reduces the rate of spoilage.
TEXT.German: Ein Kรผhlschrank ist ein elektrisches oder gasbetriebenes schrankfรถrmiges Gerรคt, das die Temperatur in seinem Inneren selbststรคndig niedrig hรคlt. Mittels eines รผblicherweise an der Rรผckseite befindlichen und per Temperaturregler geregelten Kรผhlaggregates wird das Kรผhlschrankinnere gekรผhlt. Kรผhlschrรคnke finden Verwendung fรผr die Lagerung von Nahrungsmitteln, Medikamenten, Chemikalien usw. Durch die niedrigere Temperatur laufen chemische Reaktionen und biologische Prozesse, die beispielsweise Lebensmittel ungenieรbar und Medikamente unbrauchbar werden lassen, langsamer ab.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Ch³odziarka[COLOR_REVERT] (zwycz. lodówka) - urz¹dzenie (maszyna cieplna), którego zadaniem jest obni¿enie temperatury œrodowiska ch³odzonego, kosztem doprowadzonej energii. W ch³odziarkach absorpcyjnych energia dostarczana jest w postaci ciep³a, natomiast w pozosta³ych (sprê¿arkowych, strumienicowych, termoelektrycznych) w postaci pracy. Ch³odzonym czynnikiem mo¿e byæ powietrze, woda lub inny czynnik poœrednicz¹cy (ch³odziwo).
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรดรฐรจรฆรฅรฐร รฒรฎรฐ (รข รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฎรฐรฅรทรจรจ รตรฎรซรฎรครจรซรผรญรจรช) - รฝรฒรฎ รฐร รฑรฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฐร รญรฅรญรญรปรฉ รกรปรฒรฎรขรฎรฉ รฏรฐรจรกรฎรฐ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรจรฒ รจรง รฒรฅรฏรซรฎรจรงรฎรซรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรฎรฃรฎ รฎรฒรฑรฅรชร รจ รฒรฅรฏรซรฎรขรฎรฃรฎ รญร รฑรฎรฑร (รฌรฅรตร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ, รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรฎรฃรฎ รจรซรจ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ), รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฏรฅรฐรฅรญรฎรฑรจรฒ รฒรฅรฏรซรฎ รจรง รขรญรณรฒรฐรฅรญรญรฅรฉ รทร รฑรฒรจ รตรฎรซรฎรครจรซรผรญรจรชร รขรฎ รขรญรฅรธรญรพรพ รฑรฐรฅรครณ รฒร รช, รทรฒรฎ รขรญรณรฒรฐรจ รตรฎรซรฎรครจรซรผรญรจรชร รฎรตรซร รฆรคร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รครฎ รฒรฅรฌรฏรฅรฐร รฒรณรฐรป รญรจรฆรฅ รฒรฅรฌรฏรฅรฐร รฒรณรฐรป รฎรชรฐรณรฆร รพรนรฅรฉ รฑรฐรฅรครป รฏรฎรฌรฅรนรฅรญรจรฟ. รรฎรซรฎรครจรซรผรญรฎรฅ รฎรกรฎรฐรณรครฎรขร รญรจรฅ รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รญรฅรฎรฒรบรฅรฌรซรฅรฌรฎรฉ รตรฐร รญรฅรญรจรฟ รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒรฎรข รฒรฅรตรญรจรชรจ รข รฐร รงรขรจรฒรปรต รฑรฒรฐร รญร รต. รรฎรซรฅรฅ รญรจรงรชรจรฅ รฒรฅรฌรฏรฅรฐร รฒรณรฐรป รข รงร รฌรชรญรณรฒรฎรฌ รฎรกรบรฅรฌรฅ รฏรฎรญรจรฆร รฅรฒ รฑรชรฎรฐรฎรฑรฒรผ รฐร รงรฌรญรฎรฆรฅรญรจรฟ รกร รชรฒรฅรฐรจรฉ, รฏรฎรฝรฒรฎรฌรณ รตรฎรซรฎรครจรซรผรญรจรช รงร รฌรฅรครซรฟรฅรฒ รฒรฅรฌรฏรป รฏรฎรฐรทรจ.

TEXT.German: Ersatzorgane
TEXT.Russian: รรฐรฃร รญรป

TEXT.English: An artificial organ is a man-made device that is implanted or integrated into a human to replace a natural organ, for the purpose of restoring a specific function or a group of related functions so the patient may return to as normal a life as possible. The replaced function doesn't necessarily have to be related to life support, but often is. Implied by this definition is the fact that the device must not be continuously tethered to a stationary power supply, or other stationary resources, such as filters or chemical processing units. (Periodic rapid recharging of batteries, refilling of chemicals, and/or cleaning/replacing of filters, would exclude a device from being called an artificial organ.) Thus a dialysis machine, while a very successful and critically important life support device that completely replaces the duties of a kidney, is not an artificial organ. At this time an efficient, self-contained artificial kidney has not become available.
TEXT.German: Ein kรผnstliches Organ ist ein von Menschen gemachtes Gerรคt das einem Menschen implantiert wird, um ein natรผrliches Organ zu ersetzen. Dies dient dem Zweck eine spezifische Funktion oder eine Gruppe verwandter Funktionen wiederherzustellen um dem Patient ein normales Leben zu ermรถglichen. Das ersetzte Organ muss dabei nicht zwangslรคufig eine lebenserhaltene Funktion erfรผllen, tut dies aber meistens. Diese Definition impliziert dass das Gerรคt nicht dauerhaft an eine stationรคre Energiequelle oder andere stationรคre Ressourcen wie Filter, angeschlossen sein muss. (Das schnelle periodische Aufladen von Batterien, nachfรผllen von Chemikalien und/oder sรคubern/ersetzen von Filtern wรผrde ein Gerรคt nicht als kรผnstliches Organ klassifizieren.) Somit ist eine Dialysemaschine, obwohl sie ein sehr erfolgreiches und notwendiges Gerรคt zur Lebenserhaltung ist, dass die Aufgaben der Niere komplett erfรผllt, kein kรผnstliches Organ sein. Zur Zeit sind effiziente, selbstversorgene Kunstnieren nicht verfรผgbar.
TEXT.Russian: รรฑรชรณรฑรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฎรฐรฃร รญ - รฐรณรชรฎรฒรขรฎรฐรญรฎรฅ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รจรฌรฏรซร รญรฒรจรฐรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รจรซรจ รจรญรฒรฅรฃรฐรจรฐรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รทรฅรซรฎรขรฅรชร รครซรฟ รงร รฌรฅรญรป รฅรฑรฒรฅรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรฎรฃรฎ รฎรฐรฃร รญร , รข รถรฅรซรฟรต รขรฎรฑรฑรฒร รญรฎรขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฎรฏรฐรฅรครฅรซรฅรญรญรฎรฉ รดรณรญรชรถรจรจ รจรซรจ รฃรฐรณรฏรฏรป รขรงร รจรฌรฎรฑรขรฟรงร รญรญรปรต รดรณรญรชรถรจรฉ, รฒร รช รทรฒรฎ รฏร รถรจรฅรญรฒ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รขรฅรฐรญรณรฒรผรฑรฟ รช รญรฎรฐรฌร รซรผรญรฎรฉ รฆรจรงรญรจ, รญร รฑรชรฎรซรผรชรฎ รฝรฒรฎ รขรฎรงรฌรฎรฆรญรฎ. รร รฌรฅรญรฅรญรญรปรฉ รดรณรญรชรถรจรฟ รญรฅ รฎรกรฟรงร รฒรฅรซรผรญรฎ รครฎรซรฆรญร รกรปรฒรผ รฑรขรฟรงร รญรป รฑ รฆรจรงรญรฅรฎรกรฅรฑรฏรฅรทรฅรญรจรฅรฌ, รตรฎรฒรฟ รฝรฒรฎ รกรปรขร รฅรฒ รทร รฑรฒรฎ.รรฒรฎ รฎรฏรฐรฅรครฅรซรฅรญรจรฅ รฏรฎรครฐร รงรณรฌรฅรขร รฅรฒ, รทรฒรฎ รฏรฐรจรกรฎรฐ รญรฅ รครฎรซรฆรฅรญ รกรปรฒรผ รฏรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรญรญรฎ รฏรฐรจรขรฟรงร รญรญรปรฌ รช รฑรฒร รถรจรฎรญร รฐรญรฎรฌรณ รจรฑรฒรฎรทรญรจรชรณ รฏรจรฒร รญรจรฟ รจรซรจ รครฐรณรฃรจรฌ รฑรฒร รถรจรฎรญร รฐรญรปรฌ รฐรฅรฑรณรฐรฑร รฌ, รฒร รชรจรต รชร รช รดรจรซรผรฒรฐรป รจรซรจ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รฎรกรฐร รกรฎรฒรชรจ รฅรครจรญรจรถ. (รรฅรฐรจรฎรครจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รกรปรฑรฒรฐรปรฅ รฏรฎรครงร รฐรฟรครชรจ รกร รฒร รฐรฅรฉ, รงร รฏรฐร รขรชรจ รตรจรฌรจรชร รฒรฎรข, รจ/รจรซรจ รฎรทรจรฑรฒรชรจ/รงร รฌรฅรญรป รดรจรซรผรฒรฐรฎรข, รจรฑรชรซรพรทร รฅรฒ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ รจรง รญร รงรปรขร รพรฒ รจรฑรชรณรฑรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฎรฐรฃร รญ.) รร รชรจรฌ รฎรกรฐร รงรฎรฌ, ร รฏรฏร รฐร รฒ รครซรฟ รครจร รซรจรงร , ร รฎรทรฅรญรผ รณรฑรฏรฅรธรญรฎรฉ รจ รชรฐรจรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ รขร รฆรญรปรต รฆรจรงรญรฅรฎรกรฅรฑรฏรฅรทรฅรญรจรฟ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รฏรฎรซรญรฎรฑรฒรผรพ รงร รฌรฅรญรฟรฅรฒ รดรณรญรชรถรจรจ รฏรฎรทรฅรช, ร รญรฅ รจรฑรชรณรฑรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรฎรฃรฎ รฎรฐรฃร รญร . ร รฝรฒรฎ รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รฝรดรดรฅรชรฒรจรขรญร รฟ, รฑร รฌรฎรครฎรฑรฒร รฒรฎรทรญร รฟ รจรฑรชรณรฑรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญร รฟ รฏรฎรทรชร รครฎ รฑรจรต รฏรฎรฐ รฎรฑรฒร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รญรฅรครฎรฑรฒรณรฏรญรฎรฉ.

TEXT.German: Replikatoren
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฏรซรจรชร รฒรฎรฐรป

TEXT.English: Replicators were advanced robots capable of reproducing themselves indefinitely, consuming all available resources in an area to create more of themselves. The Replicators were created at an unspecified time in the past on a Human world where an android called Reese was created. This machine had a childlike personality and was feared by the natives of the planet. In order to keep herself amused, she created the Replicators which were to be her toys. As the population of the world grew more fearful of Reese and the Replicators, she began to teach the machines to protect her and themselves. In order to accomplish this, they began the process of self replication. The Replicators grew too large in number, and Reese herself began to lose the ability to control them

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Replikatory[COLOR_REVERT], to zaawansowane roboty zdolne do reprodukcji w nieskoñczonoœæ, zu¿ywaj¹ce wszystkie dostêpne zasoby na danym obszarze, aby stworzyæ jak najwiêcej w³asnych kopii. TEXT.Russian: รรฌรฎรซร

TEXT.English: Resin in the most specific use of the term is a hydrocarbon secretion of many plants, particularly coniferous trees. Resins are valued for their chemical properties and associated uses, such as the production of varnishes, adhesives, and food glazing agents; as an important source of raw materials for organic synthesis; and as constituents of incense and perfume. Plant resins have a very long history that was documented in ancient Greece by Theophrastus, in ancient Rome by Pliny the Elder, and especially in the resins known as frankincense and myrrh, prized in ancient Egypt. These were highly prized substances, and required as incense in some religious rites. Amber is a hard fossilized resin from ancient trees.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Harz[COLOR_REVERT] werden verschiedene von Pflanzen oder Tieren abgesonderte zรคhe Flรผssigkeiten genannt, die nicht wasserlรถslich sind. Vor allem von Bรคumen lรคsst sich Harz leicht gewinnen, denn sie produzieren es z.B. nach Verletzungen, um die Wunde schnell zu verschlieรen. Als einziger tierischer Lieferant gilt die Lackschildlaus (Kerria laccifera) aus Sรผdostasien, die den Schellack liefert. Naturharz wurde frรผher vielseitig verwendet, unter anderem in der Kunst, der Medizin und im Schiffbau. Das bekannteste Produkt ist wohl das Kolophonium, ein Destillationsrรผckstand aus dem Harz von Kiefern und Fichten, der z.B. als Klebstoff fรผr Heftpflaster, in Kaugummi und zur Behandlung der Bogenhaare bei Streichinstrumenten verwendet wird. Heute werden statt Naturharzen weitestgehend Kunstharze verwendet.

TEXT.English: Rice is a dietary staple of more than half of the world's human population. Rice is often grown in paddies - shallow puddles carefully controlled to ensure appropriate water depth. It is believed that rice cultivation began simultaneously in many countries over 6500 years ago. Modern world production of rice has risen steadily from about 200 million tons in 1960 to 600 million tons in 2000.
TEXT.French: Le riz est l'รฉlรฉment de base de l'alimentation de plus de la moitiรฉ de la population mondiale. Il est souvent cultivรฉ dans des riziรจres, des รฉtendues d'eau peu profondes dont le niveau est soigneusement contrรดlรฉ. On estime que la culture du riz a dรฉbutรฉ simultanรฉment dans diffรฉrentes rรฉgions, il y a plus de 6 500 ans. La production mondiale de riz a augmentรฉ rรฉguliรจrement ces derniรจres annรฉes, passant de 200 millions de tonnes en 1960 ร 600 millions en 2000.
TEXT.German: Als [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Reis[COLOR_REVERT] werden die Getreidekรถrner der kultivierten Pflanzen der Gattung Oryza bezeichnet. Reis ist heute das Grundnahrungsmittel von mehr als 50% der Weltbevรถlkerung. Vermutlich wird Reis bereits seit etwa 6500 Jahren angebaut. Er wรคchst auf Reisfeldern, deren geringe Wassertiefe permanent รผberwacht werden muss. Interessant dabei ist, dass Reis ursprรผnglich keine Wasserpflanze war, sondern sich seit Jahrtausenden durch Zucht und natรผrliche Selektion an die รberflutung der Felder angepasst hat.[NEWLINE]Die Reisproduktion der modernen Welt steigt stรคndig an. Waren es 1960 noch 200 Millionen Tonnen jรคhrlich, lag die Reisproduktion im Jahr 2000 bereits bei รผber 600 Millionen Tonnen.
TEXT.Italian: Il riso รจ un alimento fondamentale nella dieta di piรน della metร della popolazione mondiale. Viene coltivato in piccole vasche o grandi pozze, monitorate con attenzione per poter controllare il livello dell'acqua. Si crede che la coltivazione del riso cominciรฒ contemporaneamente in molte zone, circa 6.500 anni fa. L'odierna produzione di riso รจ passata dai 200 milioni di tonnellate annui del 1960 agli oltre 600 milioni di tonnellate del 2000.
TEXT.Spanish: El arroz es una pieza fundamental de la dieta de mรกs de la mitad de la poblaciรณn humana. Esta planta se cultiva a menudo en arrozales, que son charcas cuya profundidad estรก cuidadosamente controlada. Se cree que su cultivo comenzรณ simultรกneamente en muchos paรญses hace unos 6.500 aรฑos. La producciรณn moderna de arroz ha crecido constantemente, desde doscientos millones de toneladas en 1960 a seiscientos millones en 2000.

TEXT.English: Coffee is usually sold in a roasted state, and with rare exceptions all coffee is roasted before it is consumed. It can be sold roasted by the supplier, or it can be home roasted. The roasting process influences the taste of the beverage by changing the coffee seed both physically and chemically. The seed decreases in weight as moisture is lost and increases in volume, causing it to become less dense. The density of the seed also influences the strength of the coffee and requirements for packaging. The actual roasting begins when the temperature inside the seed reaches approximately 200 ยฐC (392 ยฐF), though different varieties of seeds differ in moisture and density and therefore roast at different rates. During roasting, caramelization occurs as intense heat breaks down starches, changing them to simple sugars that begin to brown, which alters the color of the seed. Sucrose is rapidly lost during the roasting process and may disappear entirely in darker roasts. During roasting, aromatic oils and acids weaken, changing the flavor; at 205 ยฐC (401 ยฐF), other oils start to develop. One of these oils, caffeol, is created at about 200 ยฐC (392 ยฐF), which is largely responsible for coffee's aroma and flavor.

Dzia³a pobudzaj¹co i orzeŸwiaj¹co oraz przyspiesza przemianê materii. Zawiera przeciwutleniacze, które zapobiegaj¹ powstawaniu wolnych rodników. Spo¿ywana w du¿ych iloœciach mo¿e jednak byæ szkodliwa dla zdrowia. Rocznie produkuje siê oko³o 6,7 miliona ton kawy.
TEXT.Russian: รรฎรดรฅ รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รขรปรขรฎรงรจรฒรฑรฟ รจรง รฆร รฐรชรจรต รฑรฒรฐร รญ, รจ รงร รฐรฅรครชรจรฌ รจรฑรชรซรพรทรฅรญรจรฅรฌ รขรฑรฅ รชรฎรดรฅ รฎรกรฆร รฐรจรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รครฎ รฅรฃรฎ รณรฏรฎรฒรฐรฅรกรซรฅรญรจรฟ. รรญ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รฏรฐรฎรคร รขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รณรฆรฅ รฎรทรจรนรฅรญรญรปรฌ รจ รฎรกรฆร รฐรฅรญรญรปรฌ, รซรจรกรฎ รฎรญ รฎรกรฆร รฐรจรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รครฎรฌร รธรญรจรต รณรฑรซรฎรขรจรฟรต. รรฐรฎรถรฅรฑรฑ รฆร รฐรชรจ รขรซรจรฟรฅรฒ รญร รขรชรณรฑ รญร รฏรจรฒรชร , รจรงรฌรฅรญรฟรฟ รชรฎรดรฅรฉรญรปรฅ รฑรฅรฌรฟรญร , รชร รช รดรจรงรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ, รฒร รช รจ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ. รรฅรฌรฟ รฏร รคร รฅรฒ รข รขรฅรฑรฅ รฏรฎ รฌรฅรฐรฅ รจรฑรฏร รฐรฅรญรจรฟ รขรซร รฃรจ รจ รณรขรฅรซรจรทรจรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รข รฎรกรบรฅรฌรฅ, รขรฑรซรฅรครฑรฒรขรจรฅ รทรฅรฃรฎ รฎรญ รฑรฒร รญรฎรขรจรฒรฑรฟ รฌรฅรญรฅรฅ รฏรซรฎรฒรญรฎรฉ. รรซรฎรฒรญรฎรฑรฒรผ รฑรฅรฌรฟรญ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รขรซรจรฟรฅรฒ รญร รชรฐรฅรฏรฎรฑรฒรผ รชรฎรดรฅ รจ รฒรฐรฅรกรฎรขร รญรจรฟ รช รณรฏร รชรฎรขรชรฅ. รร รชรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชร รฟ รฆร รฐรชร รญร รทรจรญร รฅรฒรฑรฟ, รชรฎรฃรคร รฒรฅรฌรฏรฅรฐร รฒรณรฐร รขรญรณรฒรฐรจ รฑรฅรฌรฅรญรจ รครฎรฑรฒรจรฃร รฅรฒ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรฐรญรฎ 200 ร (392 F), รฏรฐร รขรคร รณ รฐร รงรญรปรต รฑรฎรฐรฒรฎรข รฑรฅรฌรฅรญร รฎรฒรซรจรทร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฎ รขรซร รฆรญรฎรฑรฒรจ รจ รฏรซรฎรฒรญรฎรฑรฒรจ, รจ, รฑรซรฅรครฎรขร รฒรฅรซรผรญรฎ, รงร รฏรฅรชร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฑ รฐร รงรญรฎรฉ รฑรชรฎรฐรฎรฑรฒรผรพ. ร รฏรฐรฎรถรฅรฑรฑรฅ รฎรกรฆร รฐรชรจ, รชร รฐร รฌรฅรซรจรงร รถรจรจ รฏรฐรฎรจรฑรตรฎรครจรฒ รชร รช รจรญรฒรฅรญรฑรจรขรญรฎรฅ รฐร รฑรนรฅรฏรซรฅรญรจรฅ รชรฐร รตรฌร รซรฎรข, รจรงรฌรฅรญรฟรฟ รจรต รญร รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรปรฅ รฑร รตร รฐร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฑรฒร รญรฎรขรฟรฒรฑรฟ รชรฎรฐรจรทรญรฅรขรฎรฃรฎ รถรขรฅรฒร , รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รจรงรฌรฅรญรฟรฅรฒ รถรขรฅรฒ รฑรฅรฌรฟรญ. รร รตร รฐรฎรงร รฆรฅ รกรปรฑรฒรฐรฎ รฒรฅรฐรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รขรฎ รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รฏรฐรฎรถรฅรฑรฑร รฆร รฐรชรจ รจ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รจรฑรทรฅรงรญรณรฒรผ รฏรฎรซรญรฎรฑรฒรผรพ. ร รฏรฐรฎรถรฅรฑรฑรฅ รฎรกรฆร รฐรชรจ, ร รฐรฎรฌร รฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รฌร รฑรซร รจ รชรจรฑรซรฎรฒรป รฐร รฑรนรฅรฏรซรฟรพรฒรฑรฟ, รฌรฅรญรฟรฟ รขรชรณรฑ; รฏรฐรจ 205 C (401 F), รครฐรณรฃรจรฅ รฌร รฑรซร รญร รทรจรญร รพรฒ รฐร รงรฐรณรธร รฒรผรฑรฟ. รรครญรฎ รจรง รฝรฒรจรต รฌร รฑรฅรซ, รชร รดรดรฅรฎรซรผ, รฑรฎรงรคร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฐรจ รฒรฅรฌรฏรฅรฐร รฒรณรฐรฅ รฎรชรฎรซรฎ 200 C (392 F), รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รข รงรญร รทรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฉ รฑรฒรฅรฏรฅรญรจ รฎรฒรขรฅรฒรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรฎ รงร ร รฐรฎรฌร รฒ รจ รขรชรณรฑ รชรฎรดรฅ.

TEXT.English: Rocket propellant is a material used by a rocket as, or to produce in a chemical reaction, the reaction mass (propulsive mass) that is ejected, typically with very high speed, from a rocket engine to produce thrust, and thus provide spacecraft propulsion.
TEXT.French: Le propergol de fusรฉe est un matรฉriau utilisรฉ par une fusรฉe comme, ou pour produire dans une rรฉaction chimique, la masse de rรฉaction (masse propulsive) qui est รฉjectรฉe, gรฉnรฉralement ร trรจs grande vitesse, d'un moteur de fusรฉe pour produire une poussรฉe, et ainsi fournir une propulsion ร l'engin spatial.
TEXT.Italian: Il carburante per mazzi รจ una sostanza che, grazie ad una reazione chimica, produce la spinta ad alta velocitร che permette al razzo di avanzare; รจ un tipo di propulsione che funziona in assenza di ossigeno, quindi viene usata per i viaggi spaziali.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Paliwo rakietowe[COLOR_REVERT], to materia³ u¿ywany w rakiecie jako masa reakcyjna (masa napêdowa) wyrzucana z silnika rakietowego w celu wytworzenia ci¹gu, a tym samym zapewnienia napêdu statku kosmicznego.
TEXT.Russian: รร รชรฅรฒรญรฎรฅ รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขรฎ - รฝรฒรฎ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซ, รญรฅรฎรกรตรฎรครจรฌรปรฉ รครซรฟ รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครฑรฒรขร รข รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฉ รฐรฅร รชรถรจรจ, รจรซรจ รฐรฅร รชรฒรจรขรญรฎรฉ รฌร รฑรฑรป, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐร รฟ รขรปรกรฐร รฑรปรขร รฅรฒรฑรฟ, รชร รช รฏรฐร รขรจรซรฎ, รฑ รฎรทรฅรญรผ รขรปรฑรฎรชรฎรฉ รฑรชรฎรฐรฎรฑรฒรผรพ, รฎรฒ รฐร รชรฅรฒรญรฎรฃรฎ รครขรจรฃร รฒรฅรซรฟ รครซรฟ รฑรฎรงรคร รญรจรฟ รฒรฟรฃรจ, รจ รฒร รชรจรฌ รฎรกรฐร รงรฎรฌ รฎรกรฅรฑรฏรฅรทรจรขร รพรฒ รครขรจรฃร รฒรฅรซรฟรฌ รญรณรฆรญรณรพ รชรฎรฑรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรณรพ รฑรชรฎรฐรฎรฑรฒรผ.

TEXT.English: A rope is a length of fibres, twisted or braided together to improve strength for pulling and connecting. It has tensile strength but is too flexible to provide compressive strength (i.e. it can be used for pulling, but not pushing). Rope is thicker and stronger than similarly constructed cord, line, string, and twine.
TEXT.French: Une corde est une sรฉrie de fibres, tordues ou tressรฉes ensemble pour amรฉliorer la rรฉsistance ร la traction et la cohรฉsion. Elle a une rรฉsistance ร la traction, mais est trop souple pour fournir une rรฉsistance ร la compression. La corde est plus รฉpaisse et plus forte que le cordon, la ficelle, mais la fabrication est similaire.
TEXT.Italian: Una corda รจ un insieme di fibre intrecciate o legate assieme per incrementare la resistenza agli sforzi di trazione. Ha un'ottima resistenza alla trazione, ma รจ troppo flessibile per fornire resistenza alla compressione (puรฒ essere usata per tirare, non per spingere). Le corde sono piรน spesse e piรน forti delle fibre singole di cui sono composte.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Lina[COLOR_REVERT], to odcinek w³ókien skrêconych lub splecionych ze sob¹ w celu zwiêkszenia wytrzyma³oœci podczas ci¹gniêcia i ³¹czenia. Ma wytrzyma³oœæ na rozci¹ganie, ale jest zbyt elastyczny, aby zapewniæ wytrzyma³oœæ na œciskanie (tj. mo¿na go u¿ywaæ do ci¹gniêcia, ale nie do pchania).
