W XVIII wieku Europejczycy zaczêli masowo upodobaæ sobie d¿emy, s³odycze, herbatê, kawê i inne s³odycze. Wymagania te sprawi³y, ¿e cukier sta³ siê niezwykle popularny, a jego produkcja i marketing przesz³y przez seriê boomów.

TEXT.German: Schwefel
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฐร

TEXT.English: Sulfur, in its native form, is a yellow crystaline solid. In nature, it can be found as the pure element or as sulfide and sulfate minerals. It is an essential element for life and is found in two amino acids. Its commercial uses are primarily in fertilizers but it is also widely used in gunpowder, matches, insecticides and fungicides. Gunpowder, whether black powder or smokeless powder, is a substance that burns very rapidly and is used as a propellant in firearms. As it burns, a subsonic deflagration wave is produced rather than the supersonic detonation wave which high explosives would produce. The first type of gunpowder was black powder. Historians of various cultures have postulated that it was invented between the seventh or ninth centuries by either the Arabs or the Chinese. It is also possible that black powder was invented by many cultures at around the same time. Regardless of origin, it was the first chemical propellant and the first explosive recorded in history. Black powder is a mix of sulphur, charcoal, and potassium or sodium nitrate, (sodium nitrate makes a less powerful compound), at a ratio of approximately 10:15:75. Historically, potassium nitrate was extracted from manure by a process superficially similar to composting. Nitre beds took about a year to produce crystallized potassium nitrate.
TEXT.German: Schwefel wird sowohl in der chemischen als auch in der pharmazeutischen Industrie genutzt, unter anderem zur Produktion von Schwefelsรคure, Farbstoffen, Insektiziden und Kunstdรผngern. Schwefel findet auch bei der Herstellung von Schwarzpulver oder bei anderen Sprengstoffen Verwendung. Der pharmazeutische Nutzen von Schwefel war bereits im Altertum bekannt. Nach wie vor gibt es pharmazeutische Zubereitungen, die als Wirk- bzw. Hilfsstoff Schwefel enthalten. Schwarzpulver besteht aus einer Mischung von 75% Kaliumnitrat, 15% Holzkohle, vornehmlich aus dem Holz des Faulbaums gewonnen, und 10% Schwefel, welcher absolut sรคurefrei sein muss. Salpeter dient als Sauerstofflieferant, Kohlepulver als Brennstoff und Schwefel als Brennstoff und Sensibilisierer, damit es bei kleinster Berรผhrung mit Funken zu brennen anfรคngt. Diese Bestandteile mรผssen fein zermahlen und gleichmรครig vermischt werden, wobei jeder Vorgang mehrere Stunden dauert und meistens in einer Pulvermรผhle geschieht. Danach wird das Gemisch in so genannte Kuchen feucht verpresst und getrocknet, die wiederum zerstoรen bzw. gekรถrnt werden. So erlangt man eine grรถรere Oberflรคche und Abbrandgeschwindigkeit. Das fertige Pulver wird noch getrocknet und kann dann abgefรผllt bzw. verpackt werden. Es kann verhรคltnismรครig lange (luftdicht) gelagert werden.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Siarka[COLOR_REVERT], to minera³ z gromady pierwiastków rodzimych. Nale¿y do grupy minera³ów rzadkich. Siarka jako minera³ jest w kolorze ¿ó³tym z odcieniami โ zielonymi, czerwonymi i czarnej. Powstaje wskutek rozpadu siarkowodoru lub siarczanów (g³ównie gipsu). Niewielkie iloœci siarki powstaj¹ w wyniku ekshalacji wulkanicznych. U¿ywana w przemyœle: farmaceutycznym, chemicznym (do produkcji kwasu siarkowego), drzewnym, papierniczym, nawozów sztucznych.Zazwyczaj tworzy kryszta³y o pokroju bipiramidalnym, grubotabliczkowe. Wystêpuje w skupieniach zbitych, ziemistych, ziarnistych, naciekowych, nerkowych. Tworzy te¿ naskorupienia, impregnacje i zbliŸniaczenia. Naj³adniejsze kryszta³y spotykane s¹ w druzach powsta³ych w kawernach i szczelinach. Jest krucha, przezroczysta. Wystêpuje w przyrodzie w trzech odmianach polformicznych m.in. siarka rodzima (sulfuryt), rosickyit. W temperaturze powy¿ej 95,3oC przechodzi w siarkê jednoskoœn¹, w temperaturze powy¿ej 119, 3oC topi siê. £atwo elektryzuje siê, jest z³ym przewodnikiem ciep³a, pali siê. Pod wzglêdem chemicznym jest doœæ czysta, bywa zanieczyszczona zwi¹zkami organicznymi oraz mechanicznymi domieszkami kalcytu i gipsu. Niekiedy zawiera domieszkê selenu (siarka selenowa).
TEXT.Russian: ะกะตัะฐ ะฒ ัะฒะพะตะน ะฟัะธัะพะดะฝะพะน ัะพัะผะต ะฟัะตะดััะฐะฒะปัะตั ัะพะฑะพะน ะถะตะปัะพะต ะบัะธััะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะพะต ัะฒะตัะดะพะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะพ. ะ ะฟัะธัะพะดะต ะพะฝ ะฒัััะตัะฐะตััั ะฒ ัะธััะพะผ ะฒะธะดะต ะธะปะธ ะฒ ะฒะธะดะต ััะปััะธะดะพะฒ ะธ ััะปััะฐัะพะฒ. ะญัะพ ะฝะตะทะฐะผะตะฝะธะผัะน ัะปะตะผะตะฝั ะดะปั ะถะธะทะฝะธ, ะพะฝ ัะพะดะตัะถะธััั ะฒ ะดะฒัั ะฐะผะธะฝะพะบะธัะปะพัะฐั . ะะณะพ ะบะพะผะผะตััะตัะบะพะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะฝะธะต ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ัะฒัะทะฐะฝะพ ั ัะดะพะฑัะตะฝะธัะผะธ, ะฝะพ ะพะฝะพ ัะฐะบะถะต ัะธัะพะบะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะฒ ะฟะพัะพั ะต, ัะฟะธัะบะฐั , ะธะฝัะตะบัะธัะธะดะฐั ะธ ััะฝะณะธัะธะดะฐั . ะะพัะพั , ะฑัะดั ัะพ ัะตัะฝัะน ะฟะพัะพั ะธะปะธ ะฑะตะทะดัะผะฝัะน ะฟะพัะพั , - ััะพ ะฒะตัะตััะฒะพ, ะบะพัะพัะพะต ะพัะตะฝั ะฑััััะพ ะณะพัะธั ะธ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ัะพะฟะปะธะฒะฐ ะฒ ะพะณะฝะตัััะตะปัะฝะพะผ ะพััะถะธะธ. ะะพะณะดะฐ ะพะฝ ะณะพัะธั, ะพะฑัะฐะทัะตััั ะดะพะทะฒัะบะพะฒะฐั ะฒะพะปะฝะฐ ะดะตัะปะฐะณัะฐัะธะธ, ะฐ ะฝะต ัะฒะตัั ะทะฒัะบะพะฒะฐั ะฒะพะปะฝะฐ ะดะตัะพะฝะฐัะธะธ, ะบะพัะพััั ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดัั ะฒะทััะฒัะฐััะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะฐ. ะะตัะฒัะผ ัะธะฟะพะผ ะฟะพัะพั ะฐ ะฑัะป ัะตัะฝัะน ะฟะพัะพั . ะััะพัะธะบะธ ัะฐะทะปะธัะฝัั ะบัะปัััั ะฟัะตะดะฟะพะปะพะถะธะปะธ, ััะพ ะพะฝ ะฑัะป ะธะทะพะฑัะตัะตะฝ ะผะตะถะดั ัะตะดัะผัะผ ะธะปะธ ะดะตะฒัััะผ ะฒะตะบะฐะผะธ ะปะธะฑะพ ะฐัะฐะฑะฐะผะธ, ะปะธะฑะพ ะบะธัะฐะนัะฐะผะธ. ะขะฐะบะถะต ะฒะพะทะผะพะถะฝะพ, ััะพ ัะตัะฝัะน ะฟะพัะพัะพะบ ะฑัะป ะธะทะพะฑัะตัะตะฝ ะผะฝะพะณะธะผะธ ะบัะปััััะฐะผะธ ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ ะฒ ะพะดะฝะพ ะธ ัะพ ะถะต ะฒัะตะผั. ะะตะทะฐะฒะธัะธะผะพ ะพั ะฟัะพะธัั ะพะถะดะตะฝะธั, ััะพ ะฑัะปะพ ะฟะตัะฒะพะต ั ะธะผะธัะตัะบะพะต ัะพะฟะปะธะฒะพ ะธ ะฟะตัะฒะพะต ะฒะทััะฒัะฐัะพะต ะฒะตัะตััะฒะพ, ะทะฐัะตะณะธัััะธัะพะฒะฐะฝะฝะพะต ะฒ ะธััะพัะธะธ. ะงะตัะฝัะน ะฟะพัะพัะพะบ ะฟัะตะดััะฐะฒะปัะตั ัะพะฑะพะน ัะผะตัั ัะตัั, ะดัะตะฒะตัะฝะพะณะพ ัะณะปั ะธ ะฝะธััะฐัะฐ ะบะฐะปะธั ะธะปะธ ะฝะฐััะธั (ะฝะธััะฐั ะฝะฐััะธั - ะผะตะฝะตะต ะผะพัะฝะพะต ัะพะตะดะธะฝะตะฝะธะต) ะฒ ัะพะพัะฝะพัะตะฝะธะธ ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ 10:15:75. ะััะพัะธัะตัะบะธ ัะปะพะถะธะปะพัั ัะฐะบ, ััะพ ะฝะธััะฐั ะบะฐะปะธั ะธะทะฒะปะตะบะฐะปัั ะธะท ะฝะฐะฒะพะทะฐ ัะฟะพัะพะฑะพะผ, ะฒะฝะตัะฝะต ะฟะพั ะพะถะธะผ ะฝะฐ ะบะพะผะฟะพััะธัะพะฒะฐะฝะธะต. ะะปั ะฟะพะปััะตะฝะธั ะบัะธััะฐะปะปะธะทะพะฒะฐะฝะฝะพะณะพ ะฝะธััะฐัะฐ ะบะฐะปะธั ัะปะพัะผ ัะตะปะธััั ะฟะพััะตะฑะพะฒะฐะปะพัั ะพะบะพะปะพ ะณะพะดะฐ.

TEXT.English: A superintelligence is a hypothetical agent that possesses intelligence far surpassing that of the brightest and most gifted human minds. "Superintelligence" may also refer to a property of problem-solving systems (e.g., superintelligent language translators or engineering assistants) whether or not these high-level intellectual competencies are embodied in agents that act in the world. A superintelligence may or may not be created by an intelligence explosion and associated with a technological singularity.
TEXT.Russian: ะกัะฟะตัะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบั - ััะพ ะณะธะฟะพัะตัะธัะตัะบะธะน ะฐะณะตะฝั, ะพะฑะปะฐะดะฐััะธะน ะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบัะพะผ, ะฝะฐะผะฝะพะณะพ ะฟัะตะฒะพัั ะพะดััะธะผ ะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบั ัะฐะผัั ัะผะฝัั ะธ ะพะดะฐัะตะฝะฝัั ะปัะดะตะน. ยซะกัะฟะตัะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบัยป ะผะพะถะตั ัะฐะบะถะต ะพัะฝะพัะธัััั ะบ ัะฒะพะนััะฒั ัะธััะตะผ ัะตัะตะฝะธั ะฟัะพะฑะปะตะผ (ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั, ะฟะตัะตะฒะพะดัะธะบะพะฒ ััะฟะตัะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบััะฐะปัะฝัั ัะทัะบะพะฒ ะธะปะธ ัะตั ะฝะธัะตัะบะธั ะฟะพะผะพัะฝะธะบะพะฒ), ะฝะตะทะฐะฒะธัะธะผะพ ะพั ัะพะณะพ, ะฒะพะฟะปะพัะตะฝั ะปะธ ััะธ ะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบััะฐะปัะฝัะต ะบะพะผะฟะตัะตะฝัะธะธ ะฒััะพะบะพะณะพ ััะพะฒะฝั ะฒ ะฐะณะตะฝัะฐั , ะดะตะนััะฒัััะธั ะฒ ะผะธัะต. ะกัะฟะตัะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบั ะผะพะถะตั ะธะปะธ ะฝะต ะผะพะถะตั ะฑััั ัะพะทะดะฐะฝ ะฒะทััะฒะพะผ ะธะฝัะตะปะปะตะบัะฐ ะธ ัะฒัะทะฐะฝ ั ัะตั ะฝะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะพะน ัะธะฝะณัะปััะฝะพัััั.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Superinteligencja[COLOR_REVERT], to hipotetyczny byt, który posiada inteligencjê znacznie przewy¿szaj¹c¹ inteligencjê najbystrzejszych i najbardziej utalentowanych ludzkich umys³ów. "Superinteligencja" mo¿e równie¿ odnosiæ siê do w³aœciwoœci systemów rozwi¹zywania problemów (np. superinteligentnych t³umaczy jêzyków lub asystentów in¿ynieryjnych), niezale¿nie od tego, czy te kompetencje intelektualne wysokiego poziomu s¹ zawarte w agentach dzia³aj¹cych w œwiecie. Superinteligencja mo¿e, ale nie musi, powstaæ w wyniku eksplozji inteligencji i byæ powi¹zana z osobliwoœci¹ technologiczn¹.

TEXT.Russian: รรขรฅรฐรตรฒรขรฅรฐรครปรฅ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรป

TEXT.English: A supersolid material (also known as BoseโEinstein condensate or BEC, and sometimes called "the fifth state of matter") is a state of matter which is typically formed when a gas of bosons at low densities is cooled to temperatures very close to absolute zero (-273.15 ยฐC). Under such conditions, a large fraction of bosons occupy the lowest quantum state, at which point microscopic quantum phenomena, particularly wavefunction interference, become apparent macroscopically. A supersolid material is formed by cooling a gas of extremely low density, about one-hundred-thousandth (1/100,000) the density of normal air, to ultra-low temperatures.
TEXT.Italian: Un materiale supersolido (anche conosciuto come condensato di Bose-Einstein o come "il quinto stato della materia") รจ uno stato della materia che si forma tipicamente quando un gas di bosoni a bassa densitร viene raffreddato a temperature molto vicine allo zero assoluto (-273,15 ยฐC). In tali condizioni una grande frazione di bosoni occupa lo stato quantico piรน basso e quindi i fenomeni quantistici microscopici, in particolare l'interferenza della funzione d'onda, diventano apparenti macroscopicamente. Un materiale supersolido si forma raffreddando un gas di densitร estremamente bassa (circa 1/100.000 della densitร dell'aria normale) a temperature ultra-basse.

TEXT.English: A superstrong alloy, or high-performance alloy, is an alloy with the ability to operate at a high fraction of its melting point. Several key characteristics of a superalloy are excellent mechanical strength, resistance to thermal creep deformation, good surface stability, and resistance to corrosion or oxidation. The primary application for such alloys is in aerospace and marine turbine engines.
TEXT.French: Un alliage super-rรฉsistant, ou alliage haute performance, est un alliage capable de fonctionner ร une fraction รฉlevรฉe de son point de fusion. Plusieurs caractรฉristiques clรฉs d'un superalliage sont une excellente rรฉsistance mรฉcanique, une rรฉsistance ร la dรฉformation par fluage thermique, une bonne stabilitรฉ de surface et une rรฉsistance ร la corrosion ou ร l'oxydation. L'application principale de ces alliages est dans les moteurs ร turbine aรฉrospatiaux et marins.
TEXT.Italian: Una lega super-resistente, o lega ad alte prestazioni, รจ una lega con la capacitร di operare ad una frazione elevata del suo punto di fusione. Diverse caratteristiche chiave di una superlega sono l'eccellente resistenza meccanica, la resistenza alla deformazione termica, la buona stabilitร superficiale e la resistenza alla corrosione o all'ossidazione. L'applicazione principale per tali leghe รจ nei motori aerospaziali e delle turbine marine.

TEXT.English: A synthetic diamond is diamond produced by an artificial controlled process, instead of the natural geological process. Synthetic diamonds are made of the same material as natural diamonds, i.e. pure carbon, although their properties depend on the details of the manufacturing processes. Some varieties have superior hardness, thermal conductivity and electron mobility to those of most naturally formed diamonds. It is estimated that 98% of industrial grade diamond demand is supplied with synthetic diamonds.
TEXT.Italian: Un diamante sintetico รจ un diamante prodotto da un processo artificiale controllato, anzichรฉ dal naturale processo geologico. I diamanti sintetici sono realizzati con lo stesso materiale dei diamanti naturali, ossia il carbonio puro, anche se le loro proprietร dipendono dai dettagli dei processi di fabbricazione. Alcune varietร di diamanti hanno durezza, conducibilitร termica e mobilitร elettronica superiori a quelle dei diamanti naturali. Si stima che il 98% della domanda di diamanti di livello industriale venga soddisfatta da diamanti sintetici.

TEXT.English: A synthetic rubber is any artificial elastomer. These are mainly polymers synthesized from petroleum byproducts. The first synthesized rubber was produced in 1909, by a German team at Bayer laboratory in Elberfeld, Germany; production then expanded greatly during 1940s. Synthetic rubber is used a great deal in printing on textiles, in which case it is called rubber paste, and has uses in the automotive industry for tires, door and window profiles, hoses, belts, matting, and flooring.
TEXT.French: Un caoutchouc synthรฉtique est tout รฉlastomรจre artificiel. Ce sont principalement des polymรจres synthรฉtisรฉs ร partir de sous-produits pรฉtroliers. Le premier caoutchouc synthรฉtisรฉ a รฉtรฉ produit en 1909, par une รฉquipe allemande au laboratoire Bayer ร Elberfeld, en Allemagne ; la production s'est ensuite considรฉrablement dรฉveloppรฉe au cours des annรฉes 1940. Le caoutchouc synthรฉtique est trรจs utilisรฉ dans l'impression sur textiles, auquel cas il est appelรฉ pรขte de caoutchouc, et a des utilisations dans l'industrie automobile pour les pneus, les profilรฉs de portes et de fenรชtres, les tuyaux, les courroies, les nattes et les revรชtements de sol.
TEXT.Italian: Una gomma sintetica รจ un qualsiasi elastomero artificiale. Questi sono principalmente polimeri sintetizzati da sottoprodotti del petrolio. La prima gomma sintetizzata fu prodotta nel 1909, da un team tedesco del laboratorio Bayer di Elberfeld, in Germania; la produzione si espanse notevolmente durante gli anni quaranta del XX secolo. La gomma sintetica รจ molto usata nella stampa di tessuti (nel qual caso viene chiamata pasta di gomma) e trova impiego nell'industria automobilistica per pneumatici, profili di porte e finestre, tubi flessibili, cinture, stuoie e pavimenti.

Pierwsza syntetyzowana guma zosta³a wyprodukowana w 1909 roku przez niemiecki zespó³ w laboratorium Bayer w Elberfeld w Niemczech; nastêpnie produkcja znacznie siê rozwinê³a w latach czterdziestych XX wieku. Kauczuk syntetyczny jest szeroko stosowany w druku na tekstyliach i w tym przypadku nazywany jest past¹ gumow¹ i ma zastosowanie w przemyœle motoryzacyjnym do produkcji opon, profili drzwi i okien, wê¿y, pasów, mat i pod³óg.

TEXT.English: In the Ancient Near East, clay tablets were used as a writing medium, especially for writing in cuneiform, throughout the Bronze Age and well into the Iron Age.

TEXT.English: A tank is a tracked, armoured fighting vehicle designed for front-line combat which combines operational mobility and tactical offensive and defensive capabilities. Firepower is normally provided by a large-calibre main gun in a rotating turret and secondary machine guns, while heavy armour and all-terrain mobility provide protection for the tank and its crew, allowing it to perform all primary tasks of the armoured troops on the battlefield.
TEXT.French: Un char est un vรฉhicule de combat blindรฉ ร chenilles conรงu pour le combat de premiรจre ligne qui combine mobilitรฉ opรฉrationnelle et capacitรฉs offensives et dรฉfensives tactiques. La puissance de feu est normalement fournie par un canon principal de gros calibre dans une tourelle rotative et des mitrailleuses secondaires, tandis que le blindage lourd et la mobilitรฉ tout-terrain assurent la protection du char et de son รฉquipage, lui permettant d'exรฉcuter toutes les tรขches principales des troupes blindรฉes sur le champ de bataille.
TEXT.Italian: Un carro armato รจ un veicolo cingolato da combattimento, progettato per il combattimento in prima linea; combina mobilitร operazionale, offensiva tattica e capacitร difensive. La potenza di fuoco รจ fornita da un cannone principale di grosso calibro posizionato su una torretta girevole e da molte mitragliatrici secondarie; una pesante corazza e una mobilitร su tutti i terreni forniscono protezione al carro e all'equipaggio, permettendogli di svolgere i ruoli di truppe corazzate sul campo di battaglia.

TEXT.English: A tannin is an astringent, bitter plant polyphenolic compound that binds to and precipitates proteins and various other organic compounds including amino acids and alkaloids.
TEXT.French: Un tanin est un composรฉ polyphรฉnolique vรฉgรฉtal astringent et amer qui se lie et prรฉcipite les protรฉines et divers autres composรฉs organiques, y compris les acides aminรฉs et les alcaloรฏdes.

TEXT.English: Tapestry is a form of textile art, traditionally woven on a vertical loom. However, it can also be woven on a floor loom as well. It is composed of two sets of interlaced threads, those running parallel to the length (called the warp) and those parallel to the width (called the weft); the warp threads are set up under tension on a loom, and the weft thread is passed back and forth across part or all of the warps.
TEXT.French: La tapisserie est une forme d'art textile, traditionnellement tissรฉe sur un mรฉtier vertical. Cependant, elle peut รฉgalement รชtre tissรฉe sur un mรฉtier ร tisser au sol. Elle est composรฉe de deux ensembles de fils entrelacรฉs, ceux qui courent parallรจlement ร la longueur (appelรฉs chaรฎne) et ceux parallรจles ร la largeur (appelรฉs trame) ; les fils de chaรฎne sont mis sous tension sur un mรฉtier ร tisser, et le fil de trame est passรฉ d'avant en arriรจre ร travers une partie ou la totalitรฉ des chaรฎnes.

TEXT.English: Tar is a dark brown or black viscous liquid made of hydrocarbons and free carbon, obtained from a wide variety of organic materials through destructive distillation. Tar can be produced from coal, wood, petroleum, or peat. Tar-like products can also be produced from other forms of organic matter. Tar was used as seal for roofing shingles and tar paper, to seal the hulls of ships and boats, and also a general disinfectant. Tar was also used for public humiliation, known as "tarring and feathering", but it was quickly abandoned due to the temporary and permanent physical damage caused by the burns of the tar from the application.
TEXT.Italian: Il catrame รจ un liquido viscoso marrone scuro o nero prodotto da idrocarburi e carbonio libero, ottenuto da un'ampia varietร di materiali organici attraverso distillazione distruttiva. Il catrame puรฒ essere prodotto da carbone, legno, petrolio o torba. Prodotti simili al catrame possono anche essere ottenuti da altre forme di materia organica. Il catrame veniva usato come impermeabilizzante per tegole e carta catramata, per gli scafi di navi e imbarcazioni e anche come disinfettante generale. Il catrame รจ stato anche usato come forma di tortura e punizione, nel quale una persona veniva incatramata e poi ricoperta di piume; il metodo fu rapidamente abbandonato a causa dei danni fisici temporanei e permanenti delle ustioni provocate del catrame ancora caldo.
TEXT.Russian: รรฌรฎรซร รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รชร รช รณรฏรซรฎรฒรญรจรฒรฅรซรผ รครซรฟ รชรฐรฎรขรฅรซรผรญรฎรฉ รทรฅรฐรฅรฏรจรถรป รจ รณรฏรซรฎรฒรญรฅรญรจรฟ รชรฎรฐรฏรณรฑรฎรข รฑรณรครฎรข รจ รซรฎรครฎรช. รร รฏรฐรฎรฒรฟรฆรฅรญรจรจ รฒรปรฑรฟรทรฅรซรฅรฒรจรฉ รครฐรฅรขรฅรฑรญร รฟ รฑรฌรฎรซร รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รซร รฑรผ รครซรฟ รฃรจรครฐรฎรจรงรฎรซรฟรถรจรจ รฏร รฐรณรฑร รจ รซรฎรครชรจ, รญรฎ รฑรฅรฃรฎรครญรฟ รฏร รฐรณรฑร , รฑรครฅรซร รญรญรปรฅ รจรง รขรฎรครฎรญรฅรฏรฐรฎรญรจรถร รฅรฌรฎรฃรฎ รฑรจรญรฒรฅรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฎ รฑรขรฎรฅรฉ รฏรฐรจรฐรฎรครฅ รขรฅรนรฅรฑรฒรขร รฑรขรฅรซรจ รญร รญรฅรฒ รญรฅรฎรกรตรฎรครจรฌรฎรฑรฒรผ รข รฑรฌรฎรซรฅ. รรฐรฅรขรฅรฑรญร รฟ รฑรฌรฎรซร รครฎ รฑรจรต รฏรฎรฐ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รครซรฟ รณรฏรซรฎรฒรญรฅรญรจรฟ รฒรฐร รครจรถรจรฎรญรญรปรต รครฅรฐรฅรขรฟรญรญรปรต รซรฎรครฎรช รจ รชรฐรฎรขรฅรซรผ รครฅรฐรฅรขรฟรญรญรปรต รถรฅรฐรชรขรฅรฉ, ร รฒร รชรฆรฅ รข รฏรฎรชรฐรปรฒรจรจ รญร รฐรณรฆรญรปรต รฑรฒรฅรญ รกรฐรฅรขรฅรญรทร รฒรปรต รครฎรฌรฎรข. รรฐรฎรฌรฅ รฒรฎรฃรฎ, รฑรฌรฎรซร รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฎรกรนรจรฌ รครฅรงรจรญรดรจรถรจรฐรณรพรนรจรฌ รฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฎรฌ.

TEXT.English: Tea is an aromatic beverage commonly prepared by pouring hot or boiling water over cured leaves of the Camellia sinensis, an evergreen shrub (bush) native to East Asia. After water, it is the most widely consumed drink in the world. There are many different types of tea, with vastly different profiles of flavours that include sweet, nutty, floral or grassy notes. Tea has a stimulating effect in humans primarily due to its caffeine content. Tea originated in Southwest China during the Shang dynasty, where it was used as a medicinal drink. It was popularised as a recreational drink during the Chinese Tang dynasty, and tea drinking spread to other East Asian countries. Portuguese priests and merchants introduced it to Europe during the 16th century. During the 17th century, drinking tea became fashionable among Britons, who started large-scale production and commercialisation of the plant in India.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Tee[COLOR_REVERT] ist ein heiรes Aufgussgetrรคnk aus den Blรคttern und Blattknospen der Teepflanze (Camellia sinensis). Diese immergrรผnen Strรคucher wachsen wild in subtropischem Monsunklima, als Kulturpflanze wird er heute hauptsรคchlich in China und Indien angebaut.[NEWLINE]Das alte China ist das Mutterland des Teeanbaus. Hier ist bereits aus dem 2. Jahrhundert v.Chr. eine entsprechende Steuer belegt. Tee hatte eine immense Bedeutung fรผr die chinesische und spรคter auch die japanische Kultur. Dies spiegelt sich auch heute noch in den Teezeremonien wider, die ab dem 13. Jahrhundert entwickelt wurden. Erst im 16. oder 17. Jahrhundert brachten Forschungsreisende die Teepflanze schlieรlich auch nach Europa. Das Handelsmonopol lag zunรคchst bei den Niederlรคndern und ging erst 1669 an die Britische Ostindien-Kompanie. Da der Tee als Getrรคnk im reichen Westeuropa groรen Anklang fand, legten die Briten in ihrer Kolonie Indien zahlreiche Plantagen an, um die steigende Nachfrage befriedigen zu kรถnnen, und auch heute noch gehรถrt Indien zu den grรถรten Tee produzierenden Lรคndern der Welt.
TEXT.Italian: Il tรจ รจ una bevanda aromatica comunemente preparata versando acqua calda o bollente su foglie stagionate di Camellia sinensis, un arbusto sempreverde originario dell'Asia orientale. Dopo l'acqua, รจ la bevanda piรน consumata al mondo. Esistono molti tipi diversi di tรจ, con profili di sapori molto diversi che includono note dolci, nocciolate, floreali o erbose. Il tรจ ha un effetto stimolante sull'uomo principalmente a causa del suo contenuto di caffeina. Il tรจ รจ nato nel sud-ovest della Cina durante la dinastia Shang, dove veniva usato come bevanda medicinale. Fu reso popolare come bevanda ricreativa durante la dinastia Tang cinese e il consumo di tรจ si diffuse in altri paesi dell'Asia orientale. Sacerdoti e mercanti portoghesi lo introdussero in Europa nel corso del XVI secolo. Durante il XVII secolo, bere il tรจ divenne di moda tra i britannici, che iniziarono la produzione e la commercializzazione su larga scala della pianta in India.

Camellia Sinesis (ัะฐะนะฝะพะต ัะฐััะตะฝะธะต) ะฒััะฐัะธะฒะฐัั ะฒ ััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะธั ะธ ััะฑััะพะฟะธัะตัะบะธั ัะตะณะธะพะฝะฐั ะฟะปะฐะฝะตัั, ัะฐะบะธั ะบะฐะบ ะะธัะฐะน ะธะปะธ ะะฝะดะธั. ะ ะพะดะพะผ ะธะท ะฎะณะพ-ะะพััะพัะฝะพะน ะะทะธะธ, ะพะฝ ัะฐะฝะพ ััะฐะป ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝัะผ ะฟัะพะดัะบัะพะผ ะฟะธัะฐะฝะธั ะผะฝะพะณะธั ะถะธัะตะปะตะน ะะพััะพะบะฐ. ะขะพะปัะบะพ ะฒ ัะตััะฝะฐะดัะฐัะพะผ ะธะปะธ ัะตะผะฝะฐะดัะฐัะพะผ ะฒะตะบะต ะพะฝ ะฑัะป ะฟัะตะดััะฐะฒะปะตะฝ ะะฒัะพะฟะต, ะบะพะณะดะฐ ะธััะปะตะดะพะฒะฐัะตะปะธ ะฟะตัะตัะตะบะปะธ ะทะตะผะฝะพะน ัะฐั ะฒ ะฟะพะธัะบะฐั ะทะฝะฐะฝะธะน ะธ ัะพะบัะพะฒะธั. ะงะฐะน ะฑััััะพ ััะฐะป ะฟะพะฟัะปััะฝัะผ ััะตะดะธ ะฑะพะณะฐััั ะถะธัะตะปะตะน ะะฐะฟะฐะดะฝะพะน ะะฒัะพะฟั, ะธ ะะตะปะธะบะพะฑัะธัะฐะฝะธั ัะพะทะดะฐะปะฐ ะผะฝะพะณะพัะธัะปะตะฝะฝัะต ะฟะปะฐะฝัะฐัะธะธ ะฒ ัะฒะพะตะน ะบะพะปะพะฝะธะธ ะะฝะดะธะธ, ััะพะฑั ัะดะพะฒะปะตัะฒะพัะธัั ัะฟัะพั - ะะฝะดะธั ัะตะณะพะดะฝั ะพััะฐะตััั ะบััะฟะฝะตะนัะธะผ ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดะธัะตะปะตะผ ัะฐั ะฒ ะผะธัะต.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Herbata[COLOR_REVERT], to aromatyczny napój powszechnie przygotowywany przez zalanie gor¹cej lub wrz¹cej wody peklowanych liœci Camellia sinensis, wiecznie zielonego krzewu pochodz¹cego z Azji Wschodniej. Po wodzie jest to najczêœciej spo¿ywany napój na œwiecie. Istnieje wiele ró¿nych rodzajów herbat o bardzo ró¿nych profilach smakowych, obejmuj¹cych nuty s³odkie, orzechowe, kwiatowe lub trawiaste. Herbata dzia³a stymuluj¹co na cz³owieka, przede wszystkim ze wzglêdu na zawartoœæ kofeiny. Herbata powsta³a w po³udniowo-zachodnich Chinach za czasów dynastii Shang, gdzie by³a u¿ywana jako napój leczniczy. Zosta³a spopularyzowana jako napój rekreacyjny w czasach chiñskiej dynastii Tang, a picie herbaty rozprzestrzeni³o siê na inne kraje Azji Wschodniej. Portugalscy ksiê¿a i kupcy sprowadzili j¹ do Europy w XVI wieku. W XVII wieku picie herbaty sta³o siê modne wœród Brytyjczyków, którzy rozpoczêli produkcjê i komercjalizacjê tej roœliny na du¿¹ skalê w Indiach.

TEXT.German: Fernseher
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรซรฅรขรจรงรฎรฐรป

TEXT.English: A television set (also called a television, TV set, TV, or "Telly" (UK) ) is a device that combines a tuner, display, and speakers for the purpose of viewing television. Television sets became a popular consumer product after the Second World War, using vacuum tubes and cathode ray tube displays. The addition of color to broadcast television after 1953 further increased the popularity of television sets, and an outdoor antenna became a common feature of suburban homes. The ubiquitous television set became the display device for the first generation of home computers.
TEXT.German: Ein Fernsehgerรคt oder Fernsehapparat, in den 1930er Jahren auch Ferntonkino oder das Telehor (von altgriechisch horaosehen), ist ein Gerรคt zum Empfang und zur Wiedergabe von Fernsehsignalen. Die Idee fรผr einen ersten mechanischen Fernsehapparat wurde bereits 1886 von Paul Nipkow in einem Patent dargelegt. Er gilt damit als der eigentliche Erfinder des Fernsehens. Gebrรคuchlich fรผr Fernsehgerรคte ist auch die Bezeichnung Fernsehempfรคnger.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Telewizor[COLOR_REVERT] (zwany tak¿e, TV) jest urz¹dzeniem, które ³¹czy w tuner, wyœwietlacz i g³oœniki w celu ogl¹dania telewizji. Telewizory sta³ siê popularnym produktem konsumpcyjnym po II wojnie œwiatowej, za pomoc¹ lamp pró¿niowych i rur wyœwietlacze katodowych. Dodanie koloru do transmisji telewizji po 1953 dalszy wzrost popularnoœci telewizorów i antena zewnêtrzna sta³a siê wspóln¹ cech¹ podmiejskich domów. Wszechobecne telewizor sta³ wyœwietlacz do pierwszej generacji komputerów domowych.
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรซรฅรขรจรงรฎรฐ (รฒร รชรฆรฅ รญร รงรปรขร รฅรฌรปรฉ รร, รฒรฅรซรฅรช รจรซรจ "รงรฎรฌรกรฎรฟรนรจรช" (รรฎรฑรฑรจรฟ) ) - รฝรฒรฎ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ, รฎรกรบรฅรครจรญรฟรพรนรฅรฅ รฒรพรญรฅรฐ, รครจรฑรฏรซรฅรฉ รจ รครจรญร รฌรจรชรจ รฑ รถรฅรซรผรพ รฏรฐรฎรฑรฌรฎรฒรฐร รฒรฅรซรฅรฒรฐร รญรฑรซรฟรถรจรฉ. รรฅรซรฅรขรจรงรฎรฐรป รฑรฒร รซรจ รฏรฎรฏรณรซรฟรฐรญรปรฌ รฏรฎรฒรฐรฅรกรจรฒรฅรซรผรฑรชรจรฌ รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒรฎรฌ รฏรฎรฑรซรฅ รรฒรฎรฐรฎรฉ รรจรฐรฎรขรฎรฉ รรฎรฉรญรป, รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฟ รขร รชรณรณรฌรญรปรฅ รฒรฐรณรกรชรจ รจ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรฎ-รซรณรทรฅรขรปรต รฝรชรฐร รญรป. รรฎรฟรขรซรฅรญรจรฟ รถรขรฅรฒรญรฎรฃรฎ รฒรฅรซรฅรขรจรครฅรญรจรฟ รฏรฎรฑรซรฅ 1953 รฏรฐรจรขรฅรซรฎ รช รคร รซรผรญรฅรฉรธรฅรฌรณ รฐรฎรฑรฒรณ รฏรฎรฏรณรซรฟรฐรญรฎรฑรฒรจ รฒรฅรซรฅรขรจรงรฎรฐรฎรข รจ รฑ รฎรฉ รฏรฎรฐรป รขรญรฅรธรญรฟรฟ ร รญรฒรฅรญรญร รฑรฒร รซร รตร รฐร รชรฒรฅรฐรญรฎรฉ รทรฅรฐรฒรฎรฉ รข รงร รฃรฎรฐรฎรครญรปรต รครฎรฌร รต. รรฅรงรครฅรฑรณรนรจรฉ รฒรฅรซรฅรขรจรงรฎรฐ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รฑรฒร รซ รฑร รฌรปรฌ รฏรฅรฐรขรปรฌ รฌรฎรญรจรฒรฎรฐรฎรฌ รครซรฟ รฏรฅรฐรขรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฎรชรฎรซรฅรญรจรฟ รครฎรฌร รธรญรจรต รชรฎรฌรฏรผรพรฒรฅรฐรฎรข.
รรฅรฐรฐร รชรฎรฒร

TEXT.English: Terracotta, terra cotta or terra-cotta is a clay-based ceramic where the fired body is porous. Fired terracotta is not watertight, but surface-burnishing the body before firing can decrease its porousness and a layer of glaze can make it watertight. It is suitable for use below ground to carry pressurized water (an archaic use), for garden pots or building decoration, and for oil containers, oil lamps, or ovens. Most other uses, such as for tableware, sanitary piping, or building decoration in freezing environments, require the material to be glazed.
TEXT.Italian: La terracotta รจ una ceramica a base di argilla dal corpo poroso. La terracotta infornata non รจ a tenuta stagna, ma la brunitura superficiale del corpo prima della cottura puรฒ ridurne la porositร e uno strato di smalto puรฒ renderlo a tenuta stagna. ร adatta per trasportare acqua in pressione sottoterra, per i vasi da giardino o per le decorazione di ambienti e per i contenitori di olio, le lampade a olio o i forni. La maggior parte degli altri usi, come stoviglie, tubazioni sanitarie o decorazione di edifici in ambienti freddi, richiede che la terracotta venga prima smaltata.
TEXT.Russian: ะขะตััะฐะบะพัะฐ - ััะพ ะบะตัะฐะผะธะบะฐ ะฝะฐ ะพัะฝะพะฒะต ะณะปะธะฝั, ะณะดะต ะพะฑะพะถะถะตะฝะฝะพะต ัะตะปะพ ะฟะพัะธััะพะต. ะะฑะพะถะถะตะฝะฝะฐั ัะตััะฐะบะพัะฐ ะฝะต ัะฒะปัะตััั ะฒะพะดะพะฝะตะฟัะพะฝะธัะฐะตะผะพะน, ะฝะพ ะฟะพะปะธัะพะฒะบะฐ ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพััะธ ัะตะปะฐ ะฟะตัะตะด ะพะฑะถะธะณะพะผ ะผะพะถะตั ัะผะตะฝััะธัั ะตะต ะฟะพัะธััะพััั, ะฐ ัะปะพะน ะณะปะฐะทััะธ ะผะพะถะตั ัะดะตะปะฐัั ะตะต ะฒะพะดะพะฝะตะฟัะพะฝะธัะฐะตะผะพะน. ะะฝ ะฟะพะดั ะพะดะธั ะดะปั ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะฝะธั ะฟะพะด ะทะตะผะปะตะน ะดะปั ะฟะพะดะฐัะธ ะฒะพะดั ะฟะพะด ะดะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธะตะผ (ะฐัั ะฐะธัะฝะพะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะฝะธะต), ะดะปั ัะฐะดะพะฒัั ะณะพััะบะพะฒ ะธะปะธ ัะบัะฐัะตะฝะธั ะทะดะฐะฝะธะน, ะฐ ัะฐะบะถะต ะดะปั ะตะผะบะพััะตะน ั ะผะฐัะปะพะผ, ะผะฐัะปัะฝัั ะปะฐะผะฟ ะธะปะธ ะดัั ะพะฒะพะบ. ะะปั ะฑะพะปััะธะฝััะฒะฐ ะดััะณะธั ะฟัะธะผะตะฝะตะฝะธะน, ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั ะดะปั ะธะทะณะพัะพะฒะปะตะฝะธั ะฟะพััะดั, ัะฐะฝัะตั ะฝะธัะตัะบะธั ัััะฑะพะฟัะพะฒะพะดะพะฒ ะธะปะธ ะพัะดะตะปะบะธ ะทะดะฐะฝะธะน ะฒ ััะปะพะฒะธัั ะฝะธะทะบะธั ัะตะผะฟะตัะฐััั, ััะตะฑัะตััั, ััะพะฑั ะผะฐัะตัะธะฐะป ะฑัะป ะณะปะฐะทะธัะพะฒะฐะฝ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Terakota[COLOR_REVERT], to ceramika na bazie gliny, w której wypalona bry³a jest porowata. Wypalona terakota nie jest wodoszczelna, ale polerowanie powierzchni korpusu przed wypaleniem mo¿e zmniejszyæ jej porowatoœæ, a warstwa glazury mo¿e zapewniæ wodoszczelnoœæ. Nadaje siê do stosowania pod ziemi¹ do przenoszenia wody pod ciœnieniem (zastosowanie archaiczne), do doniczek ogrodowych lub dekoracji budynków, a tak¿e do pojemników na olej, lamp oliwnych lub piekarników. Wiêkszoœæ innych zastosowañ, takich jak zastawa sto³owa, ruroci¹gi sanitarne lub dekoracja budynków w mroŸnych warunkach, wymaga, aby materia³ by³ przeszklony.

TEXT.Russian: รรจรฌรฑรชร รฟ รชรฅรฐร รฌรจรชร

TEXT.English: Terra sigillata, as an archaeological term, refers chiefly to a specific type of plain and decorated tableware made in Italy and in Gaul (France and the Rhineland) during the Roman Empire. These vessels have glossy surface slips ranging from a soft lustre to a brilliant glaze-like shine, in a characteristic colour range from pale orange to bright red; they were produced in standard shapes and sizes and were manufactured on an industrial scale and widely exported. The sigillata industries grew up in areas where there were existing traditions of pottery manufacture, and where the clay deposits proved suitable. The products of the Italian workshops are also known as "Aretine ware" (i.e. "wares from Arezzo") and have been collected and admired since the Renaissance. The wares made in the Gaulish factories are often referred to by English-speaking archaeologists as "samian ware".
TEXT.Italian: Il termine "ceramica sigillata", in archeologia, si riferisce principalmente a un tipo specifico di stoviglie semplici e decorate prodotte in Italia e in Gallia (attuali Francia e Renania) al tempo dell'Impero romano. Questi oggetti hanno una superficie lucida, con una lucentezza che andava da morbida a brillante (simile a uno smalto) e con una gamma di colori che andava dall'arancio pallido al rosso brillante. Venivano prodotte in forme e dimensioni standardizzate, fabbricate su scala industriale e ampiamente esportate. Industrie di ceramica sigillata crebbero in aree dove giร esistevano tradizioni di fabbricazione di ceramiche e dove i depositi di argilla si dimostrarono adatti. I prodotti delle officine italiane sono noti anche come "ceramiche aretine" e sono stati raccolti e ammirati fin dal Rinascimento. Gli articoli fabbricati nelle fabbriche galliche sono spesso definiti dagli archeologi di lingua inglese come "terra sigillata".
TEXT.Russian: Terra Sigillata - ััะพ ัะตัะผะธะฝ, ะธะผะตััะธะน ะฟะพ ะบัะฐะนะฝะตะน ะผะตัะต ััะธ ัะฐะทะปะธัะฝัั ะทะฝะฐัะตะฝะธั: ะบะฐะบ ะพะฟะธัะฐะฝะธะต ััะตะดะฝะตะฒะตะบะพะฒะพะน ะปะตัะตะฑะฝะพะน ะทะตะผะปะธ; ะฒ ะฐัั ะตะพะปะพะณะธะธ - ะบะฐะบ ะพะฑัะธะน ัะตัะผะธะฝ ะดะปั ะฝะตะบะพัะพััั ะฟัะตะบัะฐัะฝัั ะบัะฐัะฝัั ะดัะตะฒะฝะตัะธะผัะบะธั ะบะตัะฐะผะธัะตัะบะธั ะธะทะดะตะปะธะน ั ะณะปัะฝัะตะฒะพะน ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพัััั, ะธะทะณะพัะพะฒะปะตะฝะฝัั ะฒ ะพะฟัะตะดะตะปะตะฝะฝัั ะพะฑะปะฐัััั ะ ะธะผัะบะพะน ะธะผะฟะตัะธะธ; ะธ ัะพะฒัะตะผ ะฝะตะดะฐะฒะฝะพ, ะบะฐะบ ะพะฟะธัะฐะฝะธะต ัะพะฒัะตะผะตะฝะฝะพะน ัะตั ะฝะธะบะธ ะณะพะฝัะฐัะฝะพะณะพ ะดะตะปะฐ ัััะดะธะธ, ะฟัะตะดะฟะพะปะพะถะธัะตะปัะฝะพ ะฒะดะพั ะฝะพะฒะปะตะฝะฝะพะน ะดัะตะฒะฝะตะน ะบะตัะฐะผะธะบะพะน. ะขะตััะฐ sigillata, ะบะพัะพััะน ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะฟะตัะตะฒะพะดะธััั ะฟัะธะผะตัะฝะพ ะบะฐะบ ยซะทะฐะฟะตัะฐัะฐะฝะฝะฐั ะทะตะผะปัยป, ะพะทะฝะฐัะฐะตั ยซะณะปะธะฝะฐ ั ะผะฐะปะตะฝัะบะธะผะธ ะธะทะพะฑัะฐะถะตะฝะธัะผะธยป (ะปะฐั. Sigilla), ะฐ ะฝะต ยซะณะปะธะฝะฐ ั ะทะฐะฟะตัะฐัะฐะฝะฝะพะน (ะฝะตะฟัะพะฝะธัะฐะตะผะพะน) ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพััััยป. ะะดะฝะฐะบะพ ะฐัั ะตะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะธะน ัะตัะผะธะฝ ะฟัะธะผะตะฝัะตััั ะบ ะณะพััะบะฐะผ ั ะณะปะฐะดะบะพะน ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพัััั, ะฐ ัะฐะบะถะต ะบ ะณะพััะบะฐะผ, ัะบัะฐัะตะฝะฝัะผ ัะตะปัะตัะฝัะผะธ ัะธะณััะฐะผะธ.[PARAGRAPH:2]ะขะตััะฐ ัะธะณะธะปะปะฐัะฐ ะบะฐะบ ะฐัั ะตะพะปะพะณะธัะตัะบะธะน ัะตัะผะธะฝ ะพัะฝะพัะธััั ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ะบ ะพะฟัะตะดะตะปะตะฝะฝะพะผั ัะธะฟั ะฟัะพััะพะน ะธ ัะบัะฐัะตะฝะฝะพะน ะฟะพััะดั, ัะดะตะปะฐะฝะฝะพะน ะฒ ะัะฐะปะธะธ ะธ ะฒ ะะฐะปะปะธะธ (ะคัะฐะฝัะธั ะธ ะ ะตะนะฝัะบะฐั ะพะฑะปะฐััั) ะฒะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะฐ ะ ะธะผัะบะพะน ะธะผะฟะตัะธะธ. ะญัะธ ัะพััะดั ะธะผะตัั ะณะปัะฝัะตะฒัั ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพััั, ะฒะฐััะธัััััััั ะพั ะผัะณะบะพะณะพ ะฑะปะตัะบะฐ ะดะพ ะฑะปะตััััะตะณะพ ะณะปัะฝัะตะฒะพะณะพ ะฑะปะตัะบะฐ, ะฒ ั ะฐัะฐะบัะตัะฝะพะน ัะฒะตัะพะฒะพะน ะณะฐะผะผะต ะพั ะฑะปะตะดะฝะพ-ะพัะฐะฝะถะตะฒะพะณะพ ะดะพ ััะบะพ-ะบัะฐัะฝะพะณะพ; ะพะฝะธ ะฒัะฟััะบะฐะปะธัั ััะฐะฝะดะฐััะฝัั ัะพัะผ ะธ ัะฐะทะผะตัะพะฒ, ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดะธะปะธัั ะฒ ะฟัะพะผััะปะตะฝะฝัั ะผะฐัััะฐะฑะฐั ะธ ัะธัะพะบะพ ัะบัะฟะพััะธัะพะฒะฐะปะธัั. ะะฝะดััััะธั ัะธะณะธะปะปะฐั ะฒััะพัะปะฐ ะฒ ัะตะณะธะพะฝะฐั , ะณะดะต ัััะตััะฒะพะฒะฐะปะธ ััะฐะดะธัะธะธ ะธะทะณะพัะพะฒะปะตะฝะธั ะณะพะฝัะฐัะฝัั ะธะทะดะตะปะธะน ะธ ะณะดะต ะผะตััะพัะพะถะดะตะฝะธั ะณะปะธะฝั ะพะบะฐะทะฐะปะธัั ะฟะพะดั ะพะดััะธะผะธ. ะะทะดะตะปะธั ะธัะฐะปััะฝัะบะธั ะผะฐััะตััะบะธั , ัะฐะบะถะต ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัะต ะบะฐะบ ะธะทะดะตะปะธั Arretine, ัะพะฑะธัะฐะปะธัั ะธ ะฒะพัั ะธัะฐะปะธัั ะธะผะธ ั ัะฟะพั ะธ ะะพะทัะพะถะดะตะฝะธั. ะะทะดะตะปะธั, ะธะทะณะพัะพะฒะปะตะฝะฝัะต ะฝะฐ ะณะฐะปะปััะบะธั ัะฐะฑัะธะบะฐั , ะฐะฝะณะปะพะณะพะฒะพัััะธะต ะฐัั ะตะพะปะพะณะธ ัะฐััะพ ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐัั ะธะทะดะตะปะธัะผะธ ะธะท ะฝะฐัะพะดะฐ ัะฐะผัะฝ. ะะปะธะทะบะพัะพะดััะฒะตะฝะฝัะต ะบะตัะฐะผะธัะตัะบะธะต ัะบะฐะฝะธ, ะธะทะณะพัะพะฒะปะตะฝะฝัะต ะฒ ัะตะฒะตัะพะฐััะธะบะฐะฝัะบะธั ะธ ะฒะพััะพัะฝัั ะฟัะพะฒะธะฝัะธัั ะ ะธะผัะบะพะน ะธะผะฟะตัะธะธ, ะพะฑััะฝะพ ะฝะต ะฝะฐะทัะฒะฐัััั terra sigillata, ะฐ ะธะผะตัั ะฑะพะปะตะต ะบะพะฝะบัะตัะฝัะต ะฝะฐะทะฒะฐะฝะธั, ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั ะััะธะบะฐะฝัะบะธะต ะบัะฐัะฝัะต ะธะทะดะตะปะธั ัะบะพะปัะถะตะฝะธั. ะัะต ััะธ ะฒะธะดั ะบะตัะฐะผะธะบะธ ะฒะฐะถะฝั ะดะปั ะฐัั ะตะพะปะพะณะพะฒ: ะธั ัะฐััะพ ะผะพะถะฝะพ ัะพัะฝะพ ะดะฐัะธัะพะฒะฐัั, ะฐ ะธั ัะฐัะฟัะพัััะฐะฝะตะฝะธะต ะฟัะพะปะธะฒะฐะตั ัะฒะตั ะฝะฐ ะฐัะฟะตะบัั ะดัะตะฒะฝะตัะธะผัะบะพะน ัะบะพะฝะพะผะธะบะธ.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Terra sigillata[COLOR_REVERT], jako termin archeologiczny, odnosi siê g³ównie do okreœlonego rodzaju prostej i zdobionej zastawy sto³owej produkowanej we W³oszech i Galii (Francja i Nadrenia) w czasach Cesarstwa Rzymskiego. Przemys³ sigillata rozwin¹³ siê na obszarach, gdzie istnia³y tradycje wytwarzania ceramiki i gdzie odpowiednie okaza³y siê z³o¿a gliny. Wyroby w³oskich warsztatów nazywane s¹ tak¿e "wyrobami aretyñskimi" (tj. "wyrobami z Arezzo") i s¹ gromadzone i podziwiane od czasów renesansu. TEXT.English: A tesseract is the four-dimensional analog of the cube. It is one of the six convex regular 4-polytopes, and has 16 vertices, 32 edges, 24 square faces and 8 three-dimensional cubic faces. The word "tesseract" was coined and first used in 1888 by Charles Howard Hinton in his book [I]A New Era of Thought[/I].
TEXT.French: Un tesseract est l'analogue ร quatre dimensions du cube. C'est l'un des six 4-polytopes rรฉguliers convexes, et possรจde 16 sommets, 32 arรชtes, 24 faces carrรฉes et 8 faces cubiques tridimensionnelles. Le mot "tesseract" a รฉtรฉ inventรฉ et utilisรฉ pour la premiรจre fois en 1888 par Charles Howard Hinton dans son livre [I]A New Era of Thought[/I].
TEXT.Italian: Un tesseratto รจ l'equivalente quadri-dimensionale del cubo. ร uno dei sei politopi regolari convessi e ha 16 vertici, 32 spigoli, 24 facce quadrate e 8 facce tridimensionali cubiche. La parola "tesseratto" fu inventata e usata per la prima volta nel 1888 da Charles Howard Hinton nel suo libro [I]Una nuova era del pensiero[/I].
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Tesserakt[COLOR_REVERT] jest czterowymiarowym odpowiednikiem szeœcianu. S³owo "tesserakt" zosta³o wymyœlone i u¿yte po raz pierwszy w 1888 roku przez Charlesa Howarda Hintona w jego ksi¹¿ce [I]A New Era of Thought[/I].
TEXT.Russian: ะ ะณะตะพะผะตััะธะธ ัะตััะตัะฐะบั ัะฒะปัะตััั ัะตัััะตั ะผะตัะฝัะผ ะฐะฝะฐะปะพะณะพะผ ะบัะฑะฐ; ะขะตััะตัะฐะบั - ะบ ะบัะฑั, ะบะฐะบ ะบัะฑ - ะบ ะบะฒะฐะดัะฐัั. ะขะฐะบ ะถะต, ะบะฐะบ ะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพััั ะบัะฑะฐ ัะพััะพะธั ะธะท ัะตััะธ ะบะฒะฐะดัะฐัะฝัั ะณัะฐะฝะตะน, ะณะธะฟะตัะฟะพะฒะตัั ะฝะพััั ัะตััะตัะฐะบัะฐ ัะพััะพะธั ะธะท ะฒะพััะผะธ ะบัะฑะธัะตัะบะธั ััะตะตะบ. ะขะตััะตัะฐะบั ัะฒะปัะตััั ะพะดะฝะธะผ ะธะท ัะตััะธ ะฒัะฟัะบะปัั ะฟัะฐะฒะธะปัะฝัั 4-ะผะฝะพะณะพะณัะฐะฝะฝะธะบะพะฒ.

TEXT.English: The theremin is an early electronic musical instrument controlled without discernible physical contact from the player. It is named after its Russian inventor, Professor Lรฉon Theremin, who patented the device in 1928. The controlling section usually consists of two metal antennas which sense the position of the player's hands and control oscillators for frequency with one hand, and amplitude (volume) with the other, so it can be played without being touched. The electric signals from the theremin are amplified and sent to a loudspeaker.
TEXT.Italian: Il theremin รจ uno dei primi strumenti musicali elettronici, che puรฒ essere suonato anche senza un contatto fisico completo. Prende il nome dal suo inventore, il professore russo Lรฉon Theremin, che brevettรฒ il dispositivo nel 1928. La sezione di controllo consiste solitamente di due antenne metalliche, che rilevano la posizione delle mani del musicista e in base a esse controllano rispettivamente gli oscillatori di frequenza e l'ampiezza (ossia il volume) senza che li si tocchi direttamente. I segnali elettrici del theremin vengono poi amplificati e inviati a un altoparlante.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Theremin[COLOR_REVERT], to wczesny elektroniczny instrument muzyczny, sterowany bez zauwa¿alnego kontaktu fizycznego ze strony gracza. Zosta³ nazwany na czeœæ rosyjskiego wynalazcy, profesora Leona Theremina, który opatentowa³ urz¹dzenie w 1928 roku. Sekcja steruj¹ca sk³ada siê zwykle z dwóch metalowych anten, które wykrywaj¹ po³o¿enie d³oni gracza i steruj¹ jedn¹ rêk¹ oscylatorami czêstotliwoœci a amplitudy (g³oœnoœci) drug¹, dziêki czemu mo¿na go odtwarzaæ bez dotykania. Sygna³y elektryczne z Theremina s¹ wzmacniane i przesy³ane do g³oœnika.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]time crystal[COLOR_REVERT], or space-time crystal, is a structure that repeats in time as well as in space. Normal three-dimensional crystals have a repeating pattern in space, but remain unchanged as time passes. Time crystals repeat themselves in time as well, leading the crystal to change from moment to moment. If a discrete time translation symmetry is broken (which may be realized in open driven systems), then the system is referred to as a discrete time crystal. A discrete time crystal never reaches thermal equilibrium, as it is a type of non-equilibrium matter (a form of matter first observed in 2017).
TEXT.Italian: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]cristallo del tempo[COLOR_REVERT], o cristallo spazio-temporale, รจ una struttura che si ripete nel tempo e nello spazio. I normali cristalli tridimensionali hanno una struttura che si ripete nello spazio, ma che rimane invariata nel tempo. I cristalli del tempo si ripetono anche nel tempo, portando il cristallo a cambiare di momento in momento. Se una simmetria di traslazione temporale discreta viene interrotta (come puรฒ avvenire in sistemi a guida aperta), il sistema viene chiamato "cristallo temporale discreto". Un cristallo temporale discreto non raggiunge mai l'equilibrio termico, in quanto รจ un tipo di materia di non-equilibrio (una forma di materia osservata per la prima volta nel 2017).
TEXT.Russian: ะัะธััะฐะปะป ะฟัะพัััะฐะฝััะฒะฐ-ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะธ, ะบัะธััะฐะปะป ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะธ ะธะปะธ ัะตัััะตั ะผะตัะฝัะน ะบัะธััะฐะปะป - ััะพ ัะตะพัะตัะธัะตัะบะฐั ััััะบัััะฐ, ะฟะตัะธะพะดะธัะตัะบะฐั ะฟะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะธ ะธ ะฟัะพัััะฐะฝััะฒั. ะญัะพ ัะฐััะธััะตั ะธะดะตั ะบัะธััะฐะปะปะฐ ะดะพ ัะตัััะตั ะธะทะผะตัะตะฝะธะน. ะะดะตั ะฑัะปะฐ ะฟัะตะดะปะพะถะตะฝะฐ ะคััะฝะบะพะผ ะะธะปััะตะบะพะผ ะฒ 2012 ะณะพะดั. ะะฝ ะฟัะตะดะฟะพะปะพะถะธะป, ััะพ ะฒ ะบะพะฝััััะบัะธะธ ะฑัะดะตั ะณััะฟะฟะฐ ัะฐััะธั, ะบะพัะพััะต ะดะฒะธะถัััั ะธ ะฟะตัะธะพะดะธัะตัะบะธ ะฒะพะทะฒัะฐัะฐัััั ะฒ ัะฒะพะต ะฟะตัะฒะพะฝะฐัะฐะปัะฝะพะต ัะพััะพัะฝะธะต, ะฒะพะทะผะพะถะฝะพ, ะดะฒะธะถัััั ะฟะพ ะบััะณั, ะธ ะพะฑัะฐะทััั ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะฝะพะน ะบัะธััะฐะปะป. ะงัะพะฑั ััะพ ะฒะตัะฝะพะต ะดะฒะธะถะตะฝะธะต ัะฐะฑะพัะฐะปะพ, ัะธััะตะผะฐ ะฝะต ะดะพะปะถะฝะฐ ะธะทะปััะฐัั ัะฒะพั ัะฝะตัะณะธั ะฒัะฐัะตะฝะธั. ะญัะพั ัะธะฟ ะดะฒะธะถะตะฝะธั ะพัะปะธัะฐะตััั ะพั ะดะฒะธะถะตะฝะธั ะฟะพััะพัะฝะฝัั ัะพะบะพะฒ ะฒ ัะฒะตัั ะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฝะธะบะต, ะณะดะต ะฒัะฐัะฐััะธะตัั ะบัะฟะตัะพะฒัะบะธะต ะฟะฐัั ะฝะต ัะฒะปััััั ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะฝัะผะธ ะบัะธััะฐะปะปะฐะผะธ, ะฟะพัะพะผั ััะพ ะธั ะฒะพะปะฝะพะฒัะต ััะฝะบัะธะธ ะพะดะฝะพัะพะดะฝั, ัะพ ะตััั ััะฐะฝัะปััะธะพะฝะฝะฐั ัะธะผะผะตััะธั ะฒะพ ะฒัะตะผะตะฝะธ ะฝะต ะฝะฐัััะฐะตััั. ะกะธะผะผะตััะธั ะฑัะปะฐ ะฑั ัะฟะพะฝัะฐะฝะฝะพ ะฝะฐัััะตะฝะฐ ะฒ ะบะพะปััะตะฒะพะน ัะธััะตะผะต ะะธะปััะตะบะฐ, ะตัะปะธ ะตะต ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะต ัะพััะพัะฝะธะต ะฒัะต ะตัะต ะฒะบะปััะฐะตั ะฒ ัะตะฑั ะฝะตะฟัะตััะฒะฝะพะต ะดะฒะธะถะตะฝะธะต.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kryszta³ czasu[COLOR_REVERT], lub kryszta³ czasoprzestrzeni, to struktura, która powtarza siê w czasie i przestrzeni. Normalne trójwymiarowe kryszta³y maj¹ powtarzaj¹cy siê wzór w przestrzeni, ale pozostaj¹ niezmienione w miarê up³ywu czasu. Kryszta³y czasu równie¿ powtarzaj¹ siê w czasie, powoduj¹c zmianê kryszta³u z chwili na chwilê. Jeœli symetria translacji czasu dyskretnego zostanie z³amana (co mo¿e byæ realizowane w systemach z napêdem otwartym), wówczas system nazywany jest kryszta³em czasu dyskretnego. Kryszta³ czasu dyskretnego nigdy nie osi¹ga równowagi termicznej, poniewa¿ jest rodzajem materii nierównowagowej (forma materii zaobserwowana po raz pierwszy w 2017 r.).

TEXT.Russian: รรจรฌรณรฐรจรครป
รรซรฎรขรฟรญรญรปรฅ รฑรซรจรฒรชรจ
รรซรฎรขรฟรญรญร รฟ รฐรณรคร

TEXT.English: Tin is a silvery metal with a characteristically faint yellow hue, obtained chiefly from cassiterite. The first tin alloy used on a large scale was bronze, made of 1/8 tin and 7/8 copper, from as early as 3000 BC. After 600 BC, pure metallic tin was produced. Pewter, which is an alloy of 85โ90% tin with the remainder commonly consisting of copper, antimony, and lead, was used for flatware from the Bronze Age until the 20th century. In modern times, tin is used in many alloys, most notably tin/lead soft solders and in the manufacture of transparent, electrically conducting films of indium tin oxide in optoelectronic applications. Another large application for tin is corrosion-resistant tin plating of steel. Because of the low toxicity of inorganic tin, tin-plated steel is widely used for food packaging as tin cans. However, some organotin compounds can be almost as toxic as cyanide.
TEXT.German: Zinn, Symbol Sn, metallisches Element, das von den Leuten seit alten Zeiten benutzt worden ist. Zinn ist in der Gruppe 14 (oder IVa) der periodischen Tabelle. Die Ordnungszahl des Zinns ist 50. Zinn ist in den Grรคbern der alten รgypter gefunden worden und wurde nach Europa in den groรen Quantitรคten von Cornwall, England, wรคhrend der rรถmischen Periode exportiert. Die alten รgypter betrachteten Zinn und Leitung unterschiedliche Formen des gleichen Metalls. Zinn war der angelsรคchsische Name fรผr das Metall. Zinn ist ein weit gesuchtes Metall und wird in den Hunderten der industriellen Prozesse weltweit verwendet. In Form von Zinnblech wird es als schรผtzende Schicht fรผr kupferne Behรคlter, die verschiedenen Metalle, die in der Herstellung der Blechdosen benutzt werden, und die รคhnlichen Artikel verwendet. Zinn ist in der Produktion der allgemeinen Legierungen Bronze (Zinn und Kupfer), des Lรถtmittels (Zinn und Leitung) und der Art Metall wichtig (Zinn, Leitung und Antimon). Es wird auch als Legierung mit Titan in der Luftfahrtindustrie und als Bestandteil in etwas Insektenvertilgungsmitteln verwendet. Das Zinnsulfid, auch bekannt als Musivgold, wird in pulverisierter Form fรผr das Bronzieren der Artikel benutzt, die vom Pflaster von Paris oder von Holz gebildet werden. Die Vereinigten Staaten importieren mehr als Fรผnftel der durchschnittlichen jรคhrlichen Weltproduktion des Zinns. Die meisten des Zinns der Welt wird durch Malaysia, Brasilien, Indonesien, Thailand, Bolivien und Australien produziert. Zinn (Angelsachse, Zinn, lateinisches stannum) ist eins der frรผhesten bekannten Metalle und wurde als Bestandteil der Bronze vom Altertum verwendet. Wegen seines verhรคrtenden Effektes auf Kupfer, wurde Zinn in den Bronzewerkzeugen schon in 3500 BC benutzt. Zinnbergbau wird geglaubt, in Cornwall und im Devon (besonders Dartmoor) in den klassischen Zeiten und in einem emporkommenden Zinnhandel begonnen zu haben, der mit den Zivilisationen vom Mittelmeer entwickelt wird. Jedoch wurde das reine Metall nicht bis ungefรคhr 600 BC benutzt. Die letzte kornische Zinn-Grube, bei Sรผdcrofty nahe Camborne geschlossen 1998 4000 Jahre Bergbau in Cornwall zu einem Ende holend. Das Wort "Zinn" hat cognates in vielen germanischen und keltischen Sprachen. Das amerikanische Erbe-Wรถrterbuch spekuliert, daร das Wort von einer vor-Indo-Europรคischen Sprache geborgt wurde. In den modernen Zeiten wird das Wort "Zinn" hรคufig (unsachgemรคร) als generische Phrase fรผr jedes silbrige Metall verwendet, das in dรผnne Blรคtter kommt. Die meisten tรคglichen Gegenstรคnde, die allgemein Zinn, wie Aluminiumfolie, Getrรคnkedosen und Blechdosen genannt werden, werden wirklich vom Stahl oder vom Aluminium gebildet, obgleich Blechdosen eine dรผnne Schicht des Zinns enthalten, um Rost zu hemmen. Ebenso werden sogenannte "Zinnspielwaren" normalerweise vom Stahl gebildet und kรถnnen oder kรถnnen mรถglicherweise nicht eine dรผnne Schicht des Zinns haben, zum des Rosts zu hemmen.
TEXT.Italian: Lo stagno รจ un metallo argenteo con una caratteristica tonalitร giallo tenue, ottenuto principalmente dalla cassiterite. La prima lega di stagno usata su larga scala fu il bronzo, fatto di 1/8 di stagno e 7/8 di rame, giร nel 3000 a.C. Dopo il 600 a.C., iniziรฒ la produzione di latta metallica pura. Il peltro, che รจ una lega all'85-90% di stagno, con il resto costituito comunemente da rame, antimonio e piombo, รจ stato utilizzato per le posate dall'etร del bronzo fino al XX secolo. Nei tempi moderni, lo stagno viene utilizzato in molte leghe, in particolare nelle saldature di stagno e piombo e nella produzione di pellicole trasparenti a conduzione elettrica di ossido di indio-stagno per applicazioni optoelettroniche. Lo stagno viene utilizzato ampiamente anche nella placcatura dell'acciaio, per renderlo resistente alla corrosione. A causa della sua bassa tossicitร , l'acciaio stagnato รจ ampiamente utilizzato per il cibo in lattine. Tuttavia, alcuni composti di organotina possono essere tossici quasi quanto il cianuro.
TEXT.Russian: ะะปะพะฒะพ - ััะพ ัะตัะตะฑัะธัััะน ะผะตัะฐะปะป ั ั ะฐัะฐะบัะตัะฝัะผ ัะปะฐะฑัะผ ะถะตะปััะผ ะพััะตะฝะบะพะผ, ะฟะพะปััะฐะตะผัะน ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ะธะท ะบะฐััะธัะตัะธัะฐ. ะะตัะฒัะผ ัะธัะพะบะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะฝะฝัะผ ะพะปะพะฒัะฝะฝัะผ ัะฟะปะฐะฒะพะผ ะฑัะปะฐ ะฑัะพะฝะทะฐ, ัะดะตะปะฐะฝะฝะฐั ะธะท ะพะปะพะฒะฐ ะฝะฐ 1/8 ะธ ะผะตะดะธ ะฝะฐ 7/8, ะตัะต ั 3000 ะณ. ะดะพ ะฝ.ั. ะะพัะปะต 600 ะณ. ะดะพ ะฝ.ั. ะฑัะปะพ ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะตะดะตะฝะพ ัะธััะพะต ะผะตัะฐะปะปะธัะตัะบะพะต ะพะปะพะฒะพ. ะะปะพะฒะพ, ะฟัะตะดััะฐะฒะปัััะตะต ัะพะฑะพะน ัะฟะปะฐะฒ ะพะปะพะฒะฐ ะฝะฐ 85โ90%, ะฐ ะพััะฐะปัะฝะฐั ัะฐััั ะพะฑััะฝะพ ัะพััะพะธั ะธะท ะผะตะดะธ, ััััะผั ะธ ัะฒะธะฝัะฐ, ะธัะฟะพะปัะทะพะฒะฐะปะพัั ะดะปั ััะพะปะพะฒัั ะฟัะธะฑะพัะพะฒ ั ะฑัะพะฝะทะพะฒะพะณะพ ะฒะตะบะฐ ะดะพ ะฅะฅ ะฒะตะบะฐ. ะ ะฝะฐัะต ะฒัะตะผั ะพะปะพะฒะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะฒะพ ะผะฝะพะณะธั ัะฟะปะฐะฒะฐั , ะฒ ะฟะตัะฒัั ะพัะตัะตะดั ะฒ ะผัะณะบะธั ะฟัะธะฟะพัั ะฝะฐ ะพัะฝะพะฒะต ะพะปะพะฒะฐ ะธ ัะฒะธะฝัะฐ, ะฐ ัะฐะบะถะต ะฒ ะฟัะพะธะทะฒะพะดััะฒะต ะฟัะพะทัะฐัะฝัั ัะปะตะบััะพะฟัะพะฒะพะดััะธั ะฟะปะตะฝะพะบ ะพะบัะธะดะฐ ะธะฝะดะธั ะธ ะพะปะพะฒะฐ ะฒ ะพะฟัะพัะปะตะบััะพะฝะฝัั ััััะพะนััะฒะฐั . ะัะต ะพะดะฝะพ ะฒะฐะถะฝะพะต ะฟัะธะผะตะฝะตะฝะธะต ะพะปะพะฒะฐ - ััะพ ะบะพััะพะทะธะพะฝะฝะพ-ััะพะนะบะพะต ะปัะถะตะฝะธะต ััะฐะปะธ. ะะท-ะทะฐ ะฝะธะทะบะพะน ัะพะบัะธัะฝะพััะธ ะฝะตะพัะณะฐะฝะธัะตัะบะพะณะพ ะพะปะพะฒะฐ ะปัะถะตะฝะฐั ััะฐะปั ัะธัะพะบะพ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั ะดะปั ัะฟะฐะบะพะฒะบะธ ะฟะธัะตะฒัั ะฟัะพะดัะบัะพะฒ ะฒ ะบะฐัะตััะฒะต ะถะตัััะฝัั ะฑะฐะฝะพะบ. ะะดะฝะฐะบะพ ะฝะตะบะพัะพััะต ะพะปะพะฒะพะพัะณะฐะฝะธัะตัะบะธะต ัะพะตะดะธะฝะตะฝะธั ะผะพะณัั ะฑััั ะฟะพััะธ ัะฐะบะธะผะธ ะถะต ัะพะบัะธัะฝัะผะธ, ะบะฐะบ ัะธะฐะฝะธะด.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cyna[COLOR_REVERT] jest srebrzystym metalem o charakterystycznej lekko ¿ó³tej barwie, otrzymywanym g³ównie z kasyterytu. Pierwszym stopem cyny stosowanym na szerok¹ skalê by³ br¹z, sk³adaj¹cy siê w 1/8 z cyny i 7/8 z miedzi, ju¿ od 3000 roku p.n.e. Po roku 600 p.n.e. produkowano czyst¹ metaliczn¹ cynê. Cyna, która jest stopem cyny w 85-90%, a pozosta³a czêœæ zwykle sk³ada siê z miedzi, antymonu i o³owiu, by³a u¿ywana do produkcji sztuæców od epoki br¹zu a¿ do XX wieku. W dzisiejszych czasach cyna jest stosowana w wielu stopach, w szczególnoœci w lutach miêkkich cyna/o³ów oraz w produkcji przezroczystych, przewodz¹cych elektrycznie warstw tlenku cyny indu w zastosowaniach optoelektronicznych. Innym du¿ym zastosowaniem cyny jest odporne na korozjê cynowanie stali. Ze wzglêdu na nisk¹ toksycznoœæ nieorganicznej cyny, stal cynowana jest szeroko stosowana do pakowania ¿ywnoœci w postaci puszek blaszanych. Jednak¿e niektóre zwi¹zki cynoorganiczne mog¹ byæ prawie tak toksyczne jak cyjanek.

TEXT.English: The fundamental materials of modern tires are synthetic rubber, natural rubber, fabric and wire, along with other compound chemicals. They consist of a tread and a body. The tread provides traction while the body ensures support.
TEXT.French: Les matรฉriaux fondamentaux des pneus modernes sont le caoutchouc synthรฉtique, le caoutchouc naturel, le tissu et le fil mรฉtallique, ainsi que d'autres composรฉs chimiques. Ils se composent d'une bande de roulement et d'une carcasse. La bande de roulement assure la traction tandis que la carcasse assure le soutien.
TEXT.Italian: I materiali base dei pneumatici moderni sono gomma sintetica, gomma naturale, tessuto e fili, oltre ad altri prodotti chimici. Hanno un'anima e un corpo; l'anima fornisce trazione, mentre il corpo fornisce supporto.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Opona[COLOR_REVERT] - zewnêtrzna czêœæ ko³a o przekroju otwartym, nak³adana na felgê lub obrêcz i wype³niana powietrzem (lub innym gazem) pod ciœnieniem. Jest elementem pojazdu kontaktuj¹cym siê z pod³o¿em, zapewnia przenoszenie ciê¿aru pojazdu, si³ napêdu, hamowania i skrêtu, a tak¿e podstawow¹ amortyzacjê w czasie jazdy.
TEXT.Russian: รรฑรญรฎรขรฎรฏรฎรซร รฃร รพรนรจรฅ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซรป รฑรฎรขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญรญรปรต รธรจรญ รจรง รฑรจรญรฒรฅรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รชร รณรทรณรชร , รญร รฒรณรฐร รซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รชร รณรทรณรชร , รฒรชร รญรจ รจ รฏรฐรฎรขรฎรซรฎรชรจ, รญร รฐรฟรครณ รฑ รครฐรณรฃรจรฌ รฑรฎรฑรฒร รขรฎรฌ รตรจรฌรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรต รขรฅรนรฅรฑรฒรข. รรจรญรป รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรฒ รจรง รฏรฐรฎรฒรฅรชรฒรฎรฐร รจ รชร รฌรฅรฐรป. รรฐรฎรฒรฅรชรฒรฎรฐรป รฎรกรฅรฑรฏรฅรทรจรขร รพรฒ รฑรถรฅรฏรซรฅรญรจรฅ รข รฒรฎ รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รชร รช รชร รฌรฅรฐร รฏรฐรจรคร รฅรฒ รดรฎรฐรฌรณ.

TEXT.English: Titanium is a precious metal. Titanium is Sometimes called the "space age" metal, as it is corrosion-resistant and has the highest strength-to-weight ratio of any metal. Titanium can be alloyed with iron, aluminium, vanadium, molybdenum, among other elements, to produce strong lightweight alloys for aerospace (jet engines, missiles, and spacecraft), military, industrial process (chemicals and petro-chemicals, desalination plants, pulp, and paper), automotive, agri-food, medical prostheses, orthopaedic implants, dental and endodontic instruments and files, dental implants, sporting goods, jewellery, mobile phones, and other applications. Titanium was discovered in England by William Gregor in 1791 and named by Martin Heinrich Klaproth for the Titans of Greek mythology.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Tytan[COLOR_REVERT] jest metalem szlachetnym. Tytan jest czasami nazywany metalem "ery kosmicznej", poniewa¿ jest odporny na korozjê i ma najwy¿szy stosunek wytrzyma³oœci do masy dowolnego metalu. Tytan mo¿e byæ stopiony z ¿elaza, aluminium, wanad, molibden, miêdzy innymi elementami, do produkcji stopów lekkich do mocnych lotniczym (silniki odrzutowe, rakiet kosmicznych), oraz, wojsko, procesów przemys³owych (chemikalia i Petro-chemicznych, zak³adów odsalania wody, masy celulozowej, i papier), motoryzacyjny, rolno-spo¿ywczy, protezy medyczne, implantów ortopedycznych, instrumenty i pliki stomatologiczne i endodontyczne, implanty stomatologiczne, artyku³y sportowe, bi¿uteria, telefony komórkowe i inne aplikacje. Tytan zosta³ odkryty w Anglii przez Williama Gregora w 1791 roku i nazwany przez Martina Heinricha Klaproth dla tytanów z mitologii greckiej.

TEXT.English: It is believed that tobacco began growing in the Americas about 6,000 BCE, and by 1 BCE Native Americans were using tobacco in spiritual rituals and social gatherings. On October 15, 1492 Columbus was presented with a gift of dried tobacco leaves by the Native Americans. Soon after, it was brought to Europe where its popularity soared as people thought it cured everything from bad breath to cancer. The opposite, of course, was true. In 1847 Phillip Morris established selling hand rolled Turkish cigarettes. Cigarettes have served in every war since WW I, and have become entwined with images of war in the modern era. The active ingredient, Nicotine, is a highly toxic liquid alkaloid which has since been recognized as severely detrimental to health. The neuro-depressant is highly addictive, and also suppresses appetite while increasing blood pressure. New users experience a "high," while experienced users feel simple satisfaction.
TEXT.French: On croit que le tabac a commencรฉ ร pousser en Amรฉriques environ 6.000 avant J.C., et au premier siรจcle, les Amรฉricains indigรจnes employaient le tabac dans des rituels spirituels et des rassemblements sociaux. Le 15 octobre 1492, Christophe Colomb a รฉtรฉ prรฉsentรฉ avec un cadeau des feuilles sรจches de tabac par les Amรฉricains indigรจnes. Peu aprรจs, il a รฉtรฉ apportรฉ ร l'Europe oรน sa popularitรฉ a montรฉ comme les gens ont pensรฉ qu'elle a traitรฉ tout du mauvais souffle au cancer. L'opposรฉ, naturellement, รฉtait vrai. En Phillip 1847 Morris a รฉtabli vendre la main les cigarettes turques roulรฉes. Les cigarettes ont servi dans chaque guerre depuis WW I, et sont devenues enlacerรฉes avec des images de guerre dans l'รจre moderne. La substance active, nicotine, est un alcaloรฏde liquide fortement toxique qui a รฉtรฉ depuis identifiรฉ comme sรฉvรจrement nuisible ร la santรฉ. Le neuro--dรฉpresseur est fortement provoquant une dรฉpendance, et supprime รฉgalement l'appรฉtit tout en augmentant la tension artรฉrielle. Les nouveaux utilisateurs รฉprouvent une "haute," tandis que les utilisateurs expรฉrimentรฉs sentent la satisfaction simple.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Tabak[COLOR_REVERT] (Nicotiana) ist eine รผberwiegend in Sรผdamerika heimische Pflanzengattung in der Familie der Nachtschattengewรคchse, einige der 75 Arten wachsen aber auch in Australien und Nordamerika. Fรผr die Tabakproduktion sind nur der Virginische Tabak (Nicotiana tabacum) und der Bauern-Tabak (Nicotiana rustica) von Bedeutung.[NEWLINE]Die amerikanischen Ureinwohner nutzten Tabak schon lange vor den Europรคern als Heilmittel u.a. gegen Ohrenschmerzen, Fieber, Atemwegs- und Hauterkrankungen, aber auch bei Schlangenbissen. Schamanen nutzten fรผr ihre Rituale die schnelle rauscherzeugende Wirkung des konzentrierten Tabaksafts und den Tabakrauch, der bei Inhalation in grรถรeren Mengen Halluzinationen und Visionen verursacht. Die Alte Welt erfuhr erst durch Christoph Kolumbus von der Existenz der Pflanze. Dieser beobachtete 1492 zum ersten Mal Tabak rauchende Einheimische auf Kuba. Spanische Eroberer lernten 1499 von den Ureinwohnern Venezuelas, dass Tabak auch gekaut werden kann. Auch in Europa wurde der Tabak aber zunรคchst als Heilpflanze angebaut. In Deutschland machten erst die Soldaten des Dreiรigjรคhrigen Kriegs das Tabakrauchen in der Bevรถlkerung bekannt. Schon Mitte des 17. Jahrhunderts war die neue Sucht so populรคr geworden, dass in Kursachsen, Bayern, รsterreich und vielen kleinen Fรผrstentรผmern der Verkauf von Tabak verboten wurde und er nur noch von Apotheken als Medizin abgegeben werden durfte. Diese Kontrollversuche scheiterten aber an der groรen Anzahl an Konsumenten.[NEWLINE]Ab der Mitte des 20. Jahrhunderts wurde die starke Gesundheitsschรคdlichkeit des Rauchens allgemein bekannt. Heute gilt als gesichert, dass selbst unfreiwilliges Passivrauchen Krebs sowie Herz- und Gefรครkrankheiten verursacht.
TEXT.Russian: รร รกร รช - รฑรฅรซรผรฑรชรฎรตรฎรงรฟรฉรฑรฒรขรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒ, รขรปรฐร รกรฎรฒรปรขร รฅรฌรปรฉ รจรง รญร รฒรณรฐร รซรผรญรปรต รซรจรฑรฒรผรฅรข รฐร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฉ รฐรฎรคร Nicotiana. รร รกร รครฐรฅรขรญรจรต รฏรฐรฅรครฑรฒร รขรจรฒรฅรซรฅรฉ รญร รฐรฎรคร รฌร รฉรฟ, รชรณรฐรฟรนรจรต รฃรฐรณรกรปรฅ รฑรจรฃร รฐรป รฑ 1400 รครฎ รญ.รฝ. รร รกร รช รจรฌรฅรฅรฒ รฎรทรฅรญรผ รครซรจรญรญรณรพ รจรฑรฒรฎรฐรจรพ รกรฅรฐรฟ รญร รทร รซรฎ รฑ รชรฎรซรฎรญรจร รซรผรญรปรต รขรฐรฅรฌรฅรญ รจ รฏรฅรฐรขรฎรญร รทร รซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รรฆรฅรฉรฌรฑรฒร รณรญรฑรชรฎรฃรฎ รฏรฎรฑรฅรซรฅรญรจรฟ. รรฎรฌรฌรฅรฐรทรฅรฑรชรจ รครฎรฑรฒรณรฏรญรปรฅ รขรฎรครฟรญรฎรฉ รฒรฐรณรกรชรฅ รจรซรจ รชร รซรผรฟรญรฅ. รร รกร รช รฌรฎรฆรญรฎ รฒร รชรฆรฅ รฆรฅรขร รฒรผ, "รฏรฎรฃรฐรณรฆร รฟ" (รฏรฎรฌรฅรนรฅรญรญรปรฉ รฌรฅรฆรครณ รนรฅรชรฎรฉ รจ รครฅรฑรญรฎรฉ), รจรซรจ รญรพรตร รฒรผ รข รญรฎรฑ รฒรฎรญรชรฎ รฌรฎรซรฎรฒรปรฉ รญรพรตร รฒรฅรซรผรญรปรฉ รฒร รกร รช. รรญรฎรฃรจรฅ รฑรฒรฐร รญรป รณรฑรฒร รญร รขรซรจรขร รพรฒ รฌรจรญรจรฌร รซรผรญรปรฉ รขรฎรงรฐร รฑรฒ รครซรฟ รชรณรฐรฅรญรจรฟ, รฐรฅรฃรณรซรจรฐรณรฟ รฏรฎรชรณรฏรชรณ รจ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรฅ รฒร รกร รทรญรปรต รฏรฐรฎรครณรชรฒรฎรข. รรฑรฅ รฌรฅรฒรฎรครป รฏรฎรฒรฐรฅรกรซรฅรญรจรฟ รฒร รกร รชร รฏรฐรจรขรฎรครฟรฒ รช รฐร รงรซรจรทรญรปรฌ รชรฎรซรจรทรฅรฑรฒรขร รฌ รญรจรชรฎรฒรจรญร , รฏรฎรฃรซรฎรนร รฅรฌรฎรฃรฎ รข รชรฐรฎรขรฎรฒรฎรช รฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรฅรซรฟ. รรฅรฐรฅรง รญรฅรชรฎรฒรฎรฐรฎรฅ รขรฐรฅรฌรฟ รฐร รงรขรจรขร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฐรจรขรปรทรชร รจ รงร รขรจรฑรจรฌรฎรฑรฒรผ. รรฎรฃรซรฎรนร รฅรฌรฎรฅ รชรฎรซรจรทรฅรฑรฒรขรฎ, รทร รฑรฒรฎรฒร , รจ รฑรชรฎรฐรฎรฑรฒรผ, รฏรฎ รขรจรครจรฌรฎรฌรณ, รจรฌรฅรพรฒ รฏรฐรฟรฌรฎรฅ รฎรฒรญรฎรธรฅรญรจรฅ รช รฒรฎรฌรณ, รญร รฑรชรฎรซรผรชรฎ TEXT.English: A stone tool is, in the most general sense, any tool made either partially or entirely out of stone. Although stone tool-dependent societies and cultures still exist today, most stone tools are associated with prehistoric, particularly Stone Age cultures that have become extinct.
TEXT.French: Un outil en pierre est, au sens le plus gรฉnรฉral, tout outil fabriquรฉ partiellement ou entiรจrement en pierre. Bien que des sociรฉtรฉs et des cultures dรฉpendantes des outils en pierre existent encore aujourd'hui, la plupart des outils en pierre sont associรฉs ร des cultures prรฉhistoriques, en particulier de l'รขge de pierre, qui ont disparu.

TEXT.English: A toy is any object that can be used for play. Toys are associated commonly with children and pets. Playing with toys is often thought to be an enjoyable means of training the young for life in human society. Many items are designed to serve as toys, but goods produced for other purposes can also be used.
TEXT.French: Un jouet est tout objet qui peut รชtre utilisรฉ pour jouer. Les jouets sont communรฉment associรฉs aux enfants et aux animaux domestiques. Jouer avec des jouets est souvent considรฉrรฉ comme un moyen agrรฉable de former les jeunes ร la vie en sociรฉtรฉ humaine. De nombreux articles sont conรงus pour servir de jouets, mais des biens produits ร d'autres fins peuvent รฉgalement รชtre utilisรฉs.
TEXT.Italian: Un giocattolo รจ qualunque oggetto che puรฒ essere usato per giocare. I giocattoli vengono generalmente associati con i bambini e gli animali da compagnia. I giocattoli sono ritenuti un ottimo mezzo educativo nella societร umana; molti oggetti vengono creati come giocattoli, ma anche altri oggetti normali possono essere usati per giocare.

TEXT.Russian: รรฃรฐรณรธรชร - รฝรฒรฎ รซรพรกรฎรฉ รฏรฐรฅรครฌรฅรฒ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฉ รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รกรปรฒรผ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญ รครซรฟ รจรฃรฐรป. รรฃรฐรณรธรชรจ รฑรขรฟรงร รญรป รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฑ รครฅรฒรผรฌรจ รจ รครฎรฌร รธรญรจรฌรจ รฆรจรขรฎรฒรญรปรฌรจ. รรฅรฐรฅรครชรฎ รฑรทรจรฒร รพรฒ, รทรฒรฎ รจรฃรฐรณรธรชรจ - รฝรฒรฎ รฏรฐรจรฟรฒรญรฎรฅ รฑรฐรฅรครฑรฒรขรฎ รข รฏรฎรครฃรฎรฒรฎรขรชรฅ รฏรฎรครฐร รฑรฒร รพรนรฅรฃรฎ รฏรฎรชรฎรซรฅรญรจรฟ รช รฆรจรงรญรจ รข รทรฅรซรฎรขรฅรทรฅรฑรชรฎรฌ รฎรกรนรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ. รรญรฎรฃรจรฅ รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒรป รฏรฐรจรงรขร รญรป รฑรซรณรฆรจรฒรผ รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รจรฃรฐรณรธรชรจ, รฎรครญร รชรฎ รจ รขรฅรนรจ, รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฅรครฅรญรญรปรฅ รครซรฟ รครฐรณรฃรจรต รถรฅรซรฅรฉ, รฒร รชรฆรฅ รฌรฎรฃรณรฒ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรผรฑรฟ รข รฒร รชรฎรฌ รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ.
รรฐร รญรงรจรฑรฒรฎรฐรป

TEXT.English: A transistor is a semiconductor device used to amplify and switch electronic signals and electrical power. It is composed of semiconductor material with at least three terminals for connection to an external circuit. A voltage or current applied to one pair of the transistor's terminals changes the current through another pair of terminals. Because the controlled (output) power can be higher than the controlling (input) power, a transistor can amplify a signal. Today, some transistors are packaged individually, but many more are found embedded in integrated circuits.

Nazwa urz¹dzenia wywodzi siê od s³ów transkonduktancja(transconductance) z Œpó³przewodnikowymŒ przyrostkiem -stor jak w warystor.

TEXT.German: Tรผrkise
TEXT.Russian: รรจรฐรพรงร

TEXT.English: Turquoise is an opaque, blue-to-green mineral that is a hydrous phosphate of copper and aluminium. It is rare and valuable in finer grades and has been prized as a gem and ornamental stone for thousands of years owing to its unique hue.
TEXT.French: La turquoise est un minรฉral opaque, bleu ร vert, qui est un phosphate hydratรฉ de cuivre et d'aluminium. Elle est rare et prรฉcieuse dans les qualitรฉs supรฉrieures et a รฉtรฉ prisรฉe comme pierre prรฉcieuse et ornementale pendant des milliers d'annรฉes en raison de sa teinte unique.
TEXT.German: Das Mineral Tรผrkis ist ein eher selten vorkommendes, wasserhaltiges Kupfer-Aluminium-Phosphat aus der Mineralklasse der "Phosphate, Arsenate und Vanadate". Seiner charakteristischen blaugrรผnen Farbe verdankt die Farbe Tรผrkis ihren Namen. Die frรผhere Bezeichnung "Kallait" - lateinisch Callaina - wurde schon bei Plinius "Naturalis historia" verwendet und geht zurรผck auf das altgriechische Wort fรผr blau und grรผn schillernd. Etwa Anfang des 13. Jahrhunderts kam die franzรถsische Bezeichnung Turkoys auf, die sich ab dem frรผhen 15. Jahrhundert in die Bezeichnung pierre turquoise wandelte und รผbersetzt "tรผrkischer Stein" bedeutet. Diese Wortschรถpfung beruht jedoch auf einem Miรverstรคndnis, denn Tรผrkis wurde damals lediglich aus dem Gebiet des heutigen Iran in die Tรผrkei importiert und dort gehandelt. Heimkehrende Kreuzfahrer machten ihn schlieรlich auch in Europa bekannt. Tรผrkis gehรถrt zu den ersten Mineralen, die bergmรคnnisch abgebaut wurden, aber von den alten Minen sind heute nur noch wenige in Betrieb. Sie werden saisonabhรคngig und in kleinem Umfang, oft ohne oder nur mit geringer technischer Hilfe betrieben, da Tรผrkis relativ selten ist. In groรen Kupferminen, besonders in den USA, wird Tรผrkis jedoch oft als Nebenprodukt entdeckt. Die bedeutendsten Fundstรคtten aus geschichtlicher wie aus mengenmรครiger Sicht dรผrften wohl diejenigen der sรผdwestlichen USA, Mexiko, Sinai, Iran, China und des sรผdรถstlichen Australien sein. Einige wenige Fundstellen sind aber auch in Europa und Afrika bekannt. Tรผrkis zรคhlt zu den รคltesten Schmucksteinen und hat mit seinen zarten Pastelltรถnen schon seit dem Altertum viele Vรถlker in seinen Bann gezogen. Er schmรผckte die Herrscher des antiken รgypten, der Azteken (und wahrscheinlich auch der vor-kolumbianischen Mesoamerikaner), der Perser und Mesopotamier, sowie Adlige im Indus und teilweise auch im antiken China seit der letzten Shang-Dynastie. Tรผrkis gelangte mit den Hรคndlern der Seidenstraรe das erste Mal nach Europa. Durch Grabbeigaben ist belegt, dass die alten รgypter Tรผrkis bereits seit Vordynastischer Zeit (etwa 5500 v.Chr.) als Schmuckstein verwendeten. Die berรผhmtesten Stรผcke dรผrften allerdings aus Tutanchamuns Grab stammen. Besonders die Totenmaske des Pharaos, groรzรผgig mit Tรผrkis verziert, ist allgemein bekannt. Tรผrkis war der Edelstein der รคgyptischen Gรถttin Hathor und war bei den antiken รgyptern so begehrt, dass er als einer der ersten Edelsteine imitiert wurde. Die Azteken gestalteten mit Tรผrkis, aber auch Gold, Quarz, Malachit, Gagat, Jade, Korallen und Muscheln abschreckende, und wahrscheinlich rituelle, mit Mosaik verzierte Objekte, wie beispielsweise Masken, Messer und Schilde. Als Untergrund fรผr ein Mosaik konnten Holz, Knochen und Muscheln und als Kleber Harze, Bitumen sowie Wachs dienen. In Persien war Tรผrkis seit Jahrtausenden der Nationaledelstein. Er wurde zur Dekoration verschiedenster Alltagsobjekte (Turbane), Moscheen und anderer wichtiger Gebรคude wie der Madrassa-I Schah Hussein Moschee in Isfahan eingesetzt. Wรคhrend des Mogul Kaisertums kam der persische Stil und Gebrauch von Tรผrkis auch nach Indien, wo man ihn in edlem Goldschmuck (zusammen mit Rubinen und Diamanten) und Gebรคuden wie dem Taj Mahal bewundern kann. Persischer Tรผrkis wurde oft mit arabischer Schrift graviert und dann mit Gold verziert.

TEXT.English: A typewriter is a mechanical or electromechanical machine for writing in characters similar to those produced by printer's type by means of keyboard-operated types striking a ribbon to transfer ink or carbon impressions onto the paper. Typically one character is printed per keypress. The machine prints characters by making ink impressions of type elements similar to the sorts used in movable type letterpress printing. After their invention in the 1860s, typewriters quickly became indispensable tools for practically all writing other than personal correspondence. They were widely used by professional writers, in offices, and for business correspondence in private homes. By the end of the 1980s, word processors and personal computers had largely displaced typewriters in most of these uses in the Western world, but as of the 2010s the typewriter is still prominent in many parts of the world including India.

TEXT.Russian: รรฅรทร รฒรญรปรฅ รฌร รธรจรญรชรจ - รฝรฒรฎ รฌรฅรตร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รจรซรจ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรฌรฅรตร รญรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รฌร รธรจรญรป รครซรฟ รงร รฏรจรฑรจ รข รฑรจรฌรขรฎรซรป รฏรฎรตรฎรฆรจรฅ รญร รฒรฅ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครฟรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฐรจรญรฒรฅรฐร รฌรจ รฏรฐรจ รฏรฎรฌรฎรนรจ รชรซร รขรจร รฒรณรฐรป - รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฌรปรฅ รฏรฎ รฑรตรฅรฌรฅ รฏรฅรฐรฅรญรฎรฑร รทรฅรฐรญรจรซ รทรฅรฐรฅรง รฒรฐร รดร รฐรฅรฒรป รญร รกรณรฌร รฃรณ. รรกรปรทรญรฎ รฎรครจรญ รฑรจรฌรขรฎรซ รฏรฅรทร รฒร รฅรฒรฑรฟ รงร รฎรครญรฎ รญร รฆร รฒรจรฅ. รรฏรฏร รฐร รฒ รฏรฅรทร รฒร รฅรฒ รทรฅรฐรญรจรซร รฌรจ รฑรจรฌรขรฎรซรป รข รงร รขรจรฑรจรฌรฎรฑรฒรจ รฎรฒ รฒรจรฏร รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒรฎรข, ร รญร รซรฎรฃรจรทรญรปรต รฑรฎรฐรฒร รฌ, รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฌรปรฌ รข รฏรฎรครขรจรฆรญรปรต รซรจรฒรฅรฐร รต รจ รฏรฅรทร รฒรญรปรต รธร รกรซรฎรญร รต. รรฎรฑรซรฅ รจรต รจรงรฎรกรฐรฅรฒรฅรญรจรฟ รข 1860-รต รฃรฎรคร รต, รฏรจรธรณรนรจรฅ รฌร รธรจรญรชรจ รกรปรฑรฒรฐรฎ รฑรฒร รซรจ รญรฅรงร รฌรฅรญรจรฌรปรฌรจ รจรญรฑรฒรฐรณรฌรฅรญรฒร รฌรจ รฏรฐร รชรฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจ รขรฎ รขรฑรฟรชรฎรฉ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฏรจรฑรชรฅ รจ รซรจรทรญรฎรฉ รชรฎรฐรฐรฅรฑรฏรฎรญรครฅรญรถรจรจ. รรญรจ รธรจรฐรฎรชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รซรจรฑรผ รฏรฐรฎรดรฅรฑรฑรจรฎรญร รซรผรญรปรฌรจ รฏรจรฑร รฒรฅรซรฟรฌรจ, รข รฎรดรจรฑร รต, รจ รครซรฟ รครฅรซรฎรขรฎรฉ รฏรฅรฐรฅรฏรจรฑรชรจ รข รทร รฑรฒรญรปรต รครฎรฌร รต. ร รชรฎรญรถรณ 1980-รต รฃรฎรครฎรข, รฑ รฐร รฑรฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฐร รญรฅรญรจรฅรฌ รฒรฅรชรฑรฒรฎรขรปรต รฏรฐรฎรถรฅรฑรฑรฎรฐรฎรข รจ รฏรฅรฐรฑรฎรญร รซรผรญรปรต รชรฎรฌรฏรผรพรฒรฅรฐรฎรข รข รงร รฏร รครญรฎรฌ รฌรจรฐรฅ รฏรฅรทร รฒรญรปรฅ รฌร รธรจรญรชรจ รฏรฎรฑรฒรฅรฏรฅรญรญรฎ รขรปรตรฎรครจรซรจ รจรง รณรฏรฎรฒรฐรฅรกรซรฅรญรจรฟ, รญรฎ รฏรฎ รฑรฎรฑรฒรฎรฟรญรจรพ รญร 2010-รฅ รฃรฎรครป รฏรจรธรณรนร รฟ รฌร รธรจรญรชร รฏรฎ-รฏรฐรฅรฆรญรฅรฌรณ รญรฅรฐรฅรครชรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รขรฎ รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รทร รฑรฒรฟรต รฌรจรฐร , รขรชรซรพรทร รฟ รรญรครจรพ.

TEXT.English: Ultrafuel is a highly adaptive fuel product that can be specifically designed for any range of fuel needs (from solid fuel to liquid, to gas), with any designated degree of flamability, flash point, and durability. The pinnacle of genetically enhanced crops and biofuel chemistry, it has made completely obsolete any kind of fossil fuel. It is used in shockwave engines as a catalyst fuel, as well as primary power source for many kind of air and space vehicles.
TEXT.French: L'ultrafuel est un produit combustible hautement adaptable qui peut รชtre spรฉcifiquement conรงu pour n'importe quelle gamme de besoins en carburant (du combustible solide au liquide, au gaz), avec n'importe quel degrรฉ dรฉsignรฉ d'inflammabilitรฉ, de point d'รฉclair et de durabilitรฉ. Sommet de la chimie des cultures gรฉnรฉtiquement amรฉliorรฉes et des biocarburants, il a rendu complรจtement obsolรจte tout type de combustible fossile. Il est utilisรฉ dans les moteurs ร onde de choc comme carburant catalyseur, ainsi que comme source d'รฉnergie primaire pour de nombreux types de vรฉhicules aรฉriens et spatiaux.
TEXT.Italian: L'ultracarburante รจ un carburante altamente adattivo che puรฒ essere progettato specificamente per qualsiasi gamma di esigenze di carburante (dal combustibile solido al liquido, al gas), con qualsiasi grado designato di infiammabilitร , punto di infiammabilitร e durata. Il picco della combinazione fra colture geneticamente migliorate e chimica dei biocarburanti, ha reso completamente obsoleto qualsiasi tipo di combustibile fossile. ร utilizzato nei motori a onda d'urto come combustibile catalizzatore, nonchรฉ come fonte di energia primaria per molti tipi di veicoli aerei e spaziali.

Szczyt genetycznie ulepszonych upraw i chemii biopaliw, sprawi³, ¿e wszelkie paliwa kopalne sta³y siê ca³kowicie przestarza³e. Jest stosowany w silnikach fali uderzeniowej jako paliwo katalityczne, a tak¿e podstawowe Ÿród³o zasilania wielu rodzajów pojazdów powietrznych i kosmicznych.

TEXT.English: An unmanned aircraft, commonly known as a drone or UAV, is an aircraft without a human pilot on board and a type of unmanned vehicle. It is composed by a vehicle, a ground-based controller, and a system of communications between the two. UAVs may operate with various degrees of autonomy: either under remote control by a human operator or autonomously by onboard computers. While they originated mostly in military applications, their use is rapidly expanding to commercial, scientific, recreational, agricultural, and other applications, such as policing and surveillance, product deliveries, aerial photography, infrastructure inspections, smuggling, and drone racing.
TEXT.Italian: Un velivolo senza pilota, comunemente noto come drone o UAV, รจ un velivolo senza un pilota umano a bordo e un tipo di veicolo senza pilota. ร composto da un veicolo, un controller a terra e un sistema di comunicazione tra i due. Il volo degli UAV puรฒ operare con vari gradi di autonomia: sotto controllo remoto da parte di un operatore umano o autonomamente tramite un computer di bordo. Sebbene abbiano avuto originariamente applicazioni militari, il loro uso si sta rapidamente espandendo in applicazioni commerciali, scientifiche, ricreative, agricole e di altro tipo, come polizia e sorveglianza, consegne di prodotti, fotografia aerea, ispezioni delle infrastrutture, contrabbando e corse di droni.
TEXT.Russian: ะะตัะฟะธะปะพัะฝัะน ะปะตัะฐัะตะปัะฝัะน ะฐะฟะฟะฐัะฐั (ะะะะ), ะฒ ะฟัะพััะพัะตัะธะธ ะธะทะฒะตััะฝัะน ะบะฐะบ ะดัะพะฝ, ะฟัะตะดััะฐะฒะปัะตั ัะพะฑะพะน ะปะตัะฐัะตะปัะฝัะน ะฐะฟะฟะฐัะฐั ะฑะตะท ะฟะธะปะพัะฐ-ัะตะปะพะฒะตะบะฐ ะฝะฐ ะฑะพััั. ะะณะพ ะฟะพะปะตั ัะฟัะฐะฒะปัะตััั ะปะธะฑะพ ะฐะฒัะพะฝะพะผะฝะพ ั ะฟะพะผะพััั ะบะพะผะฟัััะตัะพะฒ ะฒ ััะฐะฝัะฟะพััะฝะพะผ ััะตะดััะฒะต, ะปะธะฑะพ ะฟะพะด ะดะธััะฐะฝัะธะพะฝะฝัะผ ัะฟัะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธะตะผ ะฟะธะปะพัะฐ ะฝะฐ ะทะตะผะปะต ะธะปะธ ะฒ ะดััะณะพะผ ััะฐะฝัะฟะพััะฝะพะผ ััะตะดััะฒะต. ะกััะตััะฒัะตั ะฑะพะปััะพะต ัะฐะทะฝะพะพะฑัะฐะทะธะต ัะพัะผ, ัะฐะทะผะตัะพะฒ, ะบะพะฝัะธะณััะฐัะธะน ะธ ั ะฐัะฐะบัะตัะธััะธะบ ะะะะ. ะััะพัะธัะตัะบะธ ะะะะ ะฑัะปะธ ะฟัะพัััะผะธ ะดะธััะฐะฝัะธะพะฝะฝะพ ะฟะธะปะพัะธััะตะผัะผะธ ะปะตัะฐัะตะปัะฝัะผะธ ะฐะฟะฟะฐัะฐัะฐะผะธ, ะฝะพ ะฐะฒัะพะฝะพะผะฝะพะต ัะฟัะฐะฒะปะตะฝะธะต ะฒัะต ัะฐัะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตััั. ะะฝะธ ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ะฒ ะพัะฝะพะฒะฝะพะผ ะดะปั ะฒะพะตะฝะฝัั ะธ ัะฟะตัะธะฐะปัะฝัั ะพะฟะตัะฐัะธะน, ะฝะพ ัะฐะบะถะต ะธัะฟะพะปัะทััััั ะฒ ะฝะตะฑะพะปััะพะผ, ะฝะพ ัะฐััััะตะผ ัะธัะปะต ะณัะฐะถะดะฐะฝัะบะธั ะฟัะธะปะพะถะตะฝะธะน, ัะฐะบะธั ะบะฐะบ ะพั ัะฐะฝะฐ ะฟัะฐะฒะพะฟะพััะดะบะฐ ะธ ะฟะพะถะฐัะพัััะตะฝะธะต, ะฐ ัะฐะบะถะต ะฝะตะฒะพะตะฝะฝะฐั ะดะตััะตะปัะฝะพััั ะฟะพ ะพะฑะตัะฟะตัะตะฝะธั ะฑะตะทะพะฟะฐัะฝะพััะธ, ะฝะฐะฟัะธะผะตั ะฝะฐะฑะปัะดะตะฝะธะต ะทะฐ ัััะฑะพะฟัะพะฒะพะดะฐะผะธ. ะะะะ ัะฐััะพ ะฟัะตะดะฟะพัะธัะฐัั ะดะปั ะผะธััะธะน, ะบะพัะพััะต ัะปะธัะบะพะผ ยซัะบััะฝั, ะณััะทะฝั ะธ ะพะฟะฐัะฝัยป ะดะปั ะฟะธะปะพัะธััะตะผัั ัะฐะผะพะปะตัะพะฒ.

TEXT.English: Unobtainium is any hypothetical, fictional, extremely rare, costly, or impossible material. Less commonly, it can refer to a device with desirable engineering properties for an application, but which are difficult or impossible to achieve. The properties of any particular unobtainium depend on the intended use. The concept of unobtainium is often applied hand-waving flippantly or humorously.
TEXT.French: L'unobtainium est tout matรฉriau hypothรฉtique, fictif, extrรชmement rare, coรปteux ou impossible. Moins couramment, il peut dรฉsigner un dispositif aux propriรฉtรฉs d'ingรฉnierie souhaitables pour une application, mais qui sont difficiles ou impossibles ร rรฉaliser. Les propriรฉtรฉs d'un unobtainium particulier dรฉpendent de l'utilisation prรฉvue. Le concept d'unobtainium est souvent appliquรฉ de maniรจre dรฉsinvolte ou humoristique.
TEXT.Italian: L'unobtainio (anche inottenio) รจ qualsiasi materiale ipotetico, immaginario, estremamente raro, costoso o impossibile. Meno comunemente, puรฒ fare riferimento a un dispositivo con proprietร ingegneristiche desiderabili per un'applicazione, ma che sono difficili o impossibili da raggiungere. Le proprietร di ogni particolare unobtainio dipendono dall'uso previsto. Il concetto viene spesso applicato agitando le mani in modo irriverente o umoristico.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Unobtainium[COLOR_REVERT] to dowolny hipotetyczny, fikcyjny, niezwykle rzadki, kosztowny lub niemo¿liwy materia³. Rzadziej mo¿e odnosiæ siê do urz¹dzenia o po¿¹danych w³aœciwoœciach technicznych dla danego zastosowania, ale które s¹ trudne lub niemo¿liwe do osi¹gniêcia. W³aœciwoœci konkretnego unobtainium zale¿¹ od zamierzonego zastosowania. Pojêcie unobtainium jest czêsto stosowane z nonszalanckim lub humorystycznym machaniem rêkami.
