TEXT.English: Uranium is a silvery-grey, weakly radioactive metal. All known uranium isotopes are unstable: the half-lives of its naturally occurring isotopes range between 159,200 years and 4.5 billion years. The most common isotopes are uranium-238 (which accounts for over 99% of uranium on Earth) and uranium-235. The latter is the only naturally occurring fissile isotope, which makes it widely used in nuclear power plants and nuclear weapons after being treated with a process called "enrichment". In sufficient concentration, these isotopes maintain a sustained nuclear chain reaction. This generates the heat in nuclear power reactors, and produces the fissile material for nuclear weapons. Depleted uranium (238U) is used in kinetic energy penetrators and armor plating.
TEXT.German: Der Verwendungszweck von Uran, einem der wertvollsten Metalle der Welt, hat sich seit seiner Entdeckung im Jahre 1789 drastisch gewandelt. Ursprรผnglich wurde es bei der Herstellung von Stahl eingesetzt, um dessen Widerstandsfรคhigkeit und Elastizitรคt zu verbessern, ohne es brรผchig zu machen, doch seit 1939 wird es nur noch selten fรผr etwas anderes als die Erzeugung atomarer Energie verwendet. Den grรถรten Beitrag leistet das schwere weiรe Metall beim Vorgang der Kernspaltung: Durch die radioaktive Manipulation von Uranatomen lassen sich Neutronen freisetzen, die sich mit einer Geschwindigkeit von bis zu 19.200 Kilometern pro Sekunde fortbewegen kรถnnen. Wenn ein solches Neutron auf den Kern eines benachbarten Atoms auftrifft, kann das Zielatom vollstรคndig zerfallen. Nicht nur das daraus resultierende Energievolumen ist enorm, vielmehr kann das zerschlagene Atom wiederum potenziell mit weiteren Atomen kollidieren. Diese Kettenreaktion ist das Phรคnomen, das allen Anwendungsformen der Kernspaltung einschlieรlich nuklearer Waffen und atomarer Energie zugrunde liegt.
TEXT.Italian: L'uranio รจ un metallo grigio-argenteo debolmente radioattivo. Tutti gli isotopi di uranio noti sono instabili: le emivite degli isotopi presenti in natura variano tra 159.200 anni e 4,5 miliardi di anni. Gli isotopi piรน comuni sono l'uranio-238 (che rappresenta oltre il 99% dell'uranio sulla Terra) e l'uranio-235. Quest'ultimo รจ l'unico isotopo fissile presente in natura, che lo rende ampiamente usato nelle centrali nucleari e nelle armi nucleari dopo essere stato trattato con un processo chiamato "arricchimento". In una concentrazione sufficiente, questi isotopi mantengono una reazione a catena nucleare sostenuta. Ciรฒ genera il calore nei reattori nucleari e produce materiale fissile per le armi nucleari. L'uranio impoverito (238U) viene utilizzato nei penetratori di energia cinetica e nella placcatura delle armature.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Uran[COLOR_REVERT] jest srebrzystoszarym, s³abo radioaktywnym metalem. Wszystkie znane izotopy uranu s¹ niestabilne: okresy pó³trwania naturalnie wystêpuj¹cych izotopów wahaj¹ siê od 159200 lat do 4,5 miliarda lat. Najpopularniejszymi izotopami s¹ uran-238 (który stanowi ponad 99% uranu na Ziemi) i uran-235. Ten ostatni jest jedynym naturalnie wystêpuj¹cym izotopem rozszczepialnym, co sprawia, ¿e jest szeroko stosowany w elektrowniach j¹drowych i broni j¹drowej po obróbce w procesie zwanym "wzbogacaniem". W wystarczaj¹cym stê¿eniu izotopy te utrzymuj¹ trwa³¹ j¹drow¹ reakcjê ³añcuchow¹. Powoduje to wytwarzanie ciep³a w reaktorach j¹drowych i wytwarzanie materia³u rozszczepialnego do broni j¹drowej. Zubo¿ony uran (238U) jest stosowany w penetratorach energii kinetycznej i poszyciach pancernych.

TEXT.English: A vacuum cleaner, also known simply as a vacuum, is a device that causes suction in order to remove debris from floors, upholstery, draperies and other surfaces. It is generally electrically driven. The dirt is collected by either a dustbag or a cyclone for later disposal. Vacuum cleaners, which are used in homes as well as in industry, exist in a variety of sizes and models. Specialized shop vacuums can be used to suck up both dust and liquids.
TEXT.French: Un aspirateur est un appareil qui crรฉe une aspiration afin d'รฉliminer les dรฉbris des sols, des meubles rembourrรฉs, des rideaux et d'autres surfaces. Il est gรฉnรฉralement alimentรฉ รฉlectriquement. La saletรฉ est collectรฉe soit par un sac ร poussiรจre, soit par un cyclone pour รชtre รฉliminรฉe ultรฉrieurement. Les aspirateurs, qui sont utilisรฉs aussi bien dans les maisons que dans l'industrie, existent en une variรฉtรฉ de tailles et de modรจles. Des aspirateurs d'atelier spรฉcialisรฉs peuvent รชtre utilisรฉs pour aspirer ร la fois la poussiรจre et les liquides.
TEXT.Italian: Un aspirapolvere รจ un dispositivo che, tramite un processo di aspirazione, rimuove detriti da pavimenti, tappezzerie, tendaggi e altre superfici. ร generalmente guidato elettricamente. Lo sporco viene raccolto in un sacchetto per la polvere o in un serbatoio per un successivo smaltimento. Gli aspirapolvere vengono utilizzati nelle case e nell'industria ed esistono in una varietร di dimensioni e modelli. Gli aspirapolvere specializzati possono essere utilizzati per aspirare sia polvere, sia liquidi.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Odkurzacz,[COLOR_REVERT], to urz¹dzenie, które powoduje zasysanie w celu usuniêcia zanieczyszczeñ z pod³óg, tapicerki, zas³on i innych powierzchni. Na ogó³ napêdzany jest elektrycznie. Brud jest zbierany do worka na kurz lub cyklonu w celu póŸniejszego usuniêcia. Odkurzacze, które s¹ u¿ywane w domach i przemyœle, wystêpuj¹ w ró¿nych rozmiarach i modelach. Specjalistyczne odkurzacze warsztatowe s³u¿¹ do odsysania zarówno kurzu, jak i cieczy.

TEXT.English: Vanilla is a flavoring derived from orchids of the genus Vanilla. Three major species of vanilla currently are grown globally, all of which derive from a species originally found in Mesoamerica. Flowers are greenish-yellow, with a diameter of 5 cm (2 in). They last only a day, and must be pollinated manually, during the morning, if a fruit is desired. Fruits are produced only on mature plants, which are generally over 3 m (10 ft) long. The fruits are 15-23 cm (6-9 in) long pods (often incorrectly called beans), and mature after about five months. Vanilla extract is obtained from this portion of the plant. Vanilla is the second-most expensive spice after saffron. Despite the expense, vanilla is highly valued for its flavor and is widely used in both commercial and domestic baking, perfume manufacture, and aromatherapy.
TEXT.German: Bei [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Vanille[COLOR_REVERT] handelt es sich um eine Gattung immergrรผner Kletterpflanzen aus der Familie der Orchideen, die in den Tropen und Subtropen heimisch ist.[NEWLINE]Die Region Veracruz am Golf von Mexiko gilt nach einer totonakischen Legende heute als Wiege der Vanille. Die Totonaken waren auch lange das einzige Volk, das um den Vanilleanbau wusste. Sie mussten nach der Unterwerfung durch die Azteken um 1460 einen Teil des Tributs in Vanille entrichten. Der erste Europรคer, der sie probiert hat, dรผrfte der spanische Conquistador Hernรกn Cortรฉs im 16. Jahrhundert gewesen sein. รber Spanien gelangte sie schlieรlich in den Rest von Europa, aber sie blieb lange den Reichen vorbehalten, denn in Spanien stand auf die illegale Ausfuhr von Vanille lange Zeit die Todesstrafe. Erst nach der Unabhรคngigkeit Mexikos 1810 gelangten Pflanzen auch in die Hรคnde der Niederlรคnder und Franzosen. Natรผrliche Vanille blieb aber weiterhin kostbar, daran รคnderte auch die Entdeckung der synthetischen Herstellung von Vanillin 1874 in Deutschland nicht viel.
TEXT.Italian: La vaniglia รจ un aroma derivato dalle orchidee del genere Vanilla. Tre specie principali di vaniglia sono attualmente coltivate a livello globale, tutte derivate da una specie originariamente trovata in Mesoamerica. I fiori sono di colore giallo-verdastro, con un diametro di 5 cm. Durano solo un giorno e devono essere impollinati manualmente, al mattino, se si desidera un frutto. I frutti sono prodotti solo su piante mature, che sono generalmente lunghe piรน di 3 m. I frutti sono baccelli lunghi 15-23 cm e maturano dopo circa cinque mesi. L'estratto di vaniglia รจ ottenuto da questa porzione della pianta. La vaniglia รจ la seconda spezia piรน costosa dopo lo zafferano. Nonostante l'alto costo, la vaniglia รจ molto apprezzata per il suo sapore ed รจ ampiamente usata nella cottura commerciale e domestica, nella produzione di profumi e nell'aromaterapia.
TEXT.Russian: ะะฐะฝะธะปั - ััะพ ะฐัะพะผะฐัะธะทะฐัะพั, ะฟะพะปััะตะฝะฝัะน ะธะท ะพัั ะธะดะตะน ัะพะดะฐ ะะฐะฝะธะปั, ะฒ ะฟะตัะฒัั ะพัะตัะตะดั ะธะท ะผะตะบัะธะบะฐะฝัะบะธั ัะฐะทะฝะพะฒะธะดะฝะพััะตะน, ะฟะปะพัะบะพะน ะฒะฐะฝะธะปะธ. ะะพะบะพะปัะผะฑะพะฒัะต ะผะตัะพะฐะผะตัะธะบะฐะฝัะบะธะต ะฝะฐัะพะดั ะฒััะฐัะธะฒะฐะปะธ ะฒะธะฝะพะณัะฐะดะฝัั ะปะพะทั Utrzymuj¹ siê tylko przez jeden dzieñ i jeœli po¿¹dany jest owoc, nale¿y je zapylaæ rêcznie rano. Owoce powstaj¹ wy³¹cznie na dojrza³ych roœlinach, które maj¹ na ogó³ ponad 3 m d³ugoœci. Owoce to str¹ki o d³ugoœci 15-23 cm (czêsto b³êdnie nazywane fasol¹), dojrzewaj¹ce po oko³o piêciu miesi¹cach. Z tej czêœci roœliny pozyskiwany jest ekstrakt waniliowy. Wanilia to druga po szafranie najdro¿sza przyprawa. Pomimo kosztów, wanilia jest wysoko ceniona ze wzglêdu na swój smak i jest szeroko stosowana zarówno w komercyjnych, jak i domowych wypiekach, produkcji perfum i aromaterapii.

TEXT.English: Vegetables are parts of plants that are consumed by humans or other animals as food. The original meaning of the term is still used to refer to all edible plant matter, including the flowers, fruits, stems, leaves, roots, and seeds. Originally, vegetables were collected from the wild by hunter-gatherers, and entered cultivation in several parts of the world, probably during the period 10,000-7,000 BC. Vegetables can be eaten either raw or cooked and play an important role in human nutrition, being mostly low in fat and carbohydrates, but high in vitamins, minerals and dietary fiber.
TEXT.Italian: Le verdure sono parti di piante che vengono consumate dall'uomo o da altri animali come cibo. Il significato originale del termine รจ ancora usato per indicare tutta la materia vegetale commestibile, inclusi fiori, frutti, steli, foglie, radici e semi. Le verdure venivano inizialmente raccolte selvatiche dai cacciatori-raccoglitori; la loro coltivazione iniziรฒ, in diverse parti del mondo, probabilmente durante il periodo 10.000-7.000 a.C. Le verdure possono essere consumate crude o cotte e svolgono un ruolo importante nell'alimentazione umana, essendo principalmente a basso contenuto di grassi e carboidrati, ma ad alto contenuto di vitamine, minerali e fibre alimentari.
TEXT.Russian: รรณรนรฅรฑรฒรขรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฅ รฎรขรฎรนรจ รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รฎรงรญร รทร รฅรฒ รฑรบรฅรครฎรกรญรฎรฅ รฐร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฅ รจรซรจ รทร รฑรฒรผ รฐร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฟ, รข รฎรฒรซรจรทรจรฅ รฎรฒ รฑรซร รครชรจรต รดรฐรณรชรฒรฎรข รจรซรจ รฑรฅรฌรฟรญ. รรกรปรทรญรฎ รฏรฎรค รฝรฒรจรฌ รจรฌรฅรพรฒ รข รขรจรครณ รซรจรฑรฒ, รฑรฒรฅรกรฅรซรผ รจรซรจ รชรฎรฐรฅรญรผ รฑรบรฅรครฎรกรญรฎรฃรฎ รฐร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฟ.

TEXT.English: Vibranium is a rare metallic substance of extraterrestrial origin. It was brought to Earth by a meteorite hit 10,000 years ago, and at the moment there are two known versions: the most common one is the so-called "Wakandan vibranium", that possesses the ability to absorb, store, and release large amounts of kinetic energy; the other one is the Antarctic vibrianium that produces vibrations of a specific wavelength that break down the molecular bonds in other metals, causing them to liquefy.
TEXT.French: Le vibranium est une substance mรฉtallique rare d'origine extraterrestre. Il a รฉtรฉ apportรฉ sur Terre par un impact de mรฉtรฉorite il y a 10 000 ans, et ร l'heure actuelle, il existe deux versions connues : la plus courante est le soi-disant "vibranium wakandais", qui possรจde la capacitรฉ d'absorber, de stocker et de libรฉrer de grandes quantitรฉs d'รฉnergie cinรฉtique ; l'autre est le vibranium antarctique qui produit des vibrations d'une longueur d'onde spรฉcifique qui dรฉcomposent les liaisons molรฉculaires dans d'autres mรฉtaux, les faisant se liquรฉfier.
TEXT.Italian: Il vibranio รจ una rara sostanza metallica di origine extraterrestre. ร stato portato sulla Terra da un meteorite colpito 10.000 anni fa e al momento ci sono due versioni conosciute: la piรน comune รจ il cosiddetto "vibranio di Wakanda", che possiede la capacitร di assorbire, immagazzinare e rilasciare grandi quantitร di energia cinetica; l'altro รจ il vibranio antartico, che produce vibrazioni su una lunghezza d'onda specifica capaci di rompere i legami molecolari in altri metalli, provocandone la liquefazione.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wibranium[COLOR_REVERT], to rzadka metaliczna substancja pochodzenia pozaziemskiego. Zosta³ przywieziony na Ziemiê w wyniku uderzenia meteorytu 10 000 lat temu i obecnie znane s¹ dwie wersje: najpopularniejsza to tzw. "Wakandan vibranium", która posiada zdolnoœæ poch³aniania, przechowywania i uwalniania du¿ych iloœci energii kinetycznej; drugim jest wibrianium antarktyczne, które wytwarza wibracje o okreœlonej d³ugoœci fali, które rozbijaj¹ wi¹zania molekularne w innych metalach, powoduj¹c ich up³ynnienie.
TEXT.Russian: ะะธะฑัะฐะฝะธัะผ - ะฒัะผััะปะตะฝะฝัะน ะผะตัะฐะปะป, ะฟะพัะฒะปัััะธะนัั ะฒ ะฐะผะตัะธะบะฐะฝัะบะธั ะบะพะผะธะบัะฐั , ะธะทะดะฐะฒะฐะตะผัั Marvel Comics. ะะฝ ะธะทะฒะตััะตะฝ ัะฒะพะตะน ัะฒัะทัั ั ะงะตัะฝะพะน ะะฐะฝัะตัะพะน, ัะฐะบ ะบะฐะบ ะตะณะพ ะบะพัััะผ ัะดะตะปะฐะฝ ะธะท ะฒะธะฑัะฐะฝะธัะผะฐ ะธ ะฝะฐะนะดะตะฝ ะฝะฐ ะตะณะพ ัะพะดะธะฝะต ะะฐะบะฐะฝะดะฐ. ะะฝัะฐัะบัะธัะตัะบะธะน ะฒะธะฑัะฐะฝะธะน ะธะปะธ ะฐะฝัะธะผะตัะฐะปะป ัะพะทะดะฐะตััั ะธัะบััััะฒะตะฝะฝัะผ ะฟััะตะผ, ะฒ ะพัะปะธัะธะต ะพั ะฝะฐัััะฐะปัะฝะพะณะพ ะธะปะธ ะฒะฐะบะฐะฝะดะธะนัะบะพะณะพ ะฒะธะฑัะฐะฝะธัะผะฐ. ะะธะฑัะฐะฝะธัะผ ัะฐะบะถะต ัะธัะพะบะพ ะธะทะฒะตััะตะฝ ะบะฐะบ ะพะดะธะฝ ะธะท ะผะฐัะตัะธะฐะปะพะฒ, ะธัะฟะพะปัะทัะตะผัั ะดะปั ัััะพะธัะตะปัััะฒะฐ ัะธัะฐ ะะฐะฟะธัะฐะฝะฐ ะะผะตัะธะบะฐ.
รรจรครฅรฎรจรฃรฐรป

TEXT.English: A video game is an electronic game that involves interaction with a user interface to generate visual feedback on a two- or three-dimensional video display device such as a touchscreen, virtual reality headset or monitor/TV set. Since the 1980s, video games have become an increasingly important part of the entertainment industry, and whether they are also a form of art is a matter of dispute. The electronic systems used to play video games are called platforms. Video games are developed and released for one or several platforms and may not be available on others.
TEXT.Italian: Un videogioco รจ un gioco elettronico che prevede l'interazione con un'interfaccia utente per generare feedback visivi su un dispositivo di visualizzazione video bidimensionale o tridimensionale come un touchscreen, un visore per realtร virtuale, un monitor o un televisore. Dagli anni ottanta del XX secolo, i videogiochi sono diventati una parte sempre piรน importante dell'industria dell'intrattenimento, al punto che si discute se considerarli anche una forma d'arte. I sistemi elettronici utilizzati per giocare ai videogiochi sono chiamati piattaforme. I videogiochi sono sviluppati e rilasciati per una o piรน piattaforme e potrebbero non essere disponibili su altre.
TEXT.Russian: รรจรครฅรฎรจรฃรฐรป - รฝรฒรฎ รฝรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรปรฅ รจรฃรฐรป, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฏรฐรฅรครฏรฎรซร รฃร รพรฒ รขรงร รจรฌรฎรครฅรฉรฑรฒรขรจรฅ รทรฅรซรฎรขรฅรชร รฑ รฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รฒรฅรซรผรฑรชรจรฌ รจรญรฒรฅรฐรดรฅรฉรฑรฎรฌ รครซรฟ รฑรฎรงรคร รญรจรฟ รขรจรงรณร รซรผรญรฎรฉ รฎรกรฐร รฒรญรฎรฉ รฑรขรฟรงรจ รญร รขรจรครฅรฎรณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฅ. รรซรฎรขรฎ รขรจรครฅรฎรจรฃรฐรป รฒรฐร รครจรถรจรฎรญรญรฎ รฎรฒรญรฎรฑรฟรฒ รช รฐร รฑรฒรฐรฎรขรปรฌ รครจรฑรฏรซรฅรฉรญรปรฌ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎรฌ, รญรฎ รฝรฒรฎ รฒรฅรฏรฅรฐรผ รฎรงรญร รทร รฅรฒ รซรพรกรฎรฉ รฒรจรฏ รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขร รฎรฒรฎรกรฐร รฆรฅรญรจรฟ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รฌรฎรฃรณรฒ รฏรฐรฎรจรงรขรฎรครจรฒรผ รครขรณรต- รจรซรจ รฒรฐรฅรตรฌรฅรฐรญรฎรฅ รจรงรฎรกรฐร รฆรฅรญรจรฅ. รรซรฅรชรฒรฐรฎรญรญรปรฅ รฑรจรฑรฒรฅรฌรป, รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฌรปรฅ, รทรฒรฎรกรป รจรฃรฐร รฒรผ รข รขรจรครฅรฎรจรฃรฐรป รญร รงรปรขร รพรฒรฑรฟ รฏรซร รฒรดรฎรฐรฌรป; รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรฐรฎรฌ รฒร รชรจรต รฝรซรฅรฌรฅรญรฒรฎรข รฟรขรซรฟรพรฒรฑรฟ รฏรฅรฐรฑรฎรญร รซรผรญรปรฅ รชรฎรฌรฏรผรพรฒรฅรฐรป รจ รจรฃรฐรฎรขรปรฅ รฏรฐรจรฑรฒร รขรชรจ. รรฒรจ รฏรซร รฒรดรฎรฐรฌรป รขร รฐรผรจรฐรณรพรฒรฑรฟ รฎรฒ รกรฎรซรผรธรจรต รรร รครฎ รฌร รซรฅรญรผรชรจรต รฏรฎรฐรฒร รฒรจรขรญรปรต รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรข. รรฏรฅรถรจร รซรจรงรจรฐรฎรขร รญรญรปรฅ รขรจรครฅรฎรจรฃรฐรป, รฒร รชรจรฅ รชร รช ร รฐรชร รครญรปรฅ รจรฃรฐรป, รญรฅรชรฎรฃรคร รฐร รฑรฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฐร รญรฅรญรญรปรฅ, รฏรฎรฑรฒรฅรฏรฅรญรญรฎ รฑรฎรชรฐร รนร รซรจรฑรผ รข รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รญรจรจ. รรจรครฅรฎรจรฃรฐรป รฑรฒร รซรจ รฎรฑรฎรกรปรฌ รขรจรครฎรฌ รจรฑรชรณรฑรฑรฒรขร รจ รฏรฐรฎรฌรปรธรซรฅรญรญรฎรฑรฒรจ.

รรจรชรจรญรฃรจ
รรฎรงร

TEXT.English: A vine is any plant with a growth habit of trailing or climbing stems, lianas or runners. Long stems serve two purposes: vine may use rock exposures, other plants, or other supports for growth rather than investing energy in a lot of supportive tissue, enabling the plant to reach sunlight with a minimum investment of energy. It may also enable plants to colonize large areas quickly, even without climbing high. Many vines are widely cultivated as an ornamental plant or used in pharmaceutical sector.
TEXT.Italian: Una vite รจ qualsiasi pianta che cresce attraverso lunghi gambi, liane o rampicanti. Gli steli lunghi hanno due scopi: la vite puรฒ usare esposizioni rocciose, altre piante o altri supporti per la crescita, piuttosto che investire energia in molti tessuti di supporto, consentendo alla pianta di raggiungere la luce solare con un investimento minimo di energia; questo consente alle piante anche di colonizzare rapidamente grandi aree, anche senza arrampicarsi in alto. Molte viti sono ampiamente coltivate come pianta ornamentale o utilizzate nel settore farmaceutico.

TEXT.English: A vintage wine is a wine made from grapes that were all, or primarily, grown and harvested in a single specified year. Wines of superior vintages, from prestigious producers and regions, will often command much higher prices than those from average vintages. This is especially the case if wines are likely to improve further with some age in the bottle. A common, though not strictly correct, usage applies the term to any wine that is perceived to be particularly old or of a particularly high quality.
TEXT.Italian: Un vino d'annata รจ un vino ottenuto da uve che sono state tutte, o per la maggioranza, coltivate e raccolte in una determinata annata. I vini di annate superiori, provenienti da produttori e regioni prestigiose, hanno spesso prezzi molto piรน alti di quelli delle annate medie. Ciรฒ รจ particolarmente vero per i vini che migliorano con un certo invecchiamento in bottiglia. Un uso comune, sebbene non strettamente corretto, del termine lo estende a qualsiasi vino che viene percepito come particolarmente vecchio o di qualitร particolarmente elevata.

TEXT.German: Vulkanisierter Kautschuk
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรงรจรญร

TEXT.English: Vulcanized rubber is a chemically processed rubber. The term "vulcanization" traditionally referred to the treatment of natural rubber with sulfur in order to harden it, then with time grew to include also hardening of other (synthetic) rubbers via various means. Vulcanization works by forming cross-links between sections of polymer chain which results in increased rigidity and durability, as well as other changes in the mechanical and electrical properties of the material.
TEXT.French: Le caoutchouc vulcanisรฉ est un caoutchouc traitรฉ chimiquement. Le terme "vulcanisation" dรฉsignait traditionnellement le traitement du caoutchouc naturel avec du soufre afin de le durcir, puis avec le temps s'est รฉtendu pour inclure รฉgalement le durcissement d'autres caoutchoucs (synthรฉtiques) par divers moyens. La vulcanisation fonctionne en formant des liaisons croisรฉes entre les sections de chaรฎne polymรจre, ce qui entraรฎne une augmentation de la rigiditรฉ et de la durabilitรฉ, ainsi que d'autres changements dans les propriรฉtรฉs mรฉcaniques et รฉlectriques du matรฉriau.
TEXT.Italian: La gomma vulcanizzata รจ una gomma trattata chimicamente. Il termine "vulcanizzazione" si riferiva tradizionalmente al trattamento della gomma naturale con lo zolfo per indurirla, poi con il tempo รจ stato esteso per includere anche il processo di indurimento di altre gomme (sintetiche) attraverso vari mezzi. La vulcanizzazione agisce formando collegamenti incrociati tra le sezioni della catena polimerica, che si traducono in una maggiore rigiditร e durata, nonchรฉ in altri cambiamenti nelle proprietร meccaniche ed elettriche del materiale.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Kauczuk wulkanizowany[COLOR_REVERT], to guma przetwarzana chemicznie. Termin "wulkanizacja" tradycyjnie odnosi³ siê do obróbki kauczuku naturalnego siark¹ w celu jego utwardzenia, a z czasem zacz¹³ obejmowaæ tak¿e utwardzanie innych (syntetycznych) kauczuków ró¿nymi sposobami. Wulkanizacja polega na tworzeniu wi¹zañ poprzecznych pomiêdzy odcinkami ³añcucha polimeru, co skutkuje zwiêkszon¹ sztywnoœci¹ i trwa³oœci¹, a tak¿e innymi zmianami w³aœciwoœci mechanicznych i elektrycznych materia³u.

TEXT.English: The walrus (Odobenus rosmarus) is a large pinniped marine mammal with a circumpolar distribution in the Arctic Ocean and adjacent subarctic seas of the Northern Hemisphere. It is the only living species in the family Odobenidae and the genus Odobenus. The species is divided into two subspecies: the Atlantic walrus (O. r. rosmarus), found in the Atlantic sector of the Arctic, and the Pacific walrus (O. r. divergens), found in the Pacific sector of the Arctic.
TEXT.French: Le morse (Odobenus rosmarus) est un grand mammifรจre marin ร nageoires avec une distribution discontinue autour du pรดle Nord dans l'ocรฉan Arctique et les mers subarctiques de l'hรฉmisphรจre Nord. Le morse est la seule espรจce vivante de la famille des Odobenidae et du genre Odobenus. Cette espรจce est subdivisรฉe en deux sous-espรจces : le morse de l'Atlantique (O. r. rosmarus) qui vit dans l'ocรฉan Atlantique et le morse du Pacifique (O. r. divergens) qui vit dans l'ocรฉan Pacifique.
TEXT.German: Das [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Walross[COLOR_REVERT] hat fรผr den Menschen schon immer eine bedeutende Rolle gespielt. Die arktischen Vรถlker verwendeten praktisch alle Kรถrperteile eines erlegten Walrosses, sei es als Nahrung oder als Material zum Heizen (Tran), zum Hรผttenbau (Knochen und Stoรzรคhne) oder zur Herstellung von Booten und Kleidung (Haut).[NEWLINE]Durch skrupellose intensive Bejagung mit modernen Waffen zur Gewinnung des Elfenbeins, aber auch durch die Verschmutzung des Lebensraums insbesondere durch รlunglรผcke wurden die Populationen stark dezimiert. Nachdem vor allem die USA und Russland eine Reihe von Schutzmaรnahmen starteten, hat sich der Bestand aber wieder etwas erholt.

Mors pe³ni³ wa¿n¹ rolê w ¿yciu mieszkañców Arktyki, którzy polowali na niego dla miêsa, skór, t³uszczu, k³ów i koœci.

TEXT.English: Wares are goods or manufactured items produced for sale or trade. They can be made from a wide range of materials, including metal, glass, ceramic, plastic, or textiles, and serve many purposes depending on their form and function. Examples include tools, utensils, cookware, and various industrial or household goods.
TEXT.French: Les articles sont des objets et des articles manufacturรฉs, fabriquรฉs ร partir de diffรฉrents types de mรฉtaux ou de matรฉriaux. Ils servent ร de nombreuses utilisations, selon leur matรฉriau et leur portรฉe, comme les outils, les ustensiles, les ustensiles de cuisine, les armes, les piรจces, les tuyaux, etc.
TEXT.Italian: Questo tipo di oggetti vengono fabbricati e realizzati con diversi tipi di metalli o materiali. Possono servire per una serie di usi, a seconda del loro materiale e del loro scopo, come utensili, pentole, armi, parti, tubi, ecc.

TEXT.English: A warp drive is a fictional faster-than-light (FTL) spacecraft propulsion concept most strongly associated with Star Trek. It allows a spacecraft to travel between distant points in space at apparent speeds exceeding that of light, often by large factors in narrative terms. Unlike some other fictional FTL methods such as jump drives or hyperspace travel, warp drive typically does not involve instantaneous relocation; instead, the journey still takes a measurable amount of time. In many depictions, a vessel at warp velocity remains within or partially interacts with normal space rather than exiting it entirely. The concept of โwarp driveโ is closely linked to science fictionโs broader exploration of FTL travel and was popularised in early form in works such as John W. Campbellโs 1931 novel Islands of Space, though modern usage is primarily shaped by Star Trek from the 1960s onward. In physics, special relativity describes how the speed of light is a fundamental limit for objects with mass moving through spacetime and shows that the energy required to accelerate such objects increases dramatically as they approach light speed. This makes faster-than-light travel through normal space impossible under known physics. Photons, which are massless particles, always travel at the speed of light. Reaching the speed of light for any object with mass would require infinite energy. However, theoretical ideas inspired by general relativity, such as the Alcubierre metric, explore whether spacetime itself could be manipulated: expanding space behind a spacecraft and contracting it in front, allowing effective faster-than-light travel without locally breaking the speed-of-light limit. These ideas remain speculative and face unresolved issues such as extreme energy requirements and physical feasibility, including the likely need for exotic matter or negative energy densities.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Napêd warp[COLOR_REVERT], to system napêdowy statku kosmicznego szybszego od œwiat³a (FTL) wystêpuj¹cy w wielu dzie³ach science fiction, w szczególnoœci w Star Trek. Statek kosmiczny wyposa¿ony w napêd warp mo¿e podró¿owaæ z prêdkoœciami wiêkszymi od œwiat³a o wiele rzêdów wielkoœci. W przeciwieñstwie do niektórych innych technologii FTL, takich jak napêd skokowy lub hipernapêd, napêd warp nie pozwala na natychmiastow¹ podró¿ miêdzy dwoma punktami, ale wymaga mierzalnego up³ywu czasu, co jest zgodne z koncepcj¹. Teoretycznie statki kosmiczne poruszaj¹ce siê z prêdkoœci¹ warp nadal wchodz¹ w interakcjê z obiektami w "normalnej przestrzeni". Ogóln¹ koncepcjê "napêdu warp" wprowadzi³ John W. Campbell w swojej powieœci Islands of Space z 1931 roku. Szczególna teoria wzglêdnoœci Einsteina stwierdza, ¿e energia i masa s¹ wymienne, dlatego prêdkoœæ przemieszczania siê œwiat³a jest niemo¿liwa do osi¹gniêcia w przypadku obiektów materialnych, które wa¿¹ wiêcej ni¿ fotony. Problem obiektu materialnego przekraczaj¹cego prêdkoœæ œwiat³a polega na tym, ¿e wymagana jest nieskoñczenie rosn¹ca iloœæ energii kinetycznej, aby móc poruszaæ siê z prêdkoœci¹ bezmasowego fotonu. Problem ten mo¿na teoretycznie rozwi¹zaæ poprzez wypaczenie przestrzeni w celu przesuniêcia obiektu, zamiast zwiêkszania w tym celu energii kinetycznej obiektu.

TEXT.English: A washing machine (also called a laundry machine or clothes washer) is a household appliance designed to clean textiles such as clothing, towels, and bedding using water and detergent. It is distinguished from dry cleaning systems, which use non-aqueous solvents and are typically carried out in specialist facilities. Washing machines clean laundry through mechanical agitation combined with water and detergent. This agitation may be produced by tumbling in a rotating drum, by an impeller, or by an agitator, depending on the design. Modern machines typically operate through automated cycles that can include filling with water, washing, rinsing, draining, and spinning. Some models also heat water internally, though this is not universal. At the end of a cycle, washing machines usually remove a significant amount of water through high-speed spinning, but they do not fully dry the laundry unless they are washer-dryer combination units.

TEXT.Russian: รรฒรจรฐร รซรผรญร รฟ รฌร รธรจรญร - รณรฑรฒรฐรฎรฉรฑรฒรขรฎ รครซรฟ รฑรฒรจรฐรชรจ รกรฅรซรผรฟ, รฒร รชรฎรฃรฎ รชร รช รฎรครฅรฆรคร รจ รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรปรญรจ. รร รงรขร รญรจรฅ รข รฎรฑรญรฎรขรญรฎรฌ รฏรฐรจรฌรฅรญรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฒรฎรซรผรชรฎ รช รฌร รธรจรญร รฌ, รชรฎรฒรฎรฐรปรฅ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรพรฒ รขรฎรครณ รข รฎรฒรซรจรทรจรฅ รฎรฒ รตรจรฌรทรจรฑรฒรชรจ (รชรฎรฒรฎรฐร รฟ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒ ร รซรผรฒรฅรฐรญร รฒรจรขรญรปรฉ รฑรฏรฎรฑรฎรก รฎรทรจรฑรฒรชรจ รฆรจรครชรฎรฑรฒรฅรฉ, รจ รฎรฑรณรนรฅรฑรฒรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑรฏรฅรถรจร รซรจรฑรฒรฎรฌ รฏรฐรฅรครฏรฐรจรฟรฒรจรฟ) รจรซรจ รณรซรผรฒรฐร รงรขรณรชรฎรขรปรต รขร รญรญ. รรฒรจรฐรชร รขรชรซรพรทร รฅรฒ รข รฑรฅรกรฟ รฏรฎรฃรฐรณรฆรฅรญรจรฅ, รงร รฌร รทรจรขร รญรจรฅ, รฑรฒรจรฐร รญรจรฅ รข รขรฎรครฅ รจ รขรปรฆรจรฌรชรณ, รฎรกรปรทรญรฎ รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรณรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฑรฎ รฑรฒรจรฐร รซรผรญรปรฌ รฏรฎรฐรฎรธรชรฎรฌ รจรซรจ รฎรฒรกรฅรซรจรขร รฒรฅรซรฅรฌ. รรฐรฎรฑรฒรฅรฉรธร รฟ รฌร รธรจรญร รฌรฎรฆรฅรฒ รฏรฐรฎรฑรฒรฎ รฎรฒรฑรฒรจรฐรปรขร รฒรผ รฎรครฅรฆรครณ รข รขรฎรครฅ, รฏรฎรชร รฎรญร รขรชรซรพรทรฅรญร ; ร รขรฒรฎรฌร รฒรจรทรฅรฑรชรจรฅ รฌร รธรจรญรป รฌรฎรฃรณรฒ รงร รฏรฎรซรญรจรฒรผ รฏรณรฑรฒรณรพรนรณรพ รฑรฒรจรฐร รซรชรณ, รขรฐร รนร รฒรผ, รจ รญร รฃรฐรฅรขร รฒรผ รขรฎรครณ. รรฎรซรผรธรจรญรฑรฒรขรฎ รฑรฒรจรฐร รซรผรญรปรต รฌร รธรจรญ รฏรฎรฒรฐรฅรกรซรฟรพรฒ รงรญร รทรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฅ รชรฎรซรจรทรฅรฑรฒรขรฎ รขรฎรครป รครฎ รชรฎรญรถร รฑรฒรจรฐรชรจ, รฎรฒรฆรจรฌร รพรฒ รกรฅรซรผรฅ, รญรฎ รญรฅ รฏรฎรซรญรฎรฑรฒรผรพ รขรปรฑรณรธรจรขร รฅรฒ รฅรฃรฎ.

TEXT.English: Waterproof cement refers to a cement-based material or system designed to reduce water permeability, typically achieved through the use of crystalline or hydrophobic admixtures added during mixing. In practice, this usually applies to mortar or concrete, where cement paste binds fine and coarse aggregates into a hardened composite over time. The waterproofing effect is commonly produced by crystalline admixtures that react with water and unhydrated cement particles, forming insoluble crystalline structures within the pore network. These growths help block capillary pores and micro-cracks, reducing the materialโs ability to transmit water. When moisture is present, remaining unhydrated cement can continue to participate in these reactions, allowing additional crystal formation that can help seal small cracks over time. However, this self-sealing capacity is dependent on mix design, environmental conditions, and crack size, and does not guarantee complete or permanent waterproofing under all conditions.
TEXT.Italian: Il cemento impermeabile รจ un materiale composito fatto da aggregato fine e grossolano unito con un cemento fluido ("pasta di cemento") che si indurisce nel tempo, con l'aggiunta di additivi cristallini durante il dosaggio per ridurre la permeabilitร . La reazione ha luogo quando acqua e particelle di cemento non idratate entrano in contatto, formando cristalli insolubili a forma di ago, che riempiono i pori e le microcrepe. La mistura di fatto si autosigilla, poichรฉ l'esposizione costante all'acqua avvia continuamente la cristallizzazione per garantire una protezione impermeabile permanente.

TEXT.English: A water bottle is a container designed for holding drinking water, allowing it to be carried from one place to another. Water bottles are typically made from plastic, glass, or metal. Many of these materials are recyclable, although this depends on the material type and local recycling systems. After World War II, plastic manufacturing scaled rapidly, and by the 1970s polyethylene terephthalate (PET) became a key material in the creation of modern water bottles.
TEXT.French: Une bouteille d'eau est un contenant utilisรฉ pour contenir de l'eau destinรฉe ร la consommation. Cela permet ร un individu de transporter ou de porter l'eau en bouteille d'un endroit ร un autre. Une bouteille d'eau est gรฉnรฉralement fabriquรฉe en plastique, en verre ou en mรฉtal, et peut donc รชtre recyclรฉe dans la plupart des cas.

TEXT.English: Beeswax is the best-known animal wax, produced by honeybees (Apis species) and used primarily to construct the honeycomb structure in which brood is raised and honey and pollen are stored. It is secreted in liquid form by specialized wax glands on the abdomen of worker bees and solidifies into thin scales, which are then molded by the bees into hexagonal comb cells.Chemically, beeswax is a complex mixture rather than a single substance. It consists mainly of long-chain aliphatic compounds, including esters of fatty acids and long-chain alcohols, hydrocarbons, and free fatty acids. One significant class of compounds present includes palmitate-based esters such as myricyl palmitate, but it is not the dominant defining component on its own. The composition of beeswax gives it useful physical properties: it is malleable when warm, solid at room temperature, water-resistant, and chemically stable. These properties make it ideal for structural use in honeycombs as well as for human applications such as candles, cosmetics, food coatings, and historical uses in art and preservation.
TEXT.French: La cire animale la plus connue est la cire d'abeille, mais d'autres insectes sรฉcrรจtent des cires. Un composant majeur de la cire d'abeille est la substance ester myricyl palmitate qui est utilisรฉe dans la construction de leurs rayons de miel.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Whales[COLOR_REVERT] are a widely distributed and diverse group of fully aquatic placental marine mammals. Whales are creatures of the open ocean: they feed, mate, give birth, suckle and raise their young at sea. Whales range in size from the 2.6 metres (8.5 ft) and 135 kilograms (298 lb) dwarf sperm whale to the 29.9 metres (98 ft) and 190 metric tons (210 short tons) blue whale, which is the largest animal species on the planet today. Whales historically were hunted for their meat, blubber, bones (for tools), oil (for lamp fuel and lubricants), baleen (for corsets and skirt hoops), and ambergris (for fixative for perfumes). Whales are now protected by international law and it is illegal to hunt them in many jurisdictions, although limited legal whaling persists in a few countries, and some whaling activities occur in contested or illegal contexts.
TEXT.German: Walfang, die organisierte Jagd auf Wale, um Tran und andere Nebenprodukte zu gewinnen, wurde bereits um 875 nach Christus ausgeรผbt. Im 16. Jahrhundert war dies der wichtigste Industriezweig der Kรผstenregionen Spaniens und Frankreichs. Die Walfangindustrie fand weltweite Verbreitung und entwickelte sich zu einem profitablen Geschรคft. Manche Meeresregionen, entlang der Wanderrouten der Wale, hatten eine besonders dichte Walpopulation und wurden Ziel groรer Walfangflotten. Das Wachstum der Walfangindustrie hielt bis weit in das 20. Jahrhundert an. Angesichts der Tatsache, dass durch diesen Raubbau mittlerweile zahlreiche Walarten vom Aussterben bedroht waren, verhรคngten viele Lรคnder im Jahre 1969 ein generelles Walfangverbot. In den spรคten Achtziger Jahren endete der industrielle Walfang weltweit.
TEXT.Italian: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]balene[COLOR_REVERT] sono un gruppo ampiamente distribuito e diversificato di mammiferi marini placentare completamente acquatici. Le balene sono creature dell'oceano: si nutrono, si accoppiano, danno alla luce, allattano e allevano i loro piccoli in mare. Le balene variano in dimensione dai 2,6 metri e 135 chilogrammi del capodoglio nano ai 29,9 metri e 190 tonnellate delle balene blu, che sono le piรน grandi creature conosciute mai vissute. Una volta cacciate incessantemente per i loro prodotti, le balene sono ora protette dal diritto internazionale. Le balene sono in genere cacciate per la carne, il grasso, le ossa (per utensili), l'olio (per lampade e lubrificanti), il fanone (per corsetti e cerchietti per gonna) e l'ambra grigia (fissativo per profumi).

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wieloryby[COLOR_REVERT], to szeroko rozpowszechniona i zró¿nicowana grupa w pe³ni wodnych ssaków ³o¿yskowych. Rozmiary wielorybów wahaj¹ siê od 2,6 m i 135 kg kaszalota kar³owatego do 29,9 m i 190 ton p³etwala b³êkitnego, który jest najwiêkszym znanym stworzeniem, jakie kiedykolwiek ¿y³o. TEXT.English: Wheat has been a staple crop for civilizations occupying temperate zones of the planet since prehistoric times. There is archaeological evidence that bread wheat was cultivated in southern Turkestan as early as 6000 BC, though naturally occurring wheat was probably used for food purposes much earlier. Throughout the temperate zones, wheat has become a major food crop. It is particularly well suited for growth in vast, open plains like those found in the central United States.
TEXT.French: Le blรฉ est une des principales ressources agricoles de nombreuses civilisations des rรฉgions tempรฉrรฉes, et ce depuis la Prรฉhistoire. Il existe des traces archรฉologiques prouvant que le blรฉ est cultivรฉ au Sud du Turkestan depuis 6 000 ans av. J.-C., mais le blรฉ sauvage est vraisemblablement consommรฉ depuis plus longtemps. Dans les rรฉgions tempรฉrรฉes, le blรฉ est le principal produit agricole alimentaire. Il est particuliรจrement bien adaptรฉ ร la culture sur de grandes plaines ouvertes, comme celles du centre des Etats-Unis.
TEXT.German: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Weizen[COLOR_REVERT] ist ein Getreide aus der Familie der Sรผรgrรคser, dass schon zu prรคhistorischen Urzeiten von den in gemรครigten klimatischen Regionen beheimateten Vรถlkern als Grundnahrungsmittel genutzt wurde. Archรคologische Funde beweisen, dass man bereits 6000 v.Chr. im sรผdlichen Turkistan Brotweizen anbaute, wรคhrend wilder Weizen vermutlich schon viel frรผher als Nahrungsmittel verwendet wurde. In Gegenden mit gemรครigtem Klima ist Weizen die wichtigste landwirtschaftliche Feldfrucht รผberhaupt. Besonders gut gedeiht Weizen auf groรen offenen Ebenen wie im Zentrum der Vereinigten Staaten.
TEXT.Italian: Il frumento รจ stato uno degli alimenti principali delle popolazioni residenti in zone temperate sin dalla preistoria. Esistono dei reperti archeologici che dimostrano che questo cereale veniva coltivato nel Turkistan giร nel 6000 a.C., malgrado sia possibile che l'utilizzo del frumento non coltivato possa essere avvenuto molto prima. ร particolarmente indicato per la coltivazioni in grandi pianure.
TEXT.Spanish: El trigo ha sido un cultivo bรกsico para las civilizaciones que ocupaban zonas templadas del planeta desde la Prehistoria. Existen pruebas arqueolรณgicas de que el trigo para hacer pan se cultivaba en el sur del Turkestรกn allรก por el aรฑo 6000 a.C., aunque el trigo natural seguramente se usaba como alimento mucho antes. Se ha convertido en el principal cultivo en todas las regiones templadas. Estรก especialmente adaptado para su cultivo en grandes llanuras abiertas como las del centro de los Estados Unidos.

TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Pszenica[COLOR_REVERT] by³a podstawow¹ upraw¹ cywilizacji zamieszkuj¹cych umiarkowane strefy planety od czasów prehistorycznych. TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]woodwind instrument[COLOR_REVERT] is a musical instrument which produces sound when the player blows air against a sharp edge or through a reed, causing the air within its resonator (usually a column of air) to vibrate. Most of these instruments are made of wood but can be made of other materials, such as metals or plastics.
TEXT.French: Un [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]instrument ร vent[COLOR_REVERT] est un instrument de musique qui produit du son lorsque le joueur souffle de l'air contre un bord tranchant ou ร travers une anche, provoquant la vibration de l'air ร l'intรฉrieur de son rรฉsonateur (gรฉnรฉralement une colonne d'air). La plupart de ces instruments sont fabriquรฉs en bois mais peuvent รชtre fabriquรฉs avec d'autres matรฉriaux, comme des mรฉtaux ou des plastiques.
TEXT.Italian: Uno [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]strumento a fiato[COLOR_REVERT] รจ uno strumento musicale che produce suoni quando il musicista soffia attraverso un'apertura dello strumento, facendo vibrare l'aria all'interno della cassa di risonanza. La maggior parte di questi strumenti sono fatti in legno, ma possono essere costruiti anche in metallo o in plastica.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Instrument dêty drewniany[COLOR_REVERT] - instrument dêty, w którym wibratorem jest drewniany stroik, b¹dŸ krawêdŸ, o któr¹ rozpraszany jest strumieñ powietrza. Sam instrument mo¿e byæ wykonany z dowolnego materia³u, np. drewna, metalu, lub tworzywa sztucznego. Zród³em dŸwiêku w instrumentach dêtych drewnianych s¹ wibracje powietrza, których czêstotliwoœæ (wysokoœæ dŸwiêku) jest regulowana przez zakrywanie otworów w korpusie instrumentu. Poniewa¿ przykrywanie wielu otworów naraz by³oby trudne lub nawet niewykonalne, w wielu instrumentach dêtych drewnianych stosuje siê mechanizm klapkowy.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Wood[COLOR_REVERT] is a porous and fibrous structural tissue found in the stems and roots of trees and other woody plants. It is a natural composite of cellulose fibers that are strong in tension and embedded in a matrix of lignin that resists compression. In a living tree it performs a support function, enabling woody plants to grow large or to stand up by themselves. It also conveys water and nutrients between the leaves, other growing tissues, and the roots. Wood has been used for thousands of years for fuel, as a construction material, for making tools and weapons, furniture and paper. During the 20th century, it emerged as a feedstock for the production of purified cellulose and its derivatives, such as cellophane and cellulose acetate.
TEXT.German: Die harten Gewebeteile von Bรคumen und Strรคuchern (insbesondere Stamm, รste und Zweige) werden [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Holz[COLOR_REVERT] genannt. Gehรถlze werden von Menschen schon seit der Frรผhgeschichte genutzt, sie zรคhlen zu den รคltesten Nutzpflanzen. Als nachwachsender Rohstoff ist Holz bis heute eines der wichtigsten Pflanzenprodukte. Wegen seiner guten thermischen Eigenschaften wird es seit der Steinzeit als Brennstoff genutzt. Als Baustoff findet man es in diversen Bauwerken in nahezu allen Epochen der Kulturgeschichte. Neben direkten Holzbauwerken wie Palisaden, Pfahlbauten, Forts, Brรผcken oder Tรผrmen aus Holz wird es auch zur Abstรผtzung (z.B. von Bergwerksschรคchten) oder als reines Dekorationselement verwendet. Holz ist auch ein guter Werkstoff zur Herstellung von Waffen (Pfeile, Bรถgen), Rรผstungen (Schilde, Harnische) und Werkzeugen (Holzbesteck, Hobel). Schnitzer und Drechsler erschaffen Schmuckstรผcke, Spielzeug und wahre Kunstobjekte aus dem vielseitigen Material.[NEWLINE]Holz ist zwar ein regenerativer Rohstoff, aber nicht unbegrenzt vorhanden. Bestehende Wรคlder mรผssen geschรผtzt werden, da es รคuรerst schwer bis unmรถglich ist, einen komplett abgeholzten Wald wieder aufzuforsten. Nach jahrhundertelangem Raubbau, besonders im Rahmen der Industrialisierung, hat sich in Europa etwa ab dem 19. Jahrhundert eine nachhaltige Form der Holznutzung durchgesetzt.
TEXT.Italian: Il [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]legno[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un tessuto strutturale poroso e fibroso trovato negli steli e nelle radici di alberi e altre piante legnose. ร un composito naturale di fibre di cellulosa in forte tensione e incorporato in una matrice di lignina che resiste alla compressione. In un albero vivente svolge una funzione di supporto, permettendo alle piante legnose di crescere grandi o di restare in piedi da sole. Trasmette anche acqua e sostanze nutritive tra le foglie, altri tessuti in crescita e le radici. Il legno รจ stato usato per migliaia di anni come combustibile, come materiale da costruzione e per fabbricare strumenti, armi, mobili e carta. Nel corso del XX secolo, รจ emerso come materia prima per la produzione di cellulosa purificata e suoi derivati, come cellophane e acetato di cellulosa.
TEXT.Russian: รรฐรฅรขรฅรฑรจรญร รฟรขรซรฟรฅรฒรฑรฟ รฒรขรฅรฐรครฎรฉ, รดรจรกรฐรฎรงรญรฎรฉ รฒรชร รญรผรพ รฎรกรญร รฐรณรฆรฅรญรญรฎรฉ รขรฎ รฌรญรฎรฃรจรต รฐร รฑรฒรฅรญรจรฟรต. รรญร รจรฑรฏรฎรซรผรงรฎรขร รซร รฑรผ รญร รฏรฐรฎรฒรฟรฆรฅรญรจรจ รขรฅรชรฎรข รข รชร รทรฅรฑรฒรขรฅ รฒรฎรฏรซรจรขร รจ รฑรฒรฐรฎรจรฒรฅรซรผรญรฎรฃรฎ รฌร รฒรฅรฐรจร รซร รครซรฟ รญรฅรฑรชรฎรซรผรชรจรต รฒรจรฏรฎรข รฆรจรซรปรต รฏรฎรฌรฅรนรฅรญรจรฉ รข รครฎรฌร รต.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Drewno[COLOR_REVERT], to porowata i w³óknista tkanka strukturalna wystêpuj¹ca w ³odygach i korzeniach drzew i innych roœlin drzewiastych. Jest to naturalny kompozyt w³ókien celulozowych o du¿ej wytrzyma³oœci na rozci¹ganie i osadzonych w matrycy z ligniny odpornej na œciskanie. W ¿ywym drzewie pe³ni funkcjê podporow¹, umo¿liwiaj¹c roœlinom drzewiastym du¿y wzrost lub samodzielne utrzymanie siê. Przenosi tak¿e wodê i sk³adniki od¿ywcze pomiêdzy liœæmi, innymi rosn¹cymi tkankami i korzeniami. Drewno od tysiêcy lat wykorzystywane jest jako opa³, materia³ konstrukcyjny, do wyrobu narzêdzi i broni, mebli i papieru. W XX wieku sta³ siê surowcem do produkcji oczyszczonej celulozy i jej pochodnych, takich jak celofan i octan celulozy.

TEXT.German: Wolle
TEXT.Russian: รรฅรฐรฑรฒรผ

TEXT.English: Wool is the textile fiber obtained from sheep and certain other animals, including cashmere from goats, mohair from goats, qiviut from muskoxen, angora from rabbits, and other types of wool from camelids. Wool has several qualities that distinguish it from hair or fur: it is crimped, it is elastic, and it grows in staples (clusters).
TEXT.French: La laine est la fibre textile obtenue ร partir des moutons et de certains autres animaux, y compris le cachemire des chรจvres, le mohair des chรจvres, le qiviut des boeufs musquรฉs, l'angora des lapins et d'autres types de laine des camรฉlidรฉs. La laine possรจde plusieurs qualitรฉs qui la distinguent des cheveux ou de la fourrure : elle est ondulรฉe, elle est รฉlastique et elle pousse en toisons (grappes).

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Xenofauna[COLOR_REVERT] is a term used to define animal life that originated on a planet other than Earth. It is distinct from lifeforms that are engineered by humans and living off Earth.

TEXT.English: A [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Yggdrasil seed[COLOR_REVERT] can sprout a highly flexible, planet-spanning tree. They can rapidly accelerate terraforming in almost any planetary environment.
TEXT.French: Une [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]graine d'Yggdrasil[COLOR_REVERT] peut faire pousser un arbre trรจs flexible qui s'รฉtend sur toute une planรจte. Elles peuvent accรฉlรฉrer rapidement la terraformation dans presque tous les environnements planรฉtaires.

TEXT.English: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Zinc[COLOR_REVERT] is a blue-silvery, slightly brittle metal. It is refined by froth flotation of the ore, roasting, and final extraction using electricity (electrowinning). Zinc is used, in various proportions, with copper to produce brass alloys. Corrosion-resistant zinc plating of iron is the major application for zinc, as well as in electrical batteries. It is also an essential mineral for humans, including prenatal and postnatal development.
TEXT.French: Le [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]zinc[COLOR_REVERT] est un mรฉtal bleu-argentรฉ, lรฉgรจrement cassant. Il est raffinรฉ par flottation par mousse du minerai, grillage et extraction finale ร l'aide d'รฉlectricitรฉ (รฉlectroextraction). Le zinc est utilisรฉ, en diverses proportions, avec le cuivre pour produire des alliages de laiton. Le placage de fer rรฉsistant ร la corrosion au zinc est la principale application du zinc, ainsi que dans les batteries รฉlectriques. C'est รฉgalement un minรฉral essentiel pour les humains, y compris pour le dรฉveloppement prรฉnatal et postnatal.
TEXT.Italian: Lo [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]zinco[COLOR_REVERT] รจ un metallo di colore blu-argenteo e leggermente fragile. Viene raffinato mediante la flottazione, la torrefazione e l'estrazione finale mediante elettricitร (elettro-estrazione). Lo zinco viene utilizzato, in varie proporzioni, con il rame per produrre leghe di ottone. La zincatura del ferro a fini anticorrosivi รจ l'applicazione principale per lo zinco, ma viene usato anche per le batterie elettriche. ร anche un minerale essenziale per l'uomo, soprattutto durante lo sviluppo prenatale e postnatale.
TEXT.Polish: [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT]Cynk[COLOR_REVERT], to niebiesko-srebrzysty, lekko kruchy metal. Odporne na korozjê cynkowanie ¿elaza jest g³ównym zastosowaniem cynku, a tak¿e akumulatorów elektrycznych. Jest tak¿e minera³em niezbêdnym dla cz³owieka, w tym dla rozwoju prenatalnego i postnatalnego.
